title: Energy-Entropy Based Intelligence Factor for Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Burcu Kartal, Burak Berk Ustundag
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Hyper-parameter optimization in artificial neural networks (ANNs) is a computationally costly process that requires multiple trials to determine a feasible configuration. In this study, we propose a novel approach in which an independent external neural network is used to predict the performance and evaluate the feasibility of the first neural network's hyper-parameter settings. Our method aims to reduce the overall computational cost by utilizing only a small part of the dataset while providing a promising and effective hyperparameter tuning process. In this study, the proposed method consists of two neural networks. The first or main network was trained with various hyperparameter configurations, and the supervisory second network used the internal structural information of the main network to predict its performance. This approach provides efficient prediction of hyperparameter suitability and minimizes the number of trial-and-error cycles. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves reliable performance predictions. The second network, trained on only 10% of the dataset, could predict the main network’s error with a prediction error below 6%. Furthermore, the method achieved generalization for different neural architectures without requiring dataset-dependent tuning.

title: Volume Encoding Gaussians: Transfer Function-Agnostic 3D Gaussians for Volume Rendering
authors: Landon Dyken, Andres Sewell, Will Usher, Nathan Debardeleben, Steve Petruzza, Sidharth Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Visualizing the large-scale datasets output by HPC resources presents a difficult challenge, as the memory and compute power required become prohibitively expensive for end user systems. Novel view synthesis techniques can address this by producing a small, interactive model of the data, requiring only a set of training images to learn from. While these models allow accessible visualization of large data and complex scenes, they do not provide the interactions needed for scientific volumes, as they do not support interactive selection of transfer functions and lighting parameters. To address this, we introduce Volume Encoding Gaussians (VEG), a 3D Gaussian-based representation for volume visualization that supports arbitrary color and opacity mappings. Unlike prior 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) methods that store color and opacity for each Gaussian, VEG decouple the visual appearance from the data representation by encoding only scalar values, enabling transfer function-agnostic rendering of 3DGS models. To ensure complete scalar field coverage, we introduce an opacity-guided training strategy, using differentiable rendering with multiple transfer functions to optimize our data representation. This allows VEG to preserve fine features across a dataset's full scalar range while remaining independent of any specific transfer function. Across a diverse set of volume datasets, we demonstrate that our method outperforms the state-of-the-art on transfer functions unseen during training, while requiring a fraction of the memory and training time.

title: ENAS-CoAtNet: Neural Architecture Search for sEMG-Based Fatigue Detection with Convolution Attention Networks
authors: Xin Li, Wenlong Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Excessive fatigue may pose a potential risk of physiological damage to the body during muscle rehabilitation. Therefore, accurate fatigue detection is particularly important. Traditional methods for fatigue detection based on surface electromyography typically rely on hand-crafted time-domain, frequency-domain, or nonlinear features combined with shallow classifiers, which suffer from limited feature representation capacity and insufficient generalization. Deep learning methods have been widely used in fatigue detection tasks. Although such methods could extract nonlinear features, the process of designing their network architectures was time-consuming and inefficient. To address these limitations, this paper proposed a fatigue detection algorithm based on efficient neural architecture search. The proposed method automatically searches for optimal network structures tailored for fatigue state representation, thereby achieving collaborative, data-driven optimization of both feature extraction and classification models. Experimental results on our self-constructed sEMG dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 95.62% in fatigue recognition, significantly outperforming traditional feature-based methods and classic deep learning architectures, thereby validating its effectiveness and superiority.

title: PVAdoptAgent: Simulating Residential Photovoltaic Adoption via an LLM Framework with Dynamic Decision Intervals
authors: Tongyun Li, Kang Yao, Junwei Liu, Fuzhen Shen, Juan Feng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Driven by the global imperative for urban carbon neutrality, this paper introduces PVAdoptAgent (Photovoltaic Adoption Agent), an LLM-based framework designed for high-fidelity simulation of residential Photovoltaic(PV) adoption behaviors. Our core contribution, the Dynamic Decision Interval (DDI) mechanism, replaces rigid fixed-step updates with a decision process triggered by external perturbations and internal cognitive evolution. This design accurately captures the decision inertia inherent in low-frequency, high-cost household investments. Additionally, a Reflexion-based memory module enables agents to evaluate historical rejection rationales, ensuring cognitive consistency over multi-year horizons. Validated against empirical data from Shenzhen (2020-2025), the framework achieves a mean annual error of 5.16%. Beyond fitting accuracy, PVAdoptAgent serves as a generative policy sandbox that processes unstructured semantic information. It provides an interpretable tool for precise energy transition policy-making and offers a novel paradigm for simulating low-frequency, high-involvement decision behaviors.

title: Integrating Large Models with RAG Technology: An Applied Research on Enterprise Intelligent Q&A Assistants
authors: Ziwu Long, Can Liu, Ruirui Zhao, Zibo Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: As enterprise digital transformation continues to advance, the demand for efficient and reliable knowledge services is increasing. Conventional enterprise support methods often suffer from scattered knowledge resources, inefficient manual search, and limited knowledge reuse. To address these issues, this paper presents an enterprise question-answering approach that combines large language models (LLMs) with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). The proposed approach covers document parsing, knowledge base construction, text embedding, semantic retrieval, reranking, and grounded answer generation. Experimental results and application practice show that the system improves answer accuracy, response efficiency, and knowledge utilization in enterprise scenarios. The study offers a practical reference for developing domain-oriented intelligent Q&A services in organizational environments.

title: Benchmarking Open-Loop Central Pattern Generator Controllers for Lower-Limb Exoskeleton Walking Trajectories
authors: Hod Pery, Tal Ben Eli, Eldad Holdengreber
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Central Pattern Generators (CPGs) enable rhythmic, biologically inspired control of robotic locomotion. This study presents a comparative evaluation of three CPG models: Hopf, Matsuoka, and sinusoidal implemented on a simplified lower-limb exoskeleton with three actuated joints per leg (hip, knee, and ankle). Each controller was simulated in MATLAB and validated using IMU measurements from a physical prototype. All models reproduced the fundamental gait frequency (0.6 Hz), though accuracy varied: hip RMSEs were below 1°, while knee and ankle errors reached up to 19° for Hopf. PCA analysis revealed that Hopf generated clean and stable cyclic patterns, the sinusoidal method produced smooth but less adaptive motion, and Matsuoka introduced richer variability with reduced regularity. Power analysis under loaded and unloaded conditions showed similar average consumption (4.9 W) but distinct stability profiles. These results demonstrate the trade-offs between simplicity, adaptability, and energy robustness in selecting CPG controllers for exoskeleton locomotion.

title: Automated Binary Code Analysis Technology Enhanced by Variable Knowledge Graph
authors: Shunda Meng, Bing Xia, Yi Lin, Jiabin Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the escalating threats to software supply chain security, binary code analysis plays a critical role in malicious software detection, vulnerability mining, and software composition analysis. However, traditional binary analysis techniques face challenges such as symbol removal, contextual semantic fragmentation, and obfuscation interference. Although emerging large language models (LLMs) possess strong semantic understanding capabilities, they often suffer from hallucinations due to a lack of domain-specific knowledge. This paper proposes an automated binary code analysis technique enhanced by a Variable Knowledge Graph (VKG). The approach first extracts variable-level entities and relationships through decompilation and static analysis, constructing a structured VKG stored in Neo4j. Second, it designs a graph-text joint reasoning engine based on ToG-2 (Think-on-Graph 2.0), which leverages multi-hop retrieval to obtain deep dataflow information from the VKG and integrates external heterogeneous textual knowledge to guide LLMs in interpretable semantic recovery. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with traditional static analysis tools and pure LLM baselines, this technique improves function identification accuracy by 18.5% and achieves an F1 score of 0.83 in variable semantic recovery, significantly reducing model hallucination rates.

title: Agent Evolution: Capability-Mechanism Co-evolution
authors: Yifei Chen, Chi Zhang, Xinshuo Zheng, Shuai Tang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Large language models are transitioning from text generators to agent systems capable of autonomous decision-making. These systems exhibit four distinct evolutionary stages: L1 fixed workflows (assisted execution), L2 endogenous single-agent (autonomous planning), L3 distributed multi-agent (collaborative division of labor), and L4 autonomous evolutionary systems (full lifecycle closed-loop). Improvements in model capabilities primarily drive stage transitions. When models cannot directly solve complex tasks, three core mechanisms compensate for capability gaps: tool invocation has evolved from regex matching to progressive context disclosure, reasoning has advanced from single-chain generation to dynamic planning with knowledge hypergraphs, and memory management has shifted from passive truncation to policy network-driven intelligent scheduling. We propose a capability-mechanism matching framework: the evolution of agents from L1 to L4 essentially reflects engineering architectures that introduce three core mechanisms to compensate for model capability gaps and enable synergistic co-evolution.

title: Random Forest Driven Dietary Habit Analysis and Preventive Health Prediction for Intelligent Nutrition Management
authors: D. Shanthi, P. Shreya, R. Sneka, S. Swetha, V. Swetha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Dietary habits play a critical role in determining overall health and the likelihood of developing chronic diseases. Increasing prevalence of lifestyle-related health disorders has created a strong need for effective dietary monitoring and preventive healthcare strategies. Many individuals follow unhealthy eating patterns that contribute to obesity, cardiovascular complications, metabolic disorders, and other nutrition-related conditions. Early identification of risky dietary behaviors can support preventive health management and promote healthier lifestyles. However, systematic dietary analysis and health risk identification remain challenging due to the complexity of nutritional patterns and variability among individuals. NutriGuard AI presents a structured framework for dietary habit analysis and preventive health prediction aimed at supporting personalized nutrition management. The proposed system analyzes dietary intake patterns, lifestyle attributes, and health indicators to determine preventive health risk levels. Data related to nutritional consumption, body mass index, physical activity levels, and metabolic indicators are collected and processed for predictive analysis. The framework enables identification of dietary behaviors associated with potential health risks and facilitates early preventive action. The proposed approach emphasizes structured data processing, predictive modeling, and health risk categorization to improve preventive healthcare assessment. Analysis of dietary patterns enables recognition of harmful nutritional behaviors and identification of individuals requiring dietary intervention. The framework contributes to improved nutritional awareness and assists healthcare monitoring systems in identifying preventive health risks. The proposed system supports efficient dietary habit monitoring, improves accuracy in preventive health risk identification, and contributes to the development of intelligent nutrition management systems. Such predictive systems can support public health monitoring, personalized dietary planning, and preventive healthcare strategies aimed at reducing lifestyle-related health risks.

title: Lightweight Encryption and Cross-Domain Collaborative Security Management Technologies for Low-Altitude Intelligent Networks
authors: Haifeng Shan, Jinyi Liu, Chen Yuan, Zhanfeng Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of lightweight secure computing and trusted collaborative management arising from the highly heterogeneous and dynamically changing node resources and complex cross-domain business collaboration in low-altitude intelligent networks. To solve this problem, firstly, a dynamic encryption strategy selection engine based on multi-dimensional resource awareness is designed to achieve adaptive matching between security strength and node computing power; secondly, a cross-domain identity alliance mechanism integrating consortium blockchain and attribute-based encryption is constructed to support fine-grained, decentralized, and trusted data sharing; finally, a lightweight Byzantine fault-tolerant consensus algorithm is proposed to ensure low-latency, reliable synchronization and automatic execution of cross-domain management strategies. The experiment shows that the scheme can reduce encryption overhead by over 77%, reduce cross-domain authentication latency by about 59.9%, and maintain a service success rate of 94.2% when 30% of nodes fail, effectively achieving collaborative optimization of security and performance under resource constraints.

title: A Unified Semantic Space-Based Backend Frame-work for Speech Spoofing Detection
authors: Yuxiao Qiu, Gang Liu, Zexuan Liu, Yuxi Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Graph-attention-based spectro-temporal modeling, as used in AASIST, relies on explicitly defined heterogeneous graph topologies whose structural necessity remains unclear and computationally costly. This work reformulates relational modeling from a unified attention semantic space by replacing customized graph attention with standard multi-head self-attention (MHSA), while preserving the original two-stream topology and feature space. Type-aware projections maintain modality heterogeneity, and a learnable attention fusion module replaces heuristic max fusion. By learning node dependencies through Query–Key–Value similarity in a fully connected attention space, the proposed model implicitly captures spectro-temporal topology without predefined edges. Experiments on ASVspoof 2019, 2021, and 2024 demonstrate competitive or superior EER compared to AASIST2 and AASIST3, improved cross-dataset robustness, and reduced inference latency and training memory. These results suggest that unified MHSA provides an efficient and expressive alternative to customized graph attention for speech spoofing detection.

title: End-To-End Imitation Learning for Socially Aware Navigation using Memory-based Trajectory and Action Planning
authors: Alejandro Olivas, Miguel Ángel Muñoz-Bañón, Fernando Torres
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The increasing deployment of service robots and autonomous vehicles operating in human-populated environments underscores the importance of planning trajectories to ensure pedestrian comfort while optimizing robot travel times. End-to-end models are promising for shortcutting the complex problem of pedestrian detection, trajectory prediction, and robot movement planning by using a single network. Imitation Learning (IL) has been widely adopted in self-driving because it enables implicit understanding of complex environments and expert driving behaviour, whereas socially aware navigation has usually relied on Reinforcement Learning (RL) approaches that learn from abstracted environment representations, prioritizing information relevant to human interaction. In this research, we present Memory-based Trajectory and Action Planning (MTAP), an IL approach extended for socially aware navigation to perform intelligent pedestrian avoidance while maintaining a deeper understanding of the environment and the expert driving behaviour. Our method predicts the trajectory and the control action commands directly from the information of the sensors and the global target, employing a recurrent layer to anticipate pedestrian movements based on past frames. The network is trained and evaluated in the CARLA simulator, where we introduce a benchmark for assessing pedestrian avoidance efficacy, presenting also new social navigation metrics to measure both trajectory efficiency and pedestrian comfort during avoidance maneuvers.

title: FORM-IDS: A Lightweight yet High-Fidelity Intrusion Detection Framework via Formal Verification and Knowledge Distillation
authors: Huale Li, Yanwei Chen, Dingzhong Cai, Zhaobin Wang, Hualong Wu, Arun Kumar Sangaiah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: In practical IoT and IIoT deployments, intrusion detection faces significant challenges due to overlapping feature distributions of mixed attack vectors and the scarcity of computational resources on edge devices. Conventional deep learning-based detectors often struggle with instability across diverse environments and high false alarm rates. To address these issues, this paper proposes FORM-IDS, a lightweight yet high-fidelity intrusion detection framework tailored for resource-constrained edge scenarios. By integrating multi-teacher relational knowledge distillation, our approach effectively transfers complex decision patterns from large-scale ensembles into a compact student network, ensuring high detection capability without the computational burden. Furthermore, a novel runtime formal verification module is introduced to enforce logical safety constraints, rigorously filtering spurious alerts and enhancing decision reliability. Extensive experiments on multiple heterogeneous datasets show that FORM-IDS achieves competitive classification performance and robust temporal generalization against both the DTL-IDS lineage baselines and two additional recent IDS baselines. Crucially, the framework significantly suppresses false positives while maintaining low inference latency, making it a promising option for real-time edge security.

title: Imitation-Pretrained Q-Guided Diffusion Policy for Sample-Efficient Autonomous Driving
authors: Xingyu Liu, Dejun Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address the low sample efficiency and unstable early-stage exploration of online reinforcement learning in autonomous driving continuous control, this paper proposes a two-stage framework that combines imitation pretraining with Q-guided diffusion policy optimization. Expert driving data collected in CARLA are first used to pretrain a diffusion policy through behavior cloning. The pretrained policy is then further optimized online by jointly minimizing the behavior cloning loss and the value learning loss. Experiments on a lane-keeping task in CARLA show that the proposed method achieves higher sample efficiency in the early training stage and exhibits a more stable learning process than the online reinforcement learning baseline without pretraining.

title: High-density, Identified Cell Recordings from Motor Cortex of Awake Behaving Macaques using Multiple 1024-channel CMOS Probes
authors: G.N. Angotzi, A.M.E. Baker, M. Vincenzi, G. Orban, J.F. Ribeiro, V. Tenorio, L. Berdondini, S. N. Baker
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective/Background: Recording bioelectric signals from large populations of single neurons in the primate brain remains challenging. Chronic implants offer limited coverage (∼100 channels) and sample fixed cortical regions, while acutely inserted electrodes allow broader access via multiple daily penetrations. We aimed to develop a CMOS-based probe with high electrode-channel density, and optimized procedures for acute large-scale single-unit recordings in behaving monkeys. Methods: We designed a novel single-shank SiNAPS CMOS probe for acute recordings in monkeys with additional integrated multiplexing circuits to reduce output lines. A multi-probe system enables synchronous sampling at 20 kHz/channel from two SINAPS-NHP probes during repeated insertions into the motor cortex of behaving macaques. We developed methods to identify neurons via antidromic activation. Results: The probe (10.7 mm × 158 μm × 50 μm) samples neural activity from 1,024 electrodes (14 × 14 μm², 30 μm pitch) arranged in four columns and reaches an electrode-channel density of 304.4 channels/mm2. A pilot hole facilitates dural penetration, and optimized insertion procedures allow recordings from diverse cortical sites. Some neurons were identified as pyramidal tract cells projecting to the spinal cord. Conclusion: Each probe enables monitoring of intracortical areas of 7.75 × 0.1 mm², detecting hundreds of single neurons per session, and reaches deep regions such as the anterior bank of the central sulcus, rich in corticospinal cells. Significance: This technology and methods unlock routine acute recordings from 2,048 channels with single-neuron resolution and cell-type identification, advancing the neurophysiological toolkit for primate research.

title: Semi Circular Crown Shaped UWB Antenna
authors: K. Sumathi, P. Rajesh Kumar, M. Mithun, I. Mohammed Haris, P. Santhosh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: In this work, the antenna for satellite application enveloped from four steps of designs with unique output for each model. Antenna dimensions are 30 by 30 by 1.6 mm; the physical structure of the antenna is made of an FR-4 dielectric material that has a relative dielectric constant of (ε) 4.4. The thickness of such material is 1.6 mm. UWB antennas are used for short-range wireless communication and high precision. The suggested antenna is suitable for x-band and c-band frequency. The proposed antenna is modeled and simulated in HFSS, and key performance characteristics including return loss (S11), gain, and voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) are examined for performance validation.

title: Dynamic Grounding Coordination for Sim-to-Real Transfer in Multi-Agent Traffic Control
authors: Wang Qiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) is a powerful paradigm for complex Urban Traffic Control (TSC), yet its real-world performance is severely hindered by the simulation-to-reality (Sim-to-Real) gap. Recent advancements, such as Joint-Local Grounded Action Transformation (JL-GAT), have effectively narrowed this gap by leveraging local information sharing among agents. However, to prevent decision conflicts known as the "cascading invalidation effect," JL-GAT relies on static, predefined coordination mechanisms like fixed patterns or probabilistic rules. This reliance limits its adaptability in dynamic and non-stationary traffic environments. To address this limitation, we propose the Dynamic Grounding Coordination (DGC) framework. DGC innovatively formulates the inter-agent grounding coordination as a real-time Maximum Weight Independent Set (MWIS) problem, which is solved using an efficient distributed greedy algorithm. This approach enables intelligent allocation of grounding opportunities to the most critical agents based on instantaneous traffic conditions. Experiments on a widely-used TSC benchmark demonstrate that DGC not only outperforms existing coordination mechanisms in standard scenarios but also exhibits superior robustness and adaptability in dynamic settings with abrupt traffic flow changes. Our work provides a more adaptive and efficient coordination solution, advancing the deployment of MARL policies in real-world traffic systems.

title: A Resilient Machine Learning Framework for Adversarially Robust Intrusion Detection in IoT Environments
authors: M. Manikandan, P Vijayaragavan, R. Buvanesvari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The widespread adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) systems in crucial sectors such as smart cities, healthcare, industrial facilities, and transportation has greatly expanded the potential points of attack for our digital infrastructure. Although machine learning-powered systems are effective at spotting malicious activities, their susceptibility to deliberate manipulation and constantly changing attack methods reduces their long-term dependability and effectiveness. This paper introduces a resilient framework for recognizing intrusions within IoT environments. It's built upon a learning approach specifically designed to withstand adversarial tactics, aiming to improve detection precision while ensuring stability even under aggressive and shifting conditions. Our proposed method integrates robust learning techniques with optimized ways of representing data. This helps to reduce the impact of malicious alterations and unfamiliar attack patterns. Extensive tests conducted on established IoT security datasets confirm that our model achieves a high level of detection accuracy, demonstrates enhanced resilience against deceptive inputs, and maintains consistent performance across diverse network traffic scenarios. By embedding resilience directly into the learning process, this framework supports sustainable cybersecurity efforts and plays a role in creating reliable and future-ready IoT security architectures.

title: Plant Disease and Pest Detection: A Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Munish Kumar Bhatia, Suresh Kaswan, Sandeep Singh Kang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Plant diseases and pests are a big threat to food security because they can kill 20 to 40% of plants around the world. Conventional methods for identifying plant diseases and pests are sluggish, subjective, and reliant on expert judgment, frequently leading to procrastination and overuse of pesticides. This paper presents a lightweight, on-device, real-time framework for detecting plant diseases and pests, utilizing an optimized MobileNetV2 backbone, transfer learning, and aggressive data augmentation. The framework is designed to work on devices with limited resources and does not need to connect to the cloud. The framework uses TensorFlow Lite to change the model's data type from FP32 to INT8 after training. This makes the model less than 10 MB in size and takes less than 50 ms to make a decision, which makes it good for real-time use. The framework was tested on the New Plant Diseases Dataset, which had 87,000 images from 38 classes. It got an accuracy of 93.89%, a macro F1-score of 0.9390, and a weighted F1-score of 0.9392. This means it was more accurate than other lightweight models that have been reported in the literature. Standardized end-to-end latency benchmarking of the framework on mobile-class CPUs demonstrates its readiness for practical deployment, thereby confirming its appropriateness as a precision agriculture tool for farmers to identify plant diseases and pests in real-time.

title: Probabilistic Modeling of Human Driver’s Natural Behavior Distribution: An Ensemble Learning and GMM Framework for Car-Following Model
authors: Huihua Gao, Hong Chen, Ting Qu, Yongfu Li, Yunfeng Hu, Bingzhao Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Probabilistic modeling of the natural distribution of human driving behavior is crucial for constructing realistic and dynamic traffic simulation environments. To capture the complex, high-dimensional, and multi-modal characteristics of driving behavior, this paper proposes an ensemble-based probabilistic modeling framework. A Bayesian Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) is employed as a base predictor to model the multi-modal distribution, while ensemble learning is utilized to reduce model bias and improve robustness. To address challenges associated with high-dimensional features and limited sample sizes, dimensionality reduction and weighted bootstrap sampling are introduced to construct diverse meta models. An error evaluation network is developed to assess prediction quality in terms of mean, variance, and reliability. To handle distributional shifts across scenarios, an adaptive meta model with online learning capability is proposed for dynamic parameter updating and improved transferability. Furthermore, a reliability–uncertainty fusion strategy inspired by mean–variance theory is designed to dynamically assign optimal weights to individual meta models. Experiments on three datasets—HighD, SPMD, and MTBD—demonstrate superior performance in accuracy, robustness, and generalization.

title: Attenuation Correction for Flexible MR Hardware in PET/MR Using MLAA-Guided Template Registration
authors: Nicole Jurjew, Simon Wan, Paul Schleyer, Mohammadreza Teimoorisichani, Cameron Anderson, Marilena Rega, Alexandra Mackenzie, David Atkinson, Kris Thielemans
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: Flexible Magnetic Resonance (Imaging) (MR) hardware, such as body coils, can cause substantial attenuation of the Positron Emission Tomography (PET) signal in PET/MR imaging. Despite this, such hardware is typically not accounted for in clinical attenuation correction (AC), as its variable positioning and invisibility in standard MR sequences present significant challenges. In this study, we investigate the use of Maximum Likelihood Estimation of Attenuation and Activity (MLAA) guided registration of template hardware attenuation maps (μ-maps) to support AC for flexible hardware on PET data without Time Of Flight (TOF) information. Assuming the patient or phantom attenuation as known, attenuation maps beyond the patient outline were reconstructed from emission data and used as reference images for the non-rigid registration of pre-defined hardware template attenuation maps. The approach was evaluated and compared to the performance of MLAA-reconstructed attenuation alone on both phantom and patient data acquired on the Siemens Biograph mMR, including <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-Fluorodeoxyglucose (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG) and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">68</sup>Ga-DOTATATE tracers, and at various count levels. For the phantom data, both MLAA-only and MLAA-guided registration of template hardware attenuation maps improved quantification. Registration-based correction generally resulted in closer agreement with the ground truth, with residual errors of up to ≈5% that were largely localized, while MLAA-only reconstructions showed deviations of up to ≈10%, tending to be more spatially extended. The method also tolerates moderate errors in MR-based AC and non-physical deformations of the hardware template, with quantification most sensitive to gross misplacement of the hardware. These findings suggest that MLAA-guided registration offers a practical and effective solution to address the current gap in attenuation correction for flexible hardware in clinical PET/MR imaging, also in non-TOF settings.

title: TES Bolometer Design and Testing for the Tomographic Ionized-carbon Mapping Experiment Millimeter Array
authors: Victoria L. Butler, James J. Bock, Dongwoo T. Chung, Abigail T. Crites, Clifford Frez, Annsley Greathouse, King Lau, Ian Lowe, Dan P. Marrone, Evan C. Mayer, Benjamin J. Vaughan, Michael Zemcov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Transition Edge Sensor (TES) bolometers are a well-established technology with a strong track record in experimental cosmology, making them ideal for current and future radio astronomy instruments. The Tomographic Ionized-carbon Mapping Experiment (TIME), in collaboration with JPL, has developed advanced silicon-nitride leg-isolated superconducting titanium detectors for 200-300 GHz observations of the Epoch of Reionization. TIME aims to fabricate a total of 1920 high-frequency (HF) and low-frequency (LF) detectors to fully populate the focal plane. TIME has successfully developed HF (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 230-325$</tex-math></inline-formula> GHz) and LF (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 183-230$</tex-math></inline-formula> GHz) wafers that are physically robust and perform well at cryogenic temperatures (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>300 mK). Recent laboratory tests have shown high optical efficiencies for the LF wafers (30-40%), but low device yield for the HFs. To address this, new HF modules have been designed with improved cabling and a reduced backshort distance, and are expected to perform similarly to LFs in a similar lab setting. We report on the development of these detectors as well as recent laboratory and on-sky tests conducted at the Arizona Radio Observatory's (ARO) 12-meter prototype antenna at Kitt Peak National Observatory.

title: Hybrid Adversarial Retraining Approach For A Proactive Network Intrusion Detection System
authors: Noora Al Roken, Hakim Hacid, Ahmed Bouridane, Abir Hussian
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Artificial intelligence provides benefits across many areas, including network security. It also enables novel attacks and new security risks. However, AI-generated attacks now outpace the capabilities of current defense systems. Therefore, it is crucial to implement proactive security measures to identify unknown threats and protect digital systems. Existing research is limited by small or outdated datasets, data from virtual machines, inefficient or complex behavior-based methods, and reliance on a single adversarial condition. To address this, our study developed a new attack-detection framework. We used a proactive approach with a hybrid retraining technique that integrates both black- and white-box attacks. The framework combines two different attack concealment methods with a two-stage classification method to enhance detection accuracy and reduce false negatives. We joined two large network attack datasets, CICIDS2017 and CSE-CIC-IDS2018, to train and test the framework. Experiments show that our intrusion detection system achieved high detection rates. The two-layer convolutional neural network, using our all-attacks adversarial retraining method, demonstrated superior generalization across 12 adversarial attack algorithms, achieving an overall accuracy of 89%. This finding shows that simpler models can still provide robust protection with our hybrid retraining approach. Our method improved accuracy by up to 7.02% on CICIDS2017 and 3.7% on CSE-CIC-IDS2018. These results demonstrate that our method provides a practical and reliable defense against real-world network security threats.

title: A Random PWM Technique to Reduce Switching Frequency Harmonics for Induction Motor Drives
authors: Shaya Abou Jawdeh, Pengwei Li Pengwei, Ali Bazzi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Conventional PWM techniques are widely used in electric drive systems. They generate substantial harmonic content in the output current waveform at the switching frequency, its harmonics, and sidebands. These harmonics significantly contribute to high frequency noise, mechanical vibrations, and electromagnetic interference (EMI), both conducted and radiated, potentially disrupting the performance of nearby systems and equipment. To mitigate the high-frequency harmonics in the output current, this paper proposes a novel random PWM technique for electric drives. The approach is based on generating different random frequencies based on a Weiner process for each phase leg of the inverter. The aim is to spread the output current harmonic spectrum and minimize the amplitude of high frequency harmonics, thereby simplifying the filtering process for enhancing power quality, reducing EMI, and addressing related problems. The effectiveness of the proposed technique is assessed through key metrics including the output current harmonic power density spectrum, total harmonic distortion and the common-mode voltage. Results are obtained using MATLAB/Simulink simulations, and experimental validation which are then analyzed and compared against other PWM techniques.

title: Multi-Objective Neural Architecture Search for Cognitive Diagnosis: Balancing Accuracy and Interpretability With Probabilistic Models
authors: Yifei Sun, Mingkai Duan, Sicheng Hou, Shi Cheng, Maoguo Gong, Zhi-Hui Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Cognitive diagnosis (CD) is a fundamental task within computational social systems, essential for personalizing learning in intelligent education by assessing a learner’s fine-grained knowledge proficiency. A critical dilemma exists in designing cognitive diagnosis models (CDMs): conventional psychometric models are interpretable but often simplistic, while complex deep learning models achieve high accuracy at the cost of becoming uninterpretable “opaque models.” This lack of transparency is a major barrier to trust and adoption in real-world educational social systems. To address this challenge, this article introduces probabilistic model-building for cognitive architecture search (PMB-CAS), a novel framework that automates the discovery of CDMs that balance diagnostic accuracy with structural interpretability. We reframe the design process as a multi-objective optimization problem, balancing diagnostic performance [area under the curve (AUC)] with a refined, operator-weighted model interpretability score (MIS) that fairly assesses topological complexity. The framework navigates a flexible, tree-based search space using a probabilistic model-building strategy to efficiently discover promising architectures. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that PMB-CAS discovers a portfolio of Pareto-optimal models that not only achieve state-of-the-art accuracy, outperforming established baselines, but also offer varying degrees of structural transparency. This work provides educators and stakeholders with a spectrum of trustworthy solutions and establishes a new paradigm for developing effective and verifiable models for computational social systems.

title: GNSS Induced Spoofing Detection Based on the Sum of Carrier Phases of Early and Late Branches
authors: Wenyi Wang, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: The Early Late Phase (ELP) is a Signal Quality Monitoring (SQM) method for spoofing detection. It detects spoofing by tracking carrier phase inconsistencies between the Early and Late branches during spoofing induction. However, after the spoofing and authentic correlation functions fully separate, the overlay distortion disappears(indicating that the receiver has been successfully spoofed). At this stage, the accumulated frequency offset introduces a common carrier-phase shift in both branches, and the phase difference cancels this offset, degrading ELP performance. To address this issue, an enhanced ELP method is proposed. The key innovation is using the sum of carrier phases from two tracking branches as a new SQM metric. Research shows that this sum exhibits a significant non-zero offset during both the spoofing induction phase and after a successful attack. Consequently, spoofing can be detected continuously and effectively. The proposed method was evaluated using the Texas Spoofing Test Battery (TEXBAT). Compared to traditional SQM metrics, it maintains high sensitivity during induction and remains effective after successful spoofing. Thus, detection efficiency and reliability are significantly improved across various scenarios. However, this method is inapplicable to spoofing scenarios lacking a cumulative carrier phase shift during the induction phase.

title: DRL-Driven Robust Topology Control and Flow Scheduling for SDN-Enabled Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Yin Wang, Na Xia, Xu Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Node failures in underwater acoustic sensor networks (UASNs), caused by network attacks or energy depletion, can trigger cascading failures and compromise service quality. To address this, we propose a joint optimization scheme to enhance network robustness and prolong network lifetime. Network topology optimization is reformulated as an integer nonlinear problem, and a centralized control framework is developed based on software-defined networking (SDN). Within this framework, a scale-free topology evolution model is introduced to generate the initial topology, aiming to enhance the network’s robustness against random attacks. Subsequently, a robustness enhancement algorithm based on actor–critic deep reinforcement learning is developed for the resulting topology. The agent interacts with the topological environment, learns and evaluates robustness under various topologies, and intelligently adjusts node links to enhance resistance to malicious attacks. Furthermore, we develop an SDN-enabled flow scheduling algorithm that balances traffic over multiple paths by minimizing the maximum energy pressure, thereby maximizing network lifetime. Numerical simulations show that the cascading robustness of our approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art schemes, including DDLP, FA-SFTC, ETFLA, and Initial-BA, by 17.88%, 38.6%, 113.62%, and 117.71%, respectively. In terms of network lifetime, our scheme consistently improves upon FA-SFTC, DDLP, ETFLA, FAA, and VODA by 6%, 58.21%, 41.95%, 27.19%, and 149.65%, respectively.

title: Multi-Port Segmented Event-Triggered Control for USV-UAV Cooperative System: A VO-driven Dynamic Cross-Bridge Strategy
authors: Guoqing Zhang, Guipeng Yao, Jiqiang Li, Shaocheng Tong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In the cruising mission of the unmanned surface vessel-unmanned aerial vehicle (USV-UAV) cooperative plant, although the UAV is capable of executing aerial maneuvers, it still faces inevitable obstacle avoidance and control challenges in narrow waterways and cross-bridge scenarios. As such, this study proposes a variable-speed multi-port segmented threshold event-triggered cooperative collision avoidance control algorithm based on the geometric velocity obstacle method (GVO). In the guidance module, first define the obstacle zone based on the detected bridge dimensions. Then, by setting safety thresholds and target points, implement a bridge collision avoidance guidance function with an automatic selection mechanism for the narrow waterway navigation mission. In the control module, in the presence of bridge obstacles, a variable-speed multi-port segmented threshold event triggering mechanism (MSETM) is designed for path tracking and collision avoidance missions to ensure the coordination of the USV-UAV cooperative plant at the beginning and end of collision avoidance. Furthermore, a multi-layer neural networks (MNNs) is adopted to approximate the system’s nonlinearities, achieving high approximation accuracy and computational efficiency. Stability of the algorithm is proven using Lyapunov stability theory, and its effectiveness is validated through simulation experiments.

title: An FPGA-Calibrated Modulo ADC for High-Dynamic-Range Signal Acquisition
authors: Zeyuan Li, Wenyi Yan, Lu Gan, Guoquan Li, Hongqing Liu
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Circuits and Systems
abstract: Conventional analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) suffer from irreversible clipping when input amplitudes exceed their fixed full-scale range. Modulo ADCs address this by folding the input signal prior to quantization; however, stable hardware operation under deep-folding conditions and experimental validation of algorithmic recoverability remain outstanding challenges. This paper presents a calibrated FPGA-based modulo ADC platform in which a finite state machine with multi-bit update control governs folding dynamics, replacing instantaneous comparator-triggered feedback to ensure deterministic and stable operation. A controlled under-compensation calibration strategy converts fold-dependent threshold mismatch and oscillatory instability into a bounded constant residual, enabling reliable folding at high folding depths. The platform achieves a dynamic range expansion exceeding two orders of magnitude while maintaining signal fidelity comparable to that of the standalone ADC. Experimental validation across diverse waveforms confirms robust signal acquisition and demonstrates the practical feasibility of dynamic-range extension using modulo sampling principles.

title:
Ferroelectric/Dielectric Spacer Engineering in Negative capacitance MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    FinFETs: An Insight into Digital, Analog/RF and Circuit Applications
authors: Kotha Venkata Ramakrishna, Sresta Valasa, Narendar Vadthiya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This work for the first time examines the idea of replacing the dielectric (DE) spacers with ferroelectric (FE) spacers in the extension areas of MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> based Negative Capacitance (NC) FinFET using a calibrated TCAD setup. Four spacer configurations i.e., both-side air spacers (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub>), source-side air with drain-side FE (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>), source-side FE with drain-side air (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>), and both-side ferroelectric (FE) spacers (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>) are investigated. Among these, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> outperforms in terms of digital and analog/RF performance. For C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> configurations an ~2 and ~1 order improvement is observed in terms of I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>/I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</sub> ratio and nearly 1.9 mV/dec change in SS when compared to C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> configurations. This suggests that opting the air spacer gives better digital performance in comparison with other configurations. However, the Negative Differential Resistance (NDR) is also noticed for the same C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> configurations. Further, an improvement of ~90.07 %, ~87.5% in g<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub> is noticed for C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> and C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> when compared to the other two configurations. The gate capacitance (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gg</sub>) is reduced for C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> boosting the cut-off frequency (f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>) to > 70% when compared to other configurations. To assess the viability of MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> NC FinFET in digital/analog IC applications, a common source (CS) amplifier and resistive load inverter is designed. Overall, these findings contribute to the reference at N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sub> node for spacer engineering optimization in MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> NC-FinFET for commercialization.

title: Discrete Fuzzy Approximation-Based Sliding Mode Current Control for Torque Ripple Suppression in Switched Reluctance Motors
authors: Xijian Lin, Shuo Zhang, Zifeng Chen, Huayu Ji, Dianxun Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Switched reluctance motors exhibit strong nonlinear magnetic characteristics, which make accurate current control challenging and lead to pronounced torque ripple. This article presents a discrete-time adaptive fuzzy sliding mode controller designed to achieve robust current tracking under model uncertainties and time-varying behavior. An integral sliding mode surface is then constructed, and a single-input fuzzy system with Gaussian membership functions is employed to approximate the ideal control law. Unlike conventional fuzzy controllers with fixed parameters, the proposed method updates the fuzzy vector parameters online using a Lyapunov-based adaptive law. In addition, an adaptive high-frequency switching gain is developed to compensate for residual approximation errors while alleviating chattering. The overall controller is derived and analyzed entirely in the discrete-time domain, enabling direct implementation on digital platforms. Experimental results demonstrate improved current tracking accuracy and significant torque ripple reduction.

title: Diverse Style Prompt Assistant for Generalized Remote Sensing Object Classification_supp1-3693680.pdf
authors: Wenda Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: Due to the existence of domain shift between different remote sensing datasets, remote sensing object classification models trained on the source domain often face the challenge of reduced accuracy when applied to unseen domains. In this paper, we propose a diverse style prompt assistant for generalized remote sensing object classification. We utilize the powerful knowledge reserve of the CLIP model to solve the domain generalization problem from the perspective of text prompts. CLIP’s text features simulate feature-space style diversity, and then diverse styles guide the classification model to generate general image features. Specifically, we first implement a style diversity loss and maximum entropy loss to train prompts to obtain multiple style prompts. Then, we introduce the style prompts to guide the optimization process of image features cross-modally within the joint feature space, enabling the classification model to generate domain-invariant features. We conduct extensive experiments on three remote sensing datasets, and the results show that our method achieves superior generalization performance compared with the state of the arts. Codes are available at https://github.com/Jia-dd/DSPA.

title: A Neuromorphic Approach to DDoS Detection: Hardware Accelerator for Integer-Quantized Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Phu Nguyen Phan Hai, Khang Pham Lo Hoang, Cam Doan Ngoc, Bao Bui Quoc, Trang Hoang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks remain a persistent threat to Internet of Things (IoT) and edge networks, where detection systems are often constrained by latency and power budgets. This paper presents a SNN based DDoS detection system that spans packet level preprocessing, integer quantized inference, and FPGA deployment. A learnable leaky integrate and fire (LIF) input encoder with per feature firing thresholds is introduced to transform normalized traffic features into spike trains in a trainable manner. The entire inference pipeline operates in integer arithmetic through quantization aware training, eliminating all floating point operations. A four stage pipelined hardware accelerator is implemented on a Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ multiprocessor system-on-chip (MPSoC). Experiments on the Bot IoT and ToN IoT datasets demonstrate competitive detection accuracy, and the accelerator achieves an inference latency of 91.58 μs, delivering a 182.1× speedup over the ARM Cortex A53 with 10.95% FPGA logic utilization.

title: Evolution of Layer1/Layer2-Triggered Mobility in 5G-Advanced: From Release 18 Foundation to Release 19 Enhancements and Beyond
authors: Dogukan Atik, Behnam Khodapanah, Sanjay Goyal, Murat Gursu, Jedrzej Stanczak, Anastasios Kakkavas, Jani-Pekka Kainulainen
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This work provides a comprehensive examination of Layer 1/Layer 2-triggered mobility (LTM) enhancements introduced in 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) Release (Rel.) 19, extending the foundational framework established in Rel. 18. The key advancement is the extension of LTM to inter-central unit (CU) scenarios, significantly broadening its applicability across diverse network deployments. This extension necessitated comprehensive standardization work addressing security key management, dual connectivity (DC) operations, and channel state information (CSI) resource coordination mechanisms. The measurement capabilities were improved by incorporating channel state information reference signal (CSI-RS)-based layer 1 (L1)-reference signal received power (RSRP) measurements and implementing event-triggered reporting to reduce signaling overhead. Additionally, conditional LTM enhances mobility robustness through autonomous user equipment (UE)-triggered cell switching, while early CSI acquisition procedures optimize post-handover throughput performance. The paper elaborates on the technical implementation of inter-CU LTM, covering network signaling architectures, security framework adaptations, and DC integration aspects. Furthermore, we explore Rel. 20 developments focusing on LTM integration with Carrier Aggregation and adaptive measurement configuration mechanisms, alongside initial considerations for 6G mobility evolution. To emphasize the importance of reducing the handover interruption time, our system-level simulation analysis quantifies the transport layer benefits of reduced interruption times, demonstrating that reducing the interruption duration in LTM to approximately 20 ms delivers significant transmission control protocol (TCP) throughput gains over conventional handover’s 100 ms interruption, especially when TCP re-transmission timeout (RTO) timers are set at 100 ms or below.

title: A Stochastic Game-Theoretic Framework for Robust Ventilator Safety: The Trust Gate Mechanism and Life Confidence Score (RLCS)
authors: Bedadipta Bain
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Mechanical ventilation requires precise synchronization between patient effort and machine actuation to prevent Ventilator-Induced Lung Injury (VILI). Conventional trigger mechanisms, typically reliant on single-sensor thresholds, differ in their ability to distinguish valid respiratory effort from sensor artifacts or complex asynchronous events such as Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA). This paper proposes a novel computational safety architecture, the Robust Life Confidence Score (RLCS), which reformulates physiological state estimation as a non-cooperative potential game. We introduce a hierarchical decision framework comprising two layers: (1) A Nash Equilibrium Controller that dynamically penalizes the global confidence score based on this variance, and (2) A Bayesian "Trust Gate" that utilizes the ensemble variance of normalized sensor beliefs to calculate a univariate Mahalanobis-type Z-score distance from consensus, effectively isolating non-cooperative (faulty) sensors. Crucially, by utilizing static reliability weights derived from hardware specifications, the system achieves a theoretical breakdown point of 50%, preventing dominance by high-variance outliers. Experimental validation on a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) testbed using an ESP32 microcontroller confirms the computational feasibility of the algorithm, demonstrating sub-100ms execution latency and deterministic fault rejection under simulated conflict scenarios. These results offer a viable pathway for implementing intelligent, low-cost safety interlocks in resource-constrained medical cyber-physical systems.

title: Optimizing Electric Vehicle Charging Load Forecasting via Ensemble of Diffusion Models
authors: Xijuan Sun, Di Wu, Michael Jenkin, Arnaud Zinflou, Boyu Wang, Benoit Boulet
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Vehicles
abstract: The global adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has experienced rapid growth in recent years. This has resulted in higher EV charging demands, placing additional pressure and challenges on existing smart grid infrastructure. Accurate and reliable short-term electric vehicle load predictions are essential to optimize smart grid operations, prevent overload of the grid and transformers, and ensure that electricity distribution aligns with actual demand. Although machine learning methods have found wide use in electric vehicle charging load forecasting, they struggle to capture complex feature patterns and accurately quantify uncertainties. In contrast, generative AI has shown great promise in various real-world applications due to its ability to model intricate data distributions and provide robust probabilistic forecasts. In this work, we leverage denoising diffusion probabilistic models, a type of generative model, to address the challenges of probabilistic electric vehicle load forecasting. Specifically, we propose an ensemble of conditional diffusion models as a unified framework to improve forecasting accuracy. We evaluate our approach on several real-world datasets across multiple forecasting horizons. The results demonstrate that the proposed method consistently improves forecasting accuracy compared with frequently used forecasting methods.

title: Empirical Study of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome Detection based on Ultrasound Images using Deep Learning
authors: Pavithra B, Dr.L.Lakshmanan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is one of the most common endocrine disorders affecting women of reproductive age and is a major cause of anovulatory infertility. Ultrasound imaging plays an important role in identifying ovarian cysts and supporting clinical diagnosis. However, manual interpretation of ultrasound images may lead to variability due to operator dependence. Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have enabled the development of computer-aided diagnostic systems that can improve the reliability and efficiency of PCOS detection. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of existing methodologies for PCOS detection, focusing on traditional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches. A total of 25 research articles is analyzed and compared based on datasets, analytical metrics, tools, and reported performance. The survey also examines commonly used databases and evaluates the strengths and limitations of existing techniques. Furthermore, key challenges and potential future research directions in AI-based PCOS detection are discussed.

title: Timing Yield-Driven Clock Skew Scheduling Based on Hybrid Distributions
authors: Kaixiang Zhu, Jiangnan Li, Lingli Wang, Wai-Shing Luk
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Clock skew scheduling is a cost-effective technique for enhancing synchronous digital VLSI systems. The technique simply involves adjusting the clock skew to meet signal path timing constraints in order to increase the clock frequency or timing yield. Traditionally, Gaussian distributions have been assumed for path delays under process variations. However, this assumption may be inappropriate due to the notable asymmetry of actual path delay distributions. In this paper, we propose the generalized extreme value (GEV) distribution as a potential alternative, along with a hybrid distribution approach that utilizes different distributions for different path delays to improve accuracy. We also analyze three parameter estimation methods: Maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), L-moments, and the method of moments (MoM) to evaluate the accuracy of the fit. Our experimental results show that the GEV distribution more accurately approximates path delays in benchmark circuits, achieving an average improvement of 40% and 30% in the Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) statistic compared to the Gaussian and Lognormal distributions, respectively. In addition, Q-Q (Quantile-Quantile) and P-P (Probability-Probability) plots are employed to visually validate the goodness-of-fit for different distributions. For timing yield-driven CSS, the hybrid method achieves the highest performance, improving yield by an average of 6% over the Gaussian model and by 2% over the GEV-based model. These results demonstrate that the hybrid distribution approach effectively captures heterogeneous path behavior and provides robust yield optimization.

title: FPGA-Accelerated Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Networks for Dynamic Terahertz Beam Steering
authors: Maya B Dhone, Uday Kumar Gundagani, Pravallika Koppula, Muppu Prathyusha, Mallareddy Adudhodla, Pavan Kumar Reddy Manellore
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Advanced terahertz communication systems demand sophisticated beam steering capabilities to optimize signal quality and coverage in dynamic environments. This work presents an FPGA-accelerated spatiotemporal graph neural network framework for real-time terahertz beam steering optimization. The proposed system models antenna arrays and propagation channels as dynamic graphs, capturing spatial relationships between array elements and temporal evolution of channel conditions. A specialized graph neural network architecture processes spatiotemporal features to predict optimal beam directions and power allocations. FPGA implementation achieves ultra-low latency processing through parallelized matrix operations and optimized memory hierarchies. Experimental validation demonstrates beam steering accuracy exceeding 94% with processing latency below 100 microseconds. The framework adapts to mobile scenarios and environmental changes, maintaining optimal beam alignment under varying conditions. Results indicate 35% improvement in signal-to-noise ratio compared to conventional beam steering methods, while reducing computational overhead by 60% through hardware acceleration. The integrated approach enables real-time optimization for next-generation terahertz wireless systems.

title: Electricity Theft Detection using GA-Optimized Random Forest
authors: M. Reha Sai, P. V. Divya Sree, Y. B. Ganga Lohitha, N. Neelima, T. V. Smitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The ongoing global issue of electricity loss through theft continues to cause major financial losses for power utilities and instability to the electric grid. A hybrid detection framework is presented for identifying non-technical revenue losses in utility billing systems through smart meter data. The proposed detection framework incorporates class imbalance correction, evolutionary optimization, and ensemble learning techniques. To tackle the problem of class imbalance, Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) is applied to increase the number of minority class instances. Optimal feature selection and hyperparameter tuning were performed using a Genetic Algorithm. A Random Forest model was then used in the final classification of billing losses. The evaluation of this proposed framework was done by using benchmark datasets and resulted in improved performance metrics such as accuracy, balanced accuracy, Matthews correlation coefficient, ROC–AUC and fewer false positive alarms in comparison with current Machine Learning and Deep Learning approaches. The proposed framework is modular, scalable, resilient, and suitable for real-world deployment.

title: STAR-RIS–Assisted UAV Communications for Dynamic Spectrum Sharing in NOMA Under Imperfect SIC: Enabling Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Ali Nazari, Ali Olfat
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The simultaneous transmission and reflection of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) enhances the spectrum efficiency (SE) of primary users (PUs) and secondary users (SUs) in cognitive radio (CR) networks by mitigating interference from incident signals. The STAR-RIS-assisted unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) can fully cover the environment due to its high mobility and fast deployment. According to the dynamic air-to-ground channels, the STAR-RIS-assisted UAV faces a challenge in determining the coefficient of the element's role during flight. To meet the requirements of dynamic channel determination with the SE approach, this paper proposes the sum rate maximization of both PUs and SUs through non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) in a CR network to jointly optimize the trajectory and transmission-reflection beamforming design of the STAR-RIS-assisted UAV, and power allocation under the imperfect successive interference cancellation (SIC). Since the non-convex joint optimization problem includes coupled variables, we develop an alternative optimization algorithm. Simulation results examine the impact of parameters on fixed and mobile users, evaluate the performance of intelligent surface modes and operating protocols, and highlight the advantages of adopted NOMA (under perfect/imperfect SIC) over OMA. The gap analysis confirms that our proposed dynamic system model numerically outperforms the benchmarks.

title: An Efficient and Verifiable Multi-Keyword Search Scheme with Forward Security
authors: Nianyi Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of cloud storage, privacy security issues arising from data outsourcing have become increasingly prominent. Searchable encryption technology allows users to perform keyword queries over encrypted data, yet most existing schemes suffer from limited functionality, low efficiency, or insufficient security. This paper proposes an efficient and verifiable multi-keyword searchable scheme supporting forward security. The scheme adopts a "filter-then-verify" query strategy, selecting the least frequent keyword as the s-term to achieve sublinear query efficiency; designs a hash accumulation-based verification mechanism to ensure the integrity and correctness of server-returned results; and implements forward security through counter-driven dynamic address updates to resist file injection attacks. Experimental results on a dataset of 10,000 documents demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves significant improvements in both single-keyword and multi-keyword query efficiency, validating its effectiveness and superiority.

title: EAG-SR: Entity Association-Guided Self-Reflection for Document-Level Enterprise Relation Extraction
authors: Xiangfeng Luo, Mengyu Han
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Document-level relation extraction (DocRE) aims to extract semantic relations between entities from an entire document. Recent large language model (LLM)-based approaches typically identify relations by filtering all potential entity pairs within a document and then performing triplet fact extraction. However, long documents often contain substantial redundant information that can obscure key relational evidence. Moreover, existing methods usually process all candidate entity pairs simultaneously, introducing noisy contextual representations and inefficient filtering of candidate relations. In addition, the lack of verification mechanisms makes LLMs prone to generating hallucinated relations. To address these challenges, we propose EAG-SR, a novel framework featuring Entity Association Guidance and Self-Reflection. Specifically, we design an entity-enhanced summarization module to emphasize entity-centric semantic information and reduce irrelevant context. We further introduce an entity-pair level retrieval module to efficiently filter candidate relations by retrieving relevant evidence for each entity pair. Finally, a self-reflection mechanism is incorporated to verify extracted relations and suppress hallucinations generated by LLMs. Experiments on two benchmark datasets, DocRED and DWIE, demonstrate that EAG-SR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an F1 score of 62.57% on DocRED.

title: Smart Indicator Turn Off System for Forgotten Appliances
authors: M Premalatha, PoovishRaja D, Vanaraj K, Vishnu A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Two-wheeler users are also very numerous; they rely on manual indicator switches when trying to convey their intentions of turning at the road. On the majority of traditional bikes, an indicator has to be turned off by the user after making a turn, and a rider may simply forget when it happens, giving wrong signals to the other cars, and posing the threat of a crash. As such, the main objective of this project is to create an automatic indicator OFF system in the two-wheelers that enhance road safety through minimization of human error and workload in the hands of the riders. The proposed system will control indicators using independent push buttons and automatically cancel them depending on the replace motion of the handle bar after turning. Handle stabilization is detected by mechanical clip switches to produce a cancellation signal when the handle is pushed in the anti-direction. A microcontroller receives the input signals and drives the indicator lamps by a relay driver circuit in order to provide safe electrical isolation. To ensure a stable operation of the control unit and switching circuitry, a controlled power supply is applied. The system designed is a reproduction of the auto-cancel indicator mechanism applied on cars but the system will be inexpensive, easy, and applicable in the practically applicable two-wheeler-based riders to enhance traffic safety and comfort to the rider.
