title: Fast Parameter Extraction of an Extended Jiles–Atherton Model Using a Physics–Constrained CNN–KAN Framework
authors: Bohua Zhang, Martin Pfost
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Accurate hysteresis modeling is essential for high–frequency magnetic components, and the Jiles–Atherton (JA) model is widely used for this purpose. However, parameter identification remains computationally demanding, as traditional optimization approaches require iterative fitting of individual hysteresis loops (typically 1–2 minutes per loop) and scale poorly to large datasets. To address this limitation, a physics–constrained convolutional neural network and Kolmogorov–Arnold Network (CNN–KAN) framework is proposed for fast parameter extraction of a delay–enhanced JA model. The proposed method directly takes the excitation waveform H(t) as input and incorporates experimentally measurable hysteresis features into a multi–objective loss function to enforce physical consistency. Using measured data ranging from 300 kHz to 1MHz from a TDK N49 toroidal ferrite core for training, the model achieves accurate reconstruction of hysteresis loops and reliable prediction of JA parameters within a single forward pass. Comparative evaluations against conventional optimization–based methods and alternative neural network architectures demonstrate a substantial reduction in computation time while maintaining high modeling accuracy. These results indicate that the proposed framework offers a scalable and efficient solution for high–frequency hysteresis modeling with the delay-enhanced JA model.

title: Realtime Wildlife Detection and Alert System using IoT Enabled Drone with Machine Learning for Anti-Poaching
authors: Prem Anand T P, Jayaganesh M, Akash T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Wildlife poaching and illegal entry into forest areas are still major concerns in protecting biodiversity and endangered species. Monitoring forest areas is difficult due to a lack of manpower and inadequate communication facilities. In this paper, a real-time wildlife detection and alert system is proposed that incorporates machine learning and IoT technologies to increase forest area monitoring. In this proposed system, a Raspberry Pi 5 is utilized as an onboard computer, an Arducam high-resolution camera is utilized to capture real-time video, and a SIM7600 4G LTE module is utilized to transmit and communicate in real-time. The main aim of this paper is to develop a YOLOv12 object detection model that can analyze and detect wildlife, intruders, and poachers in forest areas using a live video feed. A dataset is prepared and utilized to train the model to increase its ability to detect objects in an aerial environment. From the experimental results, it is observed that the proposed optimized YOLOv12 object detection model is able to detect objects with an accuracy of over 65%. Once a suspicious object is detected in a live video feed, a notification is sent to the authorities with information regarding the incident.

title: CNN-Alex Net Framework for Gallbladder Disease Detection using Ultrasound Imaging
authors: S.Prem Kumar, Yagatheepan S, Sathish Kumar P S, Pratheep P, Vijaya Kishore G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Gallbladder diseases range from benign conditions such as gallstones and inflammatory disorders to highly aggressive malignancies, where delayed diagnosis often results in poor prognosis. Ultrasonography is the primary imaging modality for gallbladder evaluation due to its non-invasive nature, low cost, and wide availability; however, its interpretation is highly operator-dependent and prone to inter-observer variability. To address these challenges, we propose a CNN–AlexNet framework for automated gallbladder disease detection using ultrasound images. The proposed system employs ultrasound-specific preprocessing techniques, including noise reduction, normalization, and data augmentation, followed by transfer learning with a fine-tuned AlexNet architecture optimized using the Adam optimizer. The model performs multi-class classification to identify five gallbladder conditions: normal, abnormal, benign tumor, malignant tumor, and stone. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves high classification accuracy with improved robustness against noise and imaging variability. The integration of a user-friendly graphical interface further enhances clinical applicability by enabling real-time analysis and interpretability. The results indicate that the proposed approach can serve as an effective computer-aided diagnostic tool to support clinicians in early and accurate gallbladder disease detection.

title: Battery Management System with IoT based Monitoring and Data Logging
authors: Vaishnavi R, Divyashree B P, Lakith G, Sharmila N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increase adaption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) requires need of intelligent and reliable battery management solutions to ensure user safety and system longevity. This study proposes an IoT based Battery Management System (BMS) for EV which continuously monitors the important parameters like voltage, current, temperature and smoke detection with the help of an ESP32 microcontroller. The measured data is continuously saved in google sheets using cloud based communication. The proposed system uses sensors to monitor normal and abnormal data, and there is a cut-off relay which disconnects the battery if fault occurs to prevent thermal runaway. It includes the integration of Blynk IoT platform for remote monitoring and notification for alerts. In addition, it contains Liquid Crystal Display (LCD) module integrated for local visualization of the battery parameters. The developed BMS system has undergone tests repetitively to minimize the false alerts, also by increasing its performance and reliability, which increases the battery safety, extended operational lifespan and enhancing energy efficiency in the EV.

title: A Multimodal Embedded System for Real-Time UAV Detection using Vision and Acoustic Signals
authors: Rohith S, Hemanth Kumar D, L. Jegan Antony Marcilin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increase of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) usage has created difficulties in surveillance, security monitoring and airspace safety. It is necessary to detect unauthorized UAV(s) in real-time in order to protect sensitive areas including critical infrastructure, restricted zones and public events. This paper presents a low-power, embedded; UAV Detection System with vision-based object detection and Acoustic signal detection for real-time UAV activity monitoring and detection. Using the YOLOv8 deep learning model to detect UAVs in video images from a surveillance camera as well as a MAX4466 microphone sensor for the detection of UAV-specific rotor noise, a complete UAV detection system can be accomplished. Through the use of data fusion of both visual and acoustic UAV detection data, our system improves reliability in the performance of UAV detection as well as reduces false alarms. Acoustic signal acquisition and preprocessing will occur using an STM32 microcontroller while an ESP32 module will handle communication and alert transmission. Alert notifications will occur in real-time when a UAV is detected through the use of LED, buzzer, GSM-based SMS alerts, and alerts via Telegram. Experimental results show that the UAV Detection System provides reliable UAV detections with the performance of real-time detection capabilities through the fusion of deep learning-based using sound to detect vision is a specialized approach for embedded UAV surveillance and is effective for UAV surveillance systems.

title: Large-Scale Seabed Sediment Classification Using Sentinel-2 Imagery and Efficient Machine Learning Pipelines
authors: Diego de Oliveira Dantas, Marta de Oliveira Barreiros, Jonathan Araujo Queiroz, Jailly Aparecida do Rosario Silva, João Regis Dos Santos Filho, Cleverson Guizan Silva, Allan Kardec Duailibe Barros Filho
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Large-scale mapping of seabed sediments is essential for environmental management, offshore engineering, and the sustainable exploitation of resources, particularly in optically complex coastal regions such as the Brazilian Equatorial Margin (BEM). Although traditional acoustic surveys offer high precision, their application is limited by operational costs and low acquisition frequency. This study presents a reproducible machine learning pipeline for regional sediment classification from Sentinel-2 imagery, systematically covering the shallow shelf from 0 to 30 m depth. The methodology combines Principal Component Analysis (PCA), a Multilayer Perceptron Neural Network (MLP), and the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), integrated into a conservative curation protocol based on historical granulometric consistency and spatial independence. The predictive scope was defined under the binary Sand vs. Mud scenario, using 2,199 spatially independent samples under 25 km spatial block validation with five folds. Among the evaluated model families, which included convolutional neural networks (CNNs), multilayer perceptrons (MLPs), support vector machines (SVMs), and EfficientNet-based models, the MLP+PCA+SMOTE pipeline was selected as the recommended configuration, achieving an accuracy of 92.64% ± 1.95, a macro F1-score of 89.04% ± 2.40, and a computational cost 6.6 times lower than that of the Custom Three-Stage CNN (CNN-Base). Although no statistically significant differences were observed relative to the convolutional architectures, the regional agreement of 84.9% with independent sedimentological mapping supports the plausibility of the identified domains. The results indicate that lightweight models based on spectral information constitute a viable and scalable alternative for regional sediment mapping in coastal environments.

title: Sinusoidal Magnet Air Gap Flux Density Emulation Based on Surface-Mounted Segmented Hybrid-Remanence PMs
authors: Amal Souissi, Imen Abdennadher, Ahmed Masmoudi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The subdivision of surface-mounted permanent magnets (SPMs) into segments with similar remanence and opening has been commonly considered with the aim to reduce the PM eddy current loss, especially in the case of fractional-slot SPM machines. In this paper, the opening and remanence of PM segments turn to be variable, yielding the so-called “segmented hybrid-remanence PMs”. This concept is introduced in order to emulate sinusoidally-shaped PMs with and without injected harmonics. Prior the investigation of the introduced concept, a comparison of conventional and sinusoidal PMs is firstly carried out in order to highlight the performance of the latter. The air gap flux density is then formulated taking into account the PM segmentation. A sizing procedure based on the developed model is proposed with the aim of minimizing the harmonic content of the air gap flux density radial component in an attempt to emulate the one of sinusoidal PMs. This leads to the identification of an interesting topology whose air gap flux density components are validated by a finite element analysis model. The paper is achieved by a comparison of the air gap flux density components of the selected topology and the SPM machine equipped with sinusoidal PMs. It is found that the difference between the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the radial components is limited to 2.23%.

title: A Novel High-Power, Wide-range PT Symmetric Wireless Power Transfer Systems with Reconfiguration-Free Arbitrary Current Ratio Adjustment
authors: Guang-Sen Guo, Zhi-Juan Liao, Zhao-Xi Lang, Yi-Jie Wang, Pan Sun, Chen-Yang Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Parity-Time (PT) symmetric mechanism has attracted extensive interest for their excellent positional robustness, but are strictly limited by the fixed current amplitude ratio determined by the inductance ratio, which greatly restricts power transfer performance. To overcome this issue, a novel PT symmetric mechanism based on a specially designed S-0 topology is proposed. Theoretical derivations of the mechanism and parameter criteria are verified experimentally. The results show that: 1) α is no longer limited by the inductance ratio L1/L2 and can be arbitrarily adjusted without hardware changes; 2) kc is independent of RL and positively correlated with α. Reducing α increases both output power and transfer distance. Moreover, kc can be used as a design parameter to determine α, ensuring high positional robustness under all conditions; 3) When conventional PT-symmetric operation fails—large primary inductance and small secondary inductance—the proposed mechanism ensures stable operation while better balancing power, efficiency, and distance.

title: A Mixed-Language Transformer Encoder Architecture for Social Media Phishing: Vietnamese-English Case Study
authors: Sam Nguyen-Xuan, Han Nguyen, Giap Dang Le, Daehee Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Social media phishing has become increasingly prevalent in regions where attackers exploit informal writing styles, multilingual expressions, and code-mixed compound structures. These characteristics pose significant challenges for detection systems built on monolingual or general-purpose multilingual models. In this paper, we propose MLTEA, a mixed-language dual-encoder architecture designed to model Vietnamese–English code-mixed compound structures commonly observed in social media phishing. The framework integrates parallel pretrained encoders, performs token-level fusion, and employs a gated reweighting mechanism to capture cross-lingual semantic dependencies. Experimental results show that MLTEA consistently outperforms monolingual, multilingual, and hybrid baselines, achieving an F1 score of 0.94 under clean conditions while maintaining strong performance under structurally obfuscated inputs, demonstrating robust generalization under controlled compound-level and code-mixed perturbations.

title: Long&short Exposures Guided Diffusion Model for Realistic Local Motion Deblurring
authors: Zhongbao Yang, Lingshun Kong, Jinshan Pan, Jiangxin Dong, Jinhui Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Removing local blur caused by moving objects is challenging, especially in low signal-to-noise ratio environments, as the moving objects are usually significantly blurry while the static background remains relatively clear. Existing methods relying on local blur detection often suffer from inaccuracies and cannot generate satisfactory results when focusing solely on blurred regions. In this paper, we present an effective diffusion model guided with long-exposure and short-exposure images for realistic local motion deblurring. Specifically, we first propose a context-based local blur detection module. Different from existing methods that rely on pixel-wise classification, we leverage contextual information to generate semantically coherent blur region identification, which preserves the integrity of blur regions. Then, we present a blurry-aware guided image restoration method to remove local blur by discriminately handling the blurry and clear regions. Finally, a structure-guided diffusion model is developed to achieve realistic image restoration by exploring useful information from the above blurry-aware guided image restoration result and the short-exposure image as guidance. The proposed method, ExpDiff, is trained in an end-to-end manner. Our extensive experimental results show that the proposed ExpDiff performs favorably against state-of-the-art methods.

title: Corner Case Detection and Generation for Autonomous Driving: An Overview
authors: Yunji Liang, Junteng Liu, Xiaokai Yan, Xiaolong Zheng, Lei Tang, Luwen Huangfu, Sagar Samtani, Zhiwen Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Safety concerns remain one of the most significant obstacles to the large-scale deployment and continued advancement of autonomous driving (AD) systems. A major underlying cause of many safety-related incidents in AD systems is suboptimal or erroneous decision-making when the vehicle encounters corner cases (CCs)—rare, unexpected, or extreme situations that fall outside typical operating conditions. Although recent advances in artificial intelligence have driven substantial progress in both autonomous driving and corner-case research, the field still lacks a coherent conceptual foundation and a systematic, widely accepted categorization of CCs. In this survey, we address this gap by offering a structured review of the existing corner-case literature along three key dimensions: understanding, detection, and generation. The key contribution is a three-level classification of corner cases—spanning data-level, model-level, and semantic-level CCs—that clarifies and disambiguates competing definitions and perspectives on AD corner cases. Building on this framework, we examine simulator-based methods for corner-case selection and data generation, and we identify open challenges, promising directions, and potential solutions to more effectively handle corner cases in autonomous driving systems.

title: Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation for Dynamic 3D Scenes Using Scene Flow Estimation_supp1-3693993.mp4
authors: Chih-Wei Huang
publish: N/A
abstract: 3D open-vocabulary semantic segmentation has shown great potential in applications such as autonomous driving and mixed reality. However, achieving accurate segmentation in dynamic environments remains challenging due to motion-induced inconsistencies. To address this issue, we incorporate scene flow as temporal information into a static semantic backbone to enhance semantic consistency and accuracy over time. Our method captures inter-frame motion cues from point cloud sequences and leverages them, together with a local clustering mechanism, to refine semantic label consistency in consecutive frames. Furthermore, we introduce a two-way scene flow-based data augmentation strategy that exploits both forward and backward motion to jointly train the model in bidirectional temporal contexts. On the large-scale nuScenes autonomous driving dataset, our method achieves a 0.4% overall improvement in hIoU and a 2.37% gain under high-motion scenes. On the synthetic object-centric dataset, it achieves a 4.53% overall hIoU improvement and a 6.09% gain in high-motion scenes, while reducing the ID switch rate by 0.5%.

title: Intelligent Cyberattack Mitigation using Q-Learning-based Dynamic Security Policy Optimization
authors: M. Uma Maheswari, V. Thilakji, S. Niraikulathu Ayyanar, G. Saravanakumar, B. Saratha, R. Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The fast advancement of cyberattacks necessitates sophisticated and adaptive protection measures that surpass static security standards. Current intrusion mitigation systems depend on predetermined rules or supervised models, which are inadequate in addressing unfamiliar and evolving threats. This study presents a framework for Intelligent Cyberattack Mitigation using Q-Learning-based Dynamic Security Policy Optimization, referred to as DQSP-QRL (Dynamic Q-Security Policy with Reinforcement Learning). The main objective is to independently acquire appropriate mitigation strategies via ongoing interaction with the network's ecosystem. The proposed approach conceptualizes cyber protection as a Markov Decision Process, whereby Q-learning iteratively refines security rules in accordance with attack intensity, system status, and response efficacy. In contrast to conventional systems, DQSP-QRL adjusts in real-time without the need for previous attack signatures. Experimental findings indicate enhanced attack detection precision, decreased reaction latency, and increased system robustness relative to rule-oriented and static machine learning methodologies. The research suggests that reinforcement learning-based policy optimization significantly improves proactive cyber protection, making it appropriate for future generations of intelligent safety systems.

title: Unveiling the Segmented Patterns of Energy Storage via Parametric Grid-resilience Value Curve
authors: Yiyang Song, Tiance Zhang, Jianxiao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The increasing frequency of extreme weather events makes resilience-oriented preventive measures essential for power grids. Specifically, quickly and accurately configuring energy storage before disasters strike becomes a critical but challenging task. To quantify the impact of energy storage on grid resilience, this paper introduces the concept of the resilience value of energy storage (RVES), defined as the savings in both the value of lost load (VoLL) and system operational costs during disasters. By treating storage capacity as a continuous parameter, we develop the grid-resilience value curve (GRVC), an affine function capturing the relationship between low-dimensional parameters and RVES. The GRVC surface can be analytically represented using the multi-parametric linear programming (MPLP) approach, offering intuitive and valuable sensitivity insights to support pre-event storage configuration. To address the combinatorial challenges in MPLP posed by time-coupled constraints in disaster scenarios, we propose two algorithmic enhancements: an umbrella constraint-based MPLP model to remove redundant constraints, and an accelerated algorithm enabling a warm start for efficient GRVC solution. Case studies on IEEE 9 bus and a real-world 121-bus system under ice storm scenarios validate both the practical relevance of GRVC in the resilient configuration of storage and the improved computational efficiency.

title: From Telettrofono to Smartphone: Social Innovation and the Ethics of Recognition
authors: Riccardo Meucci, Guanrong Chen
publish: IEEE Technology and Society Magazine
abstract: The origins of voice communication technologies are typically narrated through a sequence of industrial and scientific milestones culminating in today’s global digital networks. Yet, early developments in telephony also reveal deeply human motivations and socially embedded forms of innovation. This article compares Antonio Meucci’s nineteenth-century “telettrofono”—a low-energy, household communication device conceived to assist his ailing wife—with today’s sophisticated smartphone-based infrastructures. By examining these two endpoints of the telecommunication continuum, the article explores how communication technologies evolved from intimate human-centered solutions to complex, energy-intensive systems. It also discusses the ethics of recognition within institutional frameworks such as the IEEE Milestones Program. The goal is not to reopen historical disputes, but to promote a socially aware and inclusive memory of technological innovation, consistent with IEEE’s mission to advance technology for humanity.

title: Hybrid Behavior Analytics and ML System for Cloud User Authentication and Security Monitoring
authors: Archana Pudi, Gnanaprakasam Thangavel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Authentication and security monitoring of cloud servers have become imperative as organisations increasingly rely on cloud platforms to protect sensitive data, services, and applications. This study highlights that although multi-factor authentication (MFA) is essential for thwarting data breaches and unauthorised access, the development of a secure MFA architecture is complicated by diverse security needs and the intricacies involved in choosing suitable authentication factors. A novel Hybrid RNN-based system is suggested to address these difficulties, merging GRU, CNN, and complementing deep-learning components to analyse cloud user characteristics and improve authentication reliability. Various feature-selection techniques and fusion rules were evaluated to ascertain the most pertinent properties, guaranteeing a strong input representation for the model. The findings indicate that the proposed Hybrid RNN markedly improves Cloud Server Authentication and Security Monitoring, surpassing earlier statistical and deep-learning methods. The model demonstrates a remarkable accuracy of 98.36%, highlighting significant advancements in anomaly detection and user identification verification. The study indicates that deep-learning architectures, especially the Hybrid RNN, provide a robust basis for enhancing MFA systems on cloud platforms, facilitating improved security against unauthorised access while ensuring scalable and secure cloud operations.

title: A Hardware Trojan Detection Method Based on Recognizing the Waveform Similarity of Oscillating Circuit
authors: Ding Ding, Furong Yu, Kaibo Sun, Lei Zhang, Jianxin Wang, Chaoen Xiao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To solve the problem that the hardware Trojan occupies a small number of resources, and the triggering condition is difficult to detect, this paper proposes a Hardware Trojan detection method based on recognizing the waveform similarity of oscillation circuit. First, a model for detecting the hardware Trojan is built. Then, four hardware Trojans aimed at the AES encryption chips in the Trust-Hub library are detected respectively by using a ring oscillation circuit or a multivibrator circuit with external connection. The square wave signals at the feature points are extracted by principal component analysis and the results were obtained by correlation analysis, i.e. recognizing the waveform similarity by calculating the coefficient of association. Experimental results show that the four hardware Trojans could be identified, and one of the four Hardware Trojans only occupies 0.26% of the total chip resources. In addition, compared to the traditional detection methods based on detecting the power consumption, this method has higher accuracy and efficiency for detecting the Hardware Trojans at the inactive state.

title: Cognitive Small World LPWAN For Health Monitoring
authors: Shanthi K G, Supriya Shree M, Vernisha Shree P, Sri Vaishnavi Kumar, Sreedharshini A, Subasree A G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The continuous health monitoring is seen as a vital requirement for the early detection of diseases, care of the elderly, and proper intervention of medical services, especially in remote and disadvantaged areas. However, the conventional health monitoring systems depend considerably on either Wi-Fi or broadband connectivity, which results in a high level of power consumption, making them difficult to generalize, and also reduces their reliability. This paper proposes a Cognitive Small World Low Power Wide Area Network-based health monitoring system, which combines small-world networking and physiological sensors. Nodes are organized in a small world network structure, which provides high connectivity while ensuring the lowest energy consumption. Cognitive routing techniques are implemented, which help in improving the reliability and lifetime of sensor nodes by dynamically adjusting the path of data transmission. A gateway is established using a GSM network, which facilitates long-haul transmission even in absence of proper infrastructure like the internet. The proposed system uses the Message Queuing Telemetry Transport protocol for reliable data transmission using cloud platforms. The proposed system could monitor vital health parameters such as body temperature, heartbeats, fall detection, and eye condition indicators.

title: Analysis of Information Security Risks and Countermeasures in the Era of Big Data
authors: Zhoubin Teng, Qichen Lu, Zhuoyi Shen, Yaoyan Cai, Yirun Chi, Lei Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Software vulnerability management is a critical inherent part of the software development lifecycle, with organizations investing heavily in early flaw detection. As Zhejiang Province advances in-depth digital transformation, government information projects undergo increasingly frequent regular iterations. For instance, the 42 core projects operated and maintained by Zhejiang Provincial Department of Emergency Management see over 15 annual iterations on average, and traditional code security auditing models are ill-suited to this demand. Due to its lack of awareness of program runtime environments and custom sanitization functions, static analysis yields reports with persistently high false positive rates—25%-40% for mainstream market tools, and a relatively high 18%-28% even for government-customized ones. Extensive manual review is costly and time-consuming, failing to meet the security testing needs of high-frequency project updates.To address this core challenge, this paper proposes a hybrid security analysis framework designed to automatically convert candidate vulnerability sets into exploitable true positive sets with high accuracy. First, a static analysis module runs in parallel to generate candidate vulnerability sets, while an independent runtime information collection module operates to acquire actual execution contexts. Subsequently, a correlation mapping module integrates the two to produce a guidance set incorporating runtime contexts. Based on this guidance set, the system automatically generates customized attack configurations to filter out true positive sets. By calculating discrepancies between candidate vulnerability sets and true positive sets, potential false positive sets are derived, and sanitization patterns unrecognized by static analysis are extracted and fed back to the static analysis rule base. This mechanism enables continuous automated optimization of static analysis tools, effectively resolving the efficiency and accuracy dilemmas of code auditing in the iteration of government information projects and providing an efficient and scalable solution for modern code auditing and application security testing.

title: RumGAN: Rumor Detection via Graph Attention Network with Multi-Feature Fusion
authors: Jing Qiu, Fengping Xu, Yiheng Shen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The proliferation of rumors on social media poses severe threats to public order. Existing GNN-based rumor detection models often overlook the critical role of edge features and rely on simplistic fusion strategies for heterogeneous content-social features, which limits their ability to capture complex propagation patterns. This paper presents RumGAN, a novel rumor detection framework built on a graph attention network with multi-source feature fusion. RumGAN constructs an attributed heterogeneous graph to explicitly model the interactions between source posts, users, and repost posts. A feature fusion module with adaptive attention and cross-feature calibration is designed to unify heterogeneous representations. Furthermore, an Edge-Enhanced Graph Attention Network is proposed to incorporate propagation path features into node aggregation, significantly improving the modeling of rumor propagation dynamics. Extensive experiments on the Weibo dataset demonstrate that RumGAN outperforms state-of-the-art baselines with a F1-score of 0.88, achieving 0.89 in Precision and 0.87 in Recall. Discussions verify the efficacy of the key components.

title: Edge AI Enabled Road Inspection Vehicle for On Field Road Damage Assessment and Traffic Data Acquisition
authors: M. Durgapoorani, S. Abinaya, J. Joel Jeffrey, P. Leninpugalhanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Real-time road monitoring systems play a vital role in enhancing transportation safety, traffic efficiency, and infrastructure maintenance by continuously assessing road surface conditions and traffic dynamics. However, conventional stationary monitoring systems provide limited spatial coverage and are difficult to scale across large road networks. As a continuation of our previous work on an edge-AI-based intelligent road monitoring system using YOLOv8, this paper presents a hardware-driven, field-deployable road inspection vehicle designed for continuous on-road assessment. The proposed system integrates a Raspberry Pi–based edge computing unit with a vision module, GPS receiver, and a motorized inspection platform to enable real-time detection of potholes, cracks, and vehicles during active field traversal. The YOLOv8 model is executed locally on the embedded platform to ensure low-latency inference, while road damage severity is estimated using a hybrid metric that combines detection confidence scores and bounding-box area measurements. Each detected event is geotagged and time-stamped to support spatial mapping and historical analysis, and an event-driven communication strategy selectively transmits critical alerts to a cloud dashboard, thereby reducing bandwidth usage. Field experiments conducted on urban and semi-urban roads demonstrate reliable detection accuracy, stable real-time performance, and effective road coverage under varying traffic density and lighting conditions.

title: Digital Twin-Assisted Self-Supervised Cognitive Resource Mapping for Interference-Aware 6G Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Judy Simon, Nellore Kapileswar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Cognitive radio networks (CRNs) play a major role in 6G systems with its dynamic and intelligent spectrum analysis techniques. Nevertheless, the current resource allocation systems are highly reactive, in that they use the interference and the degradation of fairness only when the performance has been impaired. This research work proposes an Interference-aware 6G cognitive radio network Digital Twin assisted Self-supervised Cognitive Resource Mapping (DT-SSCRM). It builds a real-time digital twin that simulates spectrum occupancy, user mobility, and channel dynamics and allows predicting the interference patterns and resource demand. The approach involves self-supervised learning of representation using techniques that do not require the use of labeled data, towards the extraction of latent spectrum features, which are used to support proactive channel selection and adaptive scheduling. The framework that has been proposed implies the combination of predictive resource mapping and multi-timescale optimization to increase the stability of allocation, decrease the latency, and enhance the spectral efficiency. The simulation finds show better sum rate, interference reduction, and QoS stability than reactive federated learning-based models, which makes DT-SSCRM affordable to large and ultra-dense 6G networks.

title: PCFNet: A Sea Surface Temperature Prediction Network Fusing Physical Constraints with Frequency Domain Feature Extraction
authors: Yingbing Liu, Cong Xiao, Guangwen Peng, Wenying Du, Changjiang Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Sea surface temperature (SST) predictions are critical for marine climate research and ecosystem management. To address the deficiencies of the existing data-driven models in regard to physical consistency constraints and multiscale feature modeling, this paper proposes a deep learning model that fuses physical constraints and frequency domain features for the SST prediction. This model employs the fast fourier transform (FFT) to process spatio-temporal features. At the same time, it incorporates a constraint integration module (CIM), which embeds the physical conservation properties of the advection-diffusion equation (ADE) into the prediction process, thereby enhancing the modeling capability for the dynamic evolution patterns of SST. The experimental results on both daily and weekly scales demonstrate that the proposed PCFNet achieves consistently higher prediction accuracy compared with existing SST forecasting models. In particular, it provides more stable short-term predictions and effectively mitigates multi-step error accumulation. These results further confirm the effectiveness of integrating frequency-domain structure modeling and cross-scale interaction mechanisms. Overall, this study offers a promising and interpretable framework for SST prediction by combining physical priors with data-driven learning.

title: A Random Forest-based Approach for Estimating Full-band Solar-induced Chlorophyll Fluorescence from Photosystem II in Crops
authors: Mengjia Qi, Xinjie Liu, Liangyun Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Solar-induced chlorophyll fluorescence (SIF) serves as an efficient tool for monitoring photosynthesis. The SIF signal detected by sensors is the top-of-canopy directional SIF (SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TOC</sub>, mW/m²/nm/sr) contributed by both photosystem I (PSI) and photosystem II (PSII) in the chlorophyll molecules. However, variations in fluorescence quantum yield are primarily associated with the PSII, making PSII SIF more closely linked to vegetation gross primary productivity (GPP) than PSI SIF. To effectively apply the SIF-GPP physiologically coupled mechanical light response (MLR) model for GPP estimation, it is essential to convert SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TOC</sub> to the full-band SIF emitted by PSII (SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub>, μmol/m²/s) necessary for the MLR model. This study presents a data-driven solution aimed at achieving the accurate prediction of SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub> in crops using multi-band SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TOC</sub> and simple vegetation indices. The model was developed using the Random Forest algorithm, trained on a representative dataset generated by the Soil Canopy Observation of Photosynthesis and Energy fluxes (SCOPE) model, and the driving parameters were selected by the Maximum Relevance Minimum Redundancy (MRMR) method. Evaluation results with the simulated datasets indicate that the proposed method has good accuracy in estimating SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub> (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≥ 0.813, RMSE ≤ 6.380 μmol/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/s, rRMSE ≤ 26.79%). Furthermore, the estimated SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub> effectively tracks GPP dynamics on both seasonal and daily scales. The GPP estimates derived from applying SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub> to the MLR model exhibit strong agreement with in situ carbon flux measurements (seasonal scale: R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.81, RMSE = 5.82 μmol/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/s; daily scale: R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.77, RMSE = 3.56 μmol/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/s). Overall, the proposed data-driven method for SIF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">FULL-PSII</sub> estimation is well-compatible with the MLR model and thereby contributes to the SIF-based GPP estimation.

title: Digital Courtesy in E-Commerce: Development and Validation of a Multidimensional Measurement Gauge
authors: I-Ching Chen, Li-Qian Quan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: As the consumption landscape evolves, consumer decision-making is shifting from value-driven material pursuits to experience-oriented patterns that emphasize psychological satisfaction. However, current e-commerce evaluation systems lack courtesy assessment tools grounded in consumers’ psychological perceptions. To address this gap, this study adopts a psychological perspective on perceived courtesy and employs Structural Equation Modeling to conduct empirical research across six dimensions within the e-commerce context: personalized recommendation, respect for customer choice, cost-effectiveness, consumer service, interface design and security. The objective is to develop a psychometric gauge tailored to e-commerce environments, providing a structured measurement framework for platforms and facilitating the optimization of courtesy-oriented service design grounded in psychological principles. Findings reveal that all six dimensions significantly contribute to consumers’ perception of e-commerce courtesy, conceptualized as a higher-order psychological construct. Customer service emerges as the most influential factor, whereas value of money exerts the least impact. The other four dimensions—personalized recommendation, respect for consumer choice, interface design, and security—exhibit comparable levels of influence. This study develops an initial empirical scale for e-commerce courtesy and highlights practical insights for platforms to improve consumer satisfaction through more courteous interactions.

title: Empirical Nonlinear Behavioral Modeling for THz Amplifiers
authors: Simon Haussmann, Lea Zurmuehl, Erfan Amini, Benjamin Schoch, Dirk Schwantuschke, Axel Tessmann, Ingmar Kallfass
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: We present for the first time a comprehensive evaluation of nonlinear behavioral memory models for THz amplifiers. With the employed measurement principle, a cross-domain characterization setup, and an isolated view on THz devices is enabled. It allows the extraction of broadband AM–AM and AM–PM behavior with minimal noise contribution. Multiple datasets are employed, including two for a D-band gallium-nitride (GaN) amplifier and two for an indium-gallium-arsenide (InGaAs) H-band amplifier. Various modeling techniques, such as generalized memory polynomials (GMPs), Volterra series, and artificial neural networks (NNs), are applied to the broadband waveforms, and their modeling accuracy is compared using normalized mean square error. In addition, predistortion capabilities are assessed through the error vector magnitude (EVM) and the adjacent channel power ratio. The results show that both devices-under-test exhibit similarly high modeling accuracy with a normalized mean square error below −48 dB. Moreover, we highlight the potential and applicability of digital predistortion (DPD) for THz communication systems, predicting a reduction in the mean EVM of over 10 dB.

title: Development of a Multi-Point Flow Monitoring System for Accurate Pipeline Leakage Detection
authors: Praveen V. Pol, Vikas J. Nandeshwar, Tejas R. Kulkarni, Sejal M. Mankawade, Aditya V. Kulsange, Samadhan S. Kendre
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Small flow deviation detection needs to achieve high accuracy because it serves as a critical requirement for maintaining operational safety and system reliability in controlled pipeline systems. The research paper introduces a multiple point flow monitoring system which uses differential pulse analysis together with physical flow verification to identify leaks and theft incidents. The system needs three turbine flow sensors which will be placed at the system's inlet and outlet points and one system intermediary branch. The system detects leaks by analyzing inlet–outlet pulse imbalance which the intermediate sensor uses to measure diverted flow for leak detection while it filters out transient detection false alarms. The testing results showed that the system achieved 98% detection accuracy which produced no false positive results during normal operation tests and induced leakage tests and abrupt flow change tests. The system needs two factors which are sampling interval and embedded processing latency to determine its response time because these factors enable near real-time alarm generation. The ESP32 controller runs all decision-making operations locally which allows the system to function without needing cloud access and an IoT system allows users to monitor operations while collecting data. The proposed architecture enables industrial facilities to detect pipeline leaks through an efficient computing system which scales without needing extra pressure measurement devices.

title: Comments on “Lightweight Multi-User Public-Key Authenticated Encryption With Keyword Search”
authors: Keita Emura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Xu et al. (IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security 2025) proposed a lightweight multi-user public-key authenticated encryption with keyword search (LM-PAEKS) scheme. In this short note, we demonstrate that keyword information can be leaked from ciphertexts in the scheme. We further note that there are shortcomings in the security proof as well.

title: AI-Enabled IoT Smart Collar for Livestock Health Monitoring using CNN–LSTM in a Rental-based Rural Framework
authors: S. Satheesbabu, M. Priyanka, T. Renusree, S. K. Samyuktha, L. Sandhiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Livestock farming plays a vital role in supporting rural economies, food production, and agricultural sustainability. However, livestock health monitoring in rural environments faces challenges including delayed disease detection, limited veterinary accessibility, lack of continuous supervision, and absence of affordable intelligent monitoring infrastructure. Traditional practices rely on periodic manual observation, often failing to detect early physiological and behavioral abnormalities. Existing solutions provide partial visibility and lack integrated multi-parameter monitoring, reducing reliability for early diagnosis. Large grazing areas further increase monitoring difficulty. The proposed work introduces an intelligent livestock monitoring framework integrating multi-sensor physiological data collection with CNN–LSTM-based time-series analysis. The system continuously captures parameters including body temperature, heart rate, movement activity, rumination behavior, and location. Sequential sensor data undergo preprocessing and feature extraction before analysis to identify abnormal behavioral and physiological patterns. Real-time analysis enables early detection of diseases, stress, injuries, and abnormal activity. Security monitoring detects unusual motion and collar tampering. Automated alert generation ensures timely response. The system provides multi-sensor monitoring, intelligent analysis, early detection, and scalable capability, with experimental results demonstrating reliable classification and improved detection stability.

title: Cognitive Shield: An Interpretable Stacking Ensemble for Advanced Cyber Threat Analytics in IoT Networks
authors: Hammouda Mohamed Islam, Ning Bai, Shouyue Wang, Xiaoya Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: We present Cognitive Shield, an interpretable two-layer stacking ensemble for unified binary and multi-class intrusion detection in IoT environments. The framework integrates Random Forest, XGBoost, and Extra Trees as base learners, with Logistic Regression as a meta-learner over meta-features, alongside feature selection for high-dimensional data. Evaluated on the IoT-CAD dataset, it achieves state-of-the-art results (up to 99.95% accuracy and F1-score), outperforming classical, ensemble, and deep learning baselines. The model further incorporates SHAP and LIME to provide global and local interpretability. This combination of accuracy, robustness, and transparency makes Cognitive Shield a practical solution for real-world IoT security analytics.

title: Joint Source-Channel Coding and Decoding Based on Channel Modulation Network
authors: Ping Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In recent years, Deep Learning-based Joint Source-Channel Coding (DL JSCC) has emerged as a research hotspot in the field of wireless data transmission due to its advantages in end-to-end joint optimization. To address the challenges that existing DL JSCC schemes face in dynamically adapting to time-varying wireless channels and their low efficiency in utilizing signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) information, this paper proposes a Channel Modulation Network-based Joint Source-Channel Coding (CMJSCC) model. It dynamically adjusts the channel-wise modulation strategy of encoded features based on real-time channel SNR. Furthermore, a multi-SNR joint training strategy is adopted to enhance the model's generalization capability and reconstruction quality under varying channel conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance across different datasets and evaluation metrics.

title: Structured Channel-Pruned ResNet for Lightweight Multi-Region Bone Fracture Detection from X-Ray Images
authors: Mallika S, Sivakumar Chinnusamy, Jeban Daniel, Joshua Thangaraj Gnanasekar, Sarath Awath L.R., Santhosh M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Automated bone fracture detection using deep learning has demonstrated promising diagnostic performance; however, high computational complexity limits real-time deployment in resource-constrained healthcare environments. This paper proposes a lightweight structured channel-pruned ResNet framework for multi-region bone fracture detection from X-ray images. Using L1-norm-based channel pruning, a pretrained ResNet-18 backbone is optimized by getting rid of 40% of unnecessary convolutional filters. Then, fine-tuning is done to bring performance back to where it was. The model we suggest gets 94.2% accuracy, 92.8% sensitivity, 95.5% specificity, and an AUC of 0.96 on the test dataset. The pruned model has 42% fewer parameters, 38% fewer FLOPs, and 41% less time to make a decision than the full ResNet-18 model. The accuracy only drops by 0.9%. Experimental findings indicate that structured pruning successfully maintains diagnostic reliability while markedly enhancing computational efficiency. The proposed lightweight framework is suitable for real-time and edge-based medical AI deployment.

title: Deterministic Dual–Frequency Data Injection Against CMOS Digital Systems
authors: Masahiro Kinugawa, Yuichi Hayashi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: This article investigates the threat of deterministic, contactless data injection into complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) digital systems via radiated intentional electromagnetic interference (IEMI) from an offensive security standpoint. We present a dual-wave technique that combines two radio-frequency waves to exploit frequency-dependent coupling and nonlinear rectification at the CMOS input stage, enabling controlled shifts of the effective input voltage across the logic threshold. Compared with single-frequency irradiation, the proposed approach provides reproducible bit-level control and mitigates transition-time constraints that limit the speed of high-speed injections. We also describe a practical procedure for selecting frequency pairs and power levels without requiring prior knowledge of the target’s internal design. The method is validated on a standard CMOS inverter and on a Raspberry Pi 4 Model B, demonstrating successful universal asynchronous receiver and transmitter injection at 115.2 kb/s under gigahertz transverse electromagnetic mode-cell exposure. Finally, we discuss multilayer countermeasures ranging from enclosure and PCB practices to integrated circuit- and system-level protections, and show that dual-wave IEMI poses a realistic integrity threat that warrants consideration in both electromagnetic compatibility and security evaluations.

title: PLOSQ3D: Privacy-Preserving Location-Based Skyline Query over Encrypted 3D Spatial Data
authors: Zhuliang Jia, Suprio Ray, Rongxing Lu, Pulei Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Privacy-preserving location-based skyline queries (LBSQ) enable location-based services (LBS) over outsourced spatial data while preserving data privacy. Prior studies primar ily consider Euclidean distance as the spatial-attribute metric. However, this does not capture reachability in three-dimensional (3D) obstacle environments, where obstacles invalidate direct Euclidean distances and necessitate obstacle-aware path computations. In this work, we present PLOSQ3D, a privacy-preserving location-based skyline query framework in 3D obstacle space. We first formalize the problem, specify its privacy requirements, and identify the challenges introduced by obstacle-aware distances. Subsequently, we introduce a novel index, Secure Augmented R-Tree (SAR-Tree) that jointly encodes spatial and non-spatial at tributes, enabling effective pruning under 3D spatial constraints. To enforce privacy, we leverage Function Secret Sharing (FSS) to design three protocols: SLDC for secure dominance computation, SSPC for secure shortest-path computation, and OLDU for oblivious skyline-set update. Building on them, we carefully design the PLOSQ3D scheme. Our security analysis demonstrates strong privacy guarantees against honest-but-curious adversaries. We further conduct extensive experiments on both synthetic and real 3D datasets. Experimental results show that our framework achieves high efficiency in both computation and communication overhead, confirming its practicality for 3D spatial applications that demand spatial-awareness and privacy protection.

title: Multi-Snapshot Deep Denoising for Channel Estimation in OTFS Modulated Systems
authors: Surbhi Gehlot, Siddhi Shinde, Suraj Srivastava, Sandeep Kumar Yadav
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: A deep denoising based channel estimation framework is proposed for orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulated systems, wherein channel state information (CSI) recovery is formulated as an image restoration problem. A salient attribute of the approach is the exploitation of structural invariance in the delay Doppler (DD) domain channel over a geometric coherence time, allowing multiple OTFS frames captured during this period to serve as noisy snapshots of the approximately identical channel. These snapshots jointly enhance the effectiveness of the proposed lightweight denoiser based on nonlinear activation free network (NAFNet). The method exhibits low computational complexity, operates reliably even at low pilot signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), and can accommodate both fractional delay and fractional Doppler effects. Simulation results demonstrate significant performance gains over the existing methods.

title: Automated Cloud Risk Scoring via End-to-End CVE-ATT&CK Mapping and Flow Reconstruction
authors: Millati Pratiwi, Jaeyoung Jeong, Seunghyuk Kim, Yoon-Ho Choi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Automated cybersecurity risk assessment critically depends on bridging the long-standing gap between publicly disclosed vulnerabilities (CVEs) and adversarial techniques (MITRE ATT&CK), yet these knowledge bases have traditionally evolved in isolation. We introduce a unified, end-to-end pipeline in two stages: mapping and validation. The mapping stage leverages small language models to extract structured entities from unstructured CVE descriptions and fine-tunes a LoRA-augmented SLM model for CVE→technique mapping, while the validation stage contains an extractive QA annotation model to re-anchor and explain each mapping, and validates predicted technique flows through real exploit proofs-of-concept. On a held-out human-labeled test set of 127 samples, our mapping model achieves a Top-1 accuracy of 56% and Top-Max accuracy of 90.70%. Extended evaluation on 269 samples yields an LRAP of 0.5045 (95% CI: [0.46, 0.55]), comparable to SMET [1], the closest prior work on this task. The annotation model reaches an F1 score of 92.00%, and flow-level validation on 79 exploits confirms 98.00% of predicted sequences. By combining transparent reasoning, high coverage, and empirical validation, our approach delivers the largest public CVE-to-ATT&CK dataset to date and advances explainable, scalable vulnerability analysis and risk scoring.

title: 2D Ultrasound Elasticity Imaging of Abdominal Aortic Aneurysms Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Utsav Ratna Tuladhar, Richard Simon, Doran Mix, Michael Richards
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAA) pose a significant clinical risk due to their potential for rupture, which is often asymptomatic but can be fatal. Although maximum diameter is commonly used for risk assessment, diameter alone is insufficient as it does not capture the properties of the underlying material of the vessel wall, which play a critical role in determining the risk of rupture. To overcome this limitation, we propose a deep learning-based framework for elasticity imaging of AAAs with 2D ultrasound. Leveraging finite element simulations, we generate a diverse dataset of displacement fields with their corresponding modulus distributions. We train a model with U-Net architecture and normalized mean squared error (NMSE) to infer the spatial modulus distribution from the axial and lateral components of the displacement fields. This model is evaluated across three experimental domains: digital phantom data from 3D COMSOL simulations, physical phantom experiments using biomechanically distinct vessel models, and clinical ultrasound exams from AAA patients. Our simulated results demonstrate that the proposed deep learning model is able to reconstruct modulus distributions, achieving an NMSE score of 1.6%. Similarly, in phantom data, the predicted modular ratio closely matches the expected values, affirming the model's ability to generalize to phantom data. We compare our approach with an iterative method which shows comparable performance but higher computation time per reconstruction. In contrast, once trained offline, the deep learning method can provide quick and accurate estimates of tissue stiffness from ultrasound images, so as to provide pseudo-realtime predictions of AAA growth.

title: Energy Consumption Minimization in STAR-RIS-Assisted UAV-MEC Networks with NOMA
authors: Hamed Mohammadi, Mahrokh G. Shayesteh, Hashem Kalbkhani, Azadeh Khazali
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) network has been emerged as a key enabler for meeting the low-latency and high-computation demands of mobile users with constrained resources. This paper proposes a novel two-tier MEC framework in which a UAV is equipped with both an MEC server and a simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS). In the proposed architecture, users can partially offload tasks either via the STAR-RIS to the UAV-based MEC server or reflect them toward a terrestrial MEC server. To improve energy efficiency, we formulate a weighted energy consumption minimization problem by jointly optimizing task bit allocation and user transmit power, STAR-RIS phase shifts, and UAV trajectory under a non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) protocol. Due to the non-convex and strongly coupled nature of the problem, it is decomposed into three subproblems. Each subproblem is solved using the successive convex approximation (SCA) method, which involves the first-order Taylor series expansion and the maximum-ratio transmission (MRT) technique. Subsequently, an efficient block coordinate descent (BCD) iterative algorithm is developed, in which these three subproblems are alternately solved. These processes gradually converge to a suboptimal solution. The results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, showing significant energy savings compared to the conventional schemes such as orthogonal multiple access (OMA) and other schemes.

title: STATE: Safe and Threatening Adversarial Trajectory Engine
authors: Tonmoay Deb, Natalia Denisenko, Chongyang Gao, Valerio La Gatta, Sven de Laaf, Afke Neeleman, Lirika Sola, V.S. Subrahmanian
publish: IEEE Intelligent Systems
abstract: As terrorists ramp up drone use, the need to protect cities, regions, and utilities from drone-based terror attacks has become an urgent priority. We present STATE, a novel GAN-based framework to automatically generate a set of safe and threatening drone trajectories over a region. Using STATE, security officials can generate synthetic trajectories for regions over which no such trajectories were previously recorded, enabling them to better test planned defenses. Jointly with security officials from The Hague, we show that STATE beats six recent baselines, achieving up to 75.8% improvement in trajectory plausibility and 35.8% in threat alignment, as evaluated by police experts.
