title: Towards a co-design framework for bipedal robots
authors: Jacobo Torres-Figueroa, Eduardo Vega-Alvarado, Maria B. Calva-Yañez, Noemi Hernandez-Oliva, Edgar A. Portilla-Flores
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, a co-design framework for developing bipedal robots capable of steady energy-efficient dynamic locomotion is proposed. In contrast to conventional design methodologies, where the mechanical structure, the walking pattern generator, and the control parameters are sequentially developed, the proposed approach integrates all these components in a unified optimization process. The design problem is formulated as a multiobjective dynamic problem to simultaneously optimize the structural parameters of the robot, the walking pattern generator, and the gains of the low-level controllers. The general objective is to maximize the walked distance while minimizing the energy consumption during locomotion. A modified multiobjective version of the Differential Evolution algorithm was employed to solve the optimization problem, in combination with a mechanism for constraint handling based on Pareto Dominance. The framework generates a solution set with feasible designs, each including the optimal dimensions of the robotic links, the corresponding walking patterns and the controller gains. As a case study, a bipedal robot with anthropomorphic dimensions was synthesized and evaluated using multi-body simulation. The obtained solutions were analyzed in terms of Cost of Transport and compared to available bipedal commercial robots. These results showed that the proposed framework can generate feasible solutions, that were in average 10% more efficient than those of the commercial robots, highlighting the advantages of co-design strategies for the development of bipedal robotic systems.

title: Measuring Greenland Ice Sheet Melt based on GNSS-R and Random Forest Using FengYun-3E GNOS-R
authors: Liangyu Chen, Jiachun An, Zemin Wang, Baojun Zhang, Jingxin Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: As surface melting of the Greenland Ice Sheet (GrIS) is an important driver of sea-level rise against the backdrop of accelerating global climate system warming, high-precision monitoring of its state is crucial for understanding the polar-atmosphere coupling mechanism. The melting detection is mainly based on microwave radiometer with spatial resolution of only tens of kilometers, while GNSS-R can have higher spatial resolution of kilometers. GNSS-R ice sheet melting detection mainly uses the reflectivity threshold method, which has low accuracy and lack of discussion of physical mechanisms. This study utilizes Delay-Doppler Map (DDM) data from the GNOS-R sensor aboard China's FY-3E polar-orbiting satellite to construct an ice-sheet melting detection model based on the random forest algorithm with multiple parameters innovatively, achieving spatiotemporal dynamic inversion of the surface freeze-thaw processes over GrIS. Through comparative and combinatorial experiments with four designed feature subsets (waveform-related parameters, power-related parameters, geometric condition constraints, and spatial location constraints), it is found that increasing feature dimensions and complementarity of physical meanings significantly enhance model performance, ensuring stable and accurate freeze-thaw detection under different scattering mechanisms. The optimal model demonstrates excellent performance in multi-metric evaluations, with many evaluation metrics exceeding 96%. The spatial resolution is greatly improved while maintaining similar accuracy to the microwave radiometer. Through combined experiments on different parameter types, this study further reveals the regulatory effect of the incident angle on the scattering mechanism and characteristic response during the surface melting process of the ice sheet. Its physical essence lies in the differences in dielectric loss and dielectric uniformity of the GNSS signal between frozen and melt state of snow at different incident angles. In summary, this study provides a solution combining physical interpretability and computational efficiency for complex scene classification of multisource remote sensing data, holding significant scientific implications for improving input parameters of climate system models and enhancing the prediction accuracy of sea-level rise.

title: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Suicide Risk Assessment Using Social Media Data
authors: Tsholofelo Mokheleli, Tebogo Makaba, Patrick Ndayizigamiye, Nompumelelo Ndlovu, Hossana Twinomurinzi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate and interpretable suicide risk assessment is critical for timely intervention in mental health contexts. This study presents an explainable machine learning approach for suicide risk assessment using social media data, evaluating both multi-class and binary classification settings alongside a multi-level explainability strategy. Results show that while multi-class classification enables fine-grained risk stratification, it introduces substantial predictive complexity, with models achieving moderate performance (balanced accuracy ≈ 0.53 and ROC-AUC ≈ 0.79). In contrast, binary classification improves stability and discrimination, supporting its suitability for real-world risk prioritisation in digital mental health settings. To enhance interpretability, the study integrates SHAP and LIME to provide complementary global and local explanations. SHAP identifies key linguistic indicators such as temporal references, intent-related terms, and self-referential expressions as primary drivers of elevated risk, while LIME provides instance-level justification for individual predictions. Building on this, a counterfactual explanation method, Counterfactual Explanations with Optimised Contextual Flow (CE-OCF), is introduced. CE-OCF generates minimal, contextually coherent textual modifications that shift model predictions, enabling the simulation of both escalation and de-escalation pathways. The study advances explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) for suicide risk assessment by combining predictive modelling with actionable interpretability. These findings highlight the importance of aligning model performance with clinically relevant explanations, supporting the development of transparent and decision-supportive AI systems for early detection and intervention.

title: Development of a GPS based Smart Fire Detection using UAV-UGV Coordinated Control
authors: Yogesh Varshney, Harshit Sharma, Loveleen Sharma, P.M Tiwari, Mehtab Fatima, Kamlesh Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper shows an intelligent firefighting system that involves a UAV (quad-copter) and UGV (rover) which reduces the latency and the human risk in fire calamities. Traditional methods have very high dependency on human involvement and old systems, which can be unsafe and have high latency in hazardous environments. To address this, the systems shown involves a quad-copter with Raspberry Pi, various sensors like flame, IR camera and GPS module to have real time monitoring on the various areas and detect fire very fast. Whenever the fire is detected, the GPS location is provided by the drone wirelessly to the rover through ESP 32 module. The rover which has the brain - Arduino Mega receives the location and moves towards the point with obstacle avoidance and turns ON the pump to extinguish the fire hotspots. Testing shows reliable fire detection and effective fire suppression on the roots of the fire. The system gives a practical approach for improving the human risk and efficient in fire calamities.

title: An Attention-based Multimodal Feature Fusion Model for Stroke Prediction with Explainable Clinical Decision Support
authors: Navaneetha Krishnan Muthunambu, Sowmiya K, Manu L B, Karthic E, Nithish S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Stroke is a leading cause of mortality and long-term disability worldwide, making early risk prediction an important component of preventive healthcare. However, clinical data used for stroke prediction are heterogeneous and often include demographic, clinical, and lifestyle variables whose relative importance may vary across patients. Traditional machine learning models typically treat these features as a single homogeneous vector, which limits their ability to capture modality-specific relationships and reduces interpretability in clinical decision support systems. To address these challenges, this study proposes an attention-based multimodal feature fusion model for stroke prediction that integrates heterogeneous patient data while maintaining explainability. The framework first encodes demographic, clinical, and lifestyle features using separate modality-specific neural encoders and then dynamically fuses these representations using an attention mechanism that learns the relative importance of each modality. Experimental evaluation on the publicly available Kaggle Stroke Prediction Dataset demonstrates that the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 91% and an AUC of 0.92, outperforming traditional baseline models such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost. Additionally, modality-level attention weights and SHAP-based feature attribution provide interpretable insights, supporting transparent and trustworthy clinical decision making.

title: MVDR-Integrated TFM for Defect Detection in Geometrically Shadowed Regions
authors: Wei Li, Zheng Hu, Da Teng, Yanxun Xiang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Detecting defects in geometrically shadowed regions remains an unavoidable challenge for ultrasonic array imaging using the total focusing method (TFM). To address this limitation, the MVDR-TFM method is proposed in this work, in which minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) beamforming is integrated into the TFM framework. By minimizing the output power under a distortionless constraint, interference from strong near-field scatterers is suppressed and sidelobe leakage is reduced, thereby enhancing defect visibility in shadowed areas. In an L-shaped specimen, slot-type defects under the structural barrier are successfully identified by the proposed method, whereas conventional TFM cannot. In terms of contrast ratio and contrast-to-noise ratio, the proposed method achieves improvements of 40.44% and 80.34% over the conventional method, respectively. For super-resolution imaging of two side‑drilled holes of 1 mm diameter with a 1 mm spacing below the Rayleigh limit, the peak-to-valley amplitude difference of the resulting lateral cross-sectional profile is increased by 10.53 dB with the proposed method compared to TFM. These results demonstrate improved defect detectability and resolvability of the MVDR-TFM method, enabling more reliable ultrasonic array inspections.

title: Conformal Single-port Microwave Biosensor for Contactless Dielectric Liquid Characterization
authors: Benyamin Harkinezhad, Tomislav Markovic, Robin Evans, Kamran Ghorbani, Efstratios Skafidas, Dominique Schreurs
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a flexible, one-port microwave biosensor for contactless permittivity characterization of biological and chemical samples based on waveguide mode excitation. Unlike conventional resonator-based or multi-port microwave sensors, which rely on resonance tracking or transmission measurements, the proposed sensor operates using a single-port reflection measurement and exploits the excitation and re-coupling of a waveguide mode within the material under test (MUT). The proposed sensor employs a cylindrical coplanar waveguide (CCPW) wrapped around standard laboratory glass test tubes to excite a waveguide mode within the material under test without direct contact. The underlying coupling mechanism is analyzed using field overlap considerations, and its manifestation in the measured reflection coefficient is examined. The excitation and re-coupling of the waveguide mode give rise to characteristic loop features in the S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sub> response, which are shown to be strongly dependent on the real and imaginary parts of the sample permittivity. A dedicated deembedding procedure is introduced to isolate the intrinsic sensor response using a single reference measurement. The sensor performance is experimentally validated through measurements on aqueous solutions of ethanol, NaCl, glucose, and yeast. Sensitivity metrics derived from the extracted loop parameters demonstrate minimum sensitivities of 14.1MHz per unit ϵ ′ <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">r</sub> and 0.21 per unit tanδ. In addition, the sensor exhibits good repeatability, with maximum standard deviations of 0.0054 in magnitude and 7.52° in phase across repeated measurements up to 5 GHz. The results highlight a sensing approach that combines contactless operation, simplified measurement, and high sensitivity, offering a compelling alternative to existing microwave biosensing techniques.

title: Decoupled Neural Audio Steganography for Adaptive Sender-Side Model Updates
authors: Yanan Li, Qiyang Xiao, Mingxi Zhang, Yanzhen Ren, Lina Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Neural network–based steganography has garnered considerable attention for its strong security. However, existing approaches often suffer from excessive coupling between the embedding and extraction networks: the sender and receiver must employ paired models and maintain strict synchronization. Such synchronization not only complicates deployment but also introduces more severe potential risks of information leakage. To overcome this limitation, we propose a synchronization-free steganographic framework based on decoupled neural embedding networks, following the destruction–restoration principle. In our design, message embedding is realized through a destruction operation, while recovery is achieved using a neural network from the audio restoration domain. This decoupled architecture allows the sender to upgrade, replace, or randomize the embedding network—thus enabling dynamic model changes—without impairing the receiver’s ability to correctly extract the hidden message. As a result, synchronization-related vulnerabilities are fundamentally eliminated. Experimental results demonstrate that even under dynamic changes in the embedding network, the hidden information can still be reliably extracted, confirming both the effectiveness and enhanced security of the proposed approach.

title: CNTFET based Energy-efficient Approximate Full Adder for Low Power and High Speed Computing
authors: P. Raja, S. Jayanthi, M. Mohamed Sajid, D. Praveen, A. Sarathi Kannan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Full adders are the core of the arithmetic logic units in digital systems and its architecture has a significant effect on the speed, area of silicon and power dissipation. This paper presents a full adder cell based on the Carbon Nanotube Field Effect Transistor (CNTFET) at 32 nm technology node. A dynamic logic structure is adopted to achieve the objectives of minimizing the number of transistors and the internal switching activities, also to make the circuit simpler and the operating frequency higher. Power dissipation is also minimized by chirality vectors optimization of the evaluation-stage transistors that facilitate the minimization of leakage current. The proposed energy-efficient approximate full adder (EAFA) is evaluated through HSPICE simulation using the Stanford CNTFET model at different supply voltages. The simulation results show an average power dissipation of 0.207 μW, worst case propagation delay of 11.52 ps, and power delay product of 2.387 aJ. The proposed structure offers better energy efficiency as compared to conventional CMOS realization as well as other existing adders based on CNTFET is therefore suitable for high-speed image processing applications.

title: Adaptive Optimal PI Control for Grid-Forming Inverters With Provable Multitime-Scale Stability via Lyapunov-Constrained Learning
authors: Hamad Alduaij, Chenxu Chao, Yang Weng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Modern inverter-based power systems require controllers that adapt to changing grid conditions while maintaining strict stability across nested control loops. Existing approaches face a fundamental tradeoff: classical proportional–integral (PI) controllers offer provable stability but are static, while learning-based methods can adapt but often violate the structural stability guarantees needed for safe operation, especially in systems with tightly coupled, multitime-scale dynamics. This work proposes a distributed adaptive control framework for grid-forming inverters that combines neural gain scheduling with provable Lyapunov stability across multiple time scales. Our approach reformulates nested Lyapunov stability conditions as explicit gain-ratio inequality constraints and embeds them into both: 1) an offline augmented-Lagrangian constrained optimization for base gain synthesis and 2) online neural schedulers that adapt controller parameters to changing operating conditions while preserving stability certificates. A key contribution is the introduction of per-converter dynamic adaptation states governed by Lyapunov-passivity constraints, enabling state-dependent gain scheduling within certified stability envelopes. Implemented within a physics-informed, differentiable simulation environment, controllers tuned with our method preserve stability under severe transients, including large load steps and setpoint changes, while improving closed-loop regulation. This framework retains the familiar PI control architecture while rigorously enforcing stability margins, offering a practical stability-certified learning paradigm for emerging power-electronic systems. The adaptive control performs algorithmic gain synthesis and scheduling as operating conditions change, implemented through learned neural schedulers operating at multiple timescales.

title: RBTGA: A Multi-Strategy Collaborative Enhancement BERT-LSTM Model for Hotel Service Review Analysis
authors: Hanxuan Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Traditional Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT)-Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architectures for sentiment classification suffer from limitations including restricted contextual modeling under static masking strategies, limited long-sequence dependency modeling, and potentially increased computational cost. To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhanced architecture based on Robustly Optimized BERT Pretraining Approach (RoBERTa)-Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN)-Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU)-Attention. RoBERTa is adopted as the representation layer to improve contextual semantic encoding through optimized pre-training strategies. To replace the structurally complex LSTM, a unified TCN-GRU framework is introduced, where TCN captures multi-scale temporal patterns and GRU preserves long-term sequential information. An Attention mechanism is further applied to perform weighted aggregation of sequence features, enabling emphasis on sentiment-critical semantics. Experiments conducted on a hotel service review dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an F1 score of 0.8872 and an accuracy of 0.8462. Compared with multiple benchmark models, it yields accuracy gains ranging from 0.0045 to 0.1583 and F1 gains from 0.0016 to 0.0987. Overall, the proposed architecture provides a robust and competitive solution for complex sentiment classification tasks.

title: Optical Performance Improvement of Thin Crystalline Silicon Solar Cells through ZnS Nanoparticle Integration
authors: A. Sandeep Kumar Reddy, Arijit Bardhan Roy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: As the next-generation photovoltaic devices, thin and flexible crystalline silicon(c-Si) solar cells have generated a lot of attention because of their lightweight, bendable design and potential for incorporation into wearable and portable applications. Never the less, their smaller thickness restricts optical absorption, which results in a reduction in efficiency. In order to overcome this difficulty sophisticated light management techniques are necessary. This work investigates the application of high-refractive-material nanoparticles, such as ZnS, to enhance the hetero-junction solar cell's light management system. While ZnS nanoparticles scattering and plasmonic- like behavior help to increase optical path lengths within the active layer, their significant absorption coefficient and tunable band gap enable better photon harvesting over a wide spectrum range. Almost 7% reduction of reflectance is shown in the proposed light management structure using validated by FEM (finite element method) based simulation tool. The work describes design strategies, material integration methods, and optical simulations that show how nanoparticles can maximize photocurrent generation, reduce reflection losses, and enhance light coupling. The suggested tactics show a possible technique to achieve sophisticated nanostructured light management schemes in c-Si solar cells that are mechanically flexible, lightweight, and efficient.

title: Development of IoT-Enabled Building Management System for Advanced Safety, Energy Efficiency and Environmental Sustainability
authors: Pranay Debnath, Anurag Dey, S. Purushothaman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing need for energy efficiency, safety, and sustainability in modern infrastructure has sped up the use of Internet of Things (IoT) smart building solutions. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Smart IoT-Enabled Building Management System that integrates solar energy for smart energy use and improved safety in institutional buildings. The proposed system uses an ESP32 microcontroller as the main control unit, connected to several sensors including temperature and humidity sensors, motion detectors, gas and flame sensors, light sensors, and current and voltage sensors. This setup allows for real-time monitoring of the environment, analysis of energy use, and detection of hazards. Energy optimization occurs through automated control of appliances with relay modules, lighting based on occupancy, daylight-sensitive switching, and user-driven adjustments with potentiometers to reduce unnecessary energy use. A combined solar-grid management scheme employs a photovoltaic panel, charge controller, and battery storage to use renewable energy for important loads. Sensor data and system status are sent to a cloud platform through Wi-Fi and MQTT, which allows for remote monitoring, control, and alert notifications via a web or mobile dashboard. Testing in a college building shows better energy use, quicker hazard responses, and less reliance on grid power. The proposed system offers a scalable, budget-friendly, and sustainable approach to smart building management and helps create energy-efficient and safe smart campuses.

title: Auto-Scaling Strategies for Machine Learning Workloads: Performance, Cost, and Model Reliability: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Dhanush Ponna, Janvi Shah, Sushika Reddy Gade, Kinjal A Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Massive and real-time machine learning (ML) applications impose great demands on resource management in cloud and distributed systems because of the dynamics of workloads, dependency on GPUs, and strict latency constraints. In this paper, a systematic review of reactive, predictive, and hybrid-based auto-scaling methods are presented and customized to ML workloads on clouds-based VMs, in containerized (Kubernetes) and serverless environments. In contrast to the previous surveys, this one has a single three-dimensional comparative framework which is based on performance, cost-efficiency, and model reliability. The analyzed key trade-offs, characteristics of a platform, workload uncertainty, and cold-start latency. The paper brings into the spotlight of the new ML-specific capabilities (e.g., scaling by using learning curves) and new directions, including reinforcement learning-based controllers and energy-aware scaling. Result describe that workload sensitive, reliability sensitive approaches are important to effective machine learning deployments.

title: Improved Predictive Speed Control of Brushless Dual Rotor Motors Considering Speed Measurement Delay and Virtual Inertia Identification
authors: Hui Wu, Shuangxia Niu, Zekai Lyu, Zhenyao Sun, Mingyuan Jiang, Chunhua Liu, Wei Liu, K. T. Chau
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The brushless dual rotor motor (BLDRM), with its multiple electromechanical ports and high integration, holds significant potential for various applications such as electric continuously variable transmission (ECVT) systems and contra-rotating propellers. However, the high pole-pair ratio typically adopted between the rotors and modulation windings in magnetic field modulation design imposes stringent requirements on the dynamic speed response performance, making precise and rapid control challenging. To address these issues, an improved predictive speed control (IPSC) with a discrete third-order extended state observer (ESO) and virtual inertia identification is proposed to strengthen the transient speed performance of BLDRM. With the discrete third-order ESO, the measured speed is precisely compensated, thereby enhancing speed prediction accuracy for IPSC and further improving speed response performance. Besides, accurate parameter tuning and performance analysis are obtained within the discrete domain of the proposed IPSC control strategy. With the virtual moment of inertia as well as adaptive estimation of key parameters, the robustness against model parameter uncertainties is enhanced. Finally, experimental validation on a BLDRM drive platform confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Improved RRT-Connect Path Planning Algorithm for Robotic Arm
authors: Yubo Wan, Guowei Zhang, Mingxuan Lu, Zhongcheng Pu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address the problems of blind sampling, low search efficiency, and unsmooth paths in the traditional RRT-Connect algorithm for six-degree-of-freedom manipulator path planning, an improved RRT-Connect method is proposed. In the random sampling stage, Gaussian sampling is introduced, in which the sampling probability is adaptively adjusted according to the distance between the node and the target, thereby enhancing the directionality of the search. An adaptive step-size extension mechanism is designed to dynamically adjust the expansion step according to collision detection results, improving planning efficiency in complex environments. The length and smoothness of the path are optimized through redundant node elimination and Cantmull-Rom spline interpolation.

title: LLM-driven Dark Web Intelligence Analysis Pipeline
authors: Lei Jiang, Meng Wang, Yongqing Sun, Jiayi Wang, Haohao Zhang, Haohua Duan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Dark web platforms contain abundant early-stage cyber threat intelligence, such as discussions of exploits, malware, and underground services, which is valuable for proactive security analysis. However, dark web intelligence is highly noisy, fragmented, and temporally dispersed across heterogeneous sources, making large-scale daily analysis and reporting challenging. Existing approaches based on keyword matching, static rules, or isolated mining tasks struggle to synthesize multi-source intelligence into coherent analytical reports.In this paper, we formulate daily dark web intelligence analysis as a multi-source document synthesis problem, where selectively incorporating relevant historical context is essential for capturing threat evolution. To address this problem, we propose an LLM-driven analysis pipeline with a dual-channel retrieval mechanism that combines semantic retrieval for high recall and entity-guided structured retrieval for high precision. We further design a hierarchical summarization strategy to incrementally aggregate intelligence across time and sources, reducing long-context reasoning burden and mitigating hallucinations. Experiments on real-world dark web intelligence demonstrate that our approach generates more coherent, comprehensive, and temporally consistent reports than baseline methods, while remaining efficient for daily operational use.

title: Distributed Multi-Agent Relative Localization via Reference Frame Coordination
authors: Lingwei Xu, Xiao Shen, Chenglong Tian, Yuan Shen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Accurate and robust relative position information is fundamental for enabling self-organized cooperation in multi-agent systems, particularly for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms in the emerging low-altitude economy. However, achieving high-performance relative localization remains challenging, primarily due to the critical issues of reference frame coordination which establishes and maintains a reference frame consensus across the network. In this paper, we establish an optimization framework that bridges performance bounds and algorithm designs, enabling the derivation of optimization structures for centralized relative localization, which facilitates an alternating direction optimization algorithm for subnetwork-oriented scenarios. We then propose a distributed relative localization scheme that integrates an orientation-aware and dynamics-aware state merging mechanism for coordination across different inertial reference frames. Furthermore, we develop a spatial-temporal cooperation approach through real-time adaptive frame alignment and multi-source information fusion, effectively suppressing error accumulation. Simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed algorithms achieve superior accuracy and robustness, providing an effective solution for relative localization and enabling key applications in the low-altitude economy.

title: MAPLE: Self-Supervised Learning-Enhanced Nonlinear Dimensionality Reduction for Visual Analysis
authors: Zeyang Huang, Takanori Fujiwara, Angelos Chatzimparmpas, Wandrille Duchemin, Andreas Kerren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We present a new nonlinear dimensionality reduction method, MAPLE, that enhances UMAP by improving manifold modeling. MAPLE employs a self-supervised learning approach to more efficiently encode low-dimensional manifold geometry. Central to this approach are maximum manifold capacity representations (MMCRs), which help untangle complex manifolds by compressing variances among locally similar data points while amplifying variance among dissimilar data points. This design is particularly effective for high-dimensional data with substantial intra-cluster variance and curved manifold structures, such as biological or image data. Our qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate that MAPLE can produce clearer visual cluster separations and finer subcluster resolution than UMAP while maintaining a tractable computational cost.

title: Comparative Investigation of the Performance of Stainless Steel and 3D-Printed PLA Underwater Thruster Propellers Using FSI and Structural Analysis
authors: Md. Mashud Karim, Bhubon Thiotonius Costa, A K Jamil Shajib, Md. Ashifur Rahaman, Md. Shahidul Islam, Md. Shajratul Alam Towhid, Bishawajit Chakraborty
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: The propellers used by Unmanned Surface Vehicles (USV) and Unmanned Underwater Vehicles (UUV) are primarily made from either plastic, metal, or composite materials. Additive manufacturing can facilitate a promising method for rapid prototyping and developing innovative models. This research investigates the considerations of geometric configuration, fabrication, and performance of 3D-printed polylactic acid (PLA) propellers with a diameter of less than 100 mm using the Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) method. The investigation examines how operational deformation affects thrust and torque, comparing PLA propellers with stainless steel alternatives to identify optimal geometries. To achieve this, unidirectional Fluid-Structure Interaction (FSI) simulations using Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) and structural analysis will be carried out. Computer-aided designs of the propeller geometries were developed by MATLAB programs based on the alterable parameters (rake, skew, and thickness) of the propeller’s blades. The results find that certain geometries with moderate increases in rake and skew with greater thickness achieve higher efficiency, improving superior thrust-to-torque ratios, and making them optimal choices. However, a high increase in rake and skew accompanied by a decrease in thickness leads to a significant drop in efficiency, negatively impacting the thrust-to-torque ratio and overall performance. Although PLA propellers deform more than stainless steel, structural analysis establishes that the stresses are still below the yield strength of the material, guaranteeing operational viability. These findings demonstrate the potential of 3D-printed PLA propellers as an alternative to conventional propellers, offering a path toward a more cost-effective and efficient propulsion system for underwater applications.

title: Machine Learning for Sea State Estimation from Wave-Induced Ship Responses
authors: Remi Larnouhet, Raphael E. G. Mounet, Ulrik D. Nielsen, Gaute Storhaug
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Knowledge of ocean waves is crucial for industry and research. This study evaluates Machine Learning (ML) frameworks for predicting sea state parameters using inertial measurements of linear and angular accelerations from a container vessel during transoceanic voyages. The tested models include LightGBM, Random Forest, Support Vector Regression, and Artificial Neural Networks, with LightGBM emerging as the best-performing and fastest framework. Input discrete frequency features were derived from the power and cross-power spectral densities of heave, roll, and pitch accelerations processed via the Welch algorithm. Sensitivity analysis revealed that a cut-off frequency of $0.3-1.0 \mathrm{~Hz}$, combined with optimal smoothing in the Welch algorithm, effectively balances frequency resolution and computational efficiency, improving model performance while reducing input size

title: Research on performance optimization of parallel computing system based on big data technology
authors: Qingyang Wang, Jiamei Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Parallel computing systems occupy a central position in big data processing technologies. Among them, Hadoop MapReduce, as a classic distributed computing framework, has been widely adopted for offline batch data processing. However, due to its design prioritizing high throughput, it exhibits significant latency issues when handling "short jobs" with processing times ranging from tens of seconds to several minutes. Therefore, optimizing its execution efficiency holds significant practical importance. This study conducts an in-depth analysis of Hadoop MapReduce's task scheduling and execution processes, identifies two primary performance bottlenecks, and designs an improved version—AHadoop—compatible with standard Hadoop based on these findings. Specifically, we migrate lightweight Setup/Cleanup tasks to the JobTracker for execution, eliminating the 12-second wait caused by the heartbeat mechanism. Simultaneously, we introduce an RPC-based instant communication mechanism to effectively separate emergency events from routine cluster management messages, thereby accelerating task scheduling. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to standard Hadoop, AHadoop achieves an average overall execution efficiency improvement exceeding 25% for short jobs. For the specific WordCount, Grep, and Sort tasks, performance gains reached 35%, 38%, and 34%, respectively.
