title: Classification of RAG Retrieval Intent Based on Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Shijia Du, Xiaobo Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the rapid development of large model technology, retrieval enhanced generation has become a key technical direction to solve the problem of large model illusion and improve the accuracy of output results, and plays an indispensable role in both academic research and industrial landing. Retrieval intent recognition is the primary core of the retrieval enhanced generation system. Its classification accuracy directly affects the matching effect of retrieval strategy and the quality of document recall, and then determines the final output performance of the whole retrieval enhanced generation system. Aiming at the shortcomings of existing algorithms in the task of retrieval enhancement generation retrieval intent binary classification, this paper proposes PSO-Transformer classification algorithm. First, the correlation analysis and violin chart analysis are carried out, and then a variety of machine learning algorithms are used to conduct comparative experiments. The experimental results show that the PSO-Transformer classification algorithm proposed in this paper shows significant and comprehensive advantages in various performance indicators, which is comprehensively superior to the compared traditional machine learning algorithms. Its accuracy, recall rate and F1 value all reach 86.1%, accuracy rate reach 86.0%, and AUC value reach 94.2%. It ranks first among all comparative algorithms in classification core indicators and model distinction ability indicators, which provides effective technical support for improving the overall reliability and practicality of retrieval enhanced generation system, and is of great significance to promote the academic deepening and industrial scale application of retrieval enhanced generation technology.

title: MemGaussian: Text-to-3D Gaussian Generation via Alternating Attention and Regularization
authors: Hechuan Wan, Junjie Peng, Jiaxin Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Text-to-3D methods based on 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) can generate high-quality explicit 3D assets, but their high memory cost limits training under moderate GPU memory budgets. This paper presents MemGaussian, a memory-efficient framework built on GSGEN. To reduce memory overhead, we design an alternating attention mechanism that combines local attention with periodic lightweight global semantic-neighbor attention, thereby lowering the cost of self-attention computation. We further introduce an opacity-regularized active pruning strategy, which suppresses redundant low-contribution Gaussians early in training and alleviates uncontrolled densification. Experiments under the same settings as GSGEN show that MemGaussian reduces peak GPU memory from 13.0 GB to 9.3 GB (about 28%) and total training time by about 41%, while maintaining geometric integrity and text-image semantic consistency and improving perceptual image quality. This moves the practical training requirement from the 13 GB class to the 9–10 GB class, making the method more suitable for 12–16 GB GPUs.

title: RISC-Spend and Density-Managed Traffic Signal Management using RFID-based Violation, Theft, and Emergency Vehicle Recognition
authors: Aravinth S, Vimalesh V, P.Nisha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The intersections of the urban traffic require organized coordination to reduce the level of violations, increase emergency clearance, and ameliorate roadways security. Infrared sensing and radio frequency identification mechanisms are used to present a RISC-based density-regulated signal supervision and vehicular identification research paper. The movement of vehicles around the pedestrian crossings during limited periods is detected with infrared modules and this allows identification of the crossing of zebra crossing violations to be controlled accurately. When a violation is detected, a reader unit receives an associated radio frequency identifier, and the registered owner is notified of a penalty by automatically transmitting a message to the cellular communication interface. The automobiles can be functionally categorized into multiple radio frequency identifiers. Violation enforcement is facilitated by a special identifier, unauthorized automobile recognition by another identifier to track thefts, and emergency medical transport is a priority identifier. An emergency identifier recognition commands an abrupt switch of signal phase between restrictive to permissive mode over a specified period of time to allow the ambulance to pass through. This density-responsive signal timing balances the lanes further and minimizes congestion during peak times, as well as fast decision cycles, low latency and reliable hardware level coordination. Execution via a reduced instruction set architecture provides high decision cycles, low latency and reliability of the hardware level coordination. The experimental analysis indicates that the recognition of vehicular identifiers is 96.3% and violation is identified with 95.1%. The proposed output implementation has a reliability of 97.0 percent in signal phase actuation and message transmission. Empirical findings confirm the stability of operations, accurate implementation, and effective prioritization of emergencies and are appropriate to intelligent traffic management without a reliance on artificial intelligence.

title: A High Scalability Data Sharing Scheme for Vehicular Fog Computing Based on Blockchain
authors: Yihui Liu, Runjie Liu, Jiahui Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Vehicular fog computing requires privacy-preserving, fine-grained data sharing under frequent user/attribute changes. However, conventional CP-ABE revocation often triggers expensive key regeneration and ciphertext re-encryption, and most approaches assume a single administrative domain, making cross-region mobility hard to support. This paper proposes RKF-LU-VFC, a blockchain-enhanced CP-ABE data-sharing scheme for cross-region VFC. To remove the scalability bottleneck, RKF-LU-VFC introduces a regionalized KEK forest, where each region maintains an independent KEK subtree; revocation affects only regional branches, and cross-region handoff is synchronized by binding leaf nodes and marking revocation in the previous region. To mitigate peak-load bursts, RKF-LU-VFC adopts lazy ciphertext updating with version numbers and incremental revocation lists: ciphertexts carry a version, and RSUs apply patch updates during access or periodic maintenance when a ciphertext is outdated. The design targets consistency under lazy updating, collusion resistance, and forward/backward security in dynamic cross-region environments.

title: A Comparative Study of Gaussian-Based Surface Reconstruction Under Different Scene Conditions
authors: Ziming Fu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) provides efficient novel-view synthesis, but its geometric fidelity is often insufficient for surface reconstruction. This paper presents a comparative study of four representative Gaussian-based reconstruction pipelines, namely the baseline 3DGS pipeline, SuGaR, GS2Mesh, and VCR-GauS, under a unified evaluation protocol. Experiments on public benchmark scenes and self-captured real scenes compare rendering quality and mesh reconstruction quality in a consistent way. The results show a clear gap between photometric fidelity and geometric accuracy, and they reveal different trade-offs among efficiency, reconstruction stability, and explicit surface quality. Our study provides practical guidance for selecting Gaussian-based surface reconstruction methods under different scene conditions.

title: Energy and Trust Aware Optimal Routing in Heterogeneous IoT Network
authors: Anjana Devi Nandam, Chandra Sekhar Sanaboina
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The main issue facing IoT networks is the data transfer from sensor nodes (SNs). The lifespan of nodes is shortened by excessive usage of communication energy. Thus, it is necessary to handle network challenges such as congestion reduction, security, QoS, heterogeneity, routing, and energy efficiency (EE). For data transfer, routing methods are crucial. Data aggregation is necessary for information gathering and consolidation to reduce network lifetime, operational costs, energy consumption and traffic congestion. Route selection becomes reliable and energy-efficient when IoT data is aggregated. Thereby, this work suggests a new Energy and Trust aware Optimal Routing model in Heterogeneous IoT Network (ETORHIN). The proposed ETORHIN performs optimal routing using a newly introduced hybrid algorithm termed as Mother Optimization integrated Greylag Goose Optimization (MOI-GLGO) that combines the concepts of both Mother Optimization Algorithm (MOA) and GreyLag Goose Optimization (GLGO). Specifically, the optimal routing is done by considering the parameters, namely, energy, distance, forwarding factor and trust. From the analysis, the packet forwarding rate of nodes utilizing the MOI-GLGO algorithm is 0.836 for 200 nodes.

title: SMR-GWO: A Hybrid Metaheuristic for High-Accuracy Lightweight Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks
authors: Mohammad Yasin Safaie, Arash Khosravi, Ahmad Azarnik
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: The fast growth of Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems has intensified security issues, revealing hyperconnected systems to complex cyber attacks. This article presents SMR-GWO, a hybrid metaheuristic algorithm that uses the Slime Mould Reproduction Algorithm (SMR) for global exploration and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) for hierarchical exploitation, to enhance feature optimization in lightweight Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). Evaluated on the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset (Botnet, Brute-force, DDoS, and DoS attacks), SMR-GWO achieves close-to-perfect detection accuracy (99.99%) using only 38-44 features, outperforming six methods with fewer computations. Notably, SMR-GWO achieves the success of DDoS/DoS detection. Such performance enables real-time IDS deployment on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: Machine learning based Application specific Integrated circuit for Electrocardiogram Signal Analysis for Clinical Care Diagnosis
authors: P. Suresh Venugopal, Remya Bharathy, Pavithra, Ravindrakumar Selvaraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Recent developments in algorithmic design have enhanced the accuracy and efficiency of ECG signal processing even more. Heartbeat classification and R-wave detection are important in the assessment of cardiac health. For wearable, battery-powered systems, high energy efficiency is critical. Several existing methods using deep learning were used in complex analysis of ECG signal but the ambulatory monitoring needs a fast and low power diagnosis hardware. In this work, an improved Architecture for Machine learning module of optimized k-nearest neighbour and discrete wavelet transform (DWT) algorithm has been proposed to extract the time-frequency characteristics of ECG signals for R-wave localization and RR-interval computation with diagnosis. Experimental studies on the conventional architecture circuits were found bulky and consumed more power. The MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database have used to test the algorithms in MATLAB before proceeding to Application Specific Integrated circuit. The proposed machine learning architecture circuits consumed low power. The encoding unit was also developed for data transmission from the ASIC to external unit. The implementation was done in Field programmable gate array using Quartus software. Cyclone and Spartan 90nm and 65nm CMOS technology was used for the implementation

title: Efficient and Secure Cloud Data Auditing With ECC-Based Stub Signatures for Dynamic Integrity Verification
authors: Periasamy.S, Obulreddigari Sailaja, Muchukota Cherishma, Garisha Poojitha, Marella Tharun Sai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Cloud storage has emerged as an important platform for storing large-scale data owing to its flexibility and cost-effectiveness. Nonetheless, data integrity and data privacy in outsourced cloud storage remain a critical challenge. Traditional data auditing methods based on RSA and homomorphic cryptography have been proven to be efficient but require large keys and large computation times. To overcome the limitations of existing data auditing methods, this paper proposes an efficient cloud data auditing framework based on Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) stub signature methods. The proposed method is efficient and supports data privacy preservation through public auditing without data retrieval or decryption. It is also efficient in handling dynamic data operations such as inserting, modifying, and deleting data blocks. Third-Party Trusted Public Checker (PTPC) is used to facilitate transparent auditing while preserving data privacy. From the experimental results, it is evident that the proposed method is efficient compared to existing data auditing methods based on RSA and homomorphic cryptography.

title: On Maximizing Quality-of-Service of Mixed-Criticality Systems with Energy Constraints
authors: Yi-Wen Zhang, Lei Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Energy consumption plays a crucial role in battery-powered mixed-criticality systems (MCS). However, recent research has not considered energy consumption as a strict constraint. In this paper, we focus on the processor speed allocation issue of MCS with energy constraints, with the goal of maximizing the Quality-of-Service of low-criticality (LO) tasks in the high-criticality (HI) mode. First, this problem is formulated as a nonlinear optimization problem. Second, we propose a simple heuristic algorithm called ASHA to solve this problem on unicore platforms. The ASHA algorithm first calculates the maximum utilization of LO tasks in HI mode and then uses the greedy algorithm to assign the compensating factor of LO tasks. Third, we extend this solution to multicore platforms. Finally, experimental results indicate that our ASHA algorithm performs more effectively on a unicore platform, while Criticality-Unaware Worst Fit Decreasing algorithm shows better performance on multicore platforms.

title: ALDMAgents: An Integrated Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Apple Leaf Disease Detection and Treatment Plan Generation Based on LLMs
authors: Zhongtao Xu, Dianhui Mao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Apple leaf diseases pose a significant threat to apple yield and quality. While existing computer vision algorithms can accurately recognize disease types, subsequent treatment relies heavily on the subjective diagnosis and experience of human experts, resulting in an end-to-end disconnection. To address this gap, this paper proposes ALDMAgents, an integrated multi-agent collaborative framework based on Large Language Models (LLMs). Guided by Standard Operating Procedures (SOP), ALDMAgents structures a pipeline from image detection to treatment generation using four distinct expert roles. Furthermore, we introduce the Tree of Thoughts (ToT) reasoning method combined with a novel multi-expert LLM collaboration mechanism to overcome the single-path and single-perspective limitations of traditional agents. Experimental results demonstrate that ALDMAgents significantly outperforms mainstream Input-Output (IO), Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and Tree-of-Thoughts (ToT) methods, achieving an average treatment effectiveness of up to 89.43% and superior BLEU scores for complex diseases like Black rot, Scab, and Rust, providing an intelligent and comprehensive solution for modern orchard management.

title: Graph-Enhanced Privacy-Preserving Federated Cognitive Digital Twins for Intelligent Industrial IoT Risk Modeling
authors: V.Shanthi, M Rajaram, C. Preethibha, Abdul Jabbar K, R.K.Keerthana, Pradeep K K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The high rate of rise in the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems has left cybersecurity at a lot of risk because of the heterogeneity of devices, dynamic network structures, and distributed data creation. Traditional centralized risk modeling methods are limited in their capability to scale, maintain privacy, and be responsive to missions in the industrial settings that are mission-critical. To address these difficulties, this paper will present a Graph-Enhanced Privacy-Preserving Federated Cognitive Digital Twin (GPPF-CDT) system of smart and safe IIoT risk measurement. The architecture combines federated learning with graph neural networks to learn structural relationships between interconnected devices, with local data. A Cognitive Digital Twin layer is a persistent reflection of physical resources and network conditions, which allows anticipatory anomaly detection and the analysis of risk propagation causes. Gradient exchange is secured with the inclusion of differential privacy and secure aggregation to ensure that information is not leaked. This experimental analysis shows that the offered framework has an attack detection rate of 95.7% and model poisoning attacks resistance of 94.1%. The leakage of inferences is minimized to 6.3 and the privacy is highly ensured in the distributed training. The model works in 15 communication rounds, which is about 30% faster convergence in comparison with the combined traditional federated approaches. 16% reduction in the communication overhead enhances scalability in large scale deployments. The system stability score of 0.92 ensures high and stable performance in case of adversarial and dynamic IIoT conditions. Altogether, the statistical findings confirm the fact that GPPF-CDT is a scalable, privacy-sensitive, and robust cyber defense solution to next-generation smart manufacturing and critical infrastructure systems.

title: GAIIS 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)

title: Wheat Head Detection: A Deformable Attention-Enhanced Cross-Scale Dynamic Fusion YOLOv8 Approach
authors: Yuhuan Zhou, Yongqiang Tan, Qiang Fu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Accurate wheat head detection in complex field environments is essential for precision agriculture, yield estimation, and automated phenotyping. However, dense clustering, heavy occlusion, varying illumination, and small target sizes severely degrade the performance of conventional detectors. This paper proposes an enhanced YOLOv8 framework that integrates a Deformable Attention-Enhanced Cross-Scale Dynamic Fusion Mechanism. The model first extracts multi-scale features using CSPDarknet, then employs deformable attention for adaptive spatial sampling and a query-guided dynamic fusion layer to reweight low-level details with high-level semantics. A decoupled detection head combined with SIoU loss further refines localization. Evaluated on the Global Wheat Head Dataset 2021, the proposed method achieves 92.3% mAP@0.5 while maintaining real-time inference speed. Extensive ablation studies and visualizations confirm the effectiveness of each module. The approach offers a lightweight, interpretable solution suitable for UAVs and agricultural robots.

title: Hybrid Random Forest–Reinforcement Learning Framework for Efficient Verification of PCIe 6.0 SoC Designs
authors: Santhi K, Chellaswamy C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increasing complexity of high-speed System-on-Chip (SoC) interconnects, particularly with the adoption of PCI Express (PCIe) 6.0, poses significant challenges for conventional functional verification methodologies. Advanced features such as PAM4 signaling, forward error correction (FEC), and higher bandwidth demand intelligent verification strategies capable of accelerating coverage closure and improving corner-case detection. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid verification framework that integrates Random Forest–based anomaly classification with Reinforcement Learning–driven adaptive stimulus generation within a Universal Verification Methodology (UVM) environment. The Random Forest model analyzes simulation-generated protocol features to identify violations and predict coverage gaps, while the reinforcement learning agent dynamically guides stimulus generation toward high-risk and untested scenarios. Experimental evaluation on a PCIe 6.0 verification platform demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves 95–97% functional coverage, reduces simulation cycles by 25–40%, and improves anomaly detection accuracy to 92% compared to conventional and AI-only baseline approaches. By combining data-driven learning with traditional verification flows, the proposed approach offers a scalable, efficient, and practical solution for verifying next-generation high-speed SoC designs, particularly in resource-constrained verification environments.

title: Causal Digital Twin–Driven Trust-Aware Federated CNN–LSTM and Gradient Boosting Framework for Industrial IoT Cyber Resilience
authors: M. Arulmozhi, K Shanthi, V.Shanthi, Abdul Jabbar K, M Rajaram, Maumita Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The aggressive growth of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) technologies has turned the contemporary smart manufacturing into an intelligent way of automation, real-time monitoring, and decisions made on the basis of data. Nevertheless, the growing interconnectedness between heterogeneous industrial systems puts IIoT systems at risk of severe cybersecurity attacks, such as malicious intrusions, data breaches, and chain reactions of system failures. The traditional centralized intrusion detection solutions are either limited to scalability, privacy and the response mechanism can be sluggish, which becomes an issue in large scale distributed industrial settings. The present research is able to tackle these issues by suggesting a Causal Digital Twin-driven Trust-Aware Federated CNN-LSTM system to improve the cyber resilience and anomaly detection in IIoT networks. The suggested framework forms a Causal Digital Twin (CDT), which simulates the dynamic behavior of industrial devices and finds causal links between network traffic patterns, states of the system, and cyber threat events. The Federated Learning architecture allows decentralized collaborative training of a model based on distributed IIoT nodes without violating data privacy by sharing data, instead sharing parameters. In this context, a deep learning model (hybrid CNNLSTM) is used to learn spatial traffic characteristics and time behavioral patterns, to effectively detect cyber anomalies and forecast possible attacks. Also, a machine of trust evaluation is a Gradient Boosting-based model, which dynamically evaluates the reliability of devices and modulates their contribution during federated model aggregation to minimize the effect of compromised nodes. The experimental assessment of the proposed model on benchmark IIoT intrusion detection datasets of 701515 train, validation, and test division shows that the proposed model has an accuracy of 97.8%, 97.4%, 96.9%, and 97.1% of correctness, precision, recall, and a 97.1 F1-score, which is higher than conventional models, namely Random Forest, SVM, CNN, and standalone LSTM. Moreover, the trust-aware aggregation system lowers the false positive rate by about 18 per cent and has a detection latency of 18 ms which allows almost real-time monitoring of cyber threat. These findings demonstrate the importance of entailing digital twin technology, federated intelligence, and causal modeling development in the creation of scalable, privacy preserving, and robust cybersecurity solutions in next-generation IIoT systems.

title: Neural Motion Path: a Path-based Authoring System for Pre-rendered Character Animation_supp1-3693253.mp4
authors: Yoonsang Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Authoring high-quality character animation is essential in multimedia production, especially for pre-rendered formats such as films and TV series. These workflows are often iterative, requiring frequent adjustments and immediate visual feedback, and typically involve editing existing motion, such as mocap data. We present Neural Motion Path (NMP), a deep learning-based system designed to support this authoring process by enabling full-body motion editing through joint level motion path manipulation. While joint rotations are essential for expressive human motion, explicitly specifying them imposes a significant burden on users; NMP addresses this challenge by enabling intu itive position-only motion path editing while implicitly inferring plausible rotation trajectories. To address the inherent tension between motion plausibility and precise constraint satisfaction, NMP explicitly decouples motion synthesis from constraint enforcement within an autoregressive framework. NMP generates realistic, context-aware motion without fine tuning every path detail, making it accessible to novices while supporting the demands of detailed animation authoring. It combines a motion generator with a novel RotationNet for inferring joint rotation, and a constraint imposer that enforces end-effector constraints via an Explicit Weight Sparse Expert Model (EW-SEM). The system supports terrain adaptation and authoring operations like Concatenate, Insert, and Mix. Implemented as a Blender add-on, NMP supports real-time playback and interactive workflows. A user study with novice users shows that NMP improves satisfaction, efficiency, and perceived motion quality compared to the conventional layered keyframing approach, highlighting its potential as an accessible and effective authoring tool.

title: HiAdapter: Histopathology-induced Adapter for Pathology Foundation Models
authors: Qingyang Liu, Peng Xie, Zhehao Dai, Xiangzhi Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: With the rapid development of pathology foundation models, there is a growing demand for efficient fine-tuning strategies tailored to downstream tasks. However, existing parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches are largely task-agnostic and exhibit limited generalization to histopathological images, particularly for unseen cancers and stains, due to substantial stain variability and the complexity of tissue microenvironments. To address these challenges, we present Histopathology-induced Adapter (HiAdapter), which incorporates domain-specific insights into staining and imaging mechanisms of histopathology. HiAdapter reconstructs stain-invariant representations via a Stain-invariant Adapter (S-Adapter) and integrates morphological features through a Morphology-aware Adapter (M-Adapter), effectively bridging the gap between low-level optical properties and high-level tissue semantics. Additionally, we introduce a Pathology Prototypical Contrastive Loss (PPCLoss) to reduce inter-class similarity and mitigate intra-class heterogeneity, enhancing feature discriminability. Extensive experiments using three pathology foundation models (CTransPath, CONCH and UNI) across six benchmarks, including two public datasets, an osteosarcoma tissue classification dataset (56,178 patches) and a chondrosarcoma necrosis classification dataset (3,867 patches) for unseen cancers generalization, as well as an IHC-stained dataset (4,967 patches) and an HIF1A IHC-stained dataset (4,433 patches) for unseen stains generalization, demonstrate the effectiveness of HiAdapter in both efficiency and accuracy. HiAdapter achieves an average improvement of 2.15 in F1 and 1.55 in accuracy over the second-best performer, maintaining strong biological and diagnostic interpretability. External validation on an independent osteosarcoma dataset (9,535 patches) and WSI-level survival analysis (178 slides) further confirm the superior generalizability and underscore the potential for patient-level diagnosis and prognosis in clinical practice. Our code is available at HiAdapter.

title: ACHILLES: A Machine Learning Framework for Explainable and Generalized Automotive Intrusion Detection System
authors: Nishat I. Mowla, Kyi Thar, Sarder Fakhrul Abedin, Aamir Mahmood, Zhu Han, Mikael Gidlund, Kabir Fahria, Konstantinos Giapantzis, Antonios Lalas, Joakim Rosell, Mahshid Helali Moghadam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper addresses the need for an explainable and generalized intrusion detection system (IDS) for the in-vehicle networks (IVNs). While machine learning (ML)-based IDS solutions show promising performance, there are still some challenges, such as the lack of trustworthiness and scarcity of attack representing data, hindering their adoption in the automotive cybersecurity. To address these issues, this paper proposes a centralized ML model training and decentralized execution-based framework, namely ACHILLES, that facilitates an explainable and generalizable automotive IDS. Under ACHILLES, different ML models can be trained centrally to enhance decentralized and onboard intrusion detection performance with multiple automotive datasets. In addition, we generate standard feature formats to assess the ML model’s generalization efficacy, where the quality of generalization and explainability is evaluated with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) by identifying the importance of the feature. We also propose a meta-learning scheme to construct suitable ML models trained by the proposed standard feature formats. The proposed feature format exhibits significant performance gain during ML model training and testing with four state-of-the-art controller area network (CAN)-bus datasets containing real, advanced attacks. The experimental results indicate that developing ML models using the generated generalized features and the meta learning-based model building process leads to enhanced performance. In particular, under the dataset cross train-test setting, the proposed feature format enhances the average accuracy by 40.1% for the baseline model, 32.4% for the meta-learned DNN, and 23.6% for the meta-learned Random Forest, compared with the baseline feature format.

title: Kalman Filter-based Mobile User Channel Estimation and User Localization with Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Zhuoxuan Ju, Miloš Doroslovački
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to minimize the Mean Squared Error (MSE) in channel estimation between mobile users and rectangular Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) within wireless communication systems. Meanwhile, the user localization is realized based on the estimated Channel State Information (CSI). Here, we assume a non-linear, user’s position-dependent system model, for a user with high mobility, an RIS with multiple elements, and a base station (BS) with multiple antennas. After that, we apply the Kalman Filtering (KF) like algorithms to reduce MSE in estimating parameters of this time-variant channel model. Additionally, we propose a Non-Circular Noise Kalman Filter (NCNKF) to address scenarios with non-circular complex state-space noise. Furthermore, we apply the Discrete Space Fourier Transform (DSFT) method, combined with an interpolation technique to decrease the Normalized Root Mean Squared Error (NRMSE) for the user localization based on the estimated CSI. The Cramer-Rao Lower Bounds (CRLBs) for both the estimation of channel state and user localization are derived to check the algorithms performance in the case of fixed users, while minimum mean squared error (MMSE) bounds are employed in the case of moving users. Numerical results show that the proposed algorithms outperform the other approaches and can be very close to the CRLBs.

title: Recognizing Operational Behaviors in Robotic Space Mission via Arm-Object Interaction
authors: Zhihao Zhang, Zhaohui Dang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper introduces an Arm-Object Interaction (AOI) framework tailored for recognizing operational behaviors in space missions involving robotic manipulators. Unlike traditional space target recognition approaches that focus solely on object detection, the AOI framework incorporates semantic role modeling to associate robotic arms with the objects they interact with. To support this task, we construct an AOI dataset characterized by: (1) a taxonomy of six categories across three superclasses, including robotic arms, operated objects, and different spacecraft types; and (2) five representative behaviors-observe, carry, crawl, contract, and extend. The dataset is constructed around explicit role–verb annotations that bind Agent, Instrument, and Object to specific operational actions in space robotic-arm interactions. Building on this dataset, we define an AOI evaluation protocol aligned with role–verb outputs (triplets/quadruplets) and instantiate a MUREN-based reference baseline. Due to structural complexity and illumination variation in space, existing Human-Object Interaction (HOI) models perform suboptimally when applied directly to AOI scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that the reference baseline achieves 77.5% overall AOI mAP on our benchmark. This score is evaluated under a stricter triplet/quadruplet role–verb matching criterion than standard HOI role-AP. Under the AOI-aligned protocol, mixed Sim+Real training improves real on-orbit evaluation by 6.0 mAP, and illumination-robust fine-tuning yields a mean gain of 3.1 mAP, revealing failure modes and validating the utility of the constructed AOI dataset and the role–verb formulation. This work provides essential foundations for autonomous space mission planning and intent recognition of non-cooperative space targets.

title: ByFeDis: Toward Byzantine-Robust Heterogeneous Federated Learning via Knowledge Distillation in Railway Intelligent Control Systems
authors: Shijia Guo, Rong Sun, Jingwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Railway intelligent train control system must accommodate a wide range of scenarios due to the extensive variation in its operational environment. Federated learning based on knowledge distillation offers an efficient training paradigm for such systems by supporting model heterogeneity and reducing communication overhead. However, conventional federated knowledge distillation approaches often exhibit limited robustness against untrusted clients and do not facilitate continuous learning. In this paper, we design a Byzantine-robust federated knowledge distillation scheme, named ByFeDis, which mitigates the impact of malicious clients by removing outliers and low-value learning data, thereby improving learning efficiency. Additionally, we enhance the scalability of federated knowledge distillation in an improved scheme ByFeDis*, which is built on ByFeDis and enables continuous training for newly joined clients. Specifically, ByFeDis measures the soft labels uploaded by clients using Euclidean distance to identify poisoned soft labels that disrupt the training process. These abnormal soft labels include not only malicious perturbed samples that significantly deviate from the distribution of the true labels, but also speculative soft labels that are overly close to the true labels. To further enhance privacy protection, we employ fully homomorphic encryption to enable secure aggregation, thereby safeguarding sensitive client information. Theoretical analysis confirms that our scheme effectively preserves privacy while reducing communication complexity compared with existing approaches. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets demonstrate that ByFeDis achieves, on average, a 2% improvement in accuracy over other Byzantine-robust schemes, while ByFeDis* further enhances model accuracy by 3% and reduces premature convergence by up to 10 training rounds in subsequent learning.

title: Information-Theoretic Subspace Optimization for Robust Electromagnetic Inverse Scattering with Correntropy Criterion
authors: Tingsen Zhang, Xifeng Li, Mingwu Tu, Dongjie Bi, Yongle Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Electromagnetic inverse scattering problems (EISPs) are highly sensitive to noise due to their nonlinearity and illposedness. In practical measurements, electromagnetic signals are typically weak and are corrupted by heterogeneous noise, which further complicates stable inversion. Nevertheless, most iterative methods rely on the mean-squared error (MSE) objective and thus exhibit limited robustness to non-Gaussian perturbations, including speckle, impulse, and heavy-tailed noise that are prevalent in real-world scenarios. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a maximum correntropy criterion–based subspace optimization method (MCC-SOM) from an information-theoretic perspective, which employs the maximum correntropy criterion (MCC) as an alternative to MSE. The resulting non-convex optimization problem is solved using a half-quadratic framework, which recasts the original problem into a sequence of iteratively reweighted least-squares subproblems. This enables the adaptive suppression of outliers via a Gaussian-kernel weighting mechanism. Moreover, we provide a rigorous theoretical analysis of the algorithm’s convergence and robustness. Comprehensive experiments on diverse targets under a wide range of noise conditions demonstrate that MCC-SOM consistently and significantly outperforms the classic SOM, with particularly pronounced performance under impulse noise. This study not only introduces a high-performance algorithm for robust EISPs but also offers a validated and effective information-theoretic framework for solving challenging inverse problems in complex noise conditions.

title: Decouple then Converge: Handling Unknown Unlabeled Distributions in Long-Tailed Semi-Supervised Learning
authors: Kai Gan, Tong Wei, Min-Ling Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: While long-tailed semi-supervised learning (LTSSL) has attracted growing attention in many real-world classification tasks, existing LTSSL algorithms typically assume that labeled and unlabeled data share nearly identical class distributions. When this assumption is violated, these methods can perform poorly because they rely on biased model-generated pseudo-labels. To address this issue, we propose a simple yet effective approach called <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> for LTSSL with unknown unlabeled class distributions. Specifically, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> decouples learning into two specialized branches: a standard branch that focuses on head classes and a balanced branch that focuses on tail classes. During training, the two branches interact and gradually converge, allowing them to complement each other and ultimately achieve strong performance across all classes. Despite its simplicity, we show that <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> achieves state-of-the-art performance on a variety of standard LTSSL benchmarks, e.g., an averaged 2.7% absolute increase in test accuracy against existing algorithms when the class distributions of labeled and unlabeled data are mismatched. Even when the class distributions are identical, <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small> consistently outperforms many sophisticated LTSSL algorithms. Furthermore, we conduct extensive ablation analyses to tease apart the factors that are the most important to the success of <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DeCon</small>. The source code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Gank0078/DeCon</uri>.

title: Structural Assessment Due to Inner Bottom Knuckle Modification of 7500 DWT Bulk Carrier Under Various Loading Conditions
authors: Tuswan Tuswan, Ahmad Fauzan Zakki, Andra Aulia Azahra, Aulia Windyandari, Rosyid Satrio Ramadhan
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: The inner bottom knuckle is crucial in the ship’s bottom structure, particularly when subjected to substantial loads and extreme marine environments. This research investigates the structural Assessment due to the modification of the inner bottom knuckle of a 7500 DWT bulk carrier. Finite element analysis was conducted using FE-based software, which was analysed in different load cases according to the Common Structural Rules (CSR), including full load, heavy ballast, and normal ballast conditions, under various wave orientations such as head sea, following sea, and beam sea. The numerical analysis pinpoints a stress concentration hotspot at the central area of the inner bottom knuckle panel. It can be found that the stiffener addition to the bottom structure can increase the strength and rigidity of the ship structure.

title: Review for "A Random Forest-based Approach for Estimating Full-band Solar-induced Chlorophyll Fluorescence from Photosystem II in Crops"

title: MC-ETC:A Robust Multi-View Flow-Level Encrypted Traffic Classification Method Based on Co-Training
authors: Jiawen Zhang, Jing Li, Jiaqi Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Encrypted traffic classification plays a crucial role in network management, security protection, and resource optimization by identifying the types of applications or services in network traffic. However, current researches face two major challenges: first, encryption renders application-layer data inaccessible, making traditional manual labeling ineffective and significantly increasing annotation costs while leaving few labeled datasets available; second, existing methods rely solely on raw byte data for traffic representation, ignoring valuable packet-level features. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a co-training-based multi-view classification model, called MC-ETC. The model integrates byte-level graph structures and packet-level features to leverage complementary perspectives. Specifically, a byte-level graph based on point-wise mutual information is constructed to model the topological relationships between bytes in traffic, and a graph encoder is employed to capture local contextual dependencies among bytes. Meanwhile, a multi-scale parallel convolutional neural network(CNN) module is applied to learn packet-level features, extracting rich sequential information. Building on these components, the proposed model introduces a co-training mechanism to enable interactive optimization between the graph encoder and the CNN module. By generating pseudo-labels and selecting high-confidence samples, the model can utilize unlabeled data under limited supervision. Additionally, a triple-loss optimization strategy—comprising supervised loss, consistency loss, and discrepancy loss—is incorporated to enhance the model’s ability to learn from heterogeneous views. Experimental results on four public datasets demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art methods in flow-level encrypted traffic classification.

title: Semantic-Driven Adaptive Multi-Agent Collaboration via LLMs in Dynamic Environments
authors: Xia Wu, Zhenjie Wang, Jian Zhang, Ziwen Gao, Zhou Jiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Traditional decision-making systems in highly-dynamic and strongly adversarial environments suffer from rigid collaboration mechanisms, insufficient semantic understanding, and low decision transparency. To address these issues, this paper proposes a semantic-driven adaptive multi-agent collaborative decision-making framework powered by large language models (LLMs). The framework employs the Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct model for semantic task parsing and dynamic subtask allocation, and utilizes Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) modeling to adaptively organize agents into elastic structures—ranging from single agents to collaborative groups. During collaborative reasoning, it integrates multi-round debate and cross-group knowledge distillation strategies to achieve consensus. Finally, reinforcement learning-based adaptive weighted integration combined with Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) optimization yields a highly robust and explainable global decision scheme. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed LLM-driven multi-agent framework reduces task completion steps by 22.4%, decreases conflicts by 97.9%, and achieves a work efficiency of 94.2% compared to the single-agent LLM baseline, highlighting the effectiveness of global coordination and semantic task allocation.

title: Design of Modular and Repairable Smart Health Ring
authors: Navya Bandila, Srija Eruvuri, Rakesh Kumar Reddy Billuri, Sai Prasanth Chelluboina, Dileep Kumar Y
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increasing need for continuous, non-invasive, and real-time health assessment has made wearable health monitoring technologies extremely important. In order to provide vital health monitoring while preserving affordability, portability, and user comfort, this article describes the design and execution of a modular and repairable smart health ring. The suggested smart ring combines several sensing features into a single wearable platform, such as heart rate monitoring, blood oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) measurement, fall detection, and emergency response capabilities. The system uses motion data from inertial sensors for accurate fall detection, and optical sensing techniques for heart rate and SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> measurement. In order to ensure prompt help in the event of unexpected health events or unintentional falls, an emergency response mechanism is integrated to automatically trigger notifications. The suggested design, in contrast to traditional wearables, has a modular architecture that makes it simple to upgrade or replace individual parts, increasing the device's longevity and repairability. Due to its cost-effective design, the smart health ring can be widely used, particularly for applications involving continuous health monitoring and elder care. Accurate vital sign assessment, efficient fall detection, and dependable emergency alarm generation are all demonstrated by experimental evaluation. The findings show that the suggested smart ring provides a useful, portable, and reasonably priced option for wearable healthcare systems of the future.

title: Hybrid Multimodal DeepFake Detection using Spatial–Temporal Feature Learning
authors: Niharika Sharma, Amit Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research work proposes a hybrid method for deepfake detection which combines spatial feature extraction along with temporal consistency and multimodal learning to identify video content that is manipulated at an improved level. The framework is robust against those types of faceswap modifications and performed to a high standard on marker datasets such as DFDC, Celeb-DF and DeeperForensics. The model with these injection methods is attentive to the subtle artifacts induced during the generation of deepfake by jointly exploits spatial–temporal representations and multimodal cues, which are hardly accessible for general single-modality approaches. Experiment results demonstrate that our proposed model reached a detection accuracy of 96.8%, outperforming many recent state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks and transformer-based models. This framework defines an extensible and fault-resistant model for digital forensics, media validation, and proxy measures leading the way against misinformation in modern-day multimedia paradigms.

title: dcFCI: Robust Causal Discovery Under Latent Confounding, Unfaithfulness, and Mixed Data
authors: Adèle H. Ribeiro, Dominik Heider
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Causal discovery is central to inferring causal relationships from observational data. In the presence of latent confounding, algorithms such as Fast Causal Inference (FCI) learn a Partial Ancestral Graph (PAG) representing the true model's Markov Equivalence Class. However, their correctness critically depends on empirical faithfulness, the assumption that observed (in)dependencies perfectly reflect those of the underlying causal model, which often fails in practice due to limited sample sizes. To address this, we introduce the first non-parametric score to assess a PAG's compatibility with observed data, even with mixed variable types. This score is both necessary and sufficient to characterize structural uncertainty and distinguish between distinct PAGs. We then propose data-compatible FCI (dcFCI), the first hybrid causal discovery algorithm to jointly address latent confounding, empirical unfaithfulness, and mixed data types. dcFCI integrates our score into an (Anytime)FCI-guided search that systematically explores, ranks, and validates candidate PAGs. Experiments on synthetic and real-world scenarios demonstrate that dcFCI significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, often recovering the true PAG even in small and heterogeneous datasets. Examining top-ranked PAGs further provides valuable insights into structural uncertainty, supporting more robust and informed causal reasoning and decision-making.

title: Parametric Operator Inference to Simulate the Purging Process in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Seunghyon Kang, Hyeonghun Kim, Boris Kramer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing
abstract: This work presents the application of parametric Operator Inference (OpInf)—a nonintrusive reduced-order modeling (ROM) technique that learns a low-dimensional representation of a high-fidelity model—to the numerical model of the purging process in semiconductor manufacturing. Leveraging the data-driven nature of the OpInf framework, we aim to forecast the flow field within a plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) chamber using computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulation data. Our model simplifies the system by excluding plasma dynamics and chemical reactions, while still capturing the key features of the purging flow behavior. The parametric OpInf framework learns nine ROMs based on varying argon mass flow rates at the inlet and different outlet pressures. It then interpolates these ROMs to predict the system’s behavior for 25 parameter combinations, including 16 scenarios that are not seen in training. The parametric OpInf ROMs, trained on 36% of the data and tested on 64%, demonstrate accuracy across the entire parameter domain, with a maximum error of 9.32%. Furthermore, the ROM achieves an approximate 142-fold speedup in online computations compared to the full-order model CFD simulation. These OpInf ROMs may be used for fast and accurate predictions of the purging flow in the PECVD chamber, which could facilitate effective particle contamination control in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Continuous Business Process Improvement Driven by Large Language Models
authors: Anderson S. Araújo, Henrique S. Mamede, Vítor Santos, Vítor Filipe
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Some of the main challenges faced by organizations when applying Continuous Business Process Improvement are data fragmentation, limited explainability, weak governance, and the isolated use of Artificial Intelligence in Business Process Management. This study initially conducts a Systematic Literature Review on the topic of business process improvement enabled by Large Language Models or Artificial Intelligence in organizations, presenting a comprehensive analysis of prevailing research trends, conceptual frameworks, and persistent limitations, identifying seventeen recurring gaps that affect the effectiveness of integrating the capabilities of Large Language Models and other Artificial Intelligence technologies throughout the entire lifecycle of Continuous Business Process Improvement. As a result, we propose a Framework and its gap-oriented reference architecture that, through modular components, facilitates data integration, reasoning, validation, execution, and monitoring within a closed loop of continuous business process improvement. The framework is operationalized through six phases: Process Understanding, Process Diagnosis, Process Redesign, Process Validation, Process Execution Support, and Continuous Monitoring. The results suggest that designing the framework and architecture directly from the identified gaps creates a coherent foundation for AI-driven process improvement, enabling more reliable, explainable, and easily governed and managed solutions. The study improves the current state of the art by creating a cohesive framework for intelligent, scalable, lifecycle-integrated, and operationally deployable process optimization systems.

title: Electrochemical Engineering Process of Seawater and Brine to Obtain Renewable Electrical Energy Potential
authors: I Made Jiwa Astika, Bagiyo Suwasono, Avando Bestari, Ali Azhar
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Electrical energy is essential for communities to power lighting, heating, cooling, and the operation of mechanical equipment that facilitates the production of other energy forms. To meet these demands, various renewable energy initiatives are being explored, including the use of seawater for producing brine. The purpose of applying this method was to generate renewable electrical energy in accordance with established standards. Therefore, this study aims to identify the potential of renewable energy from the engineering of seawater into brine as an alternative source of electrical energy. The raw materials for the engineering process were obtained from seawater at Paciran Lamongan Beach and brine from the PT Samudra Sunan Drajat factory in Paciran Lamongan. This study used an electrochemical engineering process to measure the variations in the viscosity of brine with different Baume Meter degrees. The experimental results showed seawater at high tide, such as 208 mm Volts, cold-processed brine $\mathbf{0 - 1 0}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{Be}: 237 \mathrm{~mm}$ Volts, cold-processed brine 10-20 ° Be: 240 mm Volts, cold-processed brine 20-30 ° Be: 277 mm Volts, hot-processed brine 0-10 ° Be: 163 mm Volts, hot-processed brine $10-20^{\circ}$ Be: 168 mm Volts, and hot-processed brine $20-30{ }^{\circ}$ Be: 170 mm Volts. The experimental results showed that higher salt content and salinity levels in seawater corresponded to increases in voltage, current, and power output. There were differences in salinity and salt content among the samples, resulting in variations in the generated electrical potential. Cold-processed brine had a greater voltage measurement result than hot-processed brine and seawater. Subsequently, the abundant ions in seawater can act as electrolytes when used in batteries. Aluminum and copper plates serve as electrodes in a seawater battery cell, which consists of aluminum, copper, and sodium chloride ($\mathbf{N a C l}$) solution. This setup enables the flow of electrons through both the external circuit and the electrolyte, thereby generating electricity

title: PI-Embedding: Scientific Idea Representation Learning via Citation Intent and Paper Provenance
authors: Changlu Wang, Zhenan Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of scientific literature poses a significant challenge for researchers to identify core intellectual lineage and evaluate the novelty of new ideas. Existing literature retrieval methods primarily rely on surface-level semantic similarity or undirected citation networks, failing to capture the deep-seated "idea-level" connections such as methodological inheritance and motivational provenance. Furthermore, Large Language Model (LLM) based evaluators suffer from context window constraints and "sycophancy" bias. In this paper, we propose PI-Embedding (Provenance & Intent-based representation), a novel embedding framework for scientific idea retrieval. By leveraging citation intent classification and Paper Sourcing Tasks (PST), we distinguish between "background citations" and "core intellectual sources" to train a contrastive encoder. We further utilize LLMs to distill multifaceted representations (Introduction, Method, and Contribution) to mitigate noise in abstracts. Experimental results on a high-quality expert-annotated benchmark show that PI-Embedding significantly outperforms state-of-the-art models like SPECTER2 and SciNCL, achieving performance comparable to human experts in identifying core research lineages.

title: Multi-objective Joint Resource Allocation Strategy for Macro-femtocell Heterogeneous Network
authors: Jingya Ma, Hongyuan Gao, Yun Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Heterogeneous networks (HetNets) have proven to be a promising technique to handle the sharply increasing resource requirements and data traffic in the fifth generation (5G) and beyond 5G (B5G) wireless networks. Due to the complexity and heterogeneity, there exists severe interference in HetNets, it is crucial to design an optimal strategy for joint sub-channel and power allocation (JSCPA) issue, which is a hybrid optimization problem of integer programming and continuous optimization. In this paper, for the purpose of improving spectrum efficiency (SE) while reducing energy consumption, we construct a multi-objective function considering the trade-off between SE and energy efficiency (EE). A novel algorithm named quantum-inspired monarch butterfly optimization (QMBO) algorithm is proposed for the hybrid optimization problem, and through the migration behaviour, quantum evolution and non-dominated sorting, a multi-objective quantum-inspired monarch butterfly optimization (MOQMBO) algorithm that can solve multi-objective optimization problem is finally acquired, which can obtain a Pareto-optimal front (POF) giving consideration to both SE and EE. Simulation results indicate that the proposed algorithms emerge a distinguished performance superior to the existing evolution algorithms, which can improve SE while guaranteeing EE of HetNets obviously.

title: Real-Time Smart Garbage Management with Hybrid CNN-LSTM for Demand-based Vehicle Dispatch and Waste Segregation
authors: C Gayathri, K Rahul, S Samuel Santharaj, S A Sarukash, S Senthamil Kumaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Rapid urbanization and population growth have significantly increased municipal solid waste generation, exposing inefficiencies in conventional garbage collection systems that rely on static schedules and manual monitoring. These traditional approaches often result in delayed waste removal, frequent bin overflow, inefficient vehicle utilization, excessive fuel consumption, and increased environmental pollution. Additionally, the lack of real-time monitoring and safety mechanisms limits effective waste segregation and early detection of hazardous gas accumulation and bin overloading. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an IoT-based smart garbage management framework integrating multisensor monitoring with a hybrid CNN–LSTM deep learning model for real-time waste monitoring, automated segregation, and intelligent vehicle allocation. The system employs ultrasonic, moisture, inductive metal, MQ-135 gas, load cell, and GPS sensors to continuously monitor bin conditions and vehicle movement. Collected data are preprocessed and analyzed using hybrid deep learning-based sensor fusion to capture spatial correlations and temporal waste generation patterns. Experimental evaluation demonstrates superior performance compared to MLP, SVM, and KNN models. The proposed hybrid CNN–LSTM achieves a low MAE of 0.028, RMSE of 0.061, and high R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.987 with minimal prediction latency of 2.378 ms. Operational efficiency improves significantly, achieving 41.862% trip reduction, 34.517% fuel savings, 46.738% overflow reduction, and 98.612% overload detection accuracy, with overall classification accuracy of 98.767%. The framework enhances resource utilization, environmental safety, and data-driven urban sanitation planning, making it highly suitable for sustainable smart city waste management applications.

title: Accurate Delirium Risk Prediction Performance among Alzheimer’s Patients
authors: S. Chitra Devi, R Sonia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Delirium is a complex medical illness that is caused due to several factors such as neurotransmitter imbalances, cerebral perfusion abnormalities, blood–brain barrier damage and neuro-inflammation. It is the postoperative complication that affect the elderly people specially with Alzheimer's Disease (AD). There is no proper treatment developed for delirium, and it is necessary to have an early prediction to prevent critical complications of delirium in septic patients. The risk factors in delirium patients are analyzed by several researchers using diverse models. Yet, it fails to handle a large quantity of training data in the validation phase, thus it generates overfitting complexities, ensuring high mortality rates. Even with the effective diagnosis, the existing networks still have some challenges that affect the early prediction. In order to tackle several issues takes place in the prior techniques, and to attain the accurate risk prediction, a new deep learning-based delirium risk prediction model is suggested in the research work. In the beginning phase, the required delirium data for the validation is gathered from a standard database. Subsequently, the data cleaning and translation process is carried out on the collected data. Further, the cleaned and translated data are subjected to the delirium risk prediction phase. Here, the delirium risk prediction procedure is carried out through the Echo State Network with Covariance Attention (ESN-CA). Once the delirium risk prediction process is completed, different experiments are carried out to verify the performance of the developed techniques over the prior schemes.

title: From Generative to Agentic: A Survey of Autonomous AI Systems and Their Evolving Capabilities
authors: K. Kajol Sushmitha, N. Shanthi, M. Muthuraja, J. Gowthami, S. Jahaganapathi, V. Kamal Kishore
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has progressed from traditional rule-based systems that were generally not very intelligent to current advanced generative models and most recently to an even newer generation of AI called Agentic AI (or autonomous AI). Unlike traditional AI that uses pre-defined prompts to perform operations, agentic AI systems have the ability to sense their surroundings, think about their objectives, create multi-step plans and take action with very little help from people. The purpose of this paper is to provide an overview of Agentic AI; focusing on how it is structured and operates, including the types of core technologies (such as large language models (LLM), reasoning systems, memory systems, and technologies that allow agentic systems to use tools) that provide the foundation for agentic AI. In addition, important applications of agentic AI are reviewed, including, but not limited to, health care, software development, education, finance and gaming. Furthermore, the paper identifies current research issues concerning reliability, safety, governance, and ethical alignment for developing trustworthy autonomous AI systems. Finally, this paper discusses future avenues of research in developing trustworthy autonomous AI systems.
