title: Multi-Port Photonic Topology Insulator Waveguide Based on Square Lattices
authors: Weihan Sun, Jinxuan Deng, Lili Dai, Yushi Wang, Weiwen Li
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: For the quadrilateral cells, it is not easy to achieve topological phase transitions by breaking the structure symmetry. Meanwhile, the feeding structure for the corresponding topological insulator waveguide usually requires complex design. In this paper, the p4g-symmetry unit cells based on slip symmetry transformation of square lattices are constructed. This kind structure of photonic crystal has a pairwise of overlapping bands, and both sides of the band gap have mutually reversed multipole modes, which can form a topological phase transition. The topological insulators based on these unit cells can effectively propagate topological edge states, from which we can construct multi-port waveguides. Furthermore, the topological waveguides can be directly fed by the traditional microwave and millimeter-wave transmission lines, which has a broad application prospect in system integration.

title: Compelling People to Move: Recent Progress in Haptic Technologies for Human Motion Induction in IEEE Transactions on Haptics
authors: Takuto Nakamura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Haptics
abstract: Inducing human motion through haptic feedback is a long-standing objective in haptics research. Traditionally, such interaction has relied on mechanical systems that physically apply forces to the body using motors, linkages, or grounded devices. However, recent studies published in IEEE Transactions on Haptics have increasingly explored alternative approaches that evoke force or motion perception through relatively small physical stimuli. These techniques leverage perceptual characteristics of the human sensorimotor system to create compelling sensations of pulling, torque, or movement without requiring large mechanical actuation. This article reviews recent contributions in IEEE Transactions on Haptics related to technologies that “compel” users to move. In particular, we focus on three representative approaches: skin deformation-based feedback, asymmetric vibration-induced pulling illusions, and kinesthetic illusions induced by tendon vibration. We discuss representative studies, application domains such as rehabilitation, navigation, and virtual reality, and the limitations inherent to perceptual force feedback systems. Through this overview, we highlight emerging design directions for lightweight and wearable haptic interfaces capable of influencing human motion.

title: LICM: Low-texture Illumination–terrain Coupled Matching for High-resolution Martian Terrain in Chryse Planitia
authors: Yanchao Zheng, Shuanggen Jin, Changrui Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-fidelity terrain reconstruction on Mars is challenged by vast weak-texture plains and illumination-induced radiometric inconsistencies, which often cause ambiguous correspondences in medium-resolution stereo imagery such as that acquired by the High Resolution Stereo Camera (HRSC) on Mars Express (MEX). To improve matching robustness under texture scarcity and radiometric instability, we develop a Low Texture Illumination–Terrain Coupled Matching (LICM) framework. LICM characterizes local scene conditions using the Edge Structure Index (ESI), the Lighting Hazard Index (LHI), and the Twin-Image Correspondence Index (TCI), and converts these state variables into spatially varying control maps for adaptive stereo matching. These control maps drive data adaptive optimization by regulating photometric weighting, support scale, and Semi-Global Matching (SGM) smoothness penalties to preserve terrain discontinuities while stabilizing homogeneous regions. Using HRSC stereo imagery, we generate a DEM with a 20 m posting over a large area of Chryse Planitia. On a representative Oraibi crater ROI, LICM increases the effective pixel ratio (EPR) to 97.93%, and further reaches 99.14% under a low-texture mask, outperforming SGM, More Global Matching (MGM), and OpenCV Semi-Global Block Matching (SGBM) while maintaining comparable geometric consistency in triangulation error statistics. Cross-validation against a High Resolution Imaging Science Experiment (HiRISE) DEM yields an RMSE of 16.74 m, substantially lower than that of the Mars Orbiter Laser Altimeter (MOLA) DEM (63.11 m), indicating improved topographic consistency in low-texture terrain. The proposed framework provides a practical enhancement to HRSC-based regional topographic mapping for geomorphological interpretation assessment.

title: BiLSTM-SLA: A Hybrid LSTM-Based Model for Biomedical Entity Linking in Short Texts
authors: Asma Djellal, Maya Benabdelhafid, Zizette Boufaida
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Biomedical Entity Linking maps mentions of diseases, drugs, and symptoms to standard entities in a knowledge base, supporting information retrieval, question answering, and clinical decision-making. Most existing BEL research focuses on long, well-structured texts that provide rich context. In contrast, many real-world biomedical texts, such as clinical notes, tweets, or electronic health records, are short and lack sufficient context, which reduces the effectiveness of conventional methods. Supervised models also face challenges due to the complexity of biomedical terminology, including abbreviations, acronyms, synonyms, and context-dependent meanings. To overcome these limitations, we propose BiLSTM with Stacked Layers and Attention (BiLSTM-SLA); a hybrid deep learning model designed specifically for Biomedical Entity Linking in short texts. This model exploits bidirectional context via BiLSTM layers, stacked LSTMs to capture hierarchical temporal patterns, and an attention mechanism to focus on the most informative tokens. This lightweight architecture is both accurate and computationally efficient, making it suitable for real-time or resource-constrained biomedical applications. BiLSTM-SLA is assessed against two types of gold standards: general English short text datasets (KORE50 and Webscope) and biomedical datasets (NCBI (D), TAC2017ADR, and ShARe/CLEF). It achieves an accuracy of 92.87% ± 1.42 on Webscope and 87.63% ± 2.33 on KORE50, outperforming competitive baseline approaches. On biomedical datasets, it achieves 91.23% ± 1.73 on NCBI(D), 92.78% ± 0.52 on TAC2017ADR, and 91.74% ± 0.48 on ShARe/CLEF. These results highlight that BiLSTM-SLA provides a practical solution for short text biomedical Entity Linking, effectively addressing ambiguity and limited context while maintaining high accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Design of Recursive Box Filter-based Bilateral Denoising System using QCA based Parallel Prefix Adder
authors: A.Abinaya A. Abinaya, Radha N, Vinodhini V, S.Rajapriya S. Rajapriya, Seshaiah M A, Babitha Lincy R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: A common edge-preserving smoother used in the majority of image processing, computational photography, and visual computing applications is the bilateral filter (BF). In this paper, an efficient recursive box filter-based bilateral filter is implemented using a quantum-dot cellular automata (QCA) based parallel prefix adder. The recursive box filter consists of horizontal filtering and vertical filtering. The core concept behind the recursive filter is to exploit the redundancy in pixel computation by reusing previously processed data in both horizontal and vertical directions. Besides, an analysis of image denoising is also done using the proposed filter. This proposed recursive box filter is implemented in Xilinx Kintex 7 FPGA, which provides less area, low power consumption and less delay than the existing filter.

title: Autonomous Pipeline Inspection Using Lightweight Vision-Based UAV Tracking_supp4-3693978.mp4
authors: Praful Hambarde
publish: N/A
abstract: Long-range pipelines are indispensable infrastructures requiring continuous and rigorous inspections for structural health monitoring. Multi-copter (UAV) inspections face critical challenges including low-altitude flight constraints, limited computational resources for integrated visual perception and navigation control for the purpose of continuous inspection in real-time. The present work proposes an autonomous vision-based UAV low altitude inspection framework for industrial pipeline inspection. The proposed framework seamlessly integrates three innovative components: a lightweight deep learning architecture (MLA-Net) for robust pipeline segmentation, a vision-based control strategy for smooth tracking, and a sliding mode controller for real-time navigation. MLA-Net combines Cascaded Feature Extractor (CFE) blocks for hierarchical feature learning with Multi-Scale Receptive (MSR) blocks for multi-scale environmental adaption, achieving efficient pipe detection under varying conditions. The vision-based control strategy processes MLA-Net outputs through perspective projection and oriented rectangle extraction to coordinate yaw-lateral control, while a non-linear forward velocity function prevents overshooting during pipeline turns. The sliding mode controller fuses visual features with altitude data for autonomous navigation across complex pipeline networks. Extensive validation through simulation and real-time outdoor experiments demonstrates superior performance across diverse pipeline configurations including intersections and multipipe scenarios. The proposed MLA-Net achieves 0.8166 IoU while operating at ≈27 FPS with only 0.202 GFLOPs and 0.178M, significantly outperforming existing methods in computational efficiency. The integrated framework enables stable autonomous tracking across various pipeline geometries, providing a practical solution with enhanced accuracy and computational efficiency for resource-constrained UAV applications. Source code available at Autonomous vision-based pipline tracking by UAV

title: Morphometric Analysis of Milkfish (Chanos chanos) Using Image Processing and Ensemble Learning
authors: Psalmantha Allaine Ipong, Maria Mae Kiskis, Angie Ceniza-Canillo
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Milkfish (Chanos chanos) is one of the Philippines’ key aquaculture species, yet its post-harvest processes-especially sorting and weighing-remain mostly manual, making them slow and inconsistent. This study applies morphometric analysis and ensemble learning to automate size classification and weight estimation of milkfish using image data. Images collected by the researchers and a Roboflow dataset of 2,739 labeled samples were used. Ten morphometric features, including length, area, aspect ratio, and Feret diameter, were extracted. Random Forest and Gradient Boosting with Support Vector Machine as the meta-classifier were used for milkfish classification, while ensemble regression through weighted averaging was applied for weight prediction. The models achieved 95% accuracy, and a root mean square error of 139.60 grams, showing strong potential to streamline and standardize post-harvest operations in aquaculture.

title: Smart Review Analyser: A Multiplatform AI System for Aspect-based Sentiment Analysis on E-Commerce Products
authors: Desireddy Lakshmi Prasanna, L.Malleswari, Purohitam Preeti, M.Mallikarjuna Rao, D.Venkatesh, C.Manjunath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the online shopping platforms, the reviews posted there have been overwhelming, and it is very difficult to interpret the feedback posted by users or vendors. Other common sentiment analysis techniques are not very good at capturing fine-grained opinions about products attributes. To address this limitation, this paper presents a Smart Review Analyser, a multiplatform AI-based system for aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA) of e-commerce product reviews. The proposed system combines both the natural language processing mechanisms and deep learning models that automatically identify the aspects of products and define the sentiment polarities associated with them. Its architecture includes data collection, text preprocessing, aspect extraction, sentiment classification and results visualization modules and can be deployed on both the web and mobile platforms. The experimental analysis of the e-commerce review data on a large scale proves the utility of the proposed methodology to extract the aspect-level sentiments and evaluated performance in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The confusion matrix result also validation that there is strong classification regardless of the prevailing data imbalance, between the positive, negativet, and neutral sentiment. The findings show that the Smart Review Analyser can give valuable information about customer opinion and aid in the informed decision-making of consumers and businesses in the real-life context of e-commerce.

title: A Narrowband Digital Costas Loop Applied to Semi-blind Signal Recover
authors: Zhi Liang, Binbin Chen, Muye Jing, Tao Liu, Hongyan Jiang, Shuaiqiang Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Blind signal separation (BSS) refers to the extraction of unobserved source signals from a set of observed mixtures. Typically, these mixtures are obtained from multiple sensor outputs, where the source signals are assumed to be independent. Semi-blind signal separation (SBSS), by contrast, involves separating and recovering original signals from the mixtures by leveraging certain prior knowledge. This paper focuses on the semi-blind separation of mixed signals formed by the arbitrary superposition of fundamental waveforms within a specific frequency band. The Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is employed for spectral analysis to isolate the constituent source signals from the mixture. Furthermore, an improved narrowband digital Costas loop is utilized to achieve frequency synchronization between the source and the restored signals.

title: Radix-8 Unsigned Floating-Point Multiplier for Neural Networks using Digit Recoding
authors: Suraj S Nair, Ajay A, Chitra E
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Digital multiplication is a main computational bottleneck in high-speed processors and neural-network accelerators, and it must have a stringent tradeoff between throughput and hardware resource efficiency. Signed arithmetic is inherently optimal with traditional methods of multiplication, particularly Modified Booth Encoding (MBE). As a result, they add considerable redundancies of hardware to operate on the implicitly-unsigned floating-point mantissa operations common in Deep Neural Network (DNN) workloads, e.g. sign extension, negative number encoding and two-s complement generation. This study presents an optimized Radix-8 Unsigned Floating-Point Multiplier with custom Digit Recoding to avoid these architectural inefficiencies. The proposed architecture reduces the hardware routing congestion in the FPGA fabric by removing signed-logic components such as auxiliaries and eases the partial-product generation stage as well. The design has a factor of reduction of partial-product rows by $\frac{n}{3}$ which has the effect of reducing depth of the next reduction tree and reduces delay of critical path. Implemented with the Xilinx Vivado suite and tested with extensive RTL simulation, the architecture is shown to reduce silicon by 33.1% and logic power usage by 49.4% over traditional Booth-3 benchmarks. These results suggest that the methodology is quite useful in resource-constrained Edge AI systems, and offers a scalable and energy-efficient base of real-time neural-network inference.

title: TimeScape: A Multi-Resolution Timeline to Explore Historical Figures
authors: Chiteng Ma, Xinyue Chen, Keli Gao, Rui Shi, Xiaoru Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The course of history is inseparable from the actions of historical figures. From the perspective of contemporaneity, an important challenge is how to visualize large numbers of historical figures and their dynamic associations on a unified temporal scale. Yet existing large-scale data solutions often rely on aggregation strategies that diminish the visibility of key figures, while failing to provide smooth contextual transitions between overview and detail. Moreover, current approaches to representing inter-figure associations lack effective integration of the surrounding historical context. To address these challenges, we propose TimeScape, a large-scale biographical data exploration system supporting multi-resolution analysis. Anchored in absolute time, the system highlights the evolving dynamics of historical development. Through multi-level layouts, sampling, and semantic zooming, it enables efficient navigation of large-scale data, allowing users to move seamlessly between overview and detail and to “wander” freely across the historical landscape. The system further allows iterative switching of focal figures to explore concrete inter-personal associations, aligning them on the temporal axis so that these connections are grounded in traceable evidence and explicit context. Case studies and expert interviews demonstrate that TimeScape effectively supports multi-perspective understanding and exploration of large-scale biographical data, offering historians a new visualization pathway for research.

title: MF2LLM: A Multi-View Multi-Modal Fusion Framework with Large Language Models for Ponzi Scheme Detection on Ethereum
authors: Mingshun Ye, Dezhi Han, Chin-Chen Chang, Mingdong Tang, Weili Chen, Xingyu Feng
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The rapidly expanding Ethereum ecosystem has driven the flourishing of decentralized applications, but has also brought increasingly severe security risks. Ponzi scheme, in particular, pose a grave threat to platform security and user assets by luring investors with promises of high returns. The current detection methods generally suffer from limitations such as insufficient feature extraction, reliance on a single information source, and poor robustness. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel Multi-View Multi-Modal Fusion Framework with Large Language Models for Ponzi scheme detection on Ethereum, named MF2LLM. We first model the contract opcode sequence as an opcode chain graph and design a Time-Stamped Graph Encoder (TS-GE) to capture local temporal dependencies and execution flow relationships between opcodes. Concurrently, we construct an opcode semantic hypergraph based on semantic categories and design a Semantic-Weighted Hypergraph Encoder (SW-HGE) to model higher-order co-occurrence patterns and global associative features. Furthermore, we propose the Opcode Sequence Lightweighting (OSL) method, which significantly compresses the length of opcode sequences while preserving core control logic and semantic information. This provides high-quality structured input for information fusion. To this end, we perform multi-modal instruction fusion on multi-source heterogeneous features and employ LoRA to fine-tune LLMs. This enables the model to achieve cross-modal semantic reasoning and behavioural pattern recognition. Through extensive experimental validation on real-world datasets, MF2LLM demonstrates stable and superior detection performance even under conditions of highly imbalanced sample distributions. Compared to existing state-of-the-art approaches, our method outperforms across all metrics, achieving an ACC of 99.43%, Precision of 96.57%, Recall of 97.06%, and an F1-score of 96.81%. The efficiency and practical value of MF2LLM in detecting Ponzi schemes on Ethereum contribute to enhanced security for the decentralized application ecosystem. The codes are publicly available on Github: https://github.com/yemisua/MF2LLM.

title: Real-Time Tomato Leaf Disease Detection using Lightweight YOLOv11n with Web-Mobile Deployment
authors: S. Kalpana, P. S. Nandhini, P. Jeevitha, J. K. Madhana Harish, V. Mahashwin, M. K. Nishanthini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Tomato leaf diseases are serious problems affecting the productivity of agricultural businesses, and hence they should be properly and early diagnosed to manage the crop. Conventional manual inspection processes have not been satisfactory and quick to use in the actual situations. In this research, a lightweight YOLOv11n is suggested in detecting ten types of tomato leaf classes (nine diseases and normal leaves) in real-time. This model is trained on augmented dataset and it is optimized both in terms of accuracy and efficiency. The experimental results reveal good performance with a high mAP@50 of 98.25% and mAP@50-95 of 88.50% with high precision and recall. It deploys the system based on Flask-React web application and a TensorFlow Lite mobile framework to make speedy and offline inferences on edge devices. The proposed method is more effective in localizing small disease areas than the present models like YOLOv8 and EfficientNet and has low computational cost. The article demonstrates the fact that lightweight deep learning models can be extended to precision agriculture and provides farmers with an affordable and useful tool to detect diseases early and make decisions.

title: WebSocket-Based Multi-Bed IV Therapy Monitoring System Using Time-of-Flight Distance Sensing
authors: Roshan Tripathy, Sandeep Kumar Dash, Satya Narayan Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Intravenous (IV) therapy is one of the most frequently administered medical treatments, yet monitoring IV bags rely heavily on manual observation, making it vulnerable to human error and lapses in attention. While automated IoT-based saline monitoring systems have been explored in prior research, most solutions opt for the load cell approach or lack the flexibility of adjustable alert thresholds. This study presents a distributed, real-time saline monitoring architecture capable of monitoring multiple concurrent beds. Each bed is equipped with a NodeMCU and a VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensor for precise, non-contact fluid level measurement. MQTT is one of the most commonly used IoT communication protocols; however, due to its publish-subscribe model, it is dependent on brokers. This introduces potential security concerns as well as latency overhead. This work aims to solve these limitations by employing the WebSocket protocol, enabling low-latency, direct data transmission without intermediary dependencies. Security measures are integrated at both the node and server levels to safeguard patient data privacy. A direct protocol comparison confirms that the WebSocket architecture achieves 43% lower average acknowledgment latency than MQTT broker-based communication (119.8 ms vs. 208.9 ms), with substantially better worst-case behavior (369 ms vs. 1217 ms). Throughput and reliability were comparable between the two protocols. The resulting system achieves sub-second end-to-end latency and supports globally accessible, in-browser dashboards. Healthcare professionals and patient families can monitor individual or multiple beds concurrently from any internet-connected device, making this architecture a practical and scalable solution for modern clinical environments.

title: Secure Cloud based Document Sharing System with IoT Integration for Global Collaboration
authors: R.V. Viswanathan, R. Sathya, E. Aksha Pricilda Reena, R. Buvaneswaran, K. Srisanjay, R. Vajravelmaran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The objective of our paper is the exponential growth of collaboration and distributed systems around the world, secure document global collaboration sharing has become indeed an urgent one challenge within environments that contain Internet of Things and cloud infrastructures. This paper introduces an idea of an IoT document sharing system that is in-the-cloud and is integrated and assures not just confidentiality of data and restricted access but also trustworthy management of data between various institutions. In the proposed framework, collections of documents are initially gathered with the help of an IoT edge device a Raspberry Pi Zero-based on a lightweight operating system affordable data penetration unit. To ensure security at the edge level, the documents gathered are ciphered on the local side by the Advanced Encryption Standard and then forwarded to the cloud to minimize the threat of data leakage these encrypted documents are uploaded and stored in a private cloud in a secure manner environment, thereby avoiding unauthorized access and information violations during storage and the transfer stage of data. In order to bring access control under an effective control, the system has an RBAC mechanism which allows limited document access control based on predetermined users and organizational privileges. This makes sure that sharing is only done by the authorized users, read, or edit documents based on their allocated permissions. This edge encryption integration, which is based on IoT, cloud storage, and RBAC significantly improve the general security, scalability, and reliability of document sharing process.

title: Dual-space Sparse Mask Injection: A Defense Framework for Large Language Models Against Jailbreak Attacks
authors: Xiniao Deng, Rui Cai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Jailbreak attacks compromise the safety of large language models (LLMs) by inducing harmful behaviors through adversarially crafted prompts, often without explicit toxic language. Recent studies show that such attacks operate by perturbing internal representations that mediate refusal and safety-aligned behaviors. However, existing latent-space defenses typically rely on a single semantic direction, which is insufficient to capture the diverse and obfuscated semantics used in jailbreak prompts. We propose Dual-space Sparse Mask Injection (DSMI), a representation-level defense that models jailbreak behavior in two disentangled latent subspaces: a toxicity subspace encoding sensitivity to harmful content and a jailbreak subspace capturing compliance shifts under adversarial prompting. DSMI constructs sparse masks in both subspaces via contrastive representation analysis and intervenes during inference by simultaneously enhancing toxicity-aware features and suppressing jailbreak-induced activation patterns.We evaluate DSMI on multiple jailbreak benchmarks and three open-source LLMs, showing that it substantially reduces attack success rates against both token-level and semantic jailbreak methods while preserving benign task performance. Further analysis demonstrates that the two subspaces are nearly orthogonal and that dual-space intervention provides significantly stronger robustness than single-space latent defenses. These results suggest that disentangling and manipulating multiple safety-relevant representational subspaces is an effective strategy for defending LLMs against adversarial jailbreak attacks.

title: A Context-Aware Traffic Violation Detection System for Complex Real-World Traffic Scenarios
authors: D.Ganesh, M. Sunil Kumar, Pulakanti Balaji, Mudimela Deepika, Pujari Raghavendra, P.Venkateswarlu Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Traffic violations such as not wearing helmets, triple riding, over-speeding, and jumping red lights are major causes of road accidents, especially in busy urban areas. This paper presents a context-aware traffic violation detection system that uses deep learning and motion analysis to automatically identify such violations. The system uses a YOLO-based model to detect vehicles, riders, pedestrians, and helmets from surveillance footage. A tracking-by-detection approach is then applied to follow moving objects across frames, helping estimate speed and analyze motion patterns. To reduce false alarms, a context-aware reasoning module combines road layout information (like stop lines), traffic signal states, and object trajectories to confirm whether a violation has actually occurred. The proposed system was tested on 48 real-world surveillance video sequences, covering 16.5 hours of footage recorded under different traffic densities and lighting conditions. The results show strong performance, achieving F1-scores of 95.5% for helmet violations, 93.7% for triple riding, 91.8% for over-speeding, and 93.5% for red-light jumping. It also operates in real time at 24–26 frames per second. By combining detection, tracking, and contextual validation, the system improves reliability in complex traffic environments. This framework offers a practical and scalable solution for smart city traffic monitoring and automated law enforcement

title: ECG-based Coronary Risk Prediction using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Saraswathy C., Gopika S., Kasiyappan D.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The non-stationary characteristics and lack of resistance to noise severely hinders the accuracy of the automated classification systems; the electrocardiogram (ECG) signals have been extensively used in the early diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases. In overcoming these difficulties, this paper suggests a hybrid spectro-morphological learning system to perform strong ECG diseases classification. To eliminate baseline drift and high-frequency noise, raw ECG signals of the PTB Diagnostic ECG database are first smoothed with the Discrete Wavelet Transform and the Daubechies-4 wavelet. The processed signals are then normalized and the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) spectrograms are used to convert them to time-frequency representations. Scale-Invariant Feature Transform (SIFT) is used to extract salient local patterns in the spectrogram images to increase the discriminative morphological properties. These traits are then fed through a hybrid deep learning structure which combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with transformer embedding layers to account both of local spatial and long-range temporal dependencies. The use of global average pooling helps in simplicity of models and the prevention of overfitting. Experiment results show that the proposed model has a better classification performance and strength than the traditional CNN-based models, and so is effective in reliable cardiac diseases diagnosis using ECG signals.

title: Intelligent Thermal and Power Behavior Prediction in Laptop Systems using Machine Learning
authors: Manisha R. Mhetre, Tejas R. Kulkarni, Anuj A. Khomane, Samadhan S. Kendre, Aditya V. Kulsange, Soham S. Vaidya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The study presents an efficient real-time forecasting system that tracks laptop power and temperature trends. Internal performance metrics were documented throughout the study which measured ambient temperature using an external sensor and included CPU usage, RAM utilization, battery status, and temperature via operating system APIs. For activity classification, anomaly detection, battery health estimate, and temperature forecasting, the system employed a multi-model architecture that integrated Random Forest, Isolation Forest, Linear Regression and ARIMA. 90.4% classification accuracy, a temperature forecasting RMSE of roughly 3°C, and a battery health prediction within ±3% variation were all attained in the experimental evaluation of 5200 time-stamped samples. The system has secure shutdown management features as well as automatic thermal threshold recognition. The research findings show that system-level performance measurements enable effective predictive monitoring and advanced thermal protection systems in mobile computing devices.

title: Proximal policy optimization-based vegetation evolution for coverage optimization in wireless sensor network
authors: Xinyue Jia, Minghua Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Existing wireless sensor network(WSN) coverage optimization algorithm often suffer from local optimization problems, leading to suboptimal results and numerous coverage blind spots. This paper introduces Proximal policy optimization-based vegetation evolution (PPOVEGE) algorithm, which enhances the overall performance of the VEGE algorithm and effectively addresses coverage optimization problems in wireless sensor networks. comparisons of PPOVEGE with other advanced MAs on the CEC2020 benchmark functions and WSN coverage optimization demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed algorithm.

title: Multi-layered Emotional Decoupling and Style Condition Control Algorithms for AI Composition in Automatic Electronic Keyboard Music Production
authors: TingTing Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: To address the issues of high coupling in emotion expression, insufficient stability in style control, and easy imbalance in multi-track collaborative structures in automatic electronic keyboard music generation, this study proposes a multi-layered emotion decoupling and style conditional supercontrol algorithm framework. This framework constructs a joint optimization model from four levels: feature space, frequency domain, time domain, and discriminant space. This model can achieve collaborative modeling of emotion expression, style transfer, and structural coordination. First, the model constructs a shared-specific bi-subspace encoding structure to achieve shared emotion representation alignment and specific representation orthogonality constraints under multimodal input conditions. Second, a frequency domain emotion purity enhancement mechanism is introduced. This mechanism achieves emotion band projection convergence through dominant frequency selection and spectral entropy constraints, embedding spectral entropy as a low-entropy regularization term into the generator training process. Subsequently, the model constructs a style super-network modulation module, dynamically generating modulation parameters based on style conditional vectors. This mode achieves controllable remapping of emotion vectors between different style domains and combines Gramian emotion trajectory matching loss to constrain the temporal evolution path. In the rhythm generation part, emotion consistency and harmonic stability indices are jointly introduced into the reward function. Finally, a discriminative enhancement mechanism is constructed through angular spacing optimization and Jeff symmetric divergence constraints. The experiment constructed a multimodal dataset based on 5200 multitrack electronic keyboard MIDI data points and trained it on an RTX 4090 environment. Compared with the six-class comparison model, the complete model improved the emotion classification accuracy by 8.7%, reduced the spectral entropy from 2.76 to 2.18, and improved the average inter-class angular distance by 17.4%. In terms of style control, the Gram trajectory matching error decreased by about 35%. In the abrupt tempo test (120→150 BPM), the tempo drift decreased from 2.8 measures to 0.9 measures. The mean multitrack correlation increased to 0.83, and the tempo drift index decreased to 0.07. Subjective listening experiments (n=30) showed that the model was significantly better than the non-frequency domain and discriminative enhancement models in terms of emotional clarity, style stability, and structural integrity (t(29)=7.14, p<0.001).

title: Driving Style Recognition Like an Expert Using Semantic Privileged Information From Large Language Models
authors: Zhaokun Chen, Chaopeng Zhang, Xiaohan Li, Wenshuo Wang, Gentiane Venture, Junqiang Xi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Existing driving style recognition systems largely depend on low-level sensor-derived features for training, neglecting the rich semantic reasoning capability inherent to human experts. This discrepancy results in a fundamental misalignment between algorithmic classifications and expert judgments. To bridge this gap, we propose a novel framework that integrates Semantic Privileged Information (SPI) derived from large language models (LLMs) to align recognition outcomes with human-interpretable reasoning. First, we introduce DriBehavGPT, an interactive LLM-based module that generates natural-language descriptions of driving behaviors. These descriptions are then encoded into machine learning-compatible representations via text embedding and dimensionality reduction. Finally, we incorporate them as privileged information into Support Vector Machine Plus (SVM+) for training, enabling the model to approximate human-like interpretation patterns. Experiments across diverse real-world driving scenarios demonstrate that our SPI-enhanced framework consistently outperforms a wide range of baselines, achieving <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$F_{1}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-scores of 90.2% (car-following) and 91.0% (lane-changing). Importantly, SPI is exclusively used during training, while inference relies solely on sensor data, ensuring computational efficiency without sacrificing performance. These results highlight the pivotal role of semantic behavioral representations in improving recognition accuracy while advancing interpretable, human-centric driving systems.

title: CTRL: Cross-modal Transformer with Reinforcement Learning for Climate Causal Discovery
authors: Jinduo Liu, Ting Wang, Yilin Lu, Junzhong Ji
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Understanding the complex internal relationships of climate elements and revealing causal relationships in climate phenomena has attracted attention in the field of climate science and data mining. However, current causal discovery methods in climate science primarily rely on single-modal data, which limits the available information and complicates the accurate identification of climate causal relationships. In this paper, we propose a novel climate causal discovery method based on cross-modal Transformer and reinforcement learning, called CTRL. The method consists of three modules: unimodal feature representation, cross-modal feature fusion, and reinforcement learning for causal discovery. Specifically, we first design a novel unimodal feature representation method that uses the seasonal-trend decomposition based on LOESS (STL) strategy to decompose air quality and meteorological features into trend and seasonal components, with the aim of effectively capturing temporal data patterns. Next, we develop a cross-modal feature fusion method using cross-attention to capture cross-modal interactions and adaptively fuse bimodal features. Finally, we employ reinforcement learning to search for the optimal climate causal network with the best Bayesian information criterion (BIC) score. Numerous experiments show that CTRL outperforms singlemodal approaches in climate causal discovery, achieving even better results when predicting a target climate variable using only the causal features identified by CTRL. Code and data are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CTRL-2622/.

title: PRISM: Pipeline for Root-cause Investigation via Specialized Multi-agents
authors: Weiran Yan, Yichao Wu, Penghao Liang, Mengwei Yuan, Jianan Liu, Jing Yang, Xianyou Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Root Cause Analysis (RCA) in microservice architectures is a complex task requiring the correlation of multimodal telemetry data across distributed components. Existing single-agent LLM approaches often struggle with long-context reasoning and hallucination, lacking rigorous evidence verification. To address these limitations, we propose PRISM (Pipeline for Root-cause Investigation via Specialized Multi-agents), a hierarchical multi-agent framework designed to emulate the workflow of Site Reliability Engineers (SREs). PRISM decomposes the RCA process into three specialized roles: a Triage Agent that rapidly localizes suspect services using rule-based traffic analysis; an Investigator Agent that gathers objective, quantified evidence across temporal windows without making diagnoses; and an RCA Specialist Agent that synthesizes evidence to deduce the root cause. Additionally, we introduce an Evidence Search Agent that works in tandem with PRISM to iteratively refine tool configurations by collecting ground-truth evidence, ensuring high-fidelity evidence retrieval. We evaluate PRISM on a high-fidelity dataset collected from the Google Online Boutique system, covering 16 scenarios across four fault categories. Experimental results demonstrate that PRISM achieves 93.8% accuracy in service localization and 100% in fault type classification, validating the effectiveness of structured multi-agent collaboration in automated troubleshooting.

title: DO-ForceEHash-Embed: Double Oblivious Extendible Hashing for Secure Keyword Update in Confidential Computing
authors: Chenyang Wang, Jialin Chi, Guizhou Liang, Tianhuo Bao, Min Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Secure keyword update is critical for dynamic outsourced databases. Despite Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) hardware isolation, memory access patterns remain vulnerable to side-channel attacks. To achieve Double Obliviousness (DO), current tree-based or linear-scan approaches suffer severe scalability bottlenecks. We propose DO-ForceEHash-Embed, an efficient DO scheme based on extendible hashing. It leverages a forced bucket split strategy to unify insertions and deletions into a normalized sequence, masking internal access patterns. Furthermore, an embedded position strategy integrates block locations into predecessor nodes, eliminating the Path ORAM position map to reduce storage and latency. Evaluations on billion-scale datasets show an insertion latency of 2.65 ms, outperforming EnigMap by 3.9× in insertion and 30.2× in deletion efficiency. The scheme remains robust under varying EPC capacities, proving its effectiveness in memory-constrained clouds.

title: Edge-Assisted Smart Home Gateway with Hardware-Level Air-Gap for Deterministic Privacy
authors: Yuquan Chen, Mingming Zha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The proliferation of cloud-centric smart home ecosystems has introduced unprecedented privacy vulnerabilities, as sensitive acoustic data is continuously streamed to third-party servers. Conventional privacy-preserving mechanisms, such as software-defined firewalls and logical isolation, rely on probabilistic defenses that are inherently susceptible to zero-day exploits and privilege escalation. Driven by first principles, this paper proposes a novel edge-assisted smart home gateway architecture featuring a deterministic hardware-level air-gap isolation mechanism. By decoupling the Wide Area Network (WAN) and Local Area Network (LAN) into a dual-path heterogeneous topology, the system enforces absolute physical disconnection (≤ 5 ms latency) of the WAN power domain during high-sensitivity scenarios. To maintain seamless local control during the privacy lockdown, a lightweight, offline Natural Language Processing (NLP) pipeline is deployed on the edge computing node. Comprehensive evaluations of the hardware prototype demonstrate an ultra-low end-to-end actuation latency of ≤ 30 ms and absolute zero data leakage under packet sniffing tests. The proposed architecture fundamentally immunizes the smart home hub against advanced persistent threats (APTs), establishing an impenetrable physical security boundary without compromising user experience.

title: PVDI: Preserving Vital and Disrupting Irrelevant Latent Attentions for Robust Backdoor Defense
authors: Junchi Chen, Qi Chu, Haojie Yuan, Tao Gong, Kejun Zhang, NengHai Yu, Dongmei Zhang, Bin Benjamin Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Backdoor attacks pose a critical threat to the security and reliability of deep neural networks (DNNs), enabling malicious triggers to manipulate model predictions while maintaining normal performance on clean inputs. Addressing this challenge requires robust defense mechanisms that eliminate backdoors without compromising model utility. This paper introduces PVDI (Preserving Vital and Disrupting Irrelevant attentions), a novel blind purification method designed to neutralize backdoors while retaining the primary prediction capabilities of both backdoored and clean models. PVDI leverages vital and irrelevant attention mechanisms during fine-tuning: vital attention is preserved to maintain the model’s core functionality, while irrelevant attention is disrupted to neutralize backdoor behavior. Extensive evaluations across diverse datasets and attack scenarios demonstrate PVDI’s superior purification performance, achieving significant reductions in attack success rates while preserving the utility of backdoored models and minimizing adverse impact on clean models. PVDI outperforms existing state-of-the-art defenses and sets a new benchmark in backdoor defense. This work represents a significant step forward in combating backdoor attacks. Our code will be made publicly available upon publication.

title: Meta-Learning Approaches in Intracranial Tumor Segmentation: A Review with Focus on Generalization to Rare Tumors
authors: Reena. K, S.Nagendra Prabhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of intracranial tumors using magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a fundamental step in the diagnosis, treatment planning, and patient monitoring of neuro-oncology. Conventional approaches, ranging from atlas-based and level-set methods to modern deep convolutional networks, such as U-Net, have achieved notable success for well-studied tumor types such as gliomas. However, these models often fail to generalize when faced with rare or heterogeneous tumors, such as meningiomas, metastases, and pituitary lesions, for which annotated data are scarce. Meta-learning, or "learning to learn," has recently emerged as a powerful paradigm for improving adaptability and data efficiency in such scenarios. This review surveys the evolution of intracranial tumor segmentation methods, with a particular focus on meta-learning frameworks designed to address the challenges of rare tumor subtypes and cross-domain generalization (CDG). We examined optimization-based techniques (e.g., Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning) and metric-based strategies (e.g., prototypical networks), highlighting their potential to enable rapid model adaptation to new tumor distributions or missing imaging modalities. Benchmark comparisons of datasets, such as BraTS and ICTS, are discussed, along with insights into network architectures, training protocols, and clinical applicability. Finally, we outline the open challenges and future directions, emphasizing that meta-learning holds significant promise for developing robust and generalizable segmentation models that can support precision care for patients with both common and rare brain tumors.

title: SecAgg: A Secure Federated Aggregation Scheme Based on Additive Secret Sharing
authors: Yuxiao Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Federated learning enables privacy-preserving machine learning by keeping data local, but client updates remain vulnerable to malicious servers. Existing solutions have limitations: differential privacy reduces accuracy, homomorphic encryption causes high overhead, trusted execution environments face side-channel risks, and SCOTCH suffers accuracy loss and lacks weighted aggregation. This paper proposes SecAgg, a secure aggregation scheme based on additive secret sharing. Under a multi-server non-collusion assumption, SecAgg ensures FedAvg-consistent aggregation, protects update privacy, supports weighted aggregation, reduces communication cost, and improves efficiency while maintaining model accuracy.

title: cuRPQ: A High-Performance GPU-Based Framework for Processing Regular and Conjunctive Regular Path Queries
authors: Sungwoo Park, Seohyeon Kim, Min-Soo Kim
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: ScaleDoc: Scaling LLM-based Predicates over Large Document Collections
authors: Hengrui Zhang, Yulong Hui, Yihao Liu, Huanchen Zhang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: CRLB-Minimized Precoding for ISABC-Assisted IoT Networks with Uniform Planar Arrays
authors: Fangmin Xu, Yan Gong, Haiyan Cao, Zhirui Hu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: To address the dual demands of high-efficiency communication for multiple devices and high-precision positioning for multiple passive radio frequency identification (RFID) tags in the Internet of Things (IoT), this paper investigates an integrated sensing and backscatter communication (ISABC) system based on a uniform planar array (UPA). Focusing on resource-constrained IoT gateways, we aim to minimize the Cramér-Rao lower bound (CRLB) for high-precision two-dimensional (2D) direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation of passive tags via precoding design, subject to the gateway power budget and communication quality-of-service (QoS) constraints. To balance high-precision positioning with real-time processing in dynamic IoT environments, we propose a configurable framework comprising three schemes: 1) joint sensing and communication precoding (JSCP) as a performance benchmark; 2) layered-precoding communication-optimized precoding (LP-COP) to effectively balance sensing accuracy and algorithmic latency; and 3) a low-complexity fixed-beam with dynamic power allocation precoding (FB-PDPA). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed LP-COP scheme achieves near-optimal sensing accuracy comparable to the JSCP benchmark while reducing computation time by approximately 96% and maintaining strong robustness under strict signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) constraints. The proposed framework provides a flexibly configurable technical path for IoT ISABC deployments.

title: Kirchhoff-Consistent Neural Network: A Physics-Constrained and Accelerated Surrogate Model for Power Electronics Simulation
authors: Ruiheng Guo, Wentao Huang, Moduo Yu, Hongbiao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Accelerating power electronics simulation via neural networks has emerged as a promising research avenue. However, existing data-driven methods often rely heavily on specific expert priors and fail to guarantee compliance with the most fundamental circuit laws—Kirchhoff's laws. The former limits their scalability to complex systems, while the latter compromises physical fidelity. This paper proposes a high-fidelity and accelerated neural network modeling framework that relies solely on Kirchhoff's laws, while rigorously guaranteeing Kirchhoff consistency. To achieve this goal, a physics-partitioned architecture is first designed with separate current -type and voltage -type channels. Second, a Kirchhoff-consistent operator is constructed, which employs orthogonal projection to optimally project violating outputs onto the Kirchhoff constraint space, thereby strictly eliminating physical residuals during forward propagation. Additionally, a multi-step training strategy equipped with an adaptive weighting scheme is tailored to mitigate the loss imbalance inherent in full-variable simulation. The proposed method offers two distinct advantages: 1) it relies exclusively on Kirchhoff's laws—fundamental knowledge accessible to general practitioners—ensuring high scalability; and 2) it strictly adheres to KCL and KVL for all circuit nodes and loops, significantly enhancing model credibility. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high simulation fidelity with strict zero KCL/KVL residuals, delivering a substantial computational acceleration compared to commercial software (e.g., maintaining a 99.22% accuracy and achieving a 5.54- fold speedup for the investigated microgrid-level system).

title: Tracker-LLM: Enhancing Visual Object Tracking via Bi-directional Chain-of-Thought Reasoning and Spatio-Temporal Memory
authors: Zijing Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: While Vision-Language (VL) tracking has shown promise in improving tracking versatility by integrating linguistic cues, existing methods often rely on static and potentially ambiguous textual descriptions. Furthermore, these frameworks fail to leverage the deep, multi-step reasoning capabilities inherent in modern Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). To address these limitations, we propose Tracker-LLM, a novel tracking framework that treats the MLLM as an active logical reasoning hub rather than a passive text generator. We introduce a Bi-directional Chain-of-Thought (Bi-CoT) reasoning mechanism that empowers the model to autonomously derive and refine localization logic based on real-time tracking feedback. To bridge the gap between high-level abstract semantics and low-level granular visual features, we design an Asynchronous Spatio-Temporal Memory (ASTM) engine. This engine alleviates the problem of semantic obsolescence caused by target appearance evolution and environmental shifts in long-term tracking by maintaining and updating a lightweight memory pool. Furthermore, a Dynamic Background-Sensitive Awareness (DBSA) strategy is proposed to refine background descriptions iteratively through the mining of hard distractors. Extensive experiments on several mainstream benchmarks demonstrate that Tracker-LLM significantly enhances tracking robustness and precision, particularly in complex scenarios involving severe occlusions, illumination variations, and drastic appearance changes, while achieving an average end-to-end latency of 6.89 ms (145.1 FPS) and a peak speed of 215.1 FPS on GPU.

title: Megahertz Soft-Switching Wireless Power Transfer for Capsule Robots Under Dynamic Position and Orientation Variations_supp3-3693630.avi
authors: Chi Kwan Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Capsule robots for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics have gained increasing attention in recent years owing to their miniature form factor and non-invasive, painless operation. However, their limited size restricts the integration of large batteries, which poses a major challenge when advanced functions such as high-frame-rate, high-resolution video recording are required. Wireless power transfer (WPT) offers a promising solution to this limitation. In this work, we propose an MHz-range WPT scheme that leverages the high-Q characteristics of coils at MHz frequencies, enabling a significant reduction in both transmitter and receiver coil sizes, which is essential for miniaturized capsule robots. Furthermore, a three-dimensional receiver coil structure is analyzed and compared, identifying configurations with improved efficiency. A key challenge in capsule WPT is that capsule motion through the digestive tract alters the reflected impedance seen by the Class-E driver, thereby disrupting its zero-voltage switching (ZVS) operation. To address this issue, two control methods are introduced to ensure consistent soft-switching under varying capsule orientations. In addition, a power regulation algorithm is developed to stabilize the output voltage and its performance is experimentally validated. Finally, the average received power along the entire GI trajectory is evaluated under a constant transmitter current. These results establish a foundation for the development of next-generation wirelessly powered capsule robots.

title: Quantitative Steel Corrosion Assessment Using Impedance Responses of Planar Eddy Current Sensor
authors: Yen-yi Hoo, Shun Li, Khant Swe Hein, Yue Chen, Ke-yu Chen, Jin Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This study introduces an ECT impedance-based method for monitoring and quantifying corrosion progression. The proposed approach enables direct tracking of real corrosion evolution, which is characterized by irregular geometries and the formation of corrosion products with different properties. To assess the corrosion behavior under different service conditions that affect impedance signals, accelerated corrosion experiments were conducted. Results show that inductance variation exhibits exponential correlation with mass loss, influenced by the combined effects of lateral corrosion spreading and depth-wise penetration. Under conditions more representative of practical corrosion, this relationship can be approximated as linear. Corrosion products could influence the inductive response, particularly during the corrosion-suppression phase under cyclic-current conditions. Coil size was found to directly affect the effective sensing area and the stability of the eddy-current response. Larger coils provide broader sensing coverage, but smaller coils provide more stable and localized measurements. Lift-off effect is governed by the strength of electromagnetic coupling, with excessive separation reducing sensitivity. High frequency (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\gt}\mathbf{100}\,\mathbf{kHz}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) enhances signal stability and reduces disturbances caused by corrosion product phase shifts.

title: “I'm Not Mad, Just Focused”: Understanding Human Emotions in Human-Robot Collaboration
authors: Seung Chan Hong, Dana Kulić, Leimin Tian
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Human-robot collaboration (HRC) can benefit from robots' abilities to interpret human emotional states. However, current emotion recognition (ER) models in HRC often fall short, particularly due to their reliance on acted datasets and single-modality inputs like facial expressions. We propose a novel vision language model (VLM)-based ER system that leverages contextual understanding to improve emotion interpretation in HRC. We first evaluate the VLM-ER system by assessing its semantic and sentiment similarity with human annotations on an existing HRC dataset. Then, in a user study with a service robot in a collaborative delivery task, we evaluate the effects of modulating the robot's behaviour based on the user's emotional state inferred by the VLM-ER system. The results show that the proposed VLM-ER system achieves higher semantic similarity and positive sentiment alignment with human annotations compared to a baseline convolutional neural network-based system. Further, participants in the user study preferred emotion-adaptive robot behaviour facilitated by the VLM-ER system.

title: AI-Assisted Low-Power VLSI Synthesis using Reinforcement Learning for Gate-Level Power Optimization
authors: Dhanumjaya K, Sudharsan G, Balamanikandan A, Mohana Priya S, Venkata Karthika V, Sudhakar Reddy N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper presents a constrained actor-critic framework that combines an attention-pooled graph neural network encoder with a calibrated surrogate ensemble to drive low-power gate-level VLSI synthesis. The approach flattens RTL to annotated gate netlists, uses a compact message-passing GNN to produce per-gate embeddings and a pooled global state, and issues hybrid actions (discrete local edits plus continuous sizing) through an actor-critic policy. Fast surrogate GNN regressors provide inner-loop estimates for power and timing with ensemble-based uncertainty; periodic full-tool calibration (OpenSTA/FastPower) and a safety filter with a Lagrangian controller enforce timing budgets and maintain surrogate fidelity. Evaluation across small, medium, and large benchmarks shows median power savings of 10–18% relative to a baseline heuristic, typical area impact below 2%, and an effective training/runtime multiplier of about 1.8× (full-STA training rises to ~4.5× without surrogates). Calibration reduced surrogate MSE for power from ~0.024 to ~0.006 and for timing from ~0.031 to ~0.008, lowering high-uncertainty episodes from ~12% to ~3%. Ablation studies confirm the value of attention pooling and surrogate acceleration. The method offers a practical, reproducible path to integrate learning-based low-power optimization into existing EDA flows.

title: Interpretable Neural Network Modeling of Hub Coverage Performance in Road Networks
authors: Deyang Kong, Ning Guo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This research examines the evaluation of hub coverage performance in complex road networks with time-sensitive service demands. As transportation network structures become more complex and demand distribution becomes spatially heterogeneous, traditional indicator-based or optimization-focused methods have difficulty in accurately capturing the nonlinear relationships between network characteristics and coverage results. To address this issue, an interpretable learning-based neural network is proposed to quantitatively describe the mapping between multi-layer road network features and hub-level coverage performance. In the framework, accessibility indicators, network structure metrics, and demand-related features are formulated as model inputs, while demand-weighted coverage ratios across various service time thresholds are designated as output variables. The neural network is utilized to analyze the nonlinear associations between inputs and outputs, while permutation-based feature contribution analysis is implemented to improve model interpretability. A regional multi-city road network serves as a case study to validate the suggested methodology. The findings demonstrate that coverage predictability is significantly affected by the service time horizon, with longer time thresholds resulting in smoother coverage patterns and enhanced forecast accuracy. Furthermore, the feature contribution analysis indicates that accessibility- and demand-oriented variables are predominant in coverage prediction, while many traditional structural metrics demonstrate limited marginal effects. The suggested framework offers a generalizable and interpretable model for coverage-oriented analysis in transportation networks, providing methodological assistance for hub assessment and decision-making in complex road systems.

title: MonoRouter: Enforcing Monotonicity in Item Response Theory for LLM Routing
authors: Yousheng Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The development of large language models(LLMs) has led to the emergence of many small models focused on specific domains, which demonstrate outstanding performance in those areas. Researchs indicated that integrating these smaller models through effective mechanisms can achieve performance comparable to a large-scale model. LLM routing has garnered widespread attention as an efficient ensemble paradigm, dynamically allocating user queries to the optimal candidate model without the need to invoke all available models. LLM routing requires a precise alignment between model capabilities and user query difficulty. To achieve this joint modeling, Item Response Theory (IRT) has been adopted, as it effectively decouples complex queries into latent variables such as difficulty and discrimination. However, during unconstrained optimization, conventional IRT often suffers from parameter drift with negative discrimination, leading to counter-intuitive routing logic. In this paper, we introduce MonoRouter, an enhanced IRT routing framework integrated with a monotonicity constraint. Specifically, by constraining the discrimination parameter to be strictly positive, we eradicate the paradoxical negative correlation between model capability and success rate. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks using 16 open-source models demonstrate the efficacy of our method.

title: A Collaborative Privacy Protection and Deletion System for Outsourced Data
authors: Han Xue
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Outsourced data storage offers a flexible solution for remote management but introduces significant security concerns due to the loss of physical data control. Current privacy schemes often rely on trusted third parties and lack robust hardware-level security. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel privacy-preserving system based on an elliptic curve collaborative signature protocol. By integrating IC cards and a Hardware Security Module (HSM), the scheme provides hardware-level protection for private key components. The system employs a two-party collaborative architecture that splits key components between the user and the server, effectively eliminating the need for highly trusted third parties. Furthermore, we implement a lightweight key management mechanism to reduce complexity, utilizing a VSTO plugin for convenient user-side access and a Linux-based environment for the collaboration server. This approach offers significant improvements in both security and practicality for trusted data deletion in cloud environments.

title: TABLE-SMITH: Structured Data Transformation Through Class-based Segmentation
authors: M. Deepika, Sandeep Kumar, K Anirudh Sai, B Sai Charan, K Hari, Shilpa Choudhary
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: In the current era of rapid technological advancements, data plays a significant role in almost every task. Due to the integration of technology among various industries, there has been a rapid influx of data. This has raised the need to immediately develop an efficient system capable of organizing and transforming inconsistent data. Poorly formatted tables and missing records often disrupt smooth data exchange and integration. The existing tools for transformation are usually limited to single-format or database-centric workflows, deal only with isolated transformation sub-tasks, and perform transformations without explaining the rationale in a human-interpretable form. Table-Smith implements a smart approach to analyze, restructure, and consolidate tabular datasets that originate from several sources. By aligning positional feature understanding with reasoning, the system renders clean and noisy tables to produce standard machine-readable output. Powered by Python, Flask, and transformer-based large language models (LLMs), Table-Smith accelerates tasks such as data normalization, type alignment, and schema synchronization. Rigorous evaluation on multi-domain datasets illustrates that the framework accomplishes a higher accuracy in contrast with existing solutions, while offering explainable and easily deployable results. The implementation is available on GitHub.

title: An Explainable Artificial Intelligence-based Machine Learning Framework for Advanced Phishing Detection
authors: Ishan Agarwal, Sahitri Choudhury, Anugraha P, Jasmine S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Phishing email attacks have become one of the most common and harmful methods of cyber threats, as the attackers take advantage of trust, as opposed to technical flaws. Regardless of the technological progress in email security, phishing schemes are on the rise becoming increasingly sophisticated and, in many cases, This research work presents an intelligent phishing email detection system that uses machine learning and deep learning techniques to detect phishing emails with improved accuracy and reliability. The suggested system will examine the content and metadata of emails by utilizing an active pre-processing pipeline, and will classify emails with multiple learning models. This is done to give a comparative evaluation on the model performance based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and the false positive rate. According to experimental findings, the suggested method is quite useful to detect phishing emails and does not go beyond a practical tool, so it can be applied to real-life email security issues.

title: An Approach of Multistar Image Simulation in Dynamic Conditions
authors: Mingyang Zhang, Xiangbing Feng, Bin Shao, Fu. Li, Huijian Liang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: High-precision star image simulation is fundamental to advancing astronomical imaging and space navigation systems. While existing studies primarily focus on dynamic condition errors in singlestar systems, they fall short of addressing the complexities in multitarget tracking scenarios. This paper presents a comprehensive error-quantification framework for multistar star tracker simulations under dynamic motion. Unlike conventional first-order and linear point spread function (LPS) methods, the proposed approach is built based on a second-order dynamic model that explicitly incorporates accounts for higher-order kinematic terms, enabling more accurate modeling of star point motion. Comparative evaluations across four representative motion scenarios demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms both the first-order and LPS methods. These results underscore the necessity of second-order dynamics for reliable star-image simulation in demanding operational environments.

title: SDATNet: Social Diverse Adaptive TrajectorNet for Pedestrian Trajectory Prediction Under Long-Tail Behavior Distributions
authors: Zihan Jiang, Ruonan Liu, Xiuzhong Hu, Yafeng Guo, Weidong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Pedestrian trajectory prediction is vital for safety-critical applications such as autonomous driving and video surveillance. While recent advances in deep learning have significantly improved predictive accuracy, most models struggle with underrepresented and diverse behaviors present in the long-tail distribution of pedestrian trajectories. To address this challenge, we propose Social Diverse Adaptive TrajectorNet (SDATNet), a novel deep learning framework designed to enhance prediction performance on rare and atypical trajectory patterns. SDATNet introduces three key innovations: 1) the ResGhost module, which incorporates residual connections and ghost operations to enrich multi-scale social feature diversity; 2) a Dynamic Weights Variety Loss (DWVL) that adaptively emphasizes rare behaviors by weighting loss based on prediction uncertainty; and 3) an Adaptive Gaussian Distribution module that stabilizes the modeling of outlier trajectories by aligning prediction distributions with behavioral variance. The results demonstrate that the SDATNet outperforms existing methods in predicting pedestrian trajectories, offering a reliable and versatile solution for real-world applications.

title: Lightweight Framework for Liquor Identification via Multidimensional Sensor Signal Feature Extraction and Hardware Deployment
authors: Yu Wang, Zheng Lan, Lin Shi, Xiupeng Shi, Lin Xu, Jian Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The prevalence of counterfeit premium and renowned liquors underscores the critical importance of component analysis, for which the integration of semiconductor gas sensors with artificial intelligence techniques leads to significant improvements in detection efficiency and accuracy. However, challenges remain regarding the redundancy of volatile signal data and the deployment of complex neural networks on resource-constrained hardware. In this work, we construct a signal acquisition array based on four gas sensors (MQ-3B, MQ-7, MP503, and MP801), which enables robust data collection from diverse liquor samples. Leveraging a self-built dataset, we propose a multi-head joint distillation network (MCKDNet T-S) that incorporates multidimensional signal feature extraction (SFE), which reduces redundant information and enhances feature representation. The knowledge distillation framework transfers knowledge from a teacher model based on long short-term memory (LSTM) networks to a student model constructed with a unidirectional recurrent neural network (RNN), which achieves over 90% accuracy in both quality assessment and brand classification tasks, with the number of parameters and FLOPs both reduced by more than 92%. Finally, the proposed method is deployed on Jetson and Raspberry Pi platforms, which results in accuracy losses below 3% and inference times under 6 ms, thereby demonstrating its efficiency and practical value for miniaturized detection systems.

title: Enhancing the controllability of quantum systems via a static field
authors: Ruikang Liang, Eugenio Pozzoli, Monika Leibscher, Mario Sigalotti, Christiane P. Koch, Ugo Boscain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: We provide a sufficient condition for the controllability of a bilinear closed quantum system steered by a static field and a time-varying field, based on the notion of weakly conically connected spectrum. More precisely, we show that if a controlled Hamiltonian with two inputs has a weakly conically connected spectrum, then, freezing one of the two inputs at almost every constant value, the obtained single-input system is controllable. The result is illustrated with two examples, enantio-selective excitation in a chiral molecule and the driven Jaynes–Cummings Hamiltonian.

title: Colored Traveling Salesman Problems: Models, Solutions, and Applications
authors: Jun Li, MengChu Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: A colored traveling salesman problem (CTSP) can subtly and thoroughly depict the homogeneity of salesmen and the assignment relationships between them and cities by using colors. It has been proven to be a generalization of existing traveling salesman problems (TSPs) and multiple TSPs (MTSPs). Nevertheless, its solutions and applications to various areas, e.g., logistic, manufacturing, and transporation, remain to be explored. To promote the understanding, research, and applications of CTSPs, we carry out the first survey on CTSPs by: 1) organizing the CTSP development timeline and re-formalizing the existing CTSPs as well as typical variants with different objectives and constraints in a unified way; 2) comparing CTSPs with existing TSPs, MTSPs, and vehicle routing problems (VRPs), and revealing their differences and connections; 3) introducing the main principles and procedures of the exact, heuristic, large-scale, learning-based, and parallel solutions of CTSPs; 4) reviewing the typical applications of CTSPs to scheduling a variety of multimachine mission systems; and 5) discussing the future research directions of CTSPs and their potential applications. This article is not only the first survey of CTSPs but can also serve as a reference for research on related sequencing problems such as TSPs, MTSPs, and VRPs.
