title: An Integrated Secure Framework for Cyberbiosecurity
authors: Samrajesh Mault, Kodakkal Kannan Viswanathan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Today, biological research and manufacturing are becoming totally dependent on digital technologies, starting from the research and development through to the last-mile delivery of the products. The general cybersecurity standards and frameworks are broad and are not well suitable for cyber threats arising around biological research. There is a need to have an integrated and operational cybersecurity framework that meets the needs the specific needs of the area. In this paper we address this gap by reviewing existing cyberbiosecurity literature and analyzing the limitations in current practices and standards related to biosecurity. The proposed integrated secure framework uses multiple factors such as access control, network segmentation, continuous monitoring, incident response, resilience, and governance. Moreover, the framework extends existing cybersecurity standards to incorporate crucial biological assets, laboratory automation, and associated risks. The proposed framework’s objective is to enhance the cybersecurity posture of a typical biological or life science-based organization’s and protect critical systems from attacks. Our comparative evaluation using factors such as hybrid threat detection effectiveness, biological context awareness score and threat intelligence correlation accuracy shows that the proposed framework is effective in threat detection and protection.

title: An XGBoost-Driven Framework for Accurate Peak-Hour Traffic Accident Prediction
authors: BSH. Shayeez Ahamed, N.Bala Krishna, Shaik Musthakeen, Ponna Manoj, Nallagrola Amarendra, Odela Sai Sri Bhargav
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The present project makes use of a web deployment using Flask and an ensemble learning model which is powered by XGBoost to predict peak-hour accidents. It is because the XGBoost method can find the occurrence of fine non-linear relationships and the time-relations in the event of large traffic accidents data. It is also designed in a way that its design minimizes overfitting due to regularization. In contrast to the statistical and neural networks approaches, the model is trained based on the spatial-temporal properties and the intersection-level properties and assessed with the help of the RMSE, MAE, and MAPE. The trained model is deployed through flask based web server. This interface is also user-friendly as the user is able to input datasets and make predictions in real-time and interact with the outcome of the prediction. The scalability of the extension, the response time, and the property of unchanged performance of the extension makes it flexible to the purposes of decision-making and security of the urban traffic systems.

title: A Modeling Method for Prior Information Representation of Anti-Active-Jamming
authors: Tong Yu, Zhiyong Song
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Active jamming signals, which exploit radar-transmitted signals, can significantly degrade radar detection performance. Traditional anti-jamming methods typically require accurate interference parameters; their performance deteriorates substantially when parameter deviations occur. To quantify the impact, this paper proposes a prior-information-based modeling approach for characterizing jamming tolerance. Initially, typical active jamming are suppressed under accurate parameter conditions to analyze the effect of parameter errors. Subsequently, a mapping model between jamming parameter deviations and resultant jamming peak levels of pulse-compressed echoes is established. This is achieved using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network, whose hyperparameters are optimized via the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm. This model is then used to predict sidelobe performance under various deviation scenarios. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the established model accurately represents the input-output relationship and offers reliable predictive capability. The study provides a quantitative basis for designing parameter tolerance and evaluating the robustness of anti-jamming systems.

title: Design of Mac Unit for Deep Learning Application using Urdhava Tiryakbhayam Multiplier
authors: V. Govindaraj, Lekha PV, Sivaraman V, Anitha S, Manickkavasakam A, Ramesh.C
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Numerous arithmetic operations particularly multiplication and accumulation, are necessary for deep learning applications. Multiply-Accumulate (MAC) units which are frequently used for these operations are crucial in determining the speed and effectiveness of neural network calculations. When processing more datasets, traditional multiplier systems frequently experience higher power consumption and latency. The Urdhva Tiryakbhyam multiplier technique, that is one of the Vedic mathematics, is used in this design to work as a MAC unit. The suggested architecture seeks to increase hardware efficiency and computing speed. Compared to traditional multipliers, the multiplier reduces propagation delay by generating partial products in parallel form. The MAC unit supports deep learning operations like convolution and matrix multiplication by integrating the Vedic multiplier with an effective accumulator. Digital logic is used to implement the design with efficient performance in area, power and delay. The findings show that the suggested design is appropriate for deep learning accelerators since it offers quicker computation with optimal hardware consumption.

title: Quality-driven Inference Orchestration for Multi-modality Machine Learning Systems at the Edge
authors: Minh-Tri Nguyen, Anh-Dung Nguyen, Hong-Linh Truong

title: A Window-based Deep Learning Framework for Time-Series Anomaly Detection in ECG Signals
authors: Keerthana S, S. Thenmalar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) anomaly detection is essential for identifying abnormal cardiac behavior and signal artefacts in long-term monitoring systems. However, ECG signals are highly non-stationary and affected by noise, baseline drift, and patient-specific variability, which makes reliable anomaly detection challenging. This work presents a window-based deep learning framework for ECG anomaly detection using time-series reconstruction analysis. Raw ECG signals are segmented into overlapping windows and normalized to reduce amplitude variability. Time–frequency information is extracted through wavelet transformation to capture localized spectral characteristics of the ECG waveform. The processed signal windows are grouped into temporal sequences and modeled using a deep learning reconstruction framework that learns normal cardiac signal patterns. Anomalies are identified using reconstruction error analysis based on mean squared error (MSE). In addition, statistical spike detection using standardized amplitude deviation is used to identify sudden signal artefacts such as spikes or flatlines. Experimental analysis demonstrates stable training convergence and clear separation between normal and abnormal signal segments through reconstruction error visualization. The proposed framework achieves reliable anomaly localization at the window level and provides interpretable visual outputs, making it suitable for real-time ECG monitoring and anomaly detection applications.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Real-Time Multi-Camera PPE Compliance Monitoring Framework using Lightweight YOLO Models
authors: Ashwin Sathappan Vellayan, Bala M, Bhuvan Kalyan G V, Giridharan P, Janani G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Construction sites are highly hazardous environments where failure to adhere to Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) regulations significantly increases worker risk. Conventional manual surveillance is labor-intensive and impractical for large-scale operations, necessitating automated intelligent monitoring solutions. This study proposes a real-time multi-camera PPE compliance framework that integrates lightweight YOLO-based object detection models with ByteTrack, temporal persistence filtering, and zone-based violation analysis. The system processes multiple video streams concurrently to enable scalable safety monitoring. Four lightweight architectures - YOLOv8n, YOLOv8s, YOLOv8m, and YOLOv11n - were benchmarked using publicly available PPE datasets to identify the most suitable model for deployment. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv11n achieves the best accuracy-latency trade-off, attaining an mAP@50 of 88% and an average inference latency of 8.69 ms across four concurrent video streams. Furthermore, the integration of temporal filtering and contextual zone-based logic significantly reduces false-positive alerts, improving system reliability. These findings validate the effectiveness of lightweight YOLO models for robust, real-time safety monitoring in complex industrial environments.

title: Conquering Missing Views: Scalable Incomplete Multi-View Clustering with Anchor Graph Propagation
authors: Xinyu Pu, Hangjun Che, Deqiang Ouyang, Shouxi Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Recently, incomplete multi-view clustering has emerged as a powerful solution for discovering consistent cluster structures despite the presence of missing views. When handling incomplete data, existing methods usually reconstruct missing features from observed views or estimate similarities from known inter-sample relationships, yet they often fail to balance clustering performance with computational efficiency. Although anchor graph learning has shown strong effectiveness in multi-view clustering, its extension to incomplete scenarios remains a non-trivial challenge. Furthermore, the reliance on iterative optimization involving coupled variables significantly hinders the scalability of existing models. In response to these challenges, we propose SSR-a method designed for scaling, simplifying, and robustifying multi-view clustering. SSR introduces a scalable framework empowered by a novel anchor selection strategy tailored for incomplete multi-view data. In this framework, graph propagation is employed to impute missing information, enabling effective information diffusion across views. In contrast to traditional methods that rely on computationally intensive iterative optimization, SSR adopts a forward-only, low-complexity design, significantly enhancing scalability. Moreover, isolation masks are incorporated into the graph propagation process to improve robustness. Extensive experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate the superior clustering accuracy and computational efficiency of SSR, establishing it as a practical and efficient solution for multimedia analysis in real-world scenarios.

title: RCInvestigator: towards Better Investigation of Anomaly Root Causes in Cloud Computing Systems
authors: Shuhan Liu, Yunfan Zhou, Jue Zhang, Shandan Zhou, Weiwei Cui, Qingwei Lin, Thomas Moscibroda, Haidong Zhang, Di Weng, Yingcai Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Root cause analysis (RCA) is critical for maintaining the availability and efficiency of cloud computing systems. However, identifying root causes from the large-scale, high-dimensional monitoring data generated by these complex environments is a significant challenge. Current approaches often rely on time-consuming manual analysis to ensure flexibility and reliability, while recent automated methods lack the crucial insights provided by domain experts. To bridge this gap, we propose RCInvestigator, a visual analytics system that facilitates interactive root cause investigation by establishing a tight collaboration between human experts and machine analysis. Our approach addresses three key challenges: a) modeling databases for the root cause investigation, b) inferring root causes from large-scale time series, and c) building comprehensible investigation results. We demonstrate the effectiveness and utility of RCInvestigator through two real-world case studies, which received positive feedback from domain experts.

title: Comparative Analysis of Short-Circuit Behavior and Failure Mechanisms in Planar and Trench-Gate SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yifei Chang, Chengmin Li, Jiuyang Tang, Hao Guan, Qingchun Jon Zhang, Pan Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs have been widely adopted in power electronics applications. However, their robustness, particularly short-circuit robustness—remains a critical limitation. This work investigates the short-circuit characteristics of two SiC MOSFETs with planar-gate and trench-gate structures. Experimental results showed that both devices exhibited gate-voltage drop during short-circuit events, with a more pronounced voltage drop (3.8 V) observed in the planar-gate device. During the turn-off phase, the planar-gate MOSFET also showed a larger drain current tail compared to its trench-gate counterpart. In addition, both devices experienced delayed gate failure after short-circuit operation. By employing calibrated TCAD simulations, the influence of Fowler–Nordheim (FN) tunneling current and hot-carrier injection current on the gate-voltage drop was analyzed. The theoretical relationship between the drain current tail, the junction temperature and the applied voltage was derived, which explained the observed difference between the two device structures. Finally, thermomechanical stress analysis during the short circuit operation revealed the evolution of aluminum layer and the fracture mechanism of the interlayer dielectric (ILD) above the gate. These phenomena led to the formation of conductive paths, which were identified as the root cause of delayed gate failure. Analysis of failed devices using OBIRCH (Optical Beam Induced Resistance Change), FIB (Focused ion beam), and EDS (Energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy) confirmed the presence of melted aluminum and conductive channels within the ILD, validating the failure mechanism.

title: Cell-Level Characterization and Array-Induced Disturbance Analysis of a Novel Symmetric Split-Gate Flash Memory for Extended-Temperature Operation
authors: Chang Lu, Lei Shen
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: To address the unclear voltage-dependent electrical characteristics and long-range array disturb mechanism in high-reliability symmetric split-gate memory, a systematic evaluation methodology is proposed.This approach integrates a comprehensive test metric covering a wide temperature range (-40 to 125 °C) ,along with a test sequence designed to precisely isolate potential disturb sources,for reliability characterization.Through experimental testing,the temperature dependence of read current under various conditions is further revealed.TCAD simulations and key parameter extraction are employed to investigate the underlying physical mechanisms behind these phenomena.Furthermore,by analyzing the current distribution across the array,estimating the threshold voltage range based on terminal voltage configurations of the memory cell,and considering the overall array architecture,the dominant mechanisms for both erase disturb and program disturb are elucidated.This work provides critical experimental foundations for the design and modeling of high-reliability NOR Flash memory.

title: An Advanced Trellis-Coded Binary Modulation Approach to Achieve Extreme Coverage in 6G Communication Systems without Auxiliary Boosters
authors: Balaraman R, K. M. Dhanalakshmi, P. Sasireka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The paper describes the design and hardware testing of a Trellis-Coded Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (TC-OFDM) transceiver that can be used to develop a 6G wireless communication system with superior uplink coverage. It is driven by the necessity to have a reliable communication with a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) that is energy efficient. The suggested technique combines Trellis coded modulation with Coded Phase Shift Keying (CPSK) spreading to minimize peak to average power ratio (PAPR) and out of band emissions with no increase in bandwidth coding gain. Signal mapper, CPSK sequence generator, spreader, IFFT processor, and guard interval insertion are some of the important modules in the transmitter architecture. It is running on a Spartan3 FPGA and designed with Xilinx ISE Design Suite. The experimental findings show that it can run at a maximum of 148.5 MHz, consume a low power of 0.247 W and transmit data with a high effectiveness of 99.90 percent at a 1024-byte payload. Such findings affirm the effectiveness and practicality of the suggested TC-OFDM system in future 6G uplink communication, especially when there is a constraint in energy consumption and coverage.

title: TranSPAN: Analyzing Signal Property Learning in Transformer-Based Time Series Classification
authors: Nirasha N. Munasinghe, Sandareka Wickramanayake, Dulani Meedeniya
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Transformer-based architectures have recently demonstrated strong performance in time series classification (TSC). However, despite their success, limited work has investigated how these models internally capture fundamental signal characteristics present in time-series data such as amplitude, frequency, phase shift, trend, sharp transitions, and seasonality, essential for reliable temporal reasoning. In this paper, we introduce TranSPAN (Transformer Signal Property ANalysis), a post-hoc interpretability framework designed to analyze how Transformer-based TSC (TTSC) models encode core signal properties through attention mechanisms. TranSPAN systematically masks attention heads and measures the resulting degradation in signal properties, enabling the identification of signal-property–sensitive attention heads (SAHs). Using this framework, we evaluate six TTSC architectures, including vanilla Transformer, TrajFormer, SVP-T, MTM, TARNet, and GTN, across three benchmark datasets: ECG5000, FordA, and UCI HAR. The analysis reveals several key findings. First, TTSC architectures incorporating time-series–specific inductive biases capture substantially more signal properties than the vanilla Transformer. Second, architectural design significantly influences both the diversity and distribution of SAHs. Third, signal-property learning follows a hierarchical pattern across encoder layers, where early layers capture local signal characteristics (e.g., amplitude, sharp transitions) while deeper layers increasingly encode global temporal structures (e.g., trend, seasonality). Additional experiments demonstrate that optimization hyperparameters and derivative-based preprocessing influence the magnitude of signal-property sensitivity but do not fundamentally alter the hierarchical organization of learned representations. Overall, TranSPAN provides a systematic framework for analyzing signal-aware representation learning in TTSC models and offers insights that may guide the design of more interpretable and robust TSC architectures.

title: Adaptive Ensemble Learning for Heavy-Duty Vehicle Emission and Maintenance Optimization
authors: N Saikiran, T Nalini, Arulanantham G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Trucks are a major contributor of PM and NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> emissions that are very dangerous to the environment and human health. The dynamic working conditions of the modern diesel engines are not adequately represented by the traditional maintenance procedures, which are often fixed. To facilitate sustainable infrastructure by using intelligent vehicles to operate, this paper suggests an Adaptive Ensemble Framework to provide real-time emission prediction and maintenance optimization. The system is a combination of multi-modal sensor fusion, adaptive threshold learning, context-based prediction, and temporal convolution networks with attention mechanisms in order to model complex emission behavior with different working conditions. It is shown to have strong predictive performance (both in terms of R2 and 28% reduction in false-positive alarms compared to traditional methods). The proposed system enhances the efficiency of the operations and progresses the management of the low-emission, data-driven vehicle through a 34% decrease in maintenance costs and 41% reduction in unexpected downtime by enabling the maintenance decision-making process based on the actual emission profiles.

title: Design and Implementation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Identity Authentication Algorithm Integrating Radio Frequency Fingerprint and Behavioral Features
authors: Jinyi Liu, Mei Chen, Chen Yuan, Zhanfeng Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address the vulnerability of UAVs to signal replay and device spoofing attacks in open wireless environments, and the insufficient robustness of traditional single-feature authentication methods, this paper proposes a UAV identity authentication algorithm that integrates radio frequency fingerprints and behavioral features. First, a multimodal synchronous acquisition system is designed to acquire radio frequency signals and flight status data. Then, hardware fingerprints and dynamic behavior sequences are acquired through parallel feature extraction paths. Next, a dual-stream deep learning model based on CNN-LSTM (Convolutional Neural Network- Long Short-Term Memory) is constructed, and an attention mechanism is introduced to achieve adaptive weighted fusion of features. Finally, an end-to-end real-time authentication system is implemented. Experiments show that the proposed algorithm achieves an overall authentication accuracy of 98.5%, with a false acceptance rate of less than 3.5% in resistance to replay and simulated attacks, verifying the significant advantages of multimodal feature fusion in improving the uniqueness of identity identification and the anti-counterfeiting capability of the system.

title: A Multimodal Hybrid Framework Integrating Hypergraph-Spatio-Cognitive Transformers, Cross-Modal Adaptive Embeddings, and Temporal Attention Fusion Networks for Enhanced Early Detection of Autism Spectrum Disorder
authors: Avila Roshini A, Anushree S, D. Lakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is not easily detected because of the variability of their behavior outcome, physiological, and neural expression in different patients. This paper will present the idea of a hybrid triple-architecture design with Hypergraph-Spatio-Cognitive Transformers (HSCT), Cross-Modal Adaptive Embedding (CMAE), and Temporal Attention Fusion Networks (TAFN) as a way of increasing the accuracy of the diagnosis. Complex interdependencies between neuroimaging, behavioral, and physiological properties are captured in HSCT, whereas multimodal representations are harmonized to minimize redundancy and maximize the level of discriminative learning in CMAE. TAFN analyses the time-based trajectories of development, and it finds subtle patterns that reveal an early ASD risk. It was tested on 1,200 multimodal samples and attained a classification accuracy of 98% attaining superiority over graph-based models (baseline) and transformer-based models. The combination of the three components has been confirmed in ablation studies to enhance sensitivity as well as specificity and generalization. Also, the model can give interpretable information about the multimodal contributions of features such that it can be applied clinically and furthered to the developmental intervention planning. This scalable and robust method proves the possibility of screening and early ASD detection on a large scale, with the aid of AI, and provides an avenue to the incorporation of multimodal neurodevelopmental diagnostic into precision healthcare.

title: An Intelligent System for Online DC Motor Experiments in Engineering Education
authors: Kaixing Hong, Shukai Luo, Hui Lou, Suan Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Education
abstract:
Contribution: This article presents an innovative intelligent system designed for online DC motor experiments. Leveraging the high-precision DC motor control hardware, the system integrates virtual load simulation with neural network-based intelligent evaluation. This integration not only simulates the real-world loads accurately, but also enables real-time assessment of learning outcomes.

Background: Conventional DC motor experiments are typically carried out on fixed platforms in laboratories and evaluated through paper-based reports. This approach has several drawbacks, such as spatiotemporal constraints, limited load types, and the lack of process-based assessment.

Intended Outcomes: To address these issues, an online DC motor experiment system supporting remote operation has been developed. This system provides real-time feedback to help users understand DC motor operations under various loads. Moreover, the system identifies operational weaknesses and offers specific suggestions for improvement.

Application Design: The hardware implementation of the system employs an STM32 controller running a PI control algorithm, which can simulate the mechanical properties of various loads precisely. This is complemented by a web-based platform developed with ASP.NET, which enables remote operation and real-time data visualization. Furthermore, an intelligent evaluation module leverages neural network models to perform automated assessments and provide feedback on the experimental process.

Findings: The system enhances the learning experience by combining an intuitive interface with versatile load simulations under standardized procedures. Furthermore, its intelligent assessment generates automated, data-rich reports, offering key support and making it a practical tool for advancing outcome-based education.

title: GraFuz-MST: A Graph-Theoretic Framework for Scalable and Interpretable Fuzzy Rule Learning in Big Data
authors: Mohammad Nematpour, Farnaz Mahan
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: In the era of big data, extracting interpretable and scalable knowledge from massive and uncertain datasets remains a key challenge. Fuzzy rule learning provides a powerful tool for modeling uncertainty and enabling transparent decision-making; however, traditional approaches face limitations such as low scalability, high computational cost, and loss of interpretability in distributed environments. To overcome these constraints, we propose GraFuz-MST: a graph-based framework for scalable and interpretable fuzzy rule learning in big data settings. The method begins by selecting effective features through statistical approaches, followed by local granular fuzzy rule extraction using fuzzy spectral clustering at each node. The core innovation lies in a novel intelligent fusion mechanism based on the Maximum Spanning Tree (MST): a weighted graph is constructed with nodes representing local models and edge weights reflecting the similarity between rules. The MST automatically identifies central models, assigning them higher weights in the final fusion process. This approach enhances model accuracy and consistency while preserving interpretability. Furthermore, the framework dynamically adjusts the number of local models in response to data changes and workload variations. GraFuz-MST offers a balanced, graph-theory-driven solution well-suited for extracting meaningful, interpretable, and scalable knowledge from big data.

title: Design and Analysis of High-k Stacked Nanosheet Gate-All-Around FET for Improved Performance
authors: Jeevanarao Batakala, K. Kanthi Kinnera, Kundrapu Sarala, Ellapu Bhanu Prakash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance analysis of nanosheet gate-all-around (GAA) FET structures with different configurations, including underlap engineering and high-k dielectric integration. Five devices (A–E) are investigated and compared in terms of key electrical parameters such as threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf>), ON current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>), OFF current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>), subthreshold slope (SS), and drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL). The results demonstrate that high-k based nanosheet devices significantly improve performance by enhancing I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> and reducing leakage current. Among all devices, Device D exhibits superior performance with the highest I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio (67.39 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup>), lowest I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> (0.06 nA/µm), and minimal DIBL (9.03 mV/V), indicating excellent electrostatic control and effective suppression of short-channel effects (SCEs). Furthermore, improved subthreshold characteristics are observed in high-k devices, approaching near-ideal SS values. The study confirms that the combination of stacked nanosheet architecture and high-k dielectric materials provides a promising solution for next-generation nanoscale devices with enhanced performance and reduced leakage.

title: Energy-Efficient Auto-Switch-Off Socket for Reducing Phantom Load In Chargers
authors: Petchithai Velladurai, Saravanan S, Santhosh Kumaran P, Marimuthu P, R. Jenitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing use of personal electronic devices has led to increased standby or phantom energy losses due to chargers remaining plugged in after charging completion, contributing to unnecessary power consumption and carbon emissions. The objective of this work is to design a low-cost, autonomous auto-switch-off socket that eliminates standby energy usage in chargers without user intervention. The proposed methodology employs an Arduino Nano controller integrated with an ACS712 Hall-effect current sensor to continuously monitor load current and identify the transition from active charging to standby mode, supported by a vibration sensor for instant cutoff during manual unplugging. A solid-state relay ensures complete power disconnection, while an independent Li-ion battery system prevents additional standby losses from the control circuit. Experimental results demonstrate reliable detection and rapid switching, achieving true zero standby power and saving approximately 1.095 kWh per socket annually. The outcome confirms that the proposed system is effective, safe, scalable, and environmentally beneficial, offering a practical solution for intelligent energy conservation in residential and institutional settings.

title: Orthogonal Chirp-Frequency-Division Multiple Access: System, Channel Estimation, and Pilot Optimization
authors: Túlio F. Moreira, Wallace A. Martins, Moisés V. Ribeiro
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper introduces the orthogonal chirp-frequency-division multiple access (OCFDMA), a technique based on orthogonal chirp-frequency-division multiplexing that partitions the frequency band into independent chirp-based subchannels, referred to as voices. By leveraging the block-circulant property of the discrete modular chirp transform, OCFDMA assigns each user one or more voices to achieve orthogonal, interference-free, multiple-access transmissions, thereby enabling per-user processing and reducing transceiver complexity. User-oriented geometries are developed to allow flexible trade-offs between bit-error rate (BER) and data rate by adjusting the number and size of voices. Per-voice linear models are derived, and both minimum-variance unbiased and minimum mean-squared-error channel estimators are presented, along with optimal pilot designs. The proposed technique is compared with state-of-the-art chirp-based multiple-access techniques, such as orthogonal chirp-division multiple access (OCDMA), as well as orthogonal frequency-division multiple access (OFDMA)-related techniques widely adopted in current wireless standards. Numerical experiments show that OCFDMA achieves superior peruser BER, strong robustness to cyclic-prefix-length violation and carrier-frequency offset, competitive data rates, and a peak-to-average power ratio comparable to OFDMA for both downlink and uplink connections.

title: Small Object Detection in Multiclass Images based on Noise Suppression and Feature Integration
authors: Min Yao, Xiangguang Yu, Zhen Tian
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Small object detection remains challenging due to low resolution, weak semantic responses, and severe interference from complex backgrounds. Although deep feature extraction and multi-scale feature fusion can partially alleviate these issues, conventional strategies based on convolutional neural network (CNN) often suffer from progressive loss of small-object information and insufficient interaction between shallow and deep features. To address these limitations with minimal computational overhead, we propose an efficient detection framework for small object detection. Specifically, we introduce DCResNet to replace the traditional ResNet backbone, enabling more effective preservation of fine-grained details. In addition, a discriminative feature module (DFM) is designed to suppress background noise while enhancing shallow feature saliency via semantic–structural response modeling. Furthermore, we propose adaptive learning feature pyramid network (ALFPN) to strengthen cross-layer semantic correlation and multi-scale feature fusion through adaptive aggregation and joint spatial–channel enhancement. Experimental results on the NWPU VHR-10 and PASCAL VOC datasets demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art detectors, achieving a mean average precision (mAP) of 89.3% on NWPU VHR-10, and 79.2% on PASCAL VOC. Notably, these gains are obtained without a significant increase in neck-stage parameters, indicating that the proposed model maintains a favorable balance between detection accuracy and model complexity.

title: Curvilinear-Guided Wavelet Fusion for Bridge Damage Detection
authors: Jie Lin, Silu Huang, Qiang Xiao, Yubing Huang, Pan Zhou, Dongsheng Xu, Yue Qin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Bridge surface damage, such as cracks, spalling, corrosion, and seepage, often exhibits elongated geometry, weak texture, and strong background interference, which challenge conventional real-time detectors. This paper presents a bridge damage detection network that jointly improves geometric alignment, detail preservation, and global context modeling. A curvilinear-guided dynamic convolution mechanism guides sampling along damage-aware directions to better capture structured damage morphology. An orthogonal wavelet pyramid fusion strategy preserves high-frequency details while aggregating multi-scale semantic information. A dual-granularity attention design injects a lightweight global context into the fused features to suppress background noise and enhance discrimination. Together, these components form a unified detection framework for complex bridge scenes and enable accurate and efficient deployment.

title: Unlocking Grid-Independent Local Network: Optimised PV and EV-Based Storage with V2H for Local Energy Management in Western Australia
authors: Anindita Dey Troyee, Narottam Das, Amit Jyoti Datta
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates the optimal sizing of photovoltaic (PV) systems and battery energy storage systems (BESS) for residential grid-connected homes that incorporate electric vehicles (EVs) with vehicle-to-home (V2H) functionality. To reduce household electricity expenses, both particle swarm optimisation (PSO) and Bayesian optimisation (BO) techniques are considered for optimal sizing; however, the proposed framework primarily employs BO in conjunction with a custom rule-based Local Energy Management System (LEMS) due to its superior sample efficiency and convergence characteristics. The analysis accounts for uncertainties in EV availability by modelling stochastic variations in arrival/departure times and initial state of charge (SOC). Comprehensive simulations and sensitivity analyses explore the effects of V2H integration, daily export limitations, and dynamic electricity pricing. Results reveal that V2H-enabled systems can eliminate the need for a stationary battery while still achieving approximately 5% cost savings compared to setups that include PV, BESS, and EVs without V2H.

title: Near-field Wideband Frequency-Invariant FIR Filter Beamforming With Shallow Residual Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Wen Tao Li, Meng Fei Zhang, Xiao Yan Ju, Yong Qiang Hei, Xiao Wei Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The near-field antenna array synthesis problem has been known as a challenging issue because of variant wavefront curvature, which therefore attracts considerable interests to explore efficient synthesis techniques. In this paper, near-field wideband frequency-invariant (FI) beampattern synthesis is investigated, for which an efficient shallow residual convolutional neural network (CNN) approach is proposed. By formulating the beamforming task as a filter coefficient optimization problem, a set of initialized finite-impulse-response (FIR) filter coefficients is obtained to establish feasible starting solutions, which are then taken as unlabeled inputs to the network. The CNN architecture is adopted for its powerful capability in learning complex nonlinear mappings and processing high-dimensional structured data, which is effective in addressing the coupling between frequency, angle, and range in near-field scenarios, while residual connections are incorporated in shallow layers to alleviate gradient vanishing and preserve computational efficiency. The network takes complex-valued filter coefficients as dual-channel inputs, and then optimizes them via a specialized composite loss function that simultaneously enforces frequency-invariance in both angle and distance dimensions, ensures precise near-field focusing, and suppresses sidelobe levels to achieve comprehensive performance optimization. Simulation results of desired near-field wideband patterns for numerical geometric array configurations are provided to verify the effectiveness and generality of the proposed approach.

title: A Systematic Literature Review of Distributed Multi-Agent Task Allocation: Core Dimensions, Their Interrelationships, and a Repository
authors: Zitian Yang, Li Zhang, Hang Yu, Jiaxing Li, Xiaoli Lian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: As a critical and challenging research area within multi-agent systems (MAS), distributed multi-agent task allocation (D-MATA) has motivated extensive study and application across diverse domains. Although several systematic reviews exist on multi-agent task allocation (MATA), none provide an in-depth, systematic analysis of the core dimensions of D-MATA, namely applications, problems, methods, and metrics, nor explore their interrelationships. Moreover, no comprehensive repository capturing such foundational knowledge is currently available. These gaps collectively hinder the effective learning, adoption, and further development of D-MATA knowledge. To fill this gap, we conduct a systematic literature review (SLR) of 107 D-MATA studies, examining them from the perspectives of these core dimensions and discussing the potential interrelationships among these dimensions. To better support comprehensive evaluation and cross-study comparison of D-MATA methods, we propose a multi-dimensional evaluation framework based on the metrics used in the selected literature. Additionally, we provide an open knowledge repository comprising 107 problem-method-evaluation entries derived from the selected literature, supporting reproducible in-depth research. This work delivers clear guidance for both researchers and practitioners while building a systematic knowledge foundation for future investigations in the field.

title: Optimization Methods for Neuro-Fuzzy Systems: A Comprehensive Review of Financial Applications
authors: Xuefang Li, Asefeh Asemi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: This article provides a comprehensive review of the latest advancements in neuro-fuzzy systems (NFS) in the financial sector. Following the PRISMA framework, we systematically analyzed 82 studies published between 2015 and 2024. Neuro-fuzzy system approaches are categorized into three primary optimization paradigms: gradient-based, metaheuristic-based, and hybrid methods. Concurrently, financial applications are grouped into five domains: stock market prediction, risk assessment, investment decision-making, cryptocurrency studies, and fraud detection. Through content analysis, we map optimization strategies to application domains and analyze their performance characteristics. This study proposes practical guidelines for selecting suitable NFS optimization methods in different financial contexts and identifies future research directions. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first systematic review that explicitly evaluates the suitability of distinct neuro-fuzzy optimization paradigms for specific financial tasks.

title: Intelligent Decision Support System for Preliminary Assessment of Dermatological Manifestations
authors: G Megala, M Blessi, R Venkatesan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Skin diseases are the most common health problem worldwide, affecting over 1.9 billion people at any given time, but access to dermatological care is limited especially in rural and low resource areas. This research work presents an AI based tool to assist in the preliminary diagnosis of skin conditions using deep learning and image classification. The model uses advanced preprocessing, data augmentation and optimization techniques to give accurate and interpretable results. Besides automated image analysis, the tool has a clinical decision support system that provides users with home remedies and important precautions. The proposed system employs an EfficientNet-B0 based deep learning model trained on the HAM10000 dataset, demonstrating improved classification performance for dermatological image analysis. In addition, the system integrates a clinical decision support module that provides precautionary recommendations while encouraging users to seek professional medical consultation for accurate diagnosis and treatment. Overall, the proposed system shows good classification performance and is a scalable, affordable, and user-friendly AI solution for early skin disease detection and better patient care.

title: Remote State Estimation Over a Wearing Channel: Information Freshness Vs. Channel Aging
authors: Jiping Luo, George Stamatakis, Osvaldo Simeone, Nikolaos Pappas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: We study the remote estimation of a linear Gaussian system over a channel that wears out over time and with every use. The sensor can either transmit a fresh measurement in the current time slot, restore the channel quality at the cost of downtime, or remain silent. Frequent transmissions yield accurate estimates but incur significant wear on the channel. Renewing the channel too often improves channel conditions but results in poor estimation quality. What is the optimal timing to transmit measurements and restore the channel? This problem is formulated as a semi-Markov decision process (SMDP). We establish monotonicity properties of the optimal policy and propose structure-aware solution methods.

title: Target-Seeking and Threshold-Based Supervisory Control of Fuzzy Discrete Event Systems for Partial Objective Fulfillment
authors: Feng Lin, Hao Ying
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Fuzzy Discrete Event Systems (FDES) extend traditional Discrete Event Systems (DES) by incorporating fuzzy logic to handle uncertainties and vagueness in system states and events. While supervisory control of FDES has been studied before, existing approaches often assume crisp distinctions between safe and unsafe states and do not consider target states. In this paper, we substantially extend the existing results in the literature by developing three new frameworks of supervisory control. First, we introduce a threshold-based safe-state supervisory control framework where fuzzy states are classified as partially safe or unsafe based on a user-defined threshold. Second, we propose a target-seeking supervisory control framework. The framework allows supervisors to select control actions that ensure the supervised system can always reach some target fuzzy states. Third, we propose a threshold-based target-seeking supervisory control framework, where target fuzzy states are subject to user-specified thresholds. For all these frameworks, we derive necessary and sufficient conditions for existence of the supervisors and develop algorithms for calculating them online. The proposed methods enhance the flexibility and applicability of FDES supervisory control, allowing complete or partial fulfillment of control targets while ensuring threshold-based safety and target seeking. This is particularly important in real-world scenarios such as medical treatment planning, where the goal is to maximize treatment effectiveness while minimizing adverse side effects.

title: Challenges in Building and Visualizing Isolines on Tile-Based Data Quickly and Accurately
authors: Uzochukwu Felix Onyekwelu, Ramon Antonio Rodriges Zalipynis
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Modern web-based geospatial applications increasingly demand client-side processing of large scientific datasets. Web Tensor DBMSs (WT-DBMS) such as WebArrayDB enable browsers to receive and process raw tensor data through tile-based delivery mechanisms (such as WMTS with GeoTIFF tiles), shifting computational load from servers to clients. However, generating isolines from tile-based data presents fundamental challenges in maintaining topological consistency and geometric accuracy. This paper explores these challenges in the context of ERA5 climate reanalysis data, examining how traditional isoline construction algorithms must be adapted for incremental tile-by-tile processing. We describe the original CONREC-based algorithm that operates on complete grids, analyze why it cannot be directly applied to streaming tile data, and investigate four distinct approaches to tile-based construction: LineString-based, Polygon-based, Strip-based, and Buffer-based methods. Through systematic experimentation on ERA5 mean sea level pressure data (181×360 grid processed as 64×64 tiles), we demonstrate that each approach exhibits distinct trade-offs between boundary continuity, geometric accuracy, and visual artifact generation. Our analysis reveals that achieving both topological consistency and geometric fidelity in tile-based isoline generation remains an open challenge requiring further research.

title: Impact of Sensory Haptic Vibrotactile Feedback on Manual Dexterity in Augmented Reality
authors: Sarah Bonnet, Iuliia Zhurakovskaia, Heidy Daumas, Rochelle Ackerley
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: The use of immersive environments is increasing, especially in rehabilitation and other areas, like sports and gaming. Tasks performed in virtual/augmented reality (VR/AR) often have poorer motor performance, in part due to reduced tactile and proprioceptive inputs. We investigated whether vibrotactile haptic feedback could enhance motor performance within AR. We used the Box and Block Test (BBT) to investigate manual dexterity, where participants moved cubes over a partition using one hand. Participants performed the task in 8 conditions: Real, AR without feedback, and AR with haptic wristband or ring vibrotactile feedback using different vibration patterns (Impulse, Continuous, Hybrid). Movement features were extracted and machine learning was used to classify the experimental conditions. Results revealed that performance was consistently lower in the AR environment compared to the physical BBT. The conditions with the haptic wristband feedback gave similar performance, compared to the AR condition without haptic feedback, in terms of number of cubes moved and successful grasp rate. Conversely, conditions using the ring showed lower performance compared to AR conditions without haptics and the wristband conditions. Motion analysis of the movement trajectory revealed that using vibrotactile devices, particularly the ring, produced slower and shorter movements, as compared to AR alone. A Gradient Boosting machine learning model did not distinguish between all conditions, showing only some accuracy between AR, wristband, and ring conditions. Our study highlights the challenges of integrating haptic sensory enhancements in AR training environments, advocating a nuanced approach to developing haptic feedback systems that complement AR technologies more effectively.

title: AutoLegal: Toward Universal Traffic Compliance for Autonomous Vehicles Through Knowledge-Enhanced Language Models and Signal Temporal Logic
authors: Shunran Zhang, Huiyun Li, Zhengmin Jiang, Ming Sang, Jindai Liu, Leong Hou U
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Compliance verification of autonomous vehicle behavior represents a fundamental requirement for transportation system deployment. While human drivers handle natural language ambiguities intuitively, autonomous vehicles require precise formal specifications to eliminate linguistic uncertainties and ensure compliance. However, global traffic regulations exhibit substantial variations, creating significant challenges for formal specification languages in representing diverse natural language regulations across jurisdictions. Current approaches face critical limitations: manual formalization methods are labor-intensive and lack cross-regional adaptability, while existing Large Language Model (LLM)-based translation methods suffer from limited representability of traffic concept semantics and generate incorrect representations of temporal constraints and logical relationships due to insufficient reasoning accuracy. This paper presents AutoLegal, a novel translation framework leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) for systematic traffic regulation formalization. The framework integrates semantic foundations with knowledge graphs to provide LLMs with semantic anchors that ensure representability guarantees and atomic predicate consistency. To address logical reasoning demands, the framework incorporates explicit tree-structured logical expressions within Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, transforming implicit reasoning into interpretable and verifiable processes for enhanced temporal-logical relationship handling. A dual verification mechanism combining syntax validation and semantic back-translation validation with iterative refinement ensures final output quality and system robustness. Experimental results validate AutoLegal’s capability for translating traffic regulations into Signal Temporal Logic, achieving a translation success rate of 73.7% that surpasses other LLM-based methods, while the coverage of rule representations exceeds manual translation. Analysis of atomic predicate semantic representation performance and large language model hallucination mitigation capabilities further explains AutoLegal’s superior performance. The generated STL specifications demonstrate verified monitorability and semantic accuracy in real-world scenarios, thereby facilitating the advancement of intelligent transportation systems.

title: Fixed-time-stable ODE Representation of Lasso
authors: Liang Wu, Yunhong Che, Wallace Gian Yion Tanb, Efstathios Iliakis, Richard D. Braatz, Ján Drgona
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: Lasso problems arise in many areas, including signal processing, machine learning, and control, and are closely connected to sparse coding mechanisms observed in neuroscience. A continuous-time ordinary differential equation (ODE) representation of the Lasso problem not only enables its solution on analog computers but also provides a framework for interpreting neurophysiological phenomena. This article proposes a fixed-time-stable ODE representation of the Lasso problem by first transforming it into a smooth nonnegative quadratic program (QP) and then designing a projection-free Newton-based fixed-time-stable ODE system for solving the corresponding Karush–Kuhn–Tucker (KKT) conditions. Moreover, the settling time of the ODE is independent of the problem data and can be arbitrarily prescribed. Numerical experiments verify that the trajectory reaches the optimal solution within the prescribed time.

title: Maximally recoverable codes with locality and availability
authors: Umberto Martínez-Peñas, V. Lalitha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: In this work, we introduce maximally recoverable codes with locality and availability. We consider locally repairable codes (LRCs) where certain subsets of t symbols belong each to N local repair sets, which are pairwise disjoint after removing the t symbols, and which are of size r + δ − 1 and can correct δ −1 erasures locally. Classical LRCs with N disjoint repair sets and LRCs with N-availability are recovered when setting t = 1 and t = δ − 1 = 1, respectively. Allowing t > 1 enables our codes to reduce the storage overhead for the same locality and availability. In this setting, we define maximally recoverable LRCs (MR-LRCs) as those that can correct any globally correctable erasure pattern given the locality and availability constraints. We then identify a large class of global erasure patterns that can be corrected by such MR-LRCs and prove that they are all the correctable patterns when t = 1. We provide three explicit constructions of LRCs that can correct such erasure patterns (thus MR-LRCs for t = 1), based on MSRD codes, each attaining the smallest finite-field sizes for some parameter regime. Finally, we extend the known lower bound on finite-field sizes from classical MR-LRCs to our setting (for any value of t).

title: An IoT-based Low-power Wide-range LoRaWAN Network for Alternate Wetting and Drying irrigation method in Rice field
authors: Md. Zakir Hossen, Debjit Roy, M. A. Rob, Priya Lal Chandra Paul, Fazlul Hasan Siddiqui
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: IoT is a global trend, and in Bangladesh, almost every sector is gradually adopting IoT technology. Industrial IoT is growing very fast, but the agricultural sector in Bangladesh farmers still mostly depends on traditional machinery and irrigation systems. Although smart and IoT-based methods are being introduced, their use in agriculture is still limited. In Bangladesh, agricultural IoT-enabled technique automation mainly follows two approaches: cellular networks using mobile SIM cards and Wi-Fi networks. Cellular-based systems require one SIM card with an internet package for each field unit, which becomes impractical when a farmer installs more than ten units due to SIM registration limits. Wi-Fi systems overcome this problem, but it requires dedicated infrastructure and LAN cabling, which can be hampered during normal farming activities. To address these issues, the current study aimed to develop an energy efficent IoT-enabled alternate wetting and drying (AWD) irrigation system suitable for rice fields. The system was optimized and tested at Bangladesh Rice Research Institute (BRRI) research field. It used a 915 MHz microwave frequency LoRaWAN network to communicate sensor data from field units to a gateway and vice versa, which transmitted the data for real-time monitoring of field irrigation status, irrigation amount in each field, and irrigation scheduling for different field plots through smart irrigation software. For specific agricultural fields requiring irrigation, the motor must be turned ON to ensure proper irrigation, which requires a continuous 220 V AC supply when using a solenoid valve. As an alternative solution, a gate valve was designed that was approximately 96.5 times more energy-efficient than the solenoid valve and could be operated using only a 12 V DC power supply. The proposed system achieved a significant improvement over traditional irrigation practices, saving 37.29% of water and 37.06% of energy, while also achieving water and energy savings of 15.71% and 8.85%, respectively, compared to manual AWD. This system will ensure safety, low costing, energy efficiency, and economic feasibility for farmers.

title: An Efficient NTRU-Type MPHE Scheme for Sensitive Data in Cloud Computing
authors: Jianxin Gao, Ying Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: The development of cloud computing has heightened demand for sensitive data privacy. Multi party homomorphic encryption (MPHE) enables ho momorphicevaluation on encrypted data from multiple users, providing a crucial solution for secure multi-user computation in cloud computing models. NTRU-type MPHEschemes are particularly promising due to their simplicity and efficiency; however, existing solutions face three key limitations: a lack of threshold decryption (t-out-of-N), reliance on trusted third parties, and vulnerability to subfield lattice attacks. In this paper, we propose GTS, a novel NTRU-type MPHE scheme. Compared to existing schemes, GTS offers several advantages. On the one hand, we design a t-out-of-N threshold access structure, allowing GTS to facilitate threshold decryption with predefined participants. On the other hand, we combine distributed key generation and Lagrange interpolation, enabling GTS to operate without relying on trusted third parties and enhancing its resistance to subfield lattice attacks. These features effectively address the aforementioned challenges. We provide the correctness and security proofs for GTS, along with comprehensive analyses of computational and communication overheads. Experimental evaluations demonstrate significant efficiency improvements: compared to state-of-the-art methods, GTS achieves total execution time speedups of up to 2.4× and 3.7× at 128-bit security with N = 20 parties.

title: Optimal ISAC Beamforming Structure and Efficient Algorithms for Sum Rate and CRLB Balancing
authors: Tianyu Fang, Mengyuan Ma, Markku Juntti, Nir Shlezinger, A. Lee Swindlehurst, Nhan Thanh Nguyen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) has emerged as a promising paradigm to unify wireless communications and radar sensing, enabling efficient spectrum and hardware utilization. A core challenge for realizing the gains of ISAC stems from dual-functional beamforming design where the key performance metrics, such as communications sum rate and sensing Cramér–Rao lower bound (CRLB), present highly non-convex formulations. In this paper, we propose a low-complexity structured approach to ISAC beamforming optimization to simultaneously enhance spectral efficiency and sensing accuracy. Specifically, we develop a successive convex approximation (SCA)-based algorithm that transforms the original non-convex problem into a sequence of convex subproblems ensuring convergence to a stationary point. Furthermore, leveraging the proposed SCA framework and Lagrange duality, we derive the optimal beamforming structure for CRLB minimization in ISAC systems. Our analysis reveals the maximum reduction in radar streams without affecting the sensing performance. This finding enables a dimensionality reduction that enhances computational efficiency. Numerical simulations validate that our approach achieves comparable or superior performance to the considered benchmarks while requiring significantly lower computational complexity.

title: Duration-Aware Part-Attention for Robust Tool Condition Monitoring with Missing Data
authors: Qinge Xiao, Yuntao Gu, Weixuan Chen, Zhile Yang, Xiaoou Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Sensor-equipped tool condition monitoring (TCM) is crucial for automated machining, but missing data poses a significant challenge. Existing methods struggle with the complex patterns and substantial data loss common in these dynamic processes. This paper introduces a novel duration-aware part attention mechanism for robust TCM. Unlike existing attention mechanisms, ours explicitly models time-duration dependencies within sensor signals, capturing multi-scale representations of tool degradation even with incomplete data. The part-attention operator, adapted from the Swin Transformer, can dynamically weight different time segments based on their duration and relevance. We further incorporate a cross-dimensional self-attention mechanism to fuse information across multiple sensors and time steps, capturing complex relationships indicative of tool wear. We evaluate our method on real-world machining datasets with varying levels of missing data, demonstrating its superior ability to accurately monitor tool condition compared to existing methods. The results show that the duration-aware part-attention effectively captures crucial temporal dependencies, leading to robust TCM even with substantial data loss.

title: Construction method of multi disaster command and dispatch intelligence system based on knowledge graph and large model
authors: Shufeng Yang, Yanliang Guo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Addressing the issues of low accuracy and lack of practical relevance in the generation of emergency command intelligence for multiple disasters, this paper constructs a knowledge graph for multi-disaster command and dispatch, proposes a time-series-decision dual-weight adaptive retrieval enhancement mechanism, and integrates the large Qwen3-32B model to build an emergency command intelligence system. Ablation experiments based on a test dataset of 400 samples show that this method outperforms the original Qwen3-32B in terms of BERTScore and expert scoring, generating command intelligence that is more aligned with practical scenarios.

title: In Memoriam: Michael O. Rabin
authors: Eugene H. Spafford, Simson Garfinkel
publish: Communications of the ACM
abstract: None

title: Dynamic Graph Transformer with Adaptive Attention for Multi-UAV Path Planning in Uncertain Environments
authors: Yongqiang Tan, Qiang Fu, Yuhuan Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In complex dynamic environments, multi-UAV collaboration faces challenges in path planning and task allocation efficiency due to spatiotemporal interactions and uncertainties. This paper proposes a dynamic attention collaboration mechanism based on Graph Transformer for multi-UAV path planning. It encodes UAV swarm states via Graph Attention Network to extract relational features and processes task sequences with Transformer for global semantics. A dynamic fusion layer uses task queries to adjust feature weights, overcoming static mechanisms’ generalization limits. Joint learning employs multilayer perceptrons and attention pooling, outputting optimized paths via policy heads. Training integrates Proximal Policy Optimization with reward shaping for stability and robustness, enhancing efficiency and completion rates. Simulations on multi-UAV datasets show superiority over baselines like GAT, PPO, and Transformer in path lengths, rewards, and adaptability.

title: Long-Term Few-Shot Class-Incremental SEI via Decoupled Orthogonal Representation and Prototype Calibration
authors: Zengwei Wang, Jian Chen, Jie Jia, Xingwei Wang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) is important for specific emitter identification (SEI) in dynamic electromagnetic environments. However, in long-term deployments, traditional SEI methods experience catastrophic forgetting and prototype bias due to the scarcity of samples for novel emitters. In this letter, we propose a long-term FSCIL-SEI framework based on orthogonal-constrained representation learning and global-contextual prototype calibration. We prestructure a decoupled feature space by employing a predefined orthogonal codebook and covariance regularization during base training, reserving geometric margins for incremental categories. To address the statistical shift in initial prototypes in few-shot scenarios, a Transformer-based calibrator exploits global topological correlations among hardware fingerprints via meta-learning. Experimental results on the 136-emitter WiSig dataset yield an average accuracy of 96.58% and a final accuracy of 95.18% across 70 incremental sessions. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of improved long-term incremental stability over existing baselines.

title: A Dynamical Framework that Integrates Optical and SAR Imagery for Sugarcane Monitoring in the Cauca Valley
authors: Alejandro Mestre-Quereda, Tomas Martinez-Marin, Juan M. Lopez-Sanchez, Carlos Mosquera, Henk Pelgrum, Antonia Skarli, Dirk Hoekman, Corné van der Sande
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In this work, we present a novel dynamical framework for crop monitoring that integrates SAR and optical satellite imagery. The proposed methodology employs two-dimensional state transitions to explicitly model the expected temporal evolution of crops. By combining such crop modeling with multi-sensor observations, including dual-polarimetric (VV and VH) radar images gathered by Sentinel-1 and optical imagery from Sentinel-2, the developed framework produces robust and consistent estimations using the well-established Kalman Filter. Specifically, the approach is applied to retrieve the biomass values of sugarcane crops located in the Cauca Valley (Colombia), over a full growing season (from summer 2024 to summer 2025). An intensive field campaign was carried out, so that ground-truth measurements in a large number of points, located in multiple fields, were available to develop and validate the proposed integration algorithm. Results show that biomass is accurately estimated especially when all available observations, both SAR and optical, are employed. In this case, a global root mean square error of 0.103 kg, a mean absolute error of 0.064 kg and an accumulated biomass difference of 13.8 kg<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot$</tex-math></inline-formula>day are obtained after evaluating the model multiple times on randomly-selected points. The analysis also highlights a key limitation of optical imagery in the study area, as a large number of images are affected by cloud cover, resulting in long gaps within the time series and reduced estimation accuracy. Furthermore, the best performance of the proposed integrated model is achieved when all available SAR products, including dual-polarization (VV and VH) coherence and intensity products, are exploited, although very good results are also obtained using single-polarization coherence and intensity data (either at VV or VH polarizations). Comparison with alternative approaches, including partially integrated methods using only one type of information (either SAR or NDVI), and methods based solely on machine learning, indicates that the proposed methodology consistently offers superior accuracy in biomass estimation.

title: Exp-ToolPlan: Contrastive Experience Learning for Similar Tool Selection
authors: Yuhua Cheng, Mengyao Yan, Zheng Wan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Tool learning has become a key paradigm for empowering large language models (LLMs) to interact with the external world and accomplish complex real-world tasks. However, LLMs often suffer from tool confusion when facing similar tools, leading to task failure. To address this issue, this paper draws inspiration from human cognitive learning and proposes Exp-ToolPlan, that enhances similar tool selection by leveraging trial-and-error experience. Exp-ToolPlan consists of two core collaborative modules: the Contrastive Trial Module, which automatically generates confusing queries for similar tool pairs and collects execution experience, and the Experience Learning Module, which aggregates fragmented trial-and-error logs and extracts explicit differentiation rules. Extensive experiments conducted on the RestBench dataset demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Specifically, on the GPT-4o backbone, our method boosts the task success rate to 75.00% and correct rate to 85.00% on RestBench-TMDB.

title: Partitioning Areal Density Gain of Shingled Magnetic Recording
authors: Xuan Zheng, Chris Rea, Jason Gadbois
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Shingled magnetic recording provides areal density gain over conventional magnetic recording but the realized gain has been smaller than projection. To assess the limiting factors, spin stand tests have been developed to partition the difference between the two recording format into components of adjacent track erasure and side reading interference. The results show that the gain is 13% and 16% for two different heat-assisted magnetic recording head designs, dominated by the reduction in adjacent track erasure, which originates from reducing the number of adjacent track writes and removing one adjacent track to the background. This adjacent track erasure reduction is less than 1 dB, which translates to limited areal density gain. The gain is further penalized by the worse side reading interference for shingled magnetic recording. Techniques to cancel side reading interference will elevate the areal density of shingle magnetic recording more than that of conventional magnetic recording, increasing the areal density gain.

title: Design and Implementation of a Fault Tolerant Self-Healing Multi Train Reservation System
authors: Sashanth V, S Shrinidhi, Nivedha N, Arijit Bardhan Roy, S.S. Gayathri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This work presents the design and implementation of a self-healing multi train reservation system developed using Verilog HDL and verified completely within the Xilinx Vivado environment. The aim of the project is to achieve autonomous fault detection, prediction and recovery in hardware-based systems. The study is based on the system reliability degradation caused by transient and operational faults. which can cause problems to real-time services which include reservation and booking systems. To overcome these issues, a layered architecture was implemented comprising a core reservation module for seat allocation and fare computation, and a self- healing control layer integrating a Fault Detection Unit (FDU), a Predictive Monitoring Unit (PMU), and a Self-Healing Controller (SHC). A dedicated Verilog-based logging module was also developed to record system behavior into a structured CSV file, enabling cycle-level event tracking and quantitative performance analysis. The system was simulated and verified using Vivado’s simulator under various testcases such as normal booking operations, fault injection scenarios and repeated fault conditions. Hardware synthesis and implementation was performed in Vivado which depicted efficiency with 4.05%LUT, 1.11%registers and total on chip power of 0.063W while the timing analysis showed zero failing endpoints and positive slack confirming real time performance. The system successfully restores its operational stability within few cycles and this framework provides a scalable and hardware efficient approach for embedding autonomous recovery mechanisms in digital systems, enhance fault tolerance and data traceability in safety critical applications.

title: A Review of Integrating AI-driven Optimization Methods with Internet of Things for Improving System Performance: Research Challenges and Future Directions
authors: M. Ranjith Kumar, R. Sekar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) has introduced significant challenges, including high energy consumption, security vulnerabilities, and network congestion, which conventional rule-based systems struggle to manage. This study identifies a critical need for Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven optimization to handle massive data volumes from heterogeneous devices and improve overall system reliability. The research employs a comprehensive review approach, categorizing existing literature into four primary AI-driven paradigms: machine learning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and heuristic algorithms. Analysis of these methods demonstrates how specific performance issues such as routing, resource allocation, and intrusion detection are addressed. Key outcomes include the identification of critical performance measures, such as accuracy, throughput, and energy usage, alongside a discussion of persistent challenges like computational overhead and data privacy. Ultimately, the manuscript provides a roadmap for future research, suggesting that hybrid mechanisms and lightweight edge-computing models are essential for developing the next generation of scalable, secure, and energy-efficient smart systems.

title: Generalized H matrix stability for power systems
authors: Elinor Ginzburg-Ganz, Yoash Levron, Juri Belikov, George Weiss
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The small-signal stability of a power system model with arbitrary loads is investigated, with a focus on locally constant power loads in lossless networks. A central element of the proposed analysis is the matrix H, which represents the partial derivatives of generator powers with respect to their relative angles. The local stability of any equilibrium point is determined by the properties of H if the loads consume locally constant power. Specifically, an equilibrium point is asymptotically stable if H is positive definite and unstable if H is full-rank and not positive definite, while several quantitative relationships between the eigenvalues of H and the system Jacobian matrix are also established. The main contribution is that loads are allowed to be arbitrarily located within the transmission network, thereby generalizing the existing H matrix stability criterion which is valid only if loads are located at the same buses as the generators. The advantage of the proposed criterion is demonstrated through case studies involving systems with up to 2869 buses. Accurate stability assessments are maintained, where alternative methods, such as the NRPS model, are found to be overly conservative. Additionally, a computational acceleration by a factor of 340 relative to Jacobian-based methods is achieved.

title: A Systematic Review of Deep Learning Approaches in Multimodal Emotion Recognition
authors: Yiwei Su, Fadhilah Rosdi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence, multimodal emotion recognition (MER) has become an important research area. Its main goal is to enable machines to understand and interpret human emotional states by integrating audio, text, and visual signals. This systematic review follows PRISMA guidelines and analyzes 62 high-quality studies published between 2020 and 2025, providing a comprehensive overview of deep learning-based MER methods. The results indicate that the audio modality is the most frequently utilized, appearing in all reviewed studies, whereas text and visual features are commonly employed alongside it to provide semantic and nonverbal information that together form the foundation for multimodal emotion interaction. Methodologically, the field is moving toward an end-to-end deep learning paradigm. Transformer models are often used to extract text features, whereas CNNs remain the primary choice for visual features. When combining different modalities, intermediate fusion methods with attention mechanisms are commonly employed. These methods help capture interactions between modalities more effectively. The IEMOCAP, CMU-MOSEI, and MELD datasets are widely used as standard benchmarks in MER studies. Even with these developments, MER research faces several challenges. Models can be computationally demanding, aligning different types of data remains difficult, and most systems do not generalize well across languages. This review summarizes the main strengths and weaknesses of current methods. It also provides guidance for researchers to address key technical issues, improve model designs, and make MER systems more practical and adaptable to real-world scenarios.

title: A Multi-modal Imbalance-Aware Method for Microservice Anomaly Detection
authors: Yumiao Tao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Microservice architectures are widely used in cloud systems, making effective anomaly detection essential for reliable system operation. However, anomaly detection in microservices remains challenging due to complex service dependencies and the strong imbalance between normal and anomalous data. Existing graph-based methods usually focus on modeling dependency structures, but often fail to fully exploit complementary information from different data modalities and suffer from biased training and unstable decision thresholds. This paper proposes an enhanced microservice anomaly detection framework named ATMIFD that jointly considers dual-modal data and robust learning strategies.
