The proposed method integrates trace-level and metric-level information to construct richer service representations, enabling the model to capture both dependency patterns and runtime behaviors. To further improve detection performance under imbalanced data, a class-balanced training loss is adopted to reduce the dominance of normal samples. In addition, a validation-based threshold selection strategy is introduced to produce more stable anomaly decisions. Experiments on the MSDS dataset demonstrate that ATMIFD achieves an F1-score of 0.757 and it is proved to be an effective approach for detecting anomalies in microservice settings.

title: Review for "A Random Forest-based Approach for Estimating Full-band Solar-induced Chlorophyll Fluorescence from Photosystem II in Crops"
authors: Linsheng Wu
publish: N/A

title: DeepEye-SQL: A Software-Engineering-Inspired Text-to-SQL Framework
authors: Boyan Li, Chong Chen, Zhujun Xue, Yinan Mei, Yuyu Luo
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Mathematical Foundations of Poisoning Attacks on Linear Regression over Cumulative Distribution Functions
authors: Atsuki Sato, Martin Aumüller, Yusuke Matsui
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Traffic Congestion Level Prediction using Temporal, Weather, and Event based Features
authors: B.Venkata Charan Kumar, N.Venkatesh Naik, Sankala Jaswanth, Kodiganti Tharun Kumar, Shaik Nabi Rasool, Vankam Kumaraswamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Traffic congestion is a critical issue in rapidly growing urban environments, leading to increased travel time, fuel consumption, and reduced quality of life. A multi-stream traffic prediction system proposed in this paper will incorporate different forms of data such as temporal, weather, and event-related information to classify congestion conditions into three categories: MEDIUM, LOW and HIGH. Several machine learning algorithms, including Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost, Logistic Regression are implemented to analyze congestion patterns. To enhance model robustness, the data has been preprocessed using techniques such as categorical encoding, and class balancing, feature normalization before conducting an experimental evaluation based on a structured synthetic dataset that represents the hourly traffic conditions of Bangalore, India. Performance is assessed using F1-score, precision, recall, and accuracy metrics. The findings demonstrate that ensemble models, particularly XGBoost, and Random Forest provide the most reliable predictions, achieving nearly 99% accuracy when classified. The findings confirms the advantage of integrating multiple contextual features for traffic prediction. The proposed approach can support intelligent transportation systems and enable urban planners in improving traffic control and decision-making processes in smart city environments.

title: Adaptive Reinforcment based Edge Federated Learning Framework for Energy-Efficient Smart Buildings
authors: P. Raja, Vankayala Chethan Prakash, Niranjan P G, Vetrivel M, Vijayaganapathi R, Gunal P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Human comfort in smart buildings is primarily achieved through heating, ventilation, and air-conditioning (HVAC) systems, which account for a substantial share of overall building energy consumption. An Edge AI–based federated reinforcement Q-learning framework is presented for energy-efficient HVAC control in smart buildings. In the Reinforcement Based Edge Federated (RBEF) learning approach, real-time sensor data, including temperature, humidity, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentration, window or door status check and occupancy, are processed locally on resource constrained edge devices deployed in individual buildings. Each building trains a lightweight tabular Q-learning controller to learn adaptive HVAC control policies based on local indoor conditions. A federated learning mechanism based on Federated Averaging (FedAvg) is employed to periodically aggregate local Q-tables across multiple buildings, enabling collaborative learning while preserving data locality and reducing communication overhead. The learned global model is redistributed to edge nodes to improve control performance across heterogeneous buildings. Experimental results demonstrate improved energy efficiency and occupant comfort compared to conventional rule-based control, highlighting the effectiveness and practicality of RBEF learning for smart building energy management.

title: A Generative AI Framework for Privacy-Aware Audience Perception Analysis: Leveraging Large Language Models for Comment Understanding and Content Profiling
authors: QiMing Liu, JiaYi Wu, RuoLei Deng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Generative artificial intelligence has shown remarkable potential in natural language understanding, yet its application to user-generated content analysis raises important considerations regarding data privacy and content security. This paper proposes an end-to-end generative AI framework for privacy-aware audience perception analysis that processes user comments without requiring personally identifiable information. The framework leverages large language models (LLMs) across all analytical stages: temporal-context-enriched data collection, privacy-aware paraphrase augmentation, sentiment classification through prompt engineering, structured topic extraction, and audience profiling. A three-tier privacy design ensures data locality through anonymization, paraphrase-based text replacement, and support for on-premise open-weight model deployment. We collect anonymized comments and danmaku (with playback timestamps) from six Chinese documentaries on Bilibili spanning five categories. Three LLM variants (GPT-4o, Qwen-Max, DeepSeek-V3) are compared against strong supervised baselines (RoBERTa-wwm-ext, TextCNN, LDA) across four prompt strategies. GPT-4o with chain-of-thought prompting achieves 86.2% accuracy and 0.848 macro-F1, outperforming RoBERTa-wwm-ext by 4.9 percentage points. LLM-based topic extraction produces 61% higher coherence scores than LDA. Cost-benefit analysis reveals that open-weight DeepSeek-V3 achieves comparable accuracy at 18× lower cost than GPT-4o, offering a practical deployment path for production scenarios.

title: CNN Architectures for Osteosarcoma Detection: A Systematic Review and Comparative Performance Analysis
authors: R. Vijayarajeswari, M. Yuvarani, P.Menaka, G.Meena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Osteosarcoma detection remains clinically challenging despite CNNs achieving 98–99% laboratory accuracy. This systematic review reveals critical barriers: overfitting on single-center datasets; severe metric imbalance risking missed lesions, 3D/Transformer computational demands, absent multimodal integration, inadequate pediatric validation and unreported deployment metrics. Standardized multimodal platforms with federated learning and longitudinal tracking are essential for clinical adoption.

title: Beyond Sentiment: A Dual-Channel Approach to Emotion and Review Summarization for Recommendation
authors: Sungjoo Pyo, Suji Kim, Xinzhe Li, Jaekyeong Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid expansion of online services has necessitated effective recommender systems that provide information aligned with user preferences and requirements. In e-commerce, user reviews contain rich information and are extensively used as essential data for recommender systems. Many studies have integrated reviews into recommender systems by extracting positive and negative sentiments via sentiment analysis. However, reviews contain diverse user emotions that cannot be sufficiently captured by simple sentiment polarity. Moreover, focusing solely on emotional information may overlook the specific topics and semantic meanings within reviews. To address these limitations, this study proposes a dual-channel emotion- and semantic-aware recommendation (DCESR) model, which simultaneously considers both emotional and semantic information embedded in reviews. The proposed model uses pretrained language models to extract emotional features for users and items and to summarize reviews to capture their semantic features. These two types of information are then effectively integrated using a gate-based fusion mechanism to learn user and item representations for rating prediction. The experimental results obtained from Amazon.com review datasets demonstrate that the DCESR model consistently outperforms baseline models. This finding confirms that an approach that integrates both emotional and semantic information is highly effective in accurately reflecting user preferences.

title: Device-aware Optimization of Energy-efficient Deep Learning for Edge Deployment
authors: Guiling Xiang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Deep neural networks deliver strong accuracy but their energy and latency costs hinder sustainable deployment on resource constrained edge devices. We propose an energy aware framework that couples three complementary efficiency mechanisms: structured model compression, knowledge distillation, and mixed precision low bit quantization. Central to the approach is a differentiable, device specific energy surrogate learned from sparse on device profiling. The surrogate predicts latency and energy from architectural structure and precision choices, enabling end to end multi objective optimization that directly targets accuracy, latency, and energy rather than indirect proxies such as FLOPs.We validate the framework on Raspberry Pi and Jetson Nano across vision and audio tasks. On Raspberry Pi, our method achieves 79.7% top 1 on ImageNet 1k while reducing latency from 84 ms to 58 ms and energy from 505 mJ to 330 mJ compared with a strong post training quantization baseline, and it also improves over an on device NAS baseline at 64 ms and 380 mJ. On CIFAR 10 and Speech Commands, we obtain 95.7% at 14.2 ms and 78 mJ, and 95.1% at 4.3 ms and 19 mJ, respectively. These results indicate that differentiable hardware feedback can make compression and quantization decisions that translate into consistent, measurable energy savings without sacrificing accuracy.

title: Hybrid Thermal Network Optimization for Sensorless Temperature Monitoring of a PMSM Considering Speed-Dependent Cooling Effects Performable on Digital Signal Processors
authors: Thomas Krainer, Daniel Wöckinger, Gerd Bramerdorfer, Stephen Chukwulobe
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This study aims to accurately monitor crucial temperatures within an interior-rotor fractional-horsepower PMSM with an integrated cooling fan, with a focus on winding temperature. Therefore, a thermal model in the form of a lumpedparameter thermal network (LPTN) is identified by using a hybrid modeling approach in two steps. First, LPTN parameters are initialized with empirical and simplified analytic models. Special attention is given to speed-dependent thermal transitions between the motor components and their environment. These transitions are driven by convection, such as windings' cooling or bearings' heat transfer. Modeling these transitions is uniquely challenging due to a rotor-mounted cooling fan. Thus, a genetic global parameter-optimization algorithm compensates for uncertainties introduced by model simplifications. This method enables mean temperature estimation errors of less than 1 °C over specific driving cycles. Therefore, a large amount of measurement data is generated for this optimization process using a fully automated test bench, briefly discussed in this paper. Furthermore, necessary adaptations, i.e., the introduction of a power and torque observer, are presented to enable the implementation of the LPTN on a digital signal processor (DSP). Finally, the resulting thermal model is verified using independent measurement data.

title: Effect of Welding Methods and Electrode Types on Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of AISI 1045 Steel Joint
authors: Tuswan Tuswan, Gideon Morris Sostenes Siburian, Parlindung Manik, Untung Budiarto, Ahmad Firdhaus, Muhammad Luqman Hakim, Good Rindo, Zulfaidah Ariany
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: AISI 1045 medium carbon steel is extensively utilized in machinery and construction due to its strength and toughness. However, during welding, this steel is prone to microstructural changes in the heat-affected zone (HAZ), which can result in the formation of intermetallic compounds and a decline in mechanical properties. A study is necessary to examine the impact of welding methods and electrode types on the microstructure and mechanical properties of AISI 1045 steel. This research assesses three welding methods—SMAW, GTAW, and GMAW—and three electrode types—E7018, ER70S-6, and E308/ER308—on the mechanical properties and microstructural evolution of AISI 1045 steel. Mechanical property testing was conducted using tensile testing in accordance with ASTM E8M standards, while microstructure characterization employed Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) with sample preparation following ASTM E3 standards. The tensile testing revealed that GMAW welding with ER308 electrodes yielded the highest maximum tensile strength. E7018 and ER70S-6 demonstrated greater elongation at break, indicating superior ductility. SEM analysis corroborated these findings: E308/ER308 welds displayed coarse morphologies, intergranular microcracks, and regions enriched with $\mathrm{Fe}-\mathrm{Cr}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}-\mathrm{Ni}$, correlating with increased hardness but reduced toughness. In contrast, E7018 and ER70S-6 welds exhibited finer, more uniform microstructures associated with enhanced plasticity. This study suggests that electrode selection and thermal parameter control are crucial for optimizing the balance between strength and ductility in medium-carbon steel welded joints, with ER308 recommended for applications prioritizing strength and E7018/ER70S-6 for components requiring high toughness.

title: Hybrid Intrusion Detection System Integrating Decision Tree and Convolutional Neural Network with Adaptive Dynamic Loss Balancing
authors: Ni Jin, Jiachun Zheng, Zhiping Xu, Kunsan Zhang, Renguang Zheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Addressing the issues of low dynamic environmental adaptability and interpretability in intrusion detection for IoT scenarios, a hybrid intrusion detection system (IDS) model combining convolutional neural networks (CNN) and decision tree (DT) is proposed. Firstly, we design a Class-Number-Associated Attention (CNAA) module to strengthen the extraction of critical features. Secondly, an Actor-Critic network is used to dynamically adjust the weights in mixed pooling layer, and add a Dynamic Loss Balancing module to support adaptive learning of traffic features. Furthermore, attack labels are rebuilt based on severity to achieve a hierarchical classification of attacks. Finally, DT improves model interpretability and transparency. Experiments results on UNSW-NB15 show that the proposed model performs better across various metrics. This model provides security administrators with a clear and fast basis for making decisions.

title: GAIIS 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)

title: Exploiting Formal Verification for the Systematic Discovery of Corner Cases in a Behavior Planner for Automated Driving
authors: Lukas König, Franziska Henze, Christian Heinzemann, Michaela Klauck, Alberto Griggio, Stefano Tonetta, Alessandro Cimatti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In automated driving, preventing hazardous driving decisions is both an extremely important and intricate task. Typical verification and validation methods include extensive simulation and real-world testing which is expensive and, yet, cannot rule out rare but potentially hazardous corner cases. Therefore, we present an approach to enhance the verification and validation process of automated driving by applying formal verification techniques for bug finding directly to the code of a tactical behavior planner in series development. We use an open source software framework in combination with the model checker nuXmv. Our framework involves fully automatically extracting the model from C++ code, integrating it with a traffic simulator, and conducting exhaustive analysis over a broad range of possible traffic scenes using model checking. If violations, e. g., crashes, are detected, traces of the surrounding traffic participants and ego are obtained, which can be simulated with the full system to aid in error identification, or utilized to generate corner cases for conventional testing. Since model checking is usually runtime-intensive, we analyze the effects of different discretization step sizes for time and space. Our results show that the toolchain identifies formerly unknown bugs in a real series development, even with surprisingly coarse discretizations. This demonstrates that model checking can improve the development process of industrial ADAS projects.

title: Toward AI-Empowered Wireless Position Sensing: An Integrated Framework of Perception, Cognition, Decision, and Learning
authors: Yue Zhao, Zan Li, Changyuan Zhao, Yiteng Zhang, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: The rapid development of 6G, Internet of Things, and low-altitude wireless networks has greatly increased the demand for accurate wireless position sensing. This paper focuses on passive position sensing which estimates signal source’s position without active transmission by solely analyzing received signals. We identify and summarize the key factors influencing position sensing accuracy across three dimensions including network configuration, parameter acquisition, and algorithmic processing, and highlight unresolved technical challenges. To systematically address these issues, we propose a novel framework named PCDL integrating four functional modules, Perception, Cognition, Decision, and Learning. We demonstrate effectiveness of the PCDL framework by a case study, validating its ability on improving the localization accuracy for the sources in the blind zone. The PCDL framework structures an integration of artificial intelligence into the position sensing workflow, guiding the design for the future intelligent wireless position sensing system.

title: Multiclass Sleep Disorder Prediction using Ensemble Learning with Integrated SHAP and Partial Dependence Analysis
authors: S. Vairachilai, Sarath Chandra Veerla, Balajee Maram, Markala Karthik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Sleep disorders including insomnia and sleep apnea are becoming more common and are highly dependent on lifestyle and physiological factors. Prediction models required in screening and decision support must be accurate and interpretable. In this work, a detailed machine learning and ensemble learning model was created to predict sleep disorder status by using Sleep Health and Lifestyle dataset. Support Vector Machine has the highest discrimination of classes, indicating a high ROC-AUC of 0.9926, and Logistic Regression and Decision Tree also showed the highest overall results with an accuracy of 0.9467. The predictive performance was also enhanced by ensemble learning techniques, and the XGBoost model was the best-performing model, with an accuracy of 0.9733 and high ROC-AUC of 0.9851. These findings confirm the benefit of multiclass sleep disorder prediction by ensemble methods. To improve the transparency of the models, the Explainable AI methods were implemented into the framework. The suggested framework is the combination of high predictive power and strong interpretability, which makes it applicable to effective risk assessment of sleep disorders and meaningful clinical decisions.

title: A Systematic Review of Brain Tumour Categorization and Severity Identification
authors: Pillareddy Vamsheedhar Reddy, Gayathri Tippani, V Maddiletti Reddy, Sabitha Valaboju, Anil Kumar, Kota Harini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Brain tumours result from the rapid and uncontrolled proliferation of cells and can be life-threatening if not diagnosed and treated promptly. Despite developments in diagnostic imaging and analysis, accurate classification of brain tumours Remains difficult to address because of the diverse structural and spatial characteristics of tumours. The survey main aim is to provide researchers with a systematic review of brain tumour classification and severity assessment using MRI. It outlines the methodology adopted for article selection, incorporating the PRISMA model, and discusses key aspects such as brain tumour anatomy, classification strategies, role of deep learning and transfer learning in tumour analysis. The review offers a detailed comparison of existing techniques and the datasets used for their evaluation. Additionally, it explores the strengths, limitations, recent advancements, and upcoming trends in brain tumour identification and categorization.

title: Tiny-Dataset Scale-Consistent Diffusion Framework for Single SAR Image Building Height Estimation_supp1-3693803.pdf
authors: Yulun Wu
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent learning-based methods for building height estimation from a single SAR image have shown promising performance. However, existing methods are constrained by limited model learning capacity and their dependence on large-scale SAR-LiDAR paired datasets. To address these issues, this paper proposes a tiny-dataset scale-consistent diffusion framework, which comprises the following three components. i) A Stable Diffusion-based model is fine-tuned using just one GaoFen-3 SAR image to achieve task transfer. Dedicated data augmentation strategies are introduced and validated through ablation studies. ii) A novel and fast height ground truth pipeline is designed to generate radar-domain building facade points from sparse LiDAR data, with vegetation effects explicitly removed. Experimental analysis reveals that removing vegetation leads to improved building height estimation accuracy. iii) A geometry-driven transformation module is introduced to ensure a consistent height scale, making it universally applicable across images. Experiments on GaoFen-3 Spotlight SAR data demonstrate that the proposed framework, trained on only one image, outperforms current UNet- and Transformer-based baselines. Cross-sensor evaluation on TerraSAR-X further indicates that the proposed framework has a certain degree of generalization ability.

title: Autonomous Pipeline Inspection Using Lightweight Vision-Based UAV Tracking_supp3-3693978.mp4
authors: Praful Hambarde
publish: N/A
abstract: Long-range pipelines are indispensable infrastructures requiring continuous and rigorous inspections for structural health monitoring. Multi-copter (UAV) inspections face critical challenges including low-altitude flight constraints, limited computational resources for integrated visual perception and navigation control for the purpose of continuous inspection in real-time. The present work proposes an autonomous vision-based UAV low altitude inspection framework for industrial pipeline inspection. The proposed framework seamlessly integrates three innovative components: a lightweight deep learning architecture (MLA-Net) for robust pipeline segmentation, a vision-based control strategy for smooth tracking, and a sliding mode controller for real-time navigation. MLA-Net combines Cascaded Feature Extractor (CFE) blocks for hierarchical feature learning with Multi-Scale Receptive (MSR) blocks for multi-scale environmental adaption, achieving efficient pipe detection under varying conditions. The vision-based control strategy processes MLA-Net outputs through perspective projection and oriented rectangle extraction to coordinate yaw-lateral control, while a non-linear forward velocity function prevents overshooting during pipeline turns. The sliding mode controller fuses visual features with altitude data for autonomous navigation across complex pipeline networks. Extensive validation through simulation and real-time outdoor experiments demonstrates superior performance across diverse pipeline configurations including intersections and multipipe scenarios. The proposed MLA-Net achieves 0.8166 IoU while operating at ≈27 FPS with only 0.202 GFLOPs and 0.178M, significantly outperforming existing methods in computational efficiency. The integrated framework enables stable autonomous tracking across various pipeline geometries, providing a practical solution with enhanced accuracy and computational efficiency for resource-constrained UAV applications. Source code available at Autonomous vision-based pipline tracking by UAV

title: CLARE: Continual Learning for Vision-Language-Action Models via Autonomous Adapter Routing and Expansion_supp1-3693992.mp4
authors: Ralf Romer
publish: N/A

title: BurnDC: A Progressive Propagation Framework for Low Coverage Depth Completion_supp1-3693984.mp4
authors: Bin Fan
publish: N/A
abstract: The growing adoption of compact and cost-effective solid-state LiDARs has greatly advanced robotics. However, their inherently limited Field-of-View (FOV) hinders their application in tasks requiring wide-range depth perception. To overcome this limitation, we introduce the Low Coverage Depth Completion (LCDC) task, which aims to generate a full-scene depth map from a low coverage sparse depth map and a corresponding RGB image, and propose a tailored framework named BurnDC. BurnDC progressively expands the propagation frontier around reliable depth anchors via Progressive Depth Burn (PDB) and utilizes Weighted Ring Attention (WRA) to inject stable geometric context into boundary regions, achieving a controlled refinement and completion of the entire depth map. To evaluate LCDC, we construct a real-world solid-state LiDAR based benchmark, LC-TIERS, and simulate low coverage settings in NYUv2 and KITTI. Experimental results demonstrate that BurnDC significantly outperforms existing methods in low coverage scenarios, with an RMSE reduction of 10-20% over top competitors. Our work provides a promising solution for unlocking the full potential of solid-state LiDARs in various applications. Code available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yudmoe/burnDC/</uri>.

title: Decentralized Secure Cloud Storage using Blockchain Technology
authors: R. Sai Krishna, Suma S, G. Swapthika, D.Asritha, K. Karthik, CH. Mallikarjuna Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Centralized cloud storage platforms continue to face challenges related to data security, privacy, trust, and system reliability, largely due to their dependence on third-party service providers and the presence of single points of failure. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a decentralized secure cloud storage system that combines blockchain technology with the Interplanetary File System (IPFS). In the proposed design, user data is encrypted at the client side and stored off-chain in IPFS, while file metadata, ownership details, and access control rules are securely maintained on the Ethereum blockchain using smart contracts. The proposed system was implemented and evaluated through experimental analysis to examine data integrity, access control enforcement, and overall system performance. Integrity verification results show that any unauthorized modification of stored data can be accurately detected by comparing cryptographic hashes recorded on the blockchain with those of files retrieved from IPFS. The access control assessment confirms that smart contracts consistently enforce authorization policies, allowing access only to legitimate users and preventing unauthorized operations. In addition, performance evaluation indicates that storing data off-chain significantly reduces blockchain overhead, enabling scalable file storage and retrieval with reasonable latency. Overall, the experimental findings demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively improves data confidentiality, integrity, availability, and user-controlled access while maintaining system scalability. These results highlight the potential of blockchain-enabled decentralized storage systems as a practical and secure solution for managing sensitive and mission-critical data in real-world cloud environments.

title: UAdapt: Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Graph Node Classification
authors: Dengdi Sun, Yuxuan Han, Zhuanlian Ding, Bin Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Node classification on real-world graphs often suffers from limited training efficiency and unstable optimization. We observe that nodes exhibit highly imbalanced convergence behaviors: semantically clear nodes quickly stabilize, whereas boundary and minority nodes remain uncertain and volatile throughout training, making uniform optimization inefficient and limiting the attainable performance. To address this issue, we propose UAdapt, an uncertainty-aware, architecture-agnostic adaptive training strategy that can be seamlessly integrated into existing GNN frameworks without modifying backbone models. UAdapt defines a fine-grained node uncertainty measure by combining instantaneous prediction entropy with temporal prediction variance, capturing both short-term hesitation and long-term convergence instability. Based on quantile thresholds, nodes are dynamically partitioned into stable, medium, and hard states, and training resources are adaptively reallocated through node-level loss reweighting and node-level early stopping. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that UAdapt consistently improves ACC and Macro-F1, enhances training stability, and accelerates convergence. In particular, UAdapt reduces the number of epochs required to reach stable validation performance by approximately 15–19% across datasets, with larger gains on noisier heterophilous graphs.

title: Research on Real-Time Speech Recognition Model for Aircraft Cockpit Audio
authors: Lihui Wang, Haihua Huang, Yan Ji
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Aiming at the technical pain points in aircraft cockpit audio, such as strong professionalism of terms, complex noise interference, scarce labeled data, and strict real-time requirements, a non-autoregressive speech recognition scheme with a "CNN + Transformer" hybrid architecture is proposed. By optimizing the model architecture, data augmentation strategies, multi-stage training methods, and lightweight technologies, the balance between model accuracy and inference speed is achieved. Experimental results show that with a parameter scale of 200 million, the proposed model achieves a character error rate (CER) as low as 0.046 and the inference speed can reach up to 420 hours of audio data per hour. Compared with autoregressive decoding, non-autoregressive decoding speeds up by 10 times, which can meet the real-time recognition needs and provide technical support for flight operation safety risk monitoring.

title: HITL-GAN-RF: A Human-in-the-Loop Data Pipeline for Adaptive Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Payam Zohari, Dorsa Macky Aleagha, Sina Akhavi, Mohammadhadi Alayeian
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: The explosive growth of financial transactions poses a dual challenge for fraud detection: achieving both speed and precision at scale while maintaining adaptability to evolving fraud patterns. Traditional machine learning models, though powerful, are often constrained by extreme class imbalance and the absence of reliable human feedback mechanisms. To address these limitations, we introduce HITL-GAN-RF, a human-in-the-loop architectural framework designed for adaptive and trustworthy financial fraud detection in high-volume environments. Leveraging a data-centric design, the system orchestrates a Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (CTGAN) to synthesize realistic fraudulent samples from limited minority-class data, utilizing these to enhance a Random Forest (RF) classifier embedded within a modern big data pipeline. The framework operationalizes domain expertise through a feedback loop, where human verification of flagged cases continuously refines the training data and triggers model retraining via automated Airflow workflows. On a held-out test set, the proposed architecture achieves an F1-score of 85.90% and a precision of 97.01%, substantially reducing false positives compared to conventional oversampling techniques. Moreover, the pipeline demonstrates high versatility, as the CTGAN-generated data generalizes across models, elevating gradient boosting architectures like XGBoost and LightGBM to competitive F1-scores exceeding 88%. This work presents a scalable, interpretable, and continuously improving system integration that advances the operational reliability of financial fraud detection.

title: FDH-CR: Adaptive Feature Purification and Dual-Branch Hierarchical Discriminator for Multitemporal Multi-scale Cloud Removal
authors: Zhu Zhu, Zhitao Fu, Zhihao Zhang, Bo-Hui Tang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Thick cloud cover severely occludes surface information in optical remote sensing images, limiting the reliability of subsequent applications. Recent advances in multitemporal cloud removal methods have made notable progress. However, features from existing methods are often contaminated by cloud interference and temporal variation noise, masking the true surface information. Directly fusing such features limits the use of useful information and introduces artifacts. Furthermore, discriminators typically rely on single-scale constraints, which cannot adequately supervise multi-scale feature recovery, resulting in poor structure and texture restoration. To address these challenges, this paper proposes adaptive feature purification and a dual-branch hierarchical discriminator for multitemporal multi-scale cloud removal (FDH-CR), which reduces artifacts from channel conflicts and provides more comprehensive multi-scale supervision, thereby reconstructing missing surface information in scenes affected by clouds using multitemporal data. To tackle mixed channel information, a feature purification and enhancement block (FPEB) is introduced to utilize an adaptive weighting mechanism to enhance information-rich channels while suppressing redundant and conflicting features, thereby strengthening useful feature representations and reducing artifacts. Additionally, to address the limited supervisory capability of single-scale discriminators, a dual-branch hierarchical discriminator (DBHD) offers multi-scale supervision, helping generated images preserve structural and textural realism. A feature matching (FM) loss based on DBHD is innovatively adopted in the cloud removal framework, which aligns intermediate features from the discriminator to enhance semantic consistency and stabilize adversarial training. Experiments on two public remote sensing datasets demonstrate that FDH-CR achieves superior performance both quantitatively and visually, validating its effectiveness and potential.

title: TemLo: Temporal-Local Synergy Enhanced RWKV for Audio-Visual Segmentation
authors: Yunzhi Zhuge, Mengyuan Zhu, Sitong Gong, Lu Zhang, Xu Jia, Qi Xu, Hehe Fan, Huchuan Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: The core objective of Audio-Visual Segmentation (AVS) is aligning auditory cues with their corresponding visual sources in video sequences, which poses challenges due to long-range temporal dependencies and modality-specific noise (e.g., irrelevant sounds or visual clutter). Most existing methods focus on Transformer-based architectures, which, despite achieving strong performance, struggle to maintain sequence structure perception in noisy, complex scenes and fail to fully exploit intra-modal and inter-modal temporal relationships, limiting multimodal understanding and alignment. To address the challenges, we introduce TemLo, a novel framework built on the Receptance-Weighted Key-Value (RWKV) architecture for efficient temporal modeling and precise targeted representation learning. TemLo introduces modality-specific RWKV encoders that capture salient spatial and temporal dynamics within each stream, reducing intra-modal noise and ambiguity. To fully enable cross-modal interaction and integration, we further design the Acoustic Modulation Pyramid to facilitate structured audio-visual interactions at multiple temporal and spatial scales, enriching the visual representations with audio-aware semantics. Moreover, we propose the Vision-guided Modality Injector for ensuring temporally consistent and semantically aligned reasoning. Extensive experiments on standard AVS benchmarks demonstrate that TemLo sets a new state of the art, achieving significant gains over prior methodS. When employing the PVT-v2 backbone, TemLo surpasses AVSCA on the MS3 dataset with absolute improvements of 6.6 (10.28%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\uparrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>) in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {M}_{J}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and 2.8 (3.66%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\uparrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>) in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {M}_{F}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Furthermore, on the AVSS dataset, our method outperforms Co-Prop by margins of 3.0 (7.58%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\uparrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and 4.4 (9.91%<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\uparrow$</tex-math></inline-formula>) in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {M}_{J}$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {M}_{F}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, respectively. Source code and model weights will be made publicly available at TemLo.

title: HoStB-DVGNN: A Flotation Fault Recognition Method Using Higher Order Spatial–Temporal Block and Dual-Stream Variational GNN
authors: Ying Fan, Zhaohui Tang, Jin Luo, Yongfang Xie, Weihua Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: Accurate fault pattern recognition in the flotation process is crucial for rapid fault response and reducing production risks. However, dynamic flotation process data exhibit high dimensionality, nonlinearity, and nonstationarity, along with significant noise and uncertainty, which severely impact the accuracy (ACC) of fault pattern recognition. To address these challenges, we propose a fault pattern recognition method for froth flotation based on higher order spatial–temporal block and dual-stream variational graph neural networks (HoStB-DVGNNs). First, we construct a high-order spatiotemporal block using the groups of key frame images to comprehensively characterize the flotation production conditions. Then, we develop a dual-stream variational graph neural network (DVGNN) that includes an apparent feature stream and HoStB stream to effectively extract dynamic froth information. In addition, we introduce a bilateral self-supervision mechanism to build a variational autoencoder (VAE), significantly enhancing the model’s generalization performance. Finally, extensive experiments on benchmark datasets and real flotation processes validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed fault pattern recognition method.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: A Systematic Study of CSI Fingerprint Feature Representations and Deep Learning Models for Indoor Localization
authors: Jiajian Qin, Xuanzhen Xia, Hang Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address inconsistent sample organization and limited comparative analysis of feature representations in Channel State Information (CSI) fingerprint-based indoor localization, this paper proposes a unified framework for sample reorganization and feature construction using the OpenCSI dataset. Raw HDF5 data are reorganized into standardized CSI samples, from which flattened, matrix, amplitude, and phase representations are derived. Under unified experimental settings, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), baseline CNN, Amplitude-only CNN, Phase-only CNN, and Dual-branch CNN are systematically compared. Results show that deep learning models outperform KNN, CNN-based models outperform MLP, and the Amplitude-only CNN achieves the best classification accuracy of 98.41%, indicating that amplitude features are the most effective representation on the current dataset. Experiments with varying training sample sizes further demonstrate the superior data utilization efficiency of CNN-based models. In addition, improved regression experiments provide supplementary validation for the feasibility of continuous position prediction.

title: A Partitioned Stator Flux Switching PM Machine with Strongly Coupled Flux Reversal Effect
authors: Zeyu Min, Libing Jing, Tao Wang, Ronghai Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The partitioned stator flux-switching permanent magnet (PS-FSPM) machine integrates the advantages of a stator PM machine and a magnetically geared machine, while providing sufficient spatial utilization of both the inner and outer stators. This paper investigates a PS-FSPM machine featuring radially magnetized PMs alternately arranged in the outer stator. With identical winding and rotor configurations, the PMs in the inner and outer stators produce flux-switching and flux-reversal effects, respectively. Through equivalent magnetic circuit analysis, the strong coupling of flux linkage under both effects is demonstrated. The torque harmonic characteristics and torque-generation mechanisms under load conditions are further analyzed, clarifying the underlying principles of torque enhancement. Furthermore, a parameterized model is established, and global optimization is carried out for both the proposed and regular machines using the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II to obtain optimal parameter values. The comparative results of the four machines indicate that the proposed PS-FSPM machine exhibits the best performance in terms of torque, power factor, and efficiency. Finally, a prototype of the proposed machine is fabricated, and the experimental results show good agreement with the FEA predictions.

title: Proximal Policy Optimization with Predictive and Memory-Aware Battery Management for BEVs Using Hybrid Energy Storage and Battery Health-Oriented Rewards
authors: Armin Lotfy, Mohamad Alzayed, Hicham Chaoui, Loïc Boulon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The increasing use of electric vehicles (EVs) calls for the development of more sophisticated battery management strategies (BMSs) in order to maximize performance and lengthen the lifespan of components. Battery electric vehicles (BEVs) that make use of a single energy storage system (ESS) are another type of electric vehicle architecture that necessitates rigorous energy management in order to guarantee both efficiency and longevity. In order to improve the efficiency with which energy is utilized, hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) have been developed. These kinds of ESS combine super capacitors with batteries (SCs). Despite the fact that HESS designs provide enhanced power handling and a longer driving range, they also add an additional layer of complexity to the design of BMSs. In this research, an intelligent BMS for BEVs that are equipped with a battery-supercapacitor as HESS is proposed. The proposed framework integrates a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) reinforcement learning algorithm with a Convolutional Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-LSTM) predictor to forecast future driving demands while considering historical patterns and the battery's state of health (SOH). For the purpose of optimizing energy distribution and preserving battery health, the BMS makes use of multi-dimensional observations, such as the state of charge (SOC) of the battery, the SOH, the voltage of the SC, the speed of the vehicle, and the distance traveled. The proposed strategy demonstrates superior performance in both energy efficiency and battery health preservation when compared with state-of-the-art RL-based methods. Specifically, it reduces battery capacity fading by 2.38% and 1.12% relative to PPO and SAC without memory-aware mechanisms, as well as SAC with memory-aware features, while simultaneously achieving a higher final SOC than all benchmark counterparts.

title: Accuracy and congestion Improvement in Intelligent Traffic signal Control using YOLOv10-Based object Detection and Q-Learning Compared with Fixed-Time Traffic Light
authors: Priyadharshini A, Divya T, Vinoly G, Kavin V, Jothees Waran C, Surya S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Urban traffic congestion is a serious issue that stems from the rapid rise of vehicles in an area, as well as fixed-time signal control systems that cannot adapt to changing traffic conditions. Most existing solutions do not dynamically respond quickly enough to changing conditions, leading to higher delays and increased fuel consumption by drivers. In this work, we propose an advanced traffic light control system using You Only Look Once Version 10 (YOLOv10) combined with Q-learning, a reinforcement learning algorithm, where the former is used for vehicle detection and counting via real-time video images from roadside cameras, and the latter is used for dynamically adjusting traffic signal timing based on traffic density in real time. We have simulated different traffic scenarios and compared them to fixed time signal operations system. The proposed system has produced more effective operation, less delay, and better accuracy in response to signals, suggesting that it can be developed into an adaptive and scalable solution for smart cities and intelligent transportation systems.

title: ParaVul: A Parallel Large Language Model and Retrieval-Augmented Framework for Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
authors: Tenghui Huang, Jinbo Wen, Jiawen Kang, Siyong Chen, Zhengtao Li, Tao Zhang, Dongning Liu, Jiacheng Wang, Chengjun Cai, Yinqiu Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Smart contracts play a significant role in automating blockchain services. Nevertheless, vulnerabilities in smart contracts pose serious threats to blockchain security. Currently, traditional detection methods primarily rely on static analysis and formal verification, which can result in high false-positive rates and poor scalability. Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently made significant progress in smart contract vulnerability detection. However, they still face challenges such as high inference costs and substantial computational overhead. In this paper, we propose ParaVul, a parallel LLM and retrievalaugmented framework to improve the reliability and accuracy of smart contract vulnerability detection. Specifically, we first develop Sparse Low-Rank Adaptation (SLoRA), a technique for efficient LLM fine-tuning tailored to smart contract vulnerability detection. Distinct from existing LoRA methods, SLoRA inserts parallel sparse and low-rank branches after the attention projection and the feed-forward block, enabling LLMs to capture both global code semantics and localized vulnerability patterns while maintaining low training overhead. We then construct a vulnerability contract knowledge base and develop a hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that integrates Okapi BM25 with dense retrieval to provide complementary lexical and semantic evidence for smart contract vulnerability verification. Furthermore, we propose a meta-learner-based gated verification module to fuse the outputs of the SLoRA detector and the two RAG-based detectors, thereby generating the final detection results. After completing vulnerability detection, we design chain-of-thought prompts to guide LLMs to generate comprehensive vulnerability detection reports. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority of ParaVul, especially in terms of F1 scores, achieving 0.9398 for single-label detection and 0.9930 for multi-label detection.

title: Optimized Green Intelligent Transportation System Using Edge Computing with Multi-objective Metaheuristic Algorithm
authors: Shailendra Pratap Singh, Sunil Prajapat, Joel J. P. C. Rodrigues, Jianwei Niu, Ahmed Farouk Metwaly
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Intelligent Transportation System (ITS) employs traditional optimization methods, including gradient-based or evolutionary methods. These methods often fail to simultaneously balance predictive accuracy, latency, and resource efficiency while respecting strict operational constraints. Multi-objective system design under explicit constraints is also a challenge in engineering and artificial intelligence. To address these gaps, we propose a Transfer Learning-Enhanced Multi-Objective Whale Optimization Algorithm (TL-MOWOA Algorithm) for constrained multi-objective optimization of control parameters in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). The framework integrates transfer learning to accelerate convergence by reusing knowledge from related optimization tasks. In this way, it improves adaptability across diverse traffic scenarios. Evolutionary operators of the Whale Optimization Algorithm, within TL-MOWOA Algorithm, preserve population diversity and ensure robust global search. The transfer learned priors guide the search toward promising feasible regions, hence reducing the risk of stagnation. Experimental evaluation was conducted on synthetic benchmarks, including balanced and high-dimensional constrained. Results demonstrate that TL-MOWOA Algorithm consistently better state-of-the-art algorithms such as NSGA-II, NSGA-III, MOEA/D, standard WOA, and standalone TL-based EAs across multiple performance indicators, including convergence speed, Pareto front quality, and computational efficiency. The results signify that TL-MOWOA Algorithm better state-of-the-art algorithms. TL-MOWOA Algorithm achieves up to 15% reduction in average travel time, 12% reduction in fuel consumption, 20% reduction in congestion index, and 18% reduction in emissions as compared to NSGA-III, MOEA/D, standard WOA, and standalone TL-based EAs. TL-MOWOA Algorithm provides a scalable and adaptive solution for ITS optimization.

title:
P
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    LACBC: Parallel Per-Level Attention with Cross-Branch Consistency for Remote Sensing Object Detection
authors: Pengbo Liang, Wenbo Yu, Junru Zhao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Object detection in high spatial resolution remote sensing images is crucial for automatic information extraction, analysis, and understanding, yet remains challenging due to large-scale variations, dense and small objects, and cluttered backgrounds. To explicitly enhance informative regions while suppressing background noise, this letter proposes Parallel Per-Level Attention with Cross-Branch Consistency (P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>LACBC). The proposed detector places a local enhancement attention and a global relational attention in parallel at each scale, and applies a single global residual after branch fusion to stabilize optimization without repeatedly amplifying responses. A Branch Distribution Consistency Loss (BDCL) is further introduced to align the spatial focus of different branches by projecting features into a one-dimensional space and applying Kullback-Leibler divergence constraints. This alleviates the inconsistency between local detail modeling and global context modeling. To further strengthen multi-scale coherence, a lightweight Cross-Level Fusion and Residual Connection (CLFR) module aligns per-level refined features to an intermediate resolution and redistributes the fused representation back to all pyramid levels via spatial-only up/down-sampling, improving cross-scale consistency at negligible overhead. Experiments on RSOD and NWPU VHR-10 datasets demonstrate that the proposed P2LACBC achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art object detection methods, achieving mean average precision (mAP) scores of 96.20% and 94.40%, respectively.

title: Two-Stage Multi-Drone Multi-Target Association: Laplacian-Guided Feature Enhancement and SGMM-Based Error Filtering Approach
authors: Zhang Zhao, Ruigang Fu, Zixuan Xiao, Jiajun Xiang, Yu Song, Ping Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Cross-view target association is the foundation of multi-drone collaborative recognition. Existing methods often fail to fully exploit both the visual appearance and neighborhood spatial relationships of targets, making them vulnerable to large viewpoint variations and densely distributed similar objects, which frequently result in incorrect associations. To address this, we propose a two-stage cross-view target association framework, following the paradigm of “feature enhancement–error filtering” . In the feature enhancement stage, we implicitly model the neighborhood topological structure of each target and aggregate visual-semantic information from spatially adjacent targets via position-guided self-attention. Subsequently, for each target, we propose Laplacian-guided cross-attention to aggregate features of the target in another view whose appearance and spatial characteristics are most similar. In the error filtering stage, we propose a score-guided Gaussian Mixture Model (S-GMM) to filter error associations, and then find best homography matrix to achieve precise cross-view targets mapping. Our method demonstrates superior performance in scenarios with densely distributed and highly similar targets. Moreover, the homography matrix obtained in the second stage serves as a byproduct that can assist in handling target occlusions during subsequent collaborative recognition. The proposed method achieves promising results on the MDMT and M3Track dataset. The code will be released at https://github.com/bigdreaming11/LGSGMM.

title: Learning-Based Hybrid Neural Receiver for 6G-V2X Communications
authors: Osama Saleem, Mohammed Alfaqawi, Pierre Merdrignac, Abdelaziz Bensrhair, Soheyb Ribouh
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Neural receivers are emerging as key enablers for intelligent physical-layer design in next-generation wireless communications. However, the integration of learning-based architectures into practical communication systems requires careful consideration of standard compatibility and compu tational constraints. In this work, we propose a Hybrid Neural Receiver (H-NR) that operates on the frequency-domain OFDM resource grid and replaces the post-FFT baseband receiver chain, including resource grid demapping, channel estimation, equalization, demodulation, and channel decoding, within a unified end-to-end architecture. The proposed design integrates Transformer encoder blocks for global symbol inference with a Graph Neural Network (GNN) for structure-aware channel decoding. Extensive evaluations under varying vehicular speeds (0–120 km/h), carrier frequencies (5.9 and 28 GHz), and standardized UMa and CDL channel models demonstrate consistent gains of approximately 0.5 dB over the state-of-the-art TransRx neural receiver, particularly in scenarios where conventional receivers exhibit significant performance degradation. The proposed H-NR demonstrates efficient GPU utilization, achieving a throughput of 33.95 GFLOPs/s and a 121.7% improvement over the state-of-the-art TransRx. This performance, together with its ability to process diverse data modalities such as data, image, and audio, highlights its suitability for next-generation C-V2X systems

title: EffortNet: A Deep Learning Framework for Objective Assessment of Speech Enhancement Technologies Using EEG-Based Alpha Oscillations
authors: Ching-Chih Sung, Cheng-Hung Hsin, Yu-Anne Shiah, Bo-Jyun Lin, Yi-Xuan Lai, Chia-Ying Lee, Yu-Te Wang, Borching Su, Yu Tsao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: This paper presents EffortNet, a novel deep learning framework for decoding listening effort at the individual level from electroencephalography (EEG) during speech comprehension. Quantifying listening effort remains a significant challenge in speech-hearing research. We collected 64-channel EEG data from 122 participants during speech comprehension under four conditions: clean, noisy, MMSE-enhanced, and Transformerenhanced speech. Statistical analyses confirmed that alpha power (8–13 Hz) was significantly higher during noisy speech processing compared with clean or enhanced conditions, confirming its validity as an objective biomarker of listening effort. To address the substantial inter-individual variability in EEG signals, EffortNet integrates three complementary learning paradigms: self-supervised learning to leverage unlabeled data, incremental learning for progressive adaptation to individual characteristics, and transfer learning for efficient knowledge transfer to new subjects. Our experimental results demonstrate that EffortNet achieves 80.9% classification accuracy with only 40% of training data from new subjects, significantly outperforming conventional CNN (62.3%) and STAnet (61.1%) models. The probability-based metric derived from our model revealed that Transformer-enhanced speech elicited neural responses more similar to clean speech than MMSE-enhanced speech. This neural pattern aligns with objective metrics, but contrasts with subjective intelligibility ratings. This dissociation underscores the value of objective neural markers in evaluating hearing technologies, such as headsets, hearables, and assistive listening devices.

title: RCAlign: Revisiting Radar Camera Alignment by Contrastive Learning for 3D Object Detection
authors: Linhua Kong, Dongxia Chang, Lian Liu, Zisen Kong, Pengyuan Li, Yao Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recently, 3D object detection algorithms based on radar and camera fusion have shown excellent performance, setting the stage for their application in autonomous driving perception tasks. Existing methods have focused on dealing with feature misalignment caused by the domain gap between radar and camera. However, they either neglect inter-modal features interaction during alignment or fail to effectively align features at the same spatial location across modalities. To alleviate the above problems, we propose a new alignment model called Radar Camera Alignment (RCAlign). Specifically, we design a Dual-Route Alignment (DRA) module based on contrastive learning to align and fuse the features between radar and camera. This helped the network to precisely aggregate two modal features representing the same object. Moreover, considering the sparsity of radar BEV features, a Radar Feature Enhancement (RFE) module is proposed to improve the densification of radar BEV features with the knowledge distillation loss. Experiments show RCAlign achieves a new state-of-the-art on the public nuScenes benchmark in radar camera fusion for 3D Object Detection. Furthermore, the RCAlign achieves a significant performance gain (1.4% NDS and 2.9% mAP) in real-time 3D detection compared to the latest state-of-the-art method (CRT-Fusion).

title: Advanced Multi-Scale Vision Transformer with Neural Architecture Search for Thyroid Cancer Diagnosis Using Ultrasound Images
authors: Dhanalakshmi Venugopal, Mostefa Mesbah, Ahmed Ammari, Rami Hmouz, Asim Qureshi, Basit Salam
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate and interpretable differentiation of abnormal and suspicious (potentially malignant) thyroid nodules in ultrasound imaging remains a critical yet challenging task, particularly in secondary healthcare settings where rapid triage is essential. Conventional deep learning models, especially convolutional neural networks, often fail to capture long-range contextual dependencies and multi-scale morphological cues (e.g., microcalcifications, irregular margins and heterogeneous echogenicity) that are vital for reliable diagnosis. To address this, we propose a novel Multi-Scale Vision Transformer with Adaptive Feature Fusion (MSViT-AFF), a unified and explainable framework that integrates hierarchical Vision Transformer (ViT) encoders, cross-scale attention, and differentiable Neural Architecture Search (NAS). Our approach automatically learns to weight diagnostically significant scales, emphasizing high-resolution features for microcalcification detection in suspicious nodules, through an end-to-end NAS-optimized fusion mechanism. Evaluated on a rigorously curated and radiologist-validated dataset of 600 thyroid ultrasound images from Sultan Qaboos University Hospital (SQUH), comprising 300 abnormal and 300 suspicious cases, MSViT-AFF achieved 96.2% accuracy, 94.8% sensitivity, 97.1% specificity, and an AUC of 0.983 [95% CI: 0.974, 0.991], exceeding all evaluatedCNNand single-scale transformer baselines with statistically significant margins (all p < 0.01, DeLong’s test). Moreover, a Grad-CAM-based explainability layer provides pixel-level visualizations aligned with established radiological criteria, thereby enhancing clinical trust and transparency. Cross-dataset evaluation with fine-tuning on the public DDTI benchmark yielded an AUC of 0.966, providing encouraging evidence of cross-dataset transferability and generalization potential. With an inference time of only 15 ms per image, the proposed model shows promise for real-time deployment in resource-constrained secondary care units, pending prospective multi-centre validation.
