title: Recent Trends in Deep Learning-Driven Tomato Leaf Disease Detection and Severity Analysis
authors: Rashmi Suresh More, Roopalakshmi R., Rajashree Krishna, Archana Chougule
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) suffers major yield reduction along with poor crop quality due to various leaf diseases such as early blight, septoria leaf spot, late blight, and leaf curl virus. The latest innovations in deep learning have made it possible to detect these diseases with high accuracy above 95%. However, the interpretability of the models and quantitative evaluation of their severity are relatively under-researched areas. The paper gives a review of various deep learning techniques for tomato leaf disease diagnosis, including explainable AI and a severity assessment method based on 35 recent studies from 2021–2025. Our study identifies that although incorporating XAI approaches such as Grad-CAM, LIME, and SHAP offers visualized validation of the identified diseases, it does not offer a practical severity evaluation procedure. Findings reveal that almost 15% of the recent studies included explicit severity quantification and mostly include post-hoc XAI methods. Also, studies show a paradigm shift from conventional CNN toward Transformer-based hybrid models. The review concludes by highlighting a major research direction, which includes the need for lightweight, multimodal edge-based AI systems with severity assessment XAI integration for tomato leaf disease detection.

title: One-Sample-per-Symbol Non-Linear Equalization of Directly Modulated Lasers
authors: Y. H. Low, D. G. Cunningham, N. Bamiedakis, R. V. Penty
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Recently, we proposed a novel linearization method for directly-modulated lasers (DMLs), called the ABCD approximation method. This method achieves a substantial reduction in laser non-linearity, while maintaining low implementation complexity. In this paper, we introduce a novel one sample-per-symbol (SpS) implementation of the ABCD approximation method, further reducing its implementation complexity, hence improving real-world potential. As a proof-of concept, the method is experimentally demonstrated using a 7 GHz 850 nm VCSEL specified for 10 Gb/s NRZ modulation. Specifically, we implement two variants of the method based on three-tap T-spaced finite impulse response [FIR(3,T)] filters for 16 Gb/s NRZ modulation. Signal quality for each variant (16 GSa/s) is compared with conventional NRZ modulation and its corresponding three-SpS implementation (48 GSa/s). The results show a significant improvement in eye height for both variants over the conventional NRZ modulation. Additionally, the Stretched A variant yields reduced eye skew and improved eye symmetry. Moreover, the one-SpS implementation results in a small reduction in eye height of 0.98 dB and 0.57 dB for the two variants, respectively, compared to the three-SpS implementation. This demonstrates the effective mitigation of VCSEL non-linearity despite the one-SpS implementation. These findings highlight the potential of deploying low-bandwidth devices, and simplified, cost-effective driving circuits in next generation optical transmitters.

title: Environmental Assessment of Ultralightweight DC-DC Converters for Future eVTOL Aircraft with Hybrid Power Supply
authors: Luc Imperiali, Johann W. Kolar, Jonas Huber
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: This letter presents a comparative life cycle assessment of two ultralightweight, bidirectional, non-isolated buck-boost dc-dc converters for hybrid (fuel cell and battery) powertrains of electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft: A two-level (2L) SiC design and a three-level (3L) GaN flying-capacitor design. Embodied and use-phase carbon footprints are evaluated for representative urban and regional mission profiles. Whereas the specific 3L design shows nearly twice the gravimetric power density and slightly lower embodied emissions, its lower conversion efficiency consistently results in higher overall life cycle carbon footprints compared to the selected 2L design. The results demonstrate that, despite aggressive weight reduction, efficiency remains the dominant factor governing the environmental performance of airborne dc-dc converters.

title: DiffLabel: Robust Pseudo Label Generation for Semi-Supervised Temporal Language Grounding
authors: Xiaofan Song, Le Wang, Sanping Zhou, Xiaolong Sun, Mengnan Liu, Kun Xia, Gang Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Semi-Supervised Temporal Language Grounding (SS-TLG) aims at retrieving a target moment from untrimmed videos by a given language query with large-scale unlabeled videos. Similar to most semi-supervised learning tasks, the main challenge in SS-TLG is how to improve the quality of pseudo labels. Existing methods are affected by temporal location biases from datasets, which leads to unreliable pseudo labels, reducing the performance of whole semi-supervised learning process. In this paper, we propose DiffLabel, a novel approach of utilizing the diffusion model to generate robust pseudo labels for SS-TLG. Specifically, the diffusion model is incorporated into a teacher-student framework. The teacher model refines a set of randomly initialized noisy moments to the output pseudo labels in a gradual way. In the student model, target moments diffuse from pseudo labels and ground truths to random distributions, and the model learns to reverse this noising process. Due to the introduction of random noise, the model can better mitigate the interference of temporal location biases, thereby improving the robustness of pseudo labels and optimize the entire semi-supervised learning process. In addition, we propose two query-aware data perturbations to avoid semantic information redundancy and annotation deviation. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness and generalization of our approach, which outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both Charades-CD-OOD and Activitynet-CD-OOD datasets. The code will be available.

title: Learning Multi-Agent Cooperation Via Future Observations Guided Communication Graph
authors: Kangrui Ren, Zhencun Jiang, Jinfu Fan, Zhongjie Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Effective communication is imperative for the successful achievement of expected objectives in multi-agent reinforcement learning. To facilitate communication, some approaches adopt global information sharing through fully connected communication, which can lead to redundancy of information and resource wastage. Most works conceptualize the communication architecture as a learnable relation graph. Some of them focus solely on agent-level relations, neglecting higher-order connections. Although some studies incorporate group-level relations, they fall short in optimizing group generation and fail to distinguish the contribution of intra-group and inter-group communication to goal achievement. To address these challenges, we propose a novel approach to infer the Future Observations Guided Communication Graph, dubbed FOGCG, which offers a new perspective of optimizing the grouping and is designed to integrate communication needs at three levels. Evaluations on StarCraft II micromanagement tasks demonstrate that FOGCG is superior on performance to the baseline methods, validating its effectiveness in enhancing multi-agent communication.

title: Hyperspectral Image Mixed Noise Removal Based on Sparsity Constraint and Low-Rank Approximation
authors: Huiheng Zhang, Bin Zhao, Xuwen Ma, Lin Han, Meng Yang, Magnus O. Ulfarsson, Jakob Sigurdsson, Jón Atli Benediktsson
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral images (HSIs) are often corrupted by various noise during acquisition, which leads to a reduction in HSI quality. Therefore, HSI denoising is a necessary preprocessing step for their further application. In this paper, we propose an HSI denoising framework based on the sparsity constraint and low-rank approximation (SCLA). SCLA simultaneously uses the global low-rank structure, sparse prior, and local spatial-spectral smoothness of HSIs to construct a denoising model for removal of heterogeneous Gaussian noise, impulse noise, and stripe mixed noise. The Split Bregman method is used for SCLA to solve the optimization problem of our model. An ablation study is utilized to show the effectiveness of the structures of SCLA. Compared with other denoising methods, based on simulated and real experiments, SCLA achieves significant advantages in quantitative and visual assessments for removing Gaussian noise, impulse noise, and stripe mixed noise.

title: A Transformer-based Intelligent Framework for Post-Crisis Relief Coordination and Priority-Aware Resource Allocation
authors: J. Sheetal Gunwant Rathod, S. Meyyappan, T. Jenistan, J. Joffin Renold, S. Marlen Brando Arockiyam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Post-disaster relief operations frequently encounter critical operational challenges that reduce efficiency and delay life-saving interventions. One major difficulty involves fragmented communication channels. Emergency requests are often dispersed across social platforms, isolated portals, phone calls, and manual records, resulting in inconsistent information and duplication. Such fragmentation prevents consolidated visibility of demand and slows coordination among aid providers. Another significant issue concerns the absence of structured prioritization. In many relief environments, requests are processed sequentially or subjectively without systematic evaluation of urgency or severity. This leads to competition between critical medical emergencies and non-critical supply needs within the same operational queue, increasing vulnerability among affected populations. Limited coordination between resource availability and geographic demand further complicates relief delivery. Donors and volunteers may offer resources without real-time awareness of demand clusters, while coordinators struggle to match supplies efficiently to nearby affected locations. The proposed methodology addresses these challenges through centralized integration of stakeholders into a unified digital platform. Structured request entry ensures standardized data capture and reduces duplication. AI-assisted prioritization evaluates urgency based on contextual attributes, enabling systematic ranking of requests. Proximity-based resource matching connects available supplies to geographically relevant demands, minimizing response time. Verification mechanisms, role-based access control, and real-time dashboards enhance accountability, transparency, and coordinated decision-making during large-scale emergencies.

title: Automated Modeling from Medical Guidelines to BPMN Based on Large Language Models
authors: Feng Gao, Jian Chen, Yinghui Jin, Siyuan Ruan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Medical guideline texts are characterized by strong domain specificity, complex conditional logic, and implicit semantics, which make their automatic transformation into executable process models highly challenging.To address this problem, we reformulate the task as a structure-constrained, multi-stage semantic reasoning problem and propose a dependency-aware BPMN automatic modeling method based on large language models. The proposed approach explicitly decomposes the modeling process into three progressively structured semantic stages: activity and event extraction, dependency relation identification, and BPMN structure generation, thereby improving logical consistency while effectively reducing model hallucinations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves F1 scores of 0.88 and 0.87 in activity recognition and dependency extraction, respectively, and yields significant improvements in gateway identification and overall structural matching accuracy. The results indicate that the proposed approach exhibits clear advantages in modeling complex conditional branches, enhancing process structure interpretability, and controlling modeling errors, representing a novel methodological paradigm for medical guideline process modeling.

title: Code-Collaborative Intelligent Query Generation Technology for Complex Relational Data
authors: Chanjuan Tang, Zhongtian Lu, Xinqi Wang, Zhengtao Wang, Guanying Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Natural language query generation for complex graph-structured data faces challenges in domain-specific settings. Existing large language Mmdels (LLMs) often suffer from logical gaps when handling complex relational queries. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Code-Collaborative Intelligent Query Generation Framework. The framework leverages the sensitivity of Code LLMs to formal logic to parse complex topological dependencies. First, the study introduces a Dual-View Graph Representation mechanism. This mechanism constructs an object-oriented code view alongside a lightweight dictionary view. It transforms graph schema constraints into type-checking logic executable by the model, thereby mitigating structural hallucinations. Second, the paper designs a Structured Logic Planning (SLP) protocol. This protocol decouples unstructured natural language into explicit reasoning steps: path determination, semantic extraction, and schema mapping. The research further employs LoRA to fine-tune the DeepSeek-Coder backbone, enabling the model to internalize the structured reasoning paradigm. Experimental results on the constructed CRD-Instruct dataset demonstrate the superiority of the method. The model achieves a ROUGE-L score of 69.86%. It outperforms the untuned general baseline by approximately 27%. These results validate the accuracy and robustness of the approach in complex Text-to-Cypher generation tasks.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hybrid Real-Time File Integrity Monitoring System using Event-Driven Detection and Hash Verification
authors: Jaideep Mourya Kathala, Karthikeya Seeda, Ramamoorthi S, Mohamed Sithik M, Priya S, Mohammed Uveise S A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Cyber-attacks and unauthorized files are on the rise. Monitoring file integrity has become critical in modern times. Convolutional systems rely on fixed-interval polling or event-driven approaches with high latency and computational power. The proposed paper describes the Hybrid Real-Time File Integrity Monitoring System (FIMS) using adaptive polling, Event-Loss Recovery Algorithm, Change Classification Algorithm and File Criticality Scoring Algorithm that identifies and records file changes that are unauthorized with accuracy and efficiency and less computational power. The system specializes in sending real time alert on file modification, and provides an easy-to-use dashboard. The proposed system dynamically adjusts polling intervals based on runtime file activity ensuring efficient verification with less power. The system provides with an detection efficiency of 98 percent with minimal 0.8 percent of latency in false positive detection. immediate response and long-term reliability, which is a high one. solution against new threats in file integrity management.

title: Towards More Practical Label Inference Attacks against Graph-based Vertical Federated Learning
authors: Yimin Liu, Peng Jiang, Liehuang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Graph-based vertical federated learning (GVFL) enables an active party who owns a labeled graph to collaborate with passive parties who possess additional node features and edges to improve model performance. GVFL shares representations and gradients, allowing passive parties to retain their optimized bottom models, which makes previous GVFL algorithms unable to resist label inference attacks. However, most attacks assume that the attacker has access to the training data’s exact class space, the top model, or labeled auxiliary datasets from the training domain. These strong assumptions are not practical for real-world GVFL applications. In this paper, we propose Knowledge Transfer Attack (KTA), which leverages only auxiliary graphs from non-training domains to infer private labels. To address domain shift and ensure effective supervision transfer, KTA adapts a surrogate classifier in an aligned representation space while mitigating the negative influence of irrelevant outlier-class supervision. Specifically, KTA exploits the global consistency of cross-domain graphs and incorporates adaptive shift parameters into graph encoding. KTA then aligns cross-domain distributions within the shared class space and mitigates negative transfer by filtering outlier source classes. Experiments confirm the effectiveness of KTA in inferring the active party’s private labels and superiority over state-of-the-art attacks.

title: Memory-Aware External Facelist Calculation: A Data-Parallel Atomic Hash Counting Approach
authors: Spiros Tsalikis, Will Schroeder, Daniel Szafir, Kenneth Moreland
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Unstructured volumetric meshes serve as fundamental data representations in various scientific simulations and analyses. They play a crucial role in representing complex computational domains and are essential for important numerical techniques, such as finite element analysis. Whenever such a mesh is read from a file, streamed in-situ, or generated by algorithms, scientific visualization libraries rely on calculating the external surface of a geometry, named “external facelist”, to produce a polygonal mesh for rendering. Consequently, external facelist calculation has become one of the most widely used algorithms in the scientific visualization domain, necessitating optimal performance. In this paper, we explore relevant work on external facelist calculation algorithms in two common visualization libraries, VTK and Viskores, assess their performance and memory constraints, and introduce a novel memory-aware external facelist calculation algorithm employing an atomic hash counting approach. This algorithm fully leverages Viskores' dataparallel primitive operations, facilitating its execution across diverse many-core architectures. Our algorithm features the lowest memory footprint on the GPU and the second-lowest on the CPU among all evaluated methods, and it also delivers the fastest performance on both CPU and GPU. It has been made available under an open-source license in the VTK and Viskores visualization systems.

title: Corrective Distillation: Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting in LLM Fine-Tuning via External Correction of Self-Generated Errors
authors: Jianchun Jiang, Yingfei Qu, Hui Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) for downstream tasks often results in a significant decline in their general capabilities and safety alignment. This issue is typically attributed to a mismatch in distribution between the task-specific data and the model’s original training data. Self-distillation methods aim to address this issue by preserving the model’s own output distribution. However, their effectiveness is limited when the model itself produces erroneous reasoning or answers. In this study, we propose a simple yet effective framework, "Corrective Distillation" (CD), that leverages a more powerful external language model to optimize the seed model's outputs. Rather than completely rewriting the responses, the external model focuses on correcting factual and logical errors while largely preserving the original style. These corrected outputs are then used as supervision signals for further fine-tuning. We evaluated CD on the Llama-3-8B-Instruct model across multiple tasks, including mathematical reasoning, code generation, and tool usage. The results demonstrate that, compared to standard fine-tuning and self-distillation, CD enhances task performance while better preserving the model’s general capabilities. Furthermore, we observed that this method helps maintain safety-related behaviors and, in some cases, even slightly improves them. Overall, CD offers a practical method for improving task-specific performance without significantly compromising the model’s original alignment.

title: SpoofBuster: Adaptive Attention Transformer for Anti-Spoofing Face Recognition
authors: V. Jyothsna, Banka Lakshmi, Pasupula Ruchitha Nevas, Afkhan Mohammad Fazal Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: SpoofBuster is a face anti-spoofing approach based on a Vision Transformer, such as DeiT-B/16, with adaptive spatial, channel (CBAM), and temporal attention for liveness detection against print and replay attacks, while being designed to generalize to more complex presentation attacks such as 3D mask and makeup attacks. It consists of a RetinaFace detection with five-point alignment, ArcFace-based feature extraction, and cosine similarity for accurate matching. For the anti-spoofing branch, it utilizes the long-range dependency modeling capabilities of the ViT, which have been proven to outperform CNNs (ResNet, MobileNet) and other frequency/texture approaches (SVM, LBP, BSIF) on high-quality spoofs. Robustness is further accelerated using a Transformer-based temporal encoder with a lightweight self-supervised depth estimator with a learned logistic weighting scheme for cascade error mitigation. The approach is implemented using PyTorch, exported with ONNX, and optimized using TensorRT (INT8 QAT, pruning, layer fusion) for sub-100 ms on NVIDIA’s Jetson Orin and Xavier devices. Multi-domain training data is used, along with a focal loss, DANN, CORAL, and mixup/cutmix for cross-domain evaluation. Experiments show SpoofBuster surpasses EfficientNet-B0, pure 3D depth sensing, and server-only inference, providing a high-accuracy, low-latency, and privacy-preserving liveness detection solution for kiosks, access control, and mobile onboarding.

title: RTC-DDPM: A Physical-Feature-Guided Radar Tide Data Augmentation Model
authors: Yang Han, Xinglin Zhang, Hai Guo, Longfei Li, Pengfei Bai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Non-contact radar tide observation is easily disturbed by waves, meteorological factors, and other influences under complex sea conditions, resulting in nonlinear noise such as high-frequency abrupt spikes and sequence discontinuities, while measured samples under extreme environments are extremely scarce. This paper proposes a denoising diffusion probabilistic model guided by physical features (RTC-DDPM). The model extracts the missing ratio and instantaneous anomaly intensity of observational sequences through a physical feature perception module and injects these physical constraints into the diffusion process via the Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) mechanism, thereby achieving high-fidelity synthesis of tidal sequences under complex operating conditions. Experimental results show that the data generated by RTC-DDPM significantly outperform Vanilla DDPM and traditional GAN models in terms of RMSE, KL divergence, and physical feature consistency (PFC). Ablation experiments further verify the key role of the physical-constraint module in simulating high-frequency noise characteristics, providing a robust generative solution to the problem of sample scarcity in the field of marine observation.

title: QPPG: Quantum-Preconditioned Policy Gradient for Link Adaptation in Rayleigh Fading Channels
authors: Oluwaseyi Giwa, Muhammad Ahmed Mohsin, Folarin Jubril Adesola, Muhammad Ali Jamshed
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Reliable link adaptation is critical for 6G wireless communications in dynamic fading environments. However, classical reinforcement learning (RL) often suffers from unstable convergence due to the Euclidean geometry of the standard gradient updates, which ignores the curvature of the policy parameter space. We propose the quantum-preconditioned policy gradient (QPPG), a geometric optimization framework that approximates the quantum natural gradient (QNG) for classical link adaptation. We stress that the prefix quantum refers solely to the Fubini-Study metric and quantum-tomography-inspired regularization from which the preconditioner is derived, not to quantum hardware or exponential speedups. Evaluations across five network scenarios demonstrate that QPPG bridges the gap between quantum geometry and classical engineering. It achieves a 28.6% gain in throughput and a 43.8% decrease in transmit power compared to classical baselines, while exhibiting superior robustness to channel estimation errors and noise uncertainty.

title: ML-based Self-Healing Battery Management System for Smart Rural Renewable Energy Systems
authors: K Prithivi, N.Priyadharshini, S.Gomathy, Kabinaya K, Dharmendra V, Arul Murugan B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-based Battery Management System (BMS) for lithium-ion batteries in a solar-powered rural energy system to enhance safety, efficiency, and lifespan of the batteries. Traditional battery health assessment models are often inaccurate and invasive, and they do not provide real-time information about battery health and operation. To overcome this, the proposed system will use real-time sensing, embedded monitoring, and machine learning techniques to monitor and estimate battery degradation conditions. The Proposed BMS uses CatBoost algorithm to predict both battery state of health (SOH) and the remaining useful life of a battery (RUL) based on voltage, current, and temperature. Based on estimated health state, the system will activate adaptive control and self-healing mechanisms that manage the charging and discharging modes of operation in order to minimize the amount of stress on the battery and enhance the operational safety of the system. Further evaluation of the proposed framework indicates accurate prediction of battery health and reliable battery operation. Hence, this unique combination of embedded intelligence, renewable energy, and battery reliability creates the framework for rural electrification that is sustainable, reliable, and resilient.

title: Rule Driven Computational Framework For 3D Printed Multi Drug Polypills
authors: Lalith Krishna Vallamkonda, Sneha Gupta, Yashraj Joginder Nanda, Rajeswari D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increasing prevalence of polypharmacy among elderly patients has created a critical need for innovative drug delivery solutions, managing multiple medications daily has been associated with reduced adherence and elevated risk of adverse drug interactions. This paper presents a computational and AI-assisted framework for designing 3D-printable multi-drug polypill tablets that can merge complex prescription regimens into one single personalized tablet. The proposed system powers a drug compatibility dataset of over 500 pairwise interactions with an algorithmic spatial layer module. Exercising semi-solid extrusion (SSE) 3D printing technology with polymer matrices such as HPMC and PVP, the system generates safe multi-layer tablet geometries with controlled release profiles including immediate release (IR) and sustained release (SR). The rule-based model is evaluated across 4 to 5 datasets achieving 100% prediction accuracy. Results demonstrate 100% success in safe geometry generation across 50,000 randomized prescription tests with an average computation time of 0.5 seconds per design. Some of the things that will happen in the future are integrating hospital EHRs, simulating automatic dissolution, and validating clinical trials.

title: A Real-time Digital Active EMI Filter IC with Functionalities for Both Monitoring and Cancelling Conducted Emissions
authors: GyuHyeon Nam, Sangyeong Jeong, GyeongJin Choi, Jingook Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a digital active EMI filter (DAEF) based on an electromagnetic interference–management integrated circuit (EMIC) that provides real-time functionalities for both monitoring and cancelling common-mode (CM) conducted emissions (CE). The paper describes the configuration and core circuit elements of the custom EMIC. It evaluates the stability and performance of the proposed DAEF topology through loop gain and insertion loss (IL) measurements. The proposed DAEF is applied to a commercial 700 W-rated TV power board to experimentally demonstrate both its noise attenuation and monitoring functionalities. The DAEF achieves a maximum noise attenuation of 23.02 dBμV and reduces CM CE noise below the regulation limit line. Furthermore, it demonstrated accurate CE noise prediction by utilizing the monitored data and the extracted transfer function. Consequently, the proposed DAEF can offer an effective solution for CM CE noise monitoring and reduction.

title: Deep Fracture Recognition: Applying VGG-16 on Bone X-Rays
authors: Pallavi Bheemu, D Phanidhar Mithra, A Shouryah, B Sai Sumith, Cuddapah Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Detection of bone fractures through X-ray images is an important task in medical image analysis, requiring fast and accurate clinical identification. Intelligent computer-aided diagnostic systems can be developed to address this issue. This paper introduces a deep learning architecture for multi-class bone fracture classification using X-ray images. The proposed model incorporates two convolutional neural network backbones, namely VGG-16 and ResNet-50. The extracted feature representations are combined and optimized using a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), which focuses on fracture-related regions while suppressing background noise. The polished deep features are then categorized with the aid of a LightGBM gradient boosting classifier to enhance the robustness and computational power of the predictive model. To solve the problem of insufficient and imbalanced medical datasets, data augmentation and class balancing techniques are used during training. Moreover, Grad-CAM++ visualization has been added to enhance transparency in the medical field. Empirical comparison of the proposed framework on a multi-class bone fracture X-ray dataset shows that the proposed model outperforms baseline CNN models and traditional ensemble methods, achieving an accuracy of 96.3% along with higher precision, recall, and F1-score. The results demonstrate that the suggested system is effective and reliable for automated fracture detection.

title: RENI++: A Rotation-Equivariant, Scale-Invariant, Natural Illumination Prior
authors: James A. D. Gardner, Bernhard Egger, William A. P. Smith
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Inverse rendering is an ill-posed problem. Previous work has sought to resolve this by focussing on priors for object or scene shape or appearance. In this work, we instead focus on a prior for natural illuminations. Current methods rely on spherical harmonic lighting or other generic representations and, at best, a simplistic prior on the parameters. This results in limitations for the inverse setting in terms of the expressivity of the illumination conditions, especially when taking specular reflections into account. We propose a conditional neural field representation based on a variational auto-decoder and a transformer decoder. We extend Vector Neurons to build equivariance directly into our architecture, and leveraging insights from depth estimation through a scale-invariant loss function, we enable the accurate representation of High Dynamic Range (HDR) images. The result is a compact, rotation-equivariant HDR neural illumination model capable of capturing complex, high-frequency features in natural environment maps. Training our model on a curated dataset of 1.6K HDR environment maps of natural scenes, we compare it against traditional representations, demonstrate its applicability for an inverse rendering task and show environment map completion from partial observations. We share our PyTorch implementation, dataset and trained models

title: Decomposed Multi-Modality Fusion: Integrating Frames and Events for Efficient Visuomotor Policies
authors: Haoran Xu, Peixi Peng, Canming Xia, Guang Tan, Yiqian Chang, Xinyi Zhang, Luntong Li, Yonghong Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Multi-modality visuomotor policies, typically driven by complementary visual sensors such as frame-based RGB cameras and event-based Dynamic Vision Sensors (DVS), have demonstrated strong potential in adverse illumination and high-speed scenarios. Most existing approaches either adopt conventional computer-vision-style feature-level fusion to derive a unified intermediate representation for subsequent value estimation and policy learning, or estimate modality importance separately from a value-level perspective. However, they still face challenges in effectively removing modality-specific task-irrelevant information, leading to suboptimal decisions based on noisy representations. To address this issue, we propose a Decomposed Multi-Modality Representation (DMR) framework that explicitly learns combined task-relevant features (co-features) while separating RGB- and DVS-specific task-irrelevant components. The co-features are trained under task-relevant supervision, while modality-specific components are modeled through reconstruction and InfoNCE-style contrastive objectives to ensure information completeness and discriminability. In addition, existing exploration strategies in reinforcement learning typically encourage transition instability within each modality independently, which often leads to inefficient exploration with limited state-space coverage and low sample efficiency. Therefore, we introduce an intrinsic reward based on Modality Transition Discrepancy (MTD) computed on DMR representations. By quantifying discrepancies between RGB and DVS transition dynamics, MTD promotes exploration of states with meaningful cross-modality differences. Extensive experiments on the CARLA platform and the DDD20 benchmark demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse traffic conditions in both reinforcement learning and imitation learning settings.

title: Multi-View Graph-Based Code Representation Learning for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Zheng Yuan, Chun Shan, Pengzhe Heng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Vulnerability detection is essential for ensuring software reliability and security. Existing learning-based approaches often fail to fully capture fine-grained program semantics and heterogeneous structural dependencies. In particular, directly merging different code representations into a unified graph may introduce semantic ambiguity and weaken vulnerability-specific signals. To address this issue, we propose SMGCN, a semantic-aware multi-view graph framework for vulnerability detection. SMGCN independently models abstract syntax, control flow, and data flow as separate graph views, preserving their structural characteristics. A node importance recalibration mechanism is introduced to emphasize vulnerability-relevant nodes, and an attention-based fusion module adaptively integrates multi-view representations into a unified embedding for classification. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that SMGCN consistently outperforms representative learning-based and LLM-based baselines, achieving average improvements of 7.18% in accuracy and 11.0% in F1-score. Additional analyses further validate the effectiveness of multi-view modeling and node importance calibration. These results highlight the advantage of semantic-aware multi-view graph learning for robust vulnerability detection.

title: Routing and Scheduling of Hybrid Traffic for URLLC Industrial Network Under Timing Analysis_supp1-3691880.pdf
authors: Pengcheng Zhu
publish: N/A
abstract: The convergence of ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) with industrial networking has been envisioned as a leading solution for industrial internet of thing (IIoT) deployments, which increases flexibility without moderating requirements of latency and reliability. The industrial sites throughout the network demand hybrid flows composed of time-triggered (TT) traffic and event-triggered (ET) traffic, requiring stringent QoS metrics of low end-to-end (E2E) latency, minimal jitter, and low packet loss probability. However, ET traffic bears serious timeout risks, stemming from the resource preemption by TT traffic under the prioritized transmission mechanism. Motivated by the spatial and temporal isolation for alleviating overtime pressure, we propose the QoS-aware resource allocation as well as the routing and scheduling co-design for the E2E latency compression of hybrid traffic under constraints of timeliness, reliability, and determinism. To be specific, the timing analysis framework based on network calculus is established to compute the worst-case end-to-end latency (WCEL) of ET traffic. Following by that, the non-overlapping routing and as-soon-as-possible (ASAP) scheduling with radio resource adaptation is proposed to reduce the E2E latency, where the path planning selects disjointed path with minimal length from spatial domain, the time offset assignment arranges the earliest possible time offsets from temporal domain, and the resource adaptation allocates the required bandwidth, subchannels and thresholds of decoding error probabilities based on the convexity method. Simulation results validate the WCEL analysis and show the performance gain on E2E latency compression of the proposed co-design compared with other existing strategies.

title: Toward Automatic GI Tract Intervention With a Novel Shape-Aware Robotic Endoscope
authors: Guang Feng, Yuesheng Qu, Qi He, Tianjun Yang, Jianxin Cui, Bo Wei, Baojun Chen, Siyang Zuo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Endoscopic procedures often subject endoscopists to heavy workloads and significant radiation exposure, while inappropriate maneuvers can potentially cause patient discomfort or clinical complications. This work presents a novel robotic endoscope system that integrates shape perception and depth estimation for autonomous navigation in complex gastrointestinal (GI) environments. The system features a custom-designed endoscope integrated with the fiber Bragg gratings (FBGs) sensor for shape tracking and a monocular camera for visual input. The FBGs enable real-time endoscopic shape perception through precisely reconstructing the endoscopic pose during dynamic interventions. A unified control framework is proposed to enhance the system’s generality by integrating depth estimation and shape perception. The direction and velocity of endoscopic intervention are adaptively synchronized. Highly realistic GI phantoms and in vivo animal experiments demonstrate that the system successfully performs autonomous interventions in complex GI environments, with a bending angle error of 2.75$\boldsymbol{^{\circ}}$ and a mean pixel tracking error of 74.2 pixels. This work advances robotic endoscopes by enhancing perceptual accuracy and autonomy, demonstrating significant potential for clinical applications in endoscopic examinations and procedures.

title: Boundary-Aware Multi-Task Lightweight Framework for Automated Kidney Stone Segmentation and Classification in CT Images
authors: Vijayakumar N, Harish M, Manoj Kumar D, Sehnaaz I T, Yazhini S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) imaging is widely used for diagnosing kidney stone disease; however, manual interpretation of CT scans is time-consuming and subject to inter-observer variability. Automated detection systems based on deep learning can assist clinicians by improving diagnostic efficiency and accuracy. This study proposes a boundary-aware multi-task lightweight deep learning framework for automated kidney stone segmentation and classification in axial CT images. The framework integrates an enhanced U-Net architecture for precise stone segmentation with a MobileNetV3-based classifier for efficient stone classification. To improve the delineation of small and irregular stone structures, a boundary-aware loss function based on gradient-based edge supervision is incorporated into the segmentation objective. The segmentation output is further used to extract the region of interest for classification, enabling a unified multi-task learning strategy that improves feature sharing and reduces computational redundancy. Experimental evaluation on the axial CT kidney stone dataset demonstrates improved performance, achieving a Dice score of 0.92, IoU of 0.84, classification accuracy of 93%, and AUC of 0.97. The lightweight design and improved boundary precision make the proposed framework suitable for real-time clinical applications and deployment in resource-constrained healthcare environments.

title: GSEA-UAD: A Geometric Subspace–Enhanced Autoencoder for Unknown Sensor Attack Detection in Autonomous Unmanned Vehicles
authors: Yanze Wang, Xinyuan Wang, Ruixi Ji
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Reliable detection of unknown sensor attacks is essential for ensuring the operational safety of autonomous unmanned vehicles. Existing deep learning (DL) based intrusion detection methods are typically designed under closed-set assumptions, which restrict their capability to recognize emerging threats. To improve the discriminability of latent features and enhance the separability of unknown attack patterns, a geometric subspace–enhanced autoencoder (AE) is developed in this study. Sensor characteristics in both time and frequency domains are extracted through a dual-domain feature enhancement encoder, where multi-order differentials, learnable spectral features, and adaptive attention mechanisms are integrated to extract data features effectively. A geometric subspace constraint is incorporated into the latent space, where normal data and known attack types are distributed within class-specific linear subspaces. Projection distances to these sub-spaces are minimized to encourage intra-class compactness, while separation between subspaces is increased to maintain inter-class discriminability. Unknown attack detection (UAD) is achieved through a dual-metric protocol that jointly considers reconstruction error and subspace projection distance. Experiments conducted on the Safety Pilot Model Deployment (SPMD) dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves accurate identification of known attack types while effectively detecting unseen attacks, showing improved detection stability compared with advanced sensor anomaly detection models.

title: Loss-Tolerant RDMA Network over Commodity Devices
authors: Likai Wang, Zhe Wang, Yimu Yuan, Siyu Yan, Shuhan Tian, Ning Liu, Hui Yuan, Linghe Kong, Qiao Xiang, Shizhen Zhao, Di Qu, Hexiang Song, Xiaolong Zheng, Yashar Ganjali, Guihai Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: This paper proposes the concept of a “loss-tolerant” RDMA network, instantiating as NüWa. It reveals the fundamental issues under a lossy fabric — packet losses and repetitive retransmission timeouts (RTOs) cause severe performance degradation and even service interruption. The loss-tolerant RDMA must avoid “important” packet losses that trigger RTOs. However, existing loss-protection mechanisms fail to identify these packets precisely. They either generate massive misprotection or ignore selective repeat loss recovery, resulting in buffer overflows and failures of RTO protection. To tackle these issues, NüWa thoroughly analyzes distinct loss-recovery schemes and RTO reasons for commodity NICs. It designs switch mode and NIC mode to accurately identify and protect all important packets. The switch mode inherently supports the widely deployed non-programmable NICs, while the NIC mode offloads identification complexity to advanced programmable NICs. With effective RTO avoidance, it improves flow completion time (FCT) by 2 ∼ 10× compared to state-of-the-art (SOTA) solutions. In severe incast and large-scale networks, it reduces FCT by 100× compared with a lossless fabric. In storage applications, N¨uWa improves IOPS by ∼ 300% compared to vanilla lossy fabric. For typical AI Workloads, it accelerates AllReduce/AlltoAll communication by 5.5 ∼ 13.7× compared to SOTA lossy network solutions.

title: Classroom Behavior Detection Method Based on KMU-YOLO11n
authors: Jiahao Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This paper introduces KMU-YOLO11n, a lightweight object detector based on YOLOv11, designed to address the challenge of real-time student behavior detection in complex classroom environments. Through systematic architectural innovations, the model addresses the high computational complexity and limited real-time performance that hinder the deployment of existing methods on resource-constrained edge devices. Experimental results on a classroom behavior dataset show that KMU-YOLO11n maintains high accuracy while substantially reducing model complexity. Compared with the baseline YOLOv11n, it reduces the parameter count to 2.36M, computational cost to 3.3 GFLOPs, and model size to 4.9 MB, corresponding to reductions of 8.53%, 47.62%, and 5.77%, respectively. In terms of accuracy, it achieves an mAP50 of 95.71% and an mAP50-95 of 66.89%, matching the performance of the baseline. In summary, KMU-YOLO11n successfully achieves accurate, lightweight, and real-time classroom behavior detection.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning Framework for Multi-Level Crowd Density Classification in Pedestrian Surveillance Datasets
authors: Gayathri V, Gnana Ruba J, Jacklin Dasan J, A Meenapriyadharsini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Crowd monitoring is becoming increasingly essential for public safety, particularly for preventing crowd disasters that have resulted in numerous casualties worldwide. Traditional crowd monitoring systems rely on manually-set static thresholds that fail to prioritize safety-critical scenarios. This paper describes an adaptive reinforcement learning method of the multi-level classification of the crowd density in the pedestrian surveillance scene. The system presented integrates person detection with YOLOv8 in real-time and a tabular Q-learning agent, which is trained to determine the best decision boundaries using labelled surveillance data as the input. The state space of the crowd conditions is represented as a six-level space, which is categorized into three levels of alert, NO_ALERT, WARNING and EMERGENCY with an asymmetric reward structure that corresponds to the real-life safety priorities by weighing the penalties associated with missed alarms against the false alarms. Assessment of the UCSD pedestrian dataset indicates that the proposed system has 100 percent emergency detection recalls on the test data, as opposed to 90.5 percent on the stationary threshold baseline, which is an improvement in the most safety-critical case situation by 9.5 percentage points. The system has been supported by a rate of 112 frames per second, which proves practicability in real-time implementation. Full transparency Explicit Q-values give detailed disclosure of all alert decisions, and interpretability of safety critical AI applications.

title: Secure and Efficient Data Synchronization for Digital Cousins in Edge–Cloud Environments
authors: Zhurong Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The proliferation of data-intensive applications in edge–cloud environments poses significant challenges for secure and efficient data synchronization. Traditional digital twin–based approaches incur high computational and communication overhead due to frequent, full-scale synchronization with physical entities. To address these issues, this paper proposes DCCrypt, a novel encryption-based synchronization framework for digital cousins (DCs). DCCrypt integrates homomorphic encryption (HE) and secure multi-party computation (SMPC) to enable privacy-preserving distributed aggregation, supporting computation directly over encrypted data while maintaining high synchronization efficiency. Extensive experiments on large-scale edge–cloud deployments demonstrate that DCCrypt achieves significantly lower latency and higher throughput than conventional encryption schemes and a digital twin–based benchmark (DTCrypt), while reducing security overhead. These results indicate that DCCrypt effectively balances data confidentiality, computational efficiency, and scalability, providing a practical solution for secure data synchronization in dynamic edge–cloud systems.

title: Speed-Robust Feature Learning and Minor-Class Generation for Imbalanced Variable-Speed Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Lijuan Zhao, Haidong Shao, Junyu Qi, Yi Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Variable-speed operation and class imbalance are two prevalent challenges in rolling bearing fault diagnosis; However, existing methods seldom tackle both simultaneously, which limits their diagnostic effectiveness. To address the above issues, this article proposes a variational information bottleneck-based speed-robust feature learning and minor-class feature generation (VIB-FGNet) for imbalanced fault diagnosis under variable-speed conditions. The proposed method extracts speed-invariant fault representations in the latent space through the designed multiple constraints, thereby effectively suppressing condition-related disturbances. On this basis, a class-level feature generation strategy is applied in the shared cross-speed latent space, dynamically augmenting minority-class fault features via reparameterized sampling to effectively alleviate sample imbalance. Experimental results demonstrate that across different variable-speed and sample-ratio settings, the proposed method exhibits significant advantages over state-of-the-art approaches, validating its effectiveness for variable-speed and imbalanced fault diagnosis tasks.

title: ShelfNet: Dynamic Shelf-Life Modeling for Fruits and Vegetables using LSTM and IoT Sensors
authors: Peddarapu Rama Krishna, Itikala Divya, Kaushik Shamagatla, Modala Poojitha, Vellanki Chenchu Dedeepya, Saindla Rohith
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Post harvest losses of fruits and vegetables continue to be a major issue in global food supply chains, resulting in economic loss, food insecurity, and sustainability concerns. Conventional shelf life assessment is based on static expiry dates and periodic human inspections, which is not for real time environmental conditions encountered during storage and transportation. This study introduces the ShelfNet as an IoT enabled and LSTM based framework for predicting the dynamic shelf life of fruits and vegetables. ShelfNet continuously monitors temperature, humidity, and ethylene gas concentrations with low cost IoT sensors installed across the cold chain. A Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) model is used to clean, normalize, and process the acquired remaining shelflife in real time. ShelfNet dashboard converts are predictions into clear visuals and warnings to perform operational decision making. Shelf Net exceeds all existing static expiry based methods with a mean absolute error of less than one day, according to simulated experimental evaluation. The approach suggested is more feasible, scalable, and prepared for practical application in cold chain management and smart agriculture.

title: Lightweight YOLOv5 with Wavelet Convolution and Attention for Waste Detection
authors: Shunjie Wang, Zhengju Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Waste object detection models often suffer from high computational complexity, which limits their deployment efficiency in practical applications. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight YOLOv5-based framework for waste detection. The Ghost module is adopted to reduce redundant feature generation and improve computational efficiency. In addition, a wavelet convolution (WTConv) module is introduced to enhance feature representation by capturing spatial – frequency information. An efficient multi-scale attention (EMA) mechanism is further integrated to strengthen feature interaction across channels and spatial dimensions. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves detection accuracy while reducing model complexity. Compared with the baseline YOLOv5s model, the proposed framework increases mAP@0.5 from 84.1% to 87.1%, while reducing the number of parameters from 7.03M to 4.01M and decreasing the computational cost from 15.8 GFLOPs to 8.6 GFLOPs. These results demonstrate that the proposed method provides an effective solution for accurate and efficient waste detection.

title: Deep Residual Multiscale Hyper-Node Graph Networks for Adaptive IoT Cybersecurity
authors: Natrayan L, Dr.M.Sivaramkrishnan, Kiruthika Balakrishnan, M.M.Jegan, Anand Rajendran, Ramya M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increasing complexity of cyber-attacks in smart IoT systems has necessitated the development of advanced detection systems. To solve this issue, Adaptive Cyber Attack Detection in IoT using Deep Residual Multiscale Hyper-node Graph Processing Network (DRMCNN-HRGN) is presented. First, network traffic data is gathered from the NSL-KDD and BoT-IoT datasets, which include various attack scenarios. Subsequently, the gathered data goes through a preprocessing phase, in which Nadam optimization is used for feature selection, followed by data normalization to improve learning efficiency. Subsequently, the preprocessed information is passed on to the Deep Residual Multiscale Convolutional Neural Network (DRMCNN) that identifies multilevel hierarchical patterns and the Hyper-node Relational Graph Attention Network (HR-GAT) that identifies intricate interdependencies between instances of attack. Nonetheless, because hyperparameter tuning still continues to be an essential challenge in the optimization of model performance, Snow Ablation Optimizer (SAO) is incorporated in order to optimize model parameters dynamically. Cyberattacks on IoT networks are then classified in real time using the optimized model. In the end, the proposed DRMCNN-HRGN framework is implemented on Keras on TensorFlow and tested on the basis of 99.9 % accuracy. Experimental results show remarkable enhancements in attack detection, providing an efficient and reliable intrusion detection system for intelligent IoT networks.

title: Design of Permanent Magnet Linear Synchronous Motor With PCB Windings
authors: Qinfen Lu, Yutong Lin, Tenglong Feng, Yu Ji, Yanxin Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This paper investigates a small permanent magnet linear synchronous motor (PMLSM) with printed circuit board (PCB) windings. Firstly, the topology structure is designed including the PCB windings and slot pole combination. Secondly, the electromagnetic performances are investigated through finite element method (FEM). With the aim of maximizing thrust force, the structure parameters of magnetic poles are optimized. Thirdly, the thermal performances are analysed and compared at armature current with amplitude 3A when the installation platform is made of thermal insulating material or iron steel. By analysis the thermal performance under different current density, the design criterion of current density for PCB winding is proposed. Finally, the prototype is made and tested, and then both the electromagnetic and thermal performances are validated by the measurements. The research provides the reference for the design of PMLSM with PCB windings.

title: E-Navi: Environmental-Adaptive Navigation for UAVs on Resource-Constrained Platforms
authors: Boyang Li, Zhongpeng Jin, Shuai Zhao, Jiahui Liao, Tian Liu, Han Liu, Yuanhai Zhang, Kai Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The ability to adapt to changing environments is crucial for the autonomous navigation systems of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). However, existing navigation systems adopt fixed execution configurations without considering environmental dynamics based on available computing resources, e.g., with a high execution frequency and task workload. This static approach causes rigid flight strategies and excessive computations, ultimately degrading flight performance or even leading to failures in UAVs. Despite the necessity for an adaptive system, dynamically adjusting workloads remains challenging, due to difficulties in quantifying environmental complexity and modeling the relationship between environment and system configuration. Aiming at adapting to dynamic environments, this paper proposes E-Navi, an environmental-adaptive navigation system for UAVs that dynamically adjusts task executions on the CPUs in response to environmental changes based on available computational resources. Specifically, the perception-planning pipeline of UAVs navigation system is redesigned through dynamic adaptation of mapping resolution and execution frequency, driven by the quantitative environmental complexity evaluations. In addition, E-Navi supports flexible deployment across hardware platforms with varying levels of computing capability. Extensive Hardware-In-the-Loop and real-world experiments demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms the baseline method across various hardware platforms, achieving up to 53.9% navigation task workload reduction, up to 63.8% flight time savings, and delivering more stable velocity control.

title: Energy Efficiency AI Models Through Quantization and Lightweight Architecture Design
authors: N. Saikiran, Boya Kumudini Naidu, Challa Madhu Latha, L.H.Karthik Sai, Manjula Kumar Shankar, G. Ravikumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: New levels of accuracy have been achieved thanks to the advancement of AI, especially with deep learning. But new levels of accuracy entails the tremendous cost of processing. The implementation of these models on resource-constrained devices like edge devices and embedded systems is challenging. The proposed optimization strategy combines lightweight CNNs with a two-step structured pruning and post-training quantization. In contrast to conventional optimizations based on a single method, depth-wise separable convolutions and global average pooling are employed to lower arithmetic complexity before quantization in the proposed system. The MNIST handwritten digits dataset demonstrates that results with model size were reduced from 2688 KB to 12.6 KB and training time was reduced from 416.30s to 296.55s. This work presents a scalable approach for deploying AI in an energy efficient manner, demonstrating that a small drop in accuracy can yield significant savings in memory usage and energy consumption on low-power hardware.

title: Adaptive Rhythm Control Optimization via Multi-Source Keyboard Performance Data Fusion for Intelligent Music Production
authors: Hua Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive rhythm control optimization framework based on multi-source keyboard performance data fusion. This framework primarily addresses the challenge of rhythm modeling in intelligent music generation relying on a single modality and failing to characterize the dynamic features of performance. The method first constructs a unified multi-source rhythm representation space, encoding the symbolic layer, acoustic layer, behavioral layer, spatial layer, and control layer across modalities. Based on this, a five-dimensional fusion tensor structure is designed. This innovation enables strict alignment of multi-source data in the temporal dimension and completes temporal calibration of heterogeneous data through cross-source neighborhood consistency constraints. Furthermore, a multi-source attention fusion mechanism is introduced. This mechanism models the cooperative and conflict relationships between different modalities through interaction terms. At the rhythm modeling level, this study proposes a fusion-driven beat fingerprint function. This process characterizes the acceleration features of rhythm changes through multi-source response weighting and second-order time derivative enhancement terms. Simultaneously, cross-source consistency constraints are combined to improve the stability of multi-modal responses. At the generation and control layers, a hidden Markov rhythm dynamic model under fusion feature conditions is constructed. Simultaneously, the variational autoencoders and Wasserstein generative adversarial networks are combined to achieve rhythm latent space modeling and distribution alignment. Finally, the fused features are introduced into the reinforcement learning control framework. The experiments were conducted using the self-built multi-source keyboard performance dataset MKPD-Set. Results show that, compared to single-modal fusion methods, the proposed method reduces beat offset error (BOE) by approximately 28.2%. It also achieves significant improvements in rhythm consistency (FC) and generation diversity (GD). Even under 30% noise conditions, rhythm accuracy (RA) remains at a high level. This result demonstrates stronger robustness compared to the Transformer model (RA=0.801, BOE=39.5). Furthermore, subjective evaluation results show that the proposed method scores higher in rhythmic naturalness and expressiveness.

title: Harmonic Electromagnetic Neural Networks for Task-Oriented Semantic Communications
authors: Xiangyu Ding, Jiancheng An, Hao Liu, Lu Gan, Victor C.M. Leung, Mérouane Debbah
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIM) provide a promising physical architecture for efficient deployment of semantic inference in wireless systems. However, existing SIMs in practical semantic communication (SemCom) systems are limited to phase-only control, yielding limited frequency-domain degrees of freedom and insufficient capacity for hierarchical semantic encoding under constrained parameter and energy budgets. To overcome this, we propose a harmonic electromagnetic neural network (EMNN) built on SIMs, which introduces multi-harmonic modulation at each meta-atom to expand frequency-domain degrees of freedom beyond phase-only control. In addition, a multi-scale sliding-window source-encoding scheme is introduced to improve robustness against spatial variations of the system. The following layers perform frequency mixing for semantic encoding, progressively generating higher-order harmonics for additional degrees of freedom. Importantly, the multi-harmonic components are further fused into the center harmonic, producing an effective nonlinear representation gain that strengthens feature separability. To support passive implementation and improve calibration, passivity-oriented normalization and a temperature-like scaling are further adopted. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms harmonic-free baselines by 10% in classification accuracy.

title: Dynamic Model of Intentional Topic Propagation Control Based on Micro-Emotion Guidance
authors: Yunpeng Xiao, Qingyun Wei, Qian Wu, Ke Huang, Chaolong Jia, Tun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Studying the propagation law of intentional topics is crucial for understanding the propagation trend of topics. Considering the orientation of intentional topics and the changes of users’ emotional states, this article proposes a dynamic model for the propagation control of intentional topics based on micro-emotional guidance. First, considering the psychological bias in the topic network, an emotional response mechanism is proposed based on the Bayesian linear regression model. Second, considering the trust issues that may be triggered by intentional topics, this article introduces a suspicion mechanism, which incorporates the user’s verification state(V) and constraint state(C) into the propagation process to analyze the user’s attention to the topic and the propagation effect. Meanwhile, the Ising model is used to describe the probability distribution of user states and the SVICR model is constructed. Finally, considering the problem that positive and negative feedback of intentional topics will bring uncertain effects, based on the annealing cooling algorithm, an improved propagation control model is proposed to optimize the propagation probability and the recovery probability, so as to improve the propagation situation of the topic. According to the experimental results, the model proposed in this article can effectively describe and control the propagation dynamics of intentional topics.

title: Unbalanced Ternary Full Adder Architecture in CNTFET Technology: Design and Application
authors: Shams Ul Haq, Erfan Abbasian, Abdolreza Darabi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Full adders are the fundamental building blocks of arithmetic units, directly influencing system performance and efficiency. Transitioning from binary to ternary computation offers substantial improvements in energy efficiency. This paper proposes a novel ternary full adder (TFA) architecture implemented with 42 transistors using multi-threshold carbon nanotube FET (CNTFET) technology. Circuit-level simulations performed in Synopsys HSPICE at 0.9 V demonstrate that the proposed TFA achieves a reduction of 35.8%–75.2% in power consumption and 21.6%–70% in energy consumption compared to state-of-the-art designs. Reliability analysis, based on extensive Monte Carlo simulations, confirms that the proposed architecture exhibits the lowest sensitivity to variations in key CNTFET process parameters. These advantages come at the expense of a moderate delay overhead and a 1.85× increase in layout area relative to a highly compact design. To further validate its practical impact, the TFA is evaluated in an image-processing application leveraging ternary logic based on a hardware algorithm. The results indicate up to 38.86% energy savings while preserving accuracy and output quality. Additionally, a comprehensive benchmarking framework is introduced, combining hardware efficiency and quality metrics into a unified figure of merit (FoM). Under this FoM, the proposed design outperforms the best existing counterpart by approximately 76.21%. The demonstrated trade-off between energy efficiency and accuracy highlights the proposed CNTFET-based TFA as a promising candidate for the design of future high-performance and energy-aware ternary computing systems.

title: SEED: Structure-Entropy and DCT Enhanced Descriptor for Robust 4D Radar Place Recognition_supp1-3693934.mp4
authors: Lihui Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Reliable place recognition in adverse weather remains a critical challenge for autonomous navigation. While 4D millimeter-wave radar provides robust sensing capabilities, its data is characterized by sparsity, multipath noise, and measurement uncertainty. To address these challenges, we propose SEED, a two-stage method for 4D radar place recognition. We first organize static radar returns in a polar voxel map and compute an entropy-weighted feature that combines density and geometric cues to suppress unstable clutter while enhancing informative structures. We then project the voxel map into range-elevation and range-azimuth matrices, from which compact descriptors are extracted using truncated DCT. A hierarchical search first performs coarse retrieval and then refines matches. Experiments on multiple 4D radar datasets, including snowy and unstructured scenes, show that SEED outperforms the compared baselines while maintaining real-time performance on embedded hardware.

title: Enhancing Rotated Object Detection in Remote Sensing with a Parallel Hybrid Attention Mechanism
authors: Chi-Yi Tsai, Huan-Wei Hsu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate detection of rotated objects in remote sensing imagery remains challenging due to densely distributed targets, complex and cluttered backgrounds, and high foreground-background similarity. These factors amplify boundary ambiguity and noise interference, necessitating detection frameworks with enhanced robustness and precision. This study presents a Parallel Hybrid Attention (PHA) module that advances feature extraction for rotated object detection. Unlike conventional sequential attention designs, PHA integrates the Shift Window-Mixed Attention Mechanism (SW-MAM) and Coordinate Attention (CA) within a parallel fusion architecture, effectively preserving complementary spatial-channel information and mitigating feature degradation during transmission. To strengthen multi-scale feature representation, PHA is embedded into a Path Aggregation Feature Pyramid Network (PAFPN), facilitating reliable detection across diverse object scales and rotation angles. Comprehensive experiments on four benchmark datasets, MVTec, HRSC2016, HRSC2016-MS, and DOTA-v1.0, demonstrate the proposed method’s superior performance, achieving mean Average Precision (mAP) scores of 0.920, 0.971, 0.800, and 0.807, respectively. These results confirm PHA’s ability to maintain high detection accuracy in dense and cluttered scenes, establishing it as a scalable and generalizable solution for advanced rotated object detection in remote sensing applications. Our code is available at https://github.com/RVL224/PHA_YOLO2.

title: DeepVault: An NTRU-Based Data Hiding Scheme for Securing Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Ming Xu, Tongtong Guo, Ling Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: To ensure the security and reliability of confidential image transmission in underwater acoustic sensor networks (UASNs), we propose an NTRU-based data hiding scheme for securing UASNs, coined as DEEPVAULT. The DEEPVAULT is novel in three aspects. First, we propose an (r, n)-threshold Vandermonde matrix-based secret sharing (VMSS) technique, which enhances data recoverability and resistance to loss by leveraging the linear independence of Vandermonde matrices, effectively dealing with communication interruptions in UASNs. Second, we design a data hiding method based on NTRU polynomial mapping, which fully utilizes the algebraic properties of NTRU polynomials to embed secret data into encrypted coefficients. It not only enhances data concealment and security but also provides strong resistance against quantum attacks, offering robust security for image transmission in UASNs. Finally, we propose a reinforcement learning (RL)-based environment-aware optimization mechanism that adaptively adjusts the embedding factors of data aggregators according to current channel conditions, which achieves a dynamic balance among embedding rate (ER), robustness, and reliability in dynamic underwater acoustic environments. Experimental results demonstrate that DeepVault not only offers significant advantages in protecting image confidentiality, but also achieves an optimized trade-off between ER and bit error rate (BER) compared to existing schemes. Moreover, it exhibits excellent environmental adaptability and robust data security assurance in UASNs.

title: Benchmarking Lightweight YOLO Architectures for Real-Time Eggshell Defect Detection Toward Augmented-Reality Inspection in Poultry Supply Chains
authors: Adnan Miski
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Ensuring the integrity of eggshells across poultry supply chains is vital to minimize economic losses and mitigate the risk of pathogen ingress. Manual visual inspection remains inconsistent, labor-intensive, and increasingly inadequate for modern production volumes. This study conducts a comprehensive comparative evaluation of lightweight deep-learning models for automated eggshell defect detection, assessing their suitability for deployment on resource-constrained augmented-reality (AR) smart glasses. A mixed dataset was assembled by combining 186 privately collected images of brown cage-free eggs with 794 publicly available images of primarily white eggs. Ten architectures, comprising nine lightweight detectors across the YOLOv5, YOLOv8, YOLOv9, and YOLOv10 families alongside a transformer RT-DETR-l baseline, were benchmarked. The YOLOv8n model achieved the highest detection accuracy with a 5-fold cross-validated mAP@0.5:0.95 of 91.2%, demonstrating no statistically significant difference relative to RT-DETR-l. However, YOLOv10n provided the optimal accuracy-efficiency balance for AR deployment as it delivered a mAP@0.5:0.95 of 89.5% and a proxy CPU inference time of 34.2 ms, while an INT8-quantized TFLite export reduced its memory footprint to 2.59 MB. By providing a mixed-egg dataset and a thorough benchmark of lightweight detectors, this comparative study identifies YOLOv10n as the most suitable architecture for real-time AR-based inspection. These findings advance precision agriculture by outlining an efficient, empirically validated pathway toward hands-free, AI-driven quality control.
