title: Cryptography of Radiology Images for PACS system using DWT and Advanced Encryption standard with Swarm Intelligence based Optimization
authors: P. Suresh Venugopal, D..Sasikala, G.Sudha, Ravindrakumar Selvaraj, Remya Bharathy K.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Image Processing and cryptography is a domain that revolves around performing operations on digital radiology images transmitted for telemedicine system. The field of medicine has the potential to benefit from integrating such hybrid methods for telemedicine for secured data transmission. Image pre-processing, enhancement, and extraction are widely used to analyze specific data from radiology images like. X-ray and computed tomography (CT). In this paper a proposed method for cryptography in radiology image for telemedicine which increases the data security is done. Initially the message to be transmitted is encrypted by Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) algorithm. Before hiding the encrypted data in the image, it is transformed to frequency domain using DWT algorithm. After performing all the above operation, the image is sent to the receiver. The receiver side combines all the shares and decrypt them to extract the original message. The Spatial domain information can be converted to frequency domain information with support of Wavelets transform (WT) where the cryptography increases the security. In Image steganographic model, the wavelets transform partitions of low and high frequency information are represented in form of representation. For implementation MATLAB and Quartus tool were used. FPGA kit to be used for future implementation.

title: Timely reliability evaluation and optimization of wireless sensor networks considering channel capacity randomness and energy depletion
authors: Ning Wang, Tianzi Tian, Li Yang, Changzhen Zhang, Lujie Liu, Jun Yang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The real-time and reliable transmission of data packets is a critical foundation for ensuring Internet of Things applications. Therefore, how to ensure the timely reliability of wireless sensor networks has become a hotspot. However, existing timely reliability models often overlook the impacts of energy depletion and channel capacity randomness on wireless transmission. Additionally, most evaluations focus on single-hop, single-path scenarios, while practical data transmission typically requires multi-hop and multi-path strategies. To overcome the above shortcomings, this study conducts the timely reliability evaluation and optimization of wireless sensor networks considering channel capacity randomness and energy depletion. First, focusing on data transmission delay modeling, this study emphasizes the effects of energy depletion and channel capacity randomness on wireless data transmission, and further proposes a timely reliability evaluation model based on the G/G/1 queuing model. Secondly, to tackle the computational challenges of multi-hop and multi-path data transmission, this study proposes a timely reliability solving algorithm that integrates the binary decision diagrams with Monte Carlo simulation.. Building on these foundations, this study develops a periodic optimization model for signal transmission power, balancing sensor lifetime and network transmission performance. Finally, taking the military Internet as an example, the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified.

title: Numerical investigation of plated and disc blower performance and its optimization for portable computing device applications
authors: Amit Kumar, Ratul Mali, Abhilasha Niyogi, Srinivas Rao Konakalla, Mark MacDonald, Anandaroop Bhattacharya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Thermal management of mobile computing devices plays an important role in the performance and reliability of their electronic components. Centrifugal blowers are the most commonly used system-level cooling solution for these devices. In this paper, novel designs of centrifugal blowers called Plated Blowers (PB) and Disc Blowers (DB) are proposed for cooling laptop computers, where the conventional rotor blades are replaced by a new design either comprising of circular sheet metal as a rotor with punched holes and residual hanging chads or discrete blades formed along the disc beside each hole that works to displaces air. It had a similar working principle as Volumetric Resistance Blower (VRB), where the porous disc is replaced by randomly distributed small structures across a circular plate which moves the air within the blower. A numerical study was conducted on these designs to determine the flow rates under an operational angular speed of 6000 RPM. The results showed that both PB and DB designs can outperform the traditional blade fan design as well as the VRB in terms of air volume flow rate at isorpm conditions(not necessarily iso-acoustic). Like VRB which has a porous rotor, the newly discussed designs are also expected to offer a significant acoustic advantage over traditional bladed blowers, which can enable an increase in overall platform power in these mobile computing systems.

title: DFF-Net: A Dual-Branch Feature Fusion Network for River Ice Mapping via the Fusion of Microwave and Optical Imagery
authors: Haojie Li, Yuzhong Wang, Zhen Liu, Liankun Huang, Donghang Shao, Tianwen Feng, Xiaohua Hao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: River ice mapping in high-altitude cold mountainous regions is critical for understanding ice processes and preventing disasters. However, persistent cloud and snow cover often impede accurate extraction from optical satellite imagery. To overcome these challenges, a deep learning model for river ice extraction named the dual-branch feature fusion network (DFF-Net), which is based on multifeatured fusion and an attention mechanism, is proposed in this study. In this model, through the addition of different attention mechanisms, features are effectively extracted from multisource data, including optical imagery, synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data, the normalized difference snow index (NDSI), and a digital elevation model (DEM). These features are synergistically fused at both the feature and decision levels. We conducted comparative experiments on DFF-Net, NDSI, RDRI, and U-Net in clear-sky, cloud-covered, and snow-covered scenarios. The results indicate that DFF-Net achieves the best performance across all the considered accuracy metrics. Compared with those of NDSI, RDRI, and U-Net, its mIoU in the clear-sky scenario increased by 0.1198, 0.0561, and 0.045, respectively; in the cloud-covered scenario, its mIoU improved by 0.2563, 0.2959, and 0.0613, respectively; and in the snow-covered scenario, its mIoU improved by 0.2542, 0.179, and 0.073, respectively. Furthermore, the mean boundary error of DFF-Net across all three scenarios was only 3.599 pixels, thus demonstrating outstanding overall performance. The DFF-Net model effectively integrates the strengths of multiple features, thereby significantly increasing the accuracy of river ice extraction with cloud and snow distributions, and it demonstrated a strong ability to map river ice extent over large spatial scales. This study provides a reliable technical solution for high-frequency river ice monitoring in high-altitude cold mountainous regions and can support river ice research in cold regions.

title: Augmenting Human Dance Performance With a Robot Arm: An Incremental Dynamic Mapping Method
authors: Alberto Villani, Giuseppe Saviano, Domenico Prattichizzo
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine
abstract: In artistic applications of robots as dancing bodies, dimensionality reduction techniques like principal component analysis (PCA) are commonly used to project complex human motion into low-dimensional embedding spaces, making it suitable for robots with fewer degrees of freedom. However, standard PCA requires access to complete motion trajectories and fails to account for temporal correlations, thereby limiting real-time adaptability and restricting the dancer’s improvisational freedom.

title: MigrRDMA: Enabling RDMA Live Migration in the Software
authors: Xiaoyu Li, Weizhe Zhang, Fengyuan Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Live migration is critical to ensure services are not interrupted during host maintenance in data centers. On the other hand, RDMA has been widely adopted in data centers, and has attracted both academia and industry for years. However, live migration of RDMA is not supported in today’s data centers. Although modifying RDMA NICs (RNICs) to be aware of live migration has been proposed for years, it relies on extra hardware support. This paper proposes MigrRDMA, a software-based RDMA live migration system. MigrRDMA provides a software indirection layer to achieve transparent switching to new RDMA communications. Unlike previous RDMA virtualization that provides sharing and isolation, MigrRDMA’s indirection layer focuses on keeping the RDMA states on the migration source and destination identical from the perspective of applications. We implemented the MigrRDMA prototype over Mellanox RNICs. Our evaluation shows that MigrRDMA adds little downtime when migrating a container with live RDMA connections running at line rate. Besides, the MigrRDMA virtualization layer only adds 2% ∼ 9% extra overhead in the data path. When migrating Hadoop tasks, MigrRDMA only incurs an extra 3-second job completion time.

title: Using the Schur Product to Solve the Code Equivalence Problem
authors: Michele Battagliola, Rocco Mora, Paolo Santini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Given two linear codes, the Code Equivalence Problem asks to find (if it exists) an isometry mapping one code into the other. A special case is the Permutation Equivalence Problem (PEP), where the isometry must be a permutation. The hardness of PEP is crucially dependent on the hull of a code, that is, the intersection between a code and its dual. Indeed, most of the known algorithms have running time that grows exponentially with the hull dimension. Since random codes have very small hull with large probability, PEP is deemed easy for random codes. In this paper we study how the so-called Schur product between linear codes can be employed to solve PEP. The basic idea is to transform a given PEP instance by computing the square of the given codes. While it is well known that the square code operation preserves equivalence between linear codes, we show that, regardless of the hull dimension of the starting codes, their square codes have trivial hull with high probability. Furthermore, we show that as long as the code rate is sufficiently low, no additional permutations mapping the square codes exist with high probability. This effectively generates a new pair of equivalent codes with trivial hulls, where the underlying permutation remains identical to that of the original instance. This observation allows us to leverage existing hull-based attacks to recover the permutation for the square codes, and consequently, for the original codes. Furthermore, we improve this attack by exploiting the structural relationship between hulls: if a permutation maps two codes, the same permutation also maps their respective hulls. We show that by considering the square of the hull as a code in its own right, its hull also becomes trivial with high probability. This allows for the identification of new weak instances of PEP, leading to an attack whose complexity no longer depends on the initial hull dimension, as it is the case of most known algorithm. In particular, we show that our attack achieves average polynomial-time complexity (since the square of the hull, when seen as a code, intersects with its dual in a low dimensional space with large probability) as long as k < √2n or h < √2n, where n, k, and h denote the code length, dimension, and hull dimension, respectively. We corroborate our analysis, which relies on some (plausible) heuristics, with intensive numerical simulations. As a concrete application, we consider the updatable encryption scheme proposed by Albrecht, Benčina, and Lai at Eurocrypt 2025. All the recommended instances fall into the range of weak PEP instances identified in this paper; hence, they are susceptible to our attack. As a demonstration, we successfully recover the secret permutation for two of the instances claiming 128 bits of security in about 10 minutes on average on a laptop. As a fix, instances with hull dimension h >√2n should be employed.

title: Lightweight Neural Network Implementation for Vehicle Detection
authors: Haitao Lv, Dejun Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To achieve the embedded deployment of a vehicle detection model, this paper constructs a dataset by selecting 12 traffic-related categories from COCO2017 and completes model training based on YOLOv3-tiny. To address model lightweighting, channel pruning based on BN layer γ parameters is employed, BN layers are fused with convolution layers, and histogram and box plot analyses are used to determine a symmetric quantization method with an integer bit-width of 4 and a fractional bit-width of 8. Ultimately, a pruning ratio of 1/4 is chosen, achieving a halving of the parameter count at the cost of a slight decrease in mAP, meeting the deployment requirements for FPGA.

title: HandInsight: Reinforcement Learning for Generative Hand Anomaly Localization
authors: Yizhong Zhang, Tong Tong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Although image generation models have approached highly photorealistic human synthesis, the hand region remains particularly prone to structural anomalies due to complex kinematic constraints. Accurate detection and localization of hand-related generation anomalies are crucial for the evaluation and iterative optimization of generative models. The strong cross-modal understanding capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) offer a promising new direction for addressing this problem; however, they exhibit limitations in precise spatial localization and structured output generation, and supervised fine-tuning of MLLMs relies heavily on large-scale annotations while demonstrating limited generalization ability. To this end, we propose HandInsight, a reinforcement learning framework based on GRPO, which is designed to explore and enhance the spatial perception potential of MLLMs for hand anomaly localization. Experimental results show that, with only a very small amount of training data, HandInsight achieves a qualitative improvement in localization accuracy by enabling the base Qwen2.5-VL model, which originally fails completely at localization, to perform precise identification, and it demonstrates superior performance and stronger cross-model generalization capability compared with Supervised Fine-tuning (SFT).

title: Application Research of Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Yinshan Jiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the continuous advancement of big data and computing infrastructure, coupled withongoing innovations in algorithm models, artificial intelligence (AI) technology hastransitioned from laboratory research to large-scale industrial applications, emerging as thecore driver of digital economy development. Machine learning, as the foundational support of AI, empowers computers to autonomously extract patterns from data through algorithms, enabling predictive modeling and decision-making. Deep learning, a critical extension of machine learning, leverages multi-layer neural networks to simulate the information processing mechanisms of the human brain, demonstrating unparalleled advantages incomplex data mining and feature extraction. Currently, machine learning and deep learning have been widely applied in various fields such as financial risk control, medical diagnosis, industrial manufacturing, intelligent transportation, and smart homes. These technologies have given rise to a series of new business models and operational approaches, significantly enhancing production efficiency and optimizing service quality. However, during practical implementation, challenges such as inconsistent data quality, insufficient model generalization capabilities, high deployment costs, and lack of interpretability remain, which limit the full realization of their technological value. In light of this, this paper focuses on the applications and practices of machine learning and deep learning, thoroughly analyzes key implementation stages and existing bottlenecks, and explores optimization pathways, demonstrating significant theoretical and practical value.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Graph-based Hybrid Deep Learning Methods for Robust Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Shiv Ashish Dhondiyal, Sushil Chandra Dimri, Devendra Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Detecting epileptic seizures from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals is indeed a complex task due to the complicated spatio-temporal patterns and inter-channel dependencies within brain activity. The relationships are better captured by mixed ordering and interleaved data, which do not fit well in either traditional machine learning or standalone deep learning models resulting in performance below par. To overcome these limitations, this work introduces a graph-based hybrid deep learning architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Graph Neural Networks (GNN) into a single framework which is ultimately used for seizure classification. Unlike traditional approaches, the proposed method exploits EEG signals as graph-structured data (where nodes correspond to channels and edges are defined by their functional connectivity), allowing an efficient learning of spatial dependencies. The CNN part published discriminative spatial features and the LSTM learned temporal dynamics of EEG sequences. Then, the GNN is applied to refining the learned inter-channel dependencies through graph-based message passing. These features are concatenated and processed through two fully connected layers with a softmax activation for classification. Experimental evaluation on the Bonn EEG dataset shows that it achieves an accuracy of 99.12% outperforming conventional and hybrid models indicating its potential application in automated diagnosis for epilepsy at an early stage.

title: Transformer-based Multimodal Framework for Real-Time Disaster Tweet Classification and Misinformation Detection
authors: K.Lakshmi, M.Baby Vineetha, S.Shaima Safath, K.Bhumika, M.Sudhiksha, V.Lilly Grace
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing use of social media in time of disaster has facilitated the development of in-time information and dissemination; nonetheless, the fact that there are many noisy and misguiding information that is posted presents a major challenge to situational awareness that is reliable. The current methods mainly concentrate on the text-based analysis or offline multimodal processing and are limited in the dynamic, real-time contexts. The issue that will be considered in this paper is the joint disaster tweet classification and misinformation detection in real-time streaming settings. In this regard, a transformer-based multimodal framework is suggested that incorporates both textual and visual modalities via cross-modal consistency-oriented fusion mechanism. The model utilizes a single multi-task learning approach to predict relevance and content credibility of the disaster and support low-latency inferences based on real-time application. Benchmark test which was done with the proposed model shows that the model has an accuracy of 94.6% and F1-score of 93.8% in disaster classification and 92.9% in misinformation detecting. Besides, the framework sustains an average latency in inference of 31.6 ms per tweet, which implies the handling efficiency when going streaming. They suggest that the suggested method is effective in balancing predictive performance and computational efficiency, which offers a solution to the real-time disaster monitoring and decision support system on a scalable and reliable basis.

title: Preliminary Study on 3D Visualization of Marine Parcels for Marine Cadastre Implementation in Sarawak
authors: Muhammad Fahim Supian, Abdullah Hisam Omar, Muhammad Hafiz Mohd Yatim, Muhammad Syazwan Ab Razak, Fiq Schsyarenndy Asni, Awang Zamhari Awang Mahmood, Ramzi Abdillah
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: This paper presents a preliminary study on the visualization of three-dimensional (3D) marine parcels and marine space as a foundational step toward developing a 3D marine cadastre in Sarawak, Malaysia. The increasing complexity of marine spatial usage, including aquaculture, seabed infrastructure, and recreational activities, necessitates a system capable of managing overlapping rights and responsibilities across vertical zones. To address this, the study integrates multi-source geospatial technologies, namely Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) positioning, Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) photogrammetry, and bathymetric surveys. It is used for the accurate delineation and modelling of marine parcels spanning the seabed, water column, and surface. Two representative pilot sites were selected: Batang Ai, an inland reservoir used for aquaculture. Tanjung Batu is a dynamic coastal zone influenced by tidal changes. Spatial data were processed and visualized using ArcScene to generate 3D marine parcels, with each volumetric marine parcel representing specific legal and functional zones. The resulting models effectively illustrated vertical zoning potential. They demonstrated how volumetric spatial units can be structured for multi-use marine cadastre applications. The outcomes of this study contribute to the growing body of research on 3D marine cadastre and support Malaysia’s broader goals of modernizing ocean governance, improving marine spatial data infrastructure, and advancing legal frameworks for integrated marine rights management.

title: Attendance Management using Deep Face Detection and Recognition
authors: A Mohamed Anwar, Killi Naveen Kumar, Vasireddy Sudheer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Traditional attendance systems depend on manual roll call methods together with ID card and fingerprint scanner systems which create operational delays that lead to attendance errors because students can enter class without their attendance being recorded. This project creates an Attendance Management System which achieves attendance tracking through Face Detection and Recognition technology that uses computer vision and machine learning processes to complete its functions. The system establishes real time video streaming through a camera system which uses face detection algorithms to identify human faces. The facial features which the system extracts undergo processing through a trained face recognition model which matches individual faces against a previously stored database. The system records student attendance automatically after it identifies a match in the input of the student name and date and time to the database without any human intervention. The solution uses contactless technology which requires minimal processing time and provides reliable outcomes to achieve result accuracy. The system allows users to handle student records while producing attendance reports because they can convert data into different assessment formats. The system functions in classrooms and laboratories and offices and all other institutional spaces. This project develops an attendance tracking system which operates on Python and OpenCV and deep learning facial recognition technology to create a secure and scalable attendance control mechanism which increases operational efficiency while decreasing cheating incidents.

title: Statistical-mechanical analysis of adaptive filter with dead-zone nonlinearity
authors: Seiji Miyoshi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper develops a statistical-mechanical characterization of a least-mean-square (LMS) adaptive system with a piecewise-linear dead-zone nonlinearity applied to the filter output under a white-input assumption. Unlike prior analyses that replace the dead-zone by a differentiable surrogate (e.g., an error-function approximation) for tractability, we treat the original piecewise-linear characteristic exactly.We derive a deterministic description of the macroscopic learning dynamics and, in particular, obtain analytically derived steady-state fixed-point equations. The steady-state mean-square error (MSE) is obtained by solving these equations via numerical root finding, without numerically integrating the macroscopic ordinary differential equations. Monte Carlo simulations show that the proposed analysis agrees well with empirical learning curves and steady-state behavior over a wide range of conditions, and it notably improves accuracy in challenging regimes with wide dead-zone widths and large step sizes where approximation-based theories exhibit visible deviations.We also reproduce the non-monotonic dependence of the steady-state MSE on the dead-zone width observed under high background noise and large step sizes.

title: Phase Noise Impact Analysis on Sub-THz 6G Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) Systems at 240 GHz
authors: Priyanka Joshi, Sandeep Kumar Sunori, Manoj Chandra Lohani, Govind Singh Jethi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Developments in the sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems should be based on sub-terahertz (sub-THz) frequency bands to provide ultra-high data rates, massive connectivity, and integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). But operation at very high carrier frequencies like 240 GHz generates severe hardware degradation especially phase noise (PN), and this is of critical importance to communication reliability and sensing accuracy. This article is a performance analysis of a 6G sub-THz ISAC system with respect to the presence of different phase noise levels as well as the waveform settings. A Python (using Google Colab) simulation framework simulates a 240 GHz carrier at 2 GHz bandwidth and at various OFDM subcarrier rates (512, 1024, and 4096). Bit Error Rate (BER) analysis is used in the evaluation of communication performance of a 16-QAM system under varying variances of phase noise. Analytical results are used to account for the effective Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) degradation through phase noise into the BER formulation. To carry out sensing evaluation, the range estimation error is calculated based on a Cramér–Rao Bound (CRB)-inspired methodology, which is the trade-off between subcarrier separations, symbol time, and the accumulation of phase noise. As shown by simulation result, a large increase in phase noise variance leads to a substantial decrease in BER performance, at high SNR values where phase noise is the most significant impairment. It can be further enhanced by adding more subcarriers which enhance spectral resolution but increases the sensing error as a result of a build-up of phase noise with longer symbol lengths. A distinct trade-off between spectral performance, reliability of communications and sensing accuracy is observed. The spectral efficiency analysis is further demonstrated to be achievable, which underscores the performance constraints of oscillator instability at sub-THz frequencies.The results offer essential information on the design aspects of the waveform in 6G ISAC systems that include the necessity of the latest phase noise mitigation strategies, the sophisticated waveform optimization, and system-level hardware modeling. The research is relevant to realizing the knowledge of sub-THz system impairments, and contributes to the process of building reliable and correct 6G integrated communication and sensing architecture.

title: Design and Implementation of an AI-Powered Smart Railway Gate Control System using Image Processing
authors: V. Govindaraj, P.Gayathri, D.Nivitha, G.Sugan, Ramesh.C, Balasubramanian.S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Railway crossings that do not have a staffed crossing operator are often higher in the number of accidents due to lack of proper supervision of crossing gates and delayed or malfunctioning gates. In this project, the first phase of the project consisted of developing a simulated train detection system that used both image and audio processing. In the second phase, we will build the hardware system which will consist of an ESP32 microcontroller, servo motor-based automatic gate mechanism, LCD display and a warning alert for the safety of people who may be near the crossing gates. Once the train has been detected, the automatic gate will close, and an audio warning system will activate followed by a related safety message on the LCD display. Once the train passes, the gate will automatically reopen and no human intervention required. From the results of the hardware testing, we have observed improved response times and reliable gate operation. The prototype created for this project provides a low-cost option to increase safety at railroad crossings without existing safeguards.

title: Speed Sensor-based Predictive and Fast Secondary Control for Stable Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer in EV Charging Systems
authors: Vijayalakshmi R, Dharshan Sri P, Keerthi S, Sasikala N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) technology enables electric vehicles (EVs) to charge while in motion, eliminating stationary charging time and enhancing driving range. However, continuous vehicle movement causes frequent variations in magnetic coupling between transmitter and receiver coils, resulting in voltage ripple, power instability, and reduced charging efficiency. This paper proposes a stabilised DWPT system incorporating an LCC compensation network, a speed sensor–based predictive controller, and a fast secondary DC–DC control strategy. The LCC network improves resonant power transfer and minimises reactive losses, while the predictive controller utilises real-time vehicle speed information to anticipate coupling variations and proactively regulate inverter operation. A fast secondary controller further suppresses residual disturbances and ensures constant output voltage. MATLAB/Simulink simulations compare system performance under uncompensated, compensated, and controlled conditions. Results demonstrate significant ripple reduction and stable DC output near the reference value, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed approach for reliable dynamic EV charging.

title: Adaptive Micro-sleep Scheduling for Battery less IoT Sensors using Energy-Harvesting-Aware Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ben Sujin, M.Prabu, D.Joseph Jeyakumar, S. Mahaboob Basha, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Ambient energy-powered Battery less Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, which only operate when a sensor detects an environment, be it solar, vibration or radio frequency (RF) energy, provide a sustainable answer to large-scale sensing applications. However, the operation reliability is greatly restricted because of intermittent and unpredictable availability of energy. Energy management techniques are hence critical in order to extend the sensor uptime and consistent running of tasks. In this paper, an adaptive framework of micro-scheduling of micro-sleep in battery less IoT sensors that is grounded on energy-harvesting-aware reinforcement learning (RL) is presented. The proposed architecture allows the sensors to change their sleep and active states dynamically based on learning the best policies that take into account energy harvesting changes as well as task requirements. Comprehensive simulations have proven that the RL-based strategy outperforms fixed and heuristic-based scheduling strategies by a large margin in the aspects of operational availability, energy utilization efficiency and task completion rates. The findings affirm the possibility of the integration of adaptive RL methods and energy-harvesting-conscious design to facilitate long-term and maintenance-free deployment of battery less IoT devices.

authors: Prajjwal Nijhara, Jainan Tandel, Rohit Prajapati, Dip Sankar Banerjee

title: CoDec: Prefix-Shared Decoding Kernel for LLMs
authors: Zhibin Wang, Rui Ning, Chao Fang, Zhonghui Zhang, Xi Lin, Shaobo Ma, Mo Zhou, Xue Li, Zhongfeng Wang, Chengying Huan, Rong Gu, Kun Yang, Guihai Chen, Sheng Zhong, Chen Tian
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Towards an Intelligent Terminology Assistant: A Framework Integrating Small Language Model with Graph RAG
authors: Shansi Zhang, Min Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are well-suited to serve as intelligent assistants, owing to their powerful capabilities across various domains. However, their substantial computational requirements render them difficult to deploy in resource-limited environments. In this work, we aim to build a lightweight intelligent assistant targeting terminology tasks by combining small language models (SLMs) with knowledge graph-based retrieval-augmented generation, which enables efficient inference on edge devices or CPUs. First, we employ an LLM with carefully designed prompts to extract entities and analyze their relationships from source documents, constructing a knowledge graph where nodes represent terminologies and edges denote their semantic connections. During inference, the SLM extracts keywords from user queries, retrieves relevant information from the knowledge graph through keyword matching, and subsequently generates the final response. Extensive evaluation results demonstrate that our framework yields responses of superior quality across various terminology tasks, significantly outperforming baseline methods.

title: AI-Driven Continuous Authentication for Strengthening Security and Scalable Analytics in IoT-based Digital Institutions
authors: A Jegatheesan, Anitha Gopalan, B S Berlin Jeba, P. Selvaraju, Rajasekar M., S. Dhanalakshmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: With the initial explosive expansion of new Internet of Things (IoT) deployments, there is an unprecedented need to have analytics models that can utilize relatively small amounts of labeled data and that can scale to an enormous variety of applications and environments. Conventional methods of supervised learning demand large labelled datasets per novel IoT application, which provides the cost of data labelling and model construction prohibitive. In this paper, a new architecture that uses self-supervised foundation models to permit data-efficient and scalable IoT analytics is proposed. The proposed architecture will use contrastive learning, masked autoencoding, and temporal consistency goals to pretrain a single foundation model using large-scale unlabeled IoT data and then transfer it to a variety of downstream tasks with only a small number of labelled samples. A new multimodal pretraining approach unites time series informing, sensor metadata informing, and environmental context informing into a common embedding area, taking into account a broad appearance of semantics of IoT datum. The framework presents a lightweight model-adaptation system by tuning five percent of foundation model parameters to new tasks, which, with labelled data requirements reduced 100 times, can achieve state-of-the-art performance. Experimental analysis of 15 data sets of IoT benchmarking shows that the proposed foundation model can be evaluated with 92.4 percent accuracy, using only 10 labelled examples in each class, which is 47 percent higher than the performance of supervised baselines and is equivalent to fully-supervised performance with 10 times less labelled data. The results make self-supervised foundation models a revolutionary paradigm of data-efficient and scalable analytics of IoT.

title: Lightweight and Hybrid CNNs for Effective Cloud Detection from Satellite Images
authors: Prathik Kumaar.B, A.Deepak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Clouds in satellite images reduce the quality of remote sensing data and can affect many applications, such as environmental monitoring and weather analysis. Detecting clouds is an important step before using satellite images for further studies. In this work, two convolutional neural network models, MobileNetV2 and InceptionResNetV2, are used to classify satellite images into cloudy and clear classes. A dataset of satellite images was collected and resized for training and testing. Both models were trained using the same experimental setup, and their performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and confusion matrix. MobileNetV2 achieved an accuracy of 94 %, while InceptionResNetV2 achieved 92 %. MobileNetV2 also required less computation and showed faster processing compared to InceptionResNetV2.The results show that lightweight models such as MobileNetV2 can perform well for cloud detection tasks, even with a limited dataset. Future work would evaluate advanced models such as ConvNeXt, followed by cloud segmentation and removal methods, to improve the quality of satellite images.

title: Configuration-Aware Automated Design of Single-Stage BJT Amplifiers using Supervised Regression
authors: Manoj Shridhar Gudi, Vandith Yeri, Shantanu Patil, Sujata Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Designing an analog amplifier involves many subtle trade-offs. This paper introduces a framework that uses data and takes configuration into account for the automated design of single-stage BJT amplifiers. Unlike traditional methods that treat all amplifier topologies the same, this approach clearly distinguishes between Common Emitter (CE), Common Collector (CC), and Common Gate (CG) configurations. For CE amplifiers, the framework achieves high predictive accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> =0.886). In contrast, CC and CG configurations show negative R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values due to similar designs for each configuration, which reflect basic circuit-level uncertainties rather than issues with the model itself. The results offer valuable insights into when machine learning can be useful in analog circuit design. They also point out the physical limits of methods based on regression. The framework includes E24 resistor standardization and automated schematic generation.

title: Deep Learning-based Framework for Social Media Mining and Sentiment Analysis
authors: M. Gokuldhev, Gopalasetti Prabhash, M. Pavan Sujay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rapid growth of social media platforms has led to an exponential increase in user-generated data. This content provides important insights into public opinions, trends, and consumer behavior. However, extracting useful information from such a large volume of unstructured and noisy text data is a tough job. This paper presents a Deep Learning-based Social Media Mining The proposed framework integrates advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques, including sentiment analysis and topic modeling. social media posts accurately as positive, negative, or neutral, and frequency-based analysis to identify trending topics. The framework is trained and tested on a large dataset of social media posts and performs better than traditional machine learning techniques. Experimental results show better accuracy, understanding, and real-time analytical capabilities. This framework provides an effective and scalable solution for analyzing social media data for business intelligence and decision-making.

title: Hand2Voice: Increasing Human Interaction using Gesture and Voice Translation
authors: Mythily V, Deepa B, Olimathi K M, Priyadarshikaa E, Ragavendiran S, Sathiyasudhan M S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research study intends to develop am automated transcribing sign language system. The proposed device connects the members of the disabled community. The proposed deep learning and computer vision approach is a hybrid system that performs the static and dynamic sign language interpretation. A speech synthesis module enables without losing content communication to both ways: identification of signs/words.We carried out a metric investigation (using accuracy, precision and F1 score) to evaluate the technology in real life conditions. After testing on more than 10,000 samples, we found that the proposed system rated the test samples accurately 97.7% of the time. The reliability of this application was found to be high, regardless of the conditions used to test it. The proposed system worked fine in different testing environments. This application is appropriate for educational setting, smart-classroom and any place, where barriers of communication exist between hearing impaired and non-hearing impaired person.

title: DiffHash: Text-Guided Targeted Attack via Diffusion Models against Deep Hashing Image Retrieval
authors: Zechao Liu, Zheng Zhou, Xiangkun Chen, Tao Liang, Dapeng Lang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Deep hashing models have been widely adopted to tackle the challenges of large-scale image retrieval. However, these approaches face serious security risks due to their vulnerability to adversarial examples. Despite the increasing exploration of targeted attacks on deep hashing models, existing approaches still suffer from a lack of multimodal guidance, reliance on labeling information and dependence on pixel-level operations for attacks. To address these limitations, we proposed DiffHash, a novel diffusion-based targeted attack for deep hashing. Unlike traditional pixel-based attacks that directly modify specific pixels and lack multimodal guidance, our approach focuses on optimizing the latent representations of images, guided by text information generated by a Large Language Model (LLM) for the target image. Furthermore, we designed a multispace hash alignment network to align the high-dimensional image space and text space to the low-dimensional binary hash space. During reconstruction, we also incorporated text-guided attention mechanisms to refine adversarial examples, ensuring them aligned with the target semantics while maintaining visual plausibility. Extensive experiments have demonstrated that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) targeted attack methods, achieving better black-box transferability and offering more excellent stability across datasets. The code is available at https://github.com/Raineasy/DiffHash.

title: NB-IoT based Real-Time Manhole Monitoring System with Trash Removal and Automatic Gas Exhaust Mechanism
authors: Sentamilselvi M, Suganthraj P, Sujith P, Suryaprakash K, Tharunis T K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Urban sewer and drainage systems of smart cities provide essential services that protect public safety, maintain environmental cleanliness and support urban infrastructure. Existing methods for manhole monitoring use scheduled inspections together with user-reported problems to identify dangerous situations, which include gas leaks and water spills and structural damage. The system developed in this research uses a solar-powered NB-IoT monitoring system to achieve real-time monitoring, which detects dangerous conditions through its multi-sensor system and automatic gas ventilation system and garbage collection system. The system uses a microcontroller-based multi-sensor framework to monitor essential parameters, which include water level, gas concentration, temperature, and manhole cover tilt. The system uses a solar-powered battery charging unit to provide continuous power for its operations, which require minimal energy consumption in both remote and underground locations. The sensor data undergo processing before being sent to a cloud monitoring platform using NB-IoT communication, which enables real-time monitoring and alert creation. The automatic exhaust system decreases dangerous gas buildup, while the trash filtration system effectively stops drainage system blockages. The proposed system improves urban infrastructure protection because it needs fewer manual checks while offering an energy-efficient solution that can grow to meet the needs of smart city sewer monitoring systems.

title: A Time-Decay-Based Recommendation Algorithm for Vulnerability Fixers
authors: Jiayin Wei, Ting Liang, Jianqiang Han, Lin Yao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Existing vulnerability fixer recommendation methods typically match vulnerabilities with fixers based on the similarity between vulnerabilities and fixers derived from historical fixing records. However, these approaches suffer from two main limitations: they fail to account for the temporal dynamics of fixer expertise reflected in time-varying characteristics, and they overlook the collaborative relationships among vulnerability fixers. To address these issues, this paper proposes a time-decay-based vulnerability fixer recommendation model. First, a vulnerability–fixer bipartite graph is constructed, in which collaborative relationships among fixers are explicitly incorporated into the graph structure. Second, a modified Ebbinghaus forgetting function is introduced to apply temporal decay to the vulnerabilities previously fixed by each fixer, assigning higher weights to more recent interactions. This design effectively captures the evolution of fixer expertise over time. Finally, a lightweight graph convolutional network (LightGCN) is employed to learn more comprehensive vulnerability and fixer representations, which are then used for recommendation, enabling the selection of fixers that better meet real-world vulnerability fixing requirements. Extensive comparative experiments are conducted on three public datasets—EclipseJDT, LibreOffice, and Mozilla. The experimental results demonstrate that incorporating temporal information significantly improves the effectiveness of vulnerability fixer recommendation, leading to recommendations that better align with the actual fixing needs of vulnerabilities.

title: ML-based Abstract Power Modeling for Embedded System Processors
authors: Katayoon Basharkhah, Zainalabedin Navabi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: System-level power analysis is a critical requirement in modern embedded systems and mobile devices. This work focuses on enabling detailed power estimation for embedded processor cores within a core-based system-level design environment. Traditional RTL or gate-level simulation, while accurate, is too slow to support early system level decision making. To address this limitation, power modeling has emerged as a promising but challenging solution. This work introduces a novel, automated methodology for fast and cycle-accurate power modeling of embedded processor cores. The key innovation lies in using a minimal feature set mainly based on processor control signals, which significantly reduces modeling overhead. To ensure generality, we employ a residual-based model that combines linear regression and decision tree techniques to capture residual error information, making the model highly adaptive to unseen data. To obtain a power model, we explore the modeling space across various modeling alternatives, evaluating their performance in terms of accuracy and modeling effort. Experimental results demonstrate that our model achieves a cycle-by-cycle power prediction error of less than 3% and a simulation speed of 6.6 Mcycles/s. The proposed methodology is integrated within an automated framework, streamlining both dataset generation and model training processes. This automation ensures efficiency and scalability, significantly reducing manual effort for the IP developer.

title: Learning Distributional Differences in High-Dimensional Seismic Data using Modified Variational Autoencoders
authors: Tiash Ghosh, Aurobinda Routray, Mamata Jenamani, Sanjai Kumar Singh
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Seismic data plays a critical role in several geo-physical tasks. However, the statistical properties of seismic datasets vary significantly across geographical regions, leading to distributional shifts that undermine the reliability of traditional algorithms. In this paper, we propose a deep generative framework based on variational autoencoders to learn the underlying probability distributions of high-dimensional seismic data. By modeling the underlying latent representations from geologically distinct regions, we demonstrate how the proposed method can effectively capture distributional shifts. Such differences in the underlying data distributions often affect the performance of traditional seismic interpretation algorithms. Visual comparison and multivariate statistical tests of the latent space confirm the distributional differences between seismic data of different regions. Hence, we highlight the potential of VAEs as seismic data descriptors, offering a probabilistic approach to model the variability across complex high-dimensional seismic datasets.

title: Prior Based Latent Denoising Diffusion Learning for Weak Seismic Signal Reconstruction
authors: Yu Sang, Siman Li, Xiaoli Lu, Cong Tang, Kang Chen, Yunan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Ineffective recognition of weak seismic signals limits fine structural interpretation and reservoir prediction in seismic exploration. Although seismic signal reconstruction has made significant progress, the widespread issue of low Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) remains a major challenge in recovering weak seismic signals from noisy field data. To address this, we propose a prior-based Latent denoising Diffusion model for weak Seismic signal Restoration (LDSR). First, we design a Multi-Scale Information Distillation Attention (MSIDA) network to extract discriminative features from noisy inputs and aggregate the multi-scale spatial information, ensuring more stable model convergence. Second, to effectively establish continuous relationships among features in the latent space, we develop a forward Markovian restoration process involving multiple diffusion iterations. In each iteration, we integrate the MSIDA network and latent neural representation into a unified end-to-end framework. Third, we leverage Non-Subsampled Shearlet Transform (NSST) sub-bands to represent high-frequency prior, which is expected to enhance the details of reconstructed seismic signals. When evaluated on synthetic and logging forward data, our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies validate the effectiveness of each component in LDSR. When processed on real field data, LDSR successfully restores more effective weak signals near the target layers.

title: Hybrid Frame-Enhanced LiDAR/GNSS/IMU Fusion for Accurate and Robust Mapping in Mixed Traffic Environments
authors: Yunshui Zhou, Hongmao Qin, Dengxiang Chang, Runbang Zhang, Shengjie Huang, Yougang Bian, Manjiang Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) plays a pivotal role in enabling autonomous driving systems to operate in mixed-traffic environments, where autonomous vehicles (AVs) coexist with human-driven vehicles (HDVs) and vulnerable road users (VRUs). Such environments introduce challenges including sensor occlusion, interaction dynamics, and long-term drift under degraded signals of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS). This paper presents a hybrid frame-enhanced LiDAR/GNSS/IMU fusion framework that provides robust localization and mapping to support safe and efficient autonomous driving in complex traffic. First, a novel hybrid frame-based local mapping scheme is proposed, integrating recent consecutive and historical keyframes. The recent consecutive frames are added to a sliding window and tightly coupled with Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) measurements, effectively mitigating IMU drift due to the shorter time intervals between frames. Second, we design an effective keyframe selection method based on geometric similarity and motion state estimation, which reduces the number of keyframes while preserving sufficient geometric information for robust LiDAR frame registration even during rapid motion. Finally, to guarantee global consistency and suppress long-term drift, pose graph optimization is employed to integrate GNSS data, keyframe pose estimated by local mapping, and loop closure detections, while incorporating covariance information that reflects the varying accuracy of different sensors. Extensive experiments on real-world dataset demonstrate that our method achieves centimeter-level accuracy in global mapping and robust performance under challenging conditions.

title: Influence of &#x03BC;m-level profile slopes on vacuum DC flashover: a model guided design optimization_supp1-3691149.docx
authors: Sile Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Surface microstructures (<1 mm) have important influences on the surface insulation strength of insulating materials, which are typically quantified by surface roughness. However, conventional roughness parameters, such as the arithmetic mean height of profile (Ra), cannot distinguish complex microstructures, potentially resulting in different insulation performances of materials with the same roughness. This study proposes a parameter, the sum of unit-length profile slopes (denoted as Rk), which is suitable for characterizing the surface roughness, especially the surface with a structure of regular grooves. Alumina ceramic samples with the same Ra and different Rk are obtained by laser surface grooving, and the effect of Rk on surface insulation performance is studied systematically. It is found that as Rk increased, the surface conductivity showed an upward trend, the surface potential decay of the samples accelerated, the secondary electron yield decreased, and the vacuum flashover voltage increased. Properly adjusting Rk can effectively enhance the strength of the surface insulation of alumina ceramic. The combination of Ra and Rk can serve as a useful tool for the design of surface structures on insulating materials.

title: RAGExplorer: A Visual Analytics System for the Comparative Diagnosis of RAG Systems
authors: Haoyu Tian, Yingchaojie Feng, Zhen Wen, Haoxuan Li, Minfeng Zhu, Wei Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The advent of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has significantly enhanced the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to produce factually accurate and up-to-date responses. However, the performance of a RAG system is not determined by a single component but emerges from a complex interplay of modular choices, such as embedding models and retrieval algorithms. This creates a vast and often opaque configuration space, making it challenging for developers to understand performance trade-offs and identify optimal designs. To address this challenge, we present RAGExplorer, a visual analytics system for the systematic comparison and diagnosis of RAG configurations. RAGExplorer guides users through a seamless macro-to-micro analytical workflow. Initially, it empowers developers to survey the performance landscape across numerous configurations, allowing for a high-level understanding of which design choices are most effective. For a deeper analysis, the system enables users to drill down into individual failure cases, investigate how differences in retrieved information contribute to errors, and interactively test hypotheses by manipulating the provided context to observe the resulting impact on the generated answer. We demonstrate the effectiveness of RAGExplorer through detailed case studies and user studies, validating its ability to empower developers in navigating the complex RAG design space.

title: Camera-Radar Data Based Vision Language Model for Automotive Driving Scene Description
authors: Pavan Aakash Rangaraj, Tunc Alkanat, Ashish Pandharipande
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Multiple sensor modalities like camera and radar are used to support automated driving applications. In order to train machine learning models, it is crucial to collect data across diverse operational design domains (ODDs) during data acquisition. We propose CRaFT-SD (Camera Radar Fusion for Textual Scene Description), a multi-modal visual language model (VLM) architecture with camera and radar data inputs to describe ODDs in both sensor domains. Specifically, the proposed sequential VLM architecture uses a fine-tuned radar VLM with range-azimuth maps as input to generate a text input, which is fused with a VLM with image input to create a unified text scene description. The obtained text scene descriptions characterize ODD attributes like weather type, road condition, target class, and radar clutter attributed to objects while capturing meaningful range and azimuth cues. The proposed approach is evaluated using the RADIal and CARRADA public datasets to showcase model performance across diverse ODD scenarios qualitatively and using quantitative metrics.

title: Design and Control of Centimeter-Scale Reconfigurable Aquatic Modular Robots_supp1-3693985.mp4
authors: Kevin Macauley
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents the Centimeter-scale Autonomous Reconfigurable Platform (CARP), a low-cost, self-assembling Autonomous Surface Vehicle (ASV) designed for modular and cooperative maritime operations. CARP features a 90 mm square top section with four independent active latching mechanisms, enabling dynamic connection and disconnection between units. The vehicle's motion is controlled using a Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR)-based optimal controller with weak formulation-based dynamic parameter estimation for improved performance and adaptability. We demonstrate CARP's capabilities through trajectory tracking experiments and multi-robot reconfiguration into collective structures. CARP's compact, low-cost, and modular design enables rapid fabrication and deployment of multiple vehicles, making it an effective platform for developing and testing control, reconfiguration, and swarm robotics algorithms.

title: Low-speed Sensorless Control Strategy for Switched Reluctance Motors Based on Reluctance Characteristic Position Detection
authors: Zebin Yang, Lang Wang, Xiaodong Sun, Chao Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: To address the problems of reluctance discontinuity and integral drift in sensorless control of switched reluctance motors (SRMs) during low-speed operation, this paper proposes a sensorless SRM control scheme based on high-frequency (HF) voltage injection and reluctance correction. First, HF voltage is injected into the non-conducting phase to obtain the phase reluctance over a full cycle, thereby resolving the discontinuity problem of the reluctance curve. Then, an amplitude correction algorithm (ACA) and a drift compensation algorithm (DCA) are designed to correct the reluctance and suppress drift in the reluctance curve. Subsequently, joint position estimation is performed by detecting the negative zero-crossing of the slope at the misaligned position of the reluctance and the slope detection interval at the position where the rotor and stator begin to overlap. This approach does not rely on a single characteristic point, significantly improving the robustness and accuracy of position estimation. Finally, the proposed sensorless control strategy is experimentally validated on a six-phase 12/10 SRM test platform.

title: Resonance based Object Sensing System for Soft Robotic Skin in Human Cooperative Robots
authors: Vikas Kumar, Shivansh Awasthi, Vikash Sharma, Santosh Parajuli, Samaresh Das, Thomas George Thundat, Ankur Gupta
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents a resonance-based object sensing system (ROSS) for soft robotic skin applications to enhance human safety in human cooperative robots (HCRs). The proposed system utilizes the single-conductor power transfer (SCPT) methodology, enabling the ROSS coil to be integrated into the soft skin of robotic arm end effectors or fingers while operating with minimal power consumption (-16 to -56 dBm). The ROSS coil provides passive voltage gain due to its high quality factor (Q > 550). During human interaction with the soft skin of ROSS, it operates at 1.4 MHz and functions at its self-resonant frequency (SRF) in the radio-frequency (RF) band. The DC output from the passive RF-DC converter circuit remains sufficiently high after conversion losses and can be directly interfaced with a microcontroller unit (MCU) for real-time testing of the ROSS. The ROSS responds by varying its DC output voltage depending on the type of object in contact, including insulating and conducting materials, as well as humans. It also differentiates the nature of interaction, such as gentle human touch and higher applied force (≥21 N) exerted by the human hand on the soft skin of the ROSS. This design shows potential to enhance both safety and selectivity when integrated into the soft skin of robotic arm end effectors or fingers for future industrial HCR applications.

title: Response Analysis of the Darlington Transistor Amplification Mode under Transient Electrical Stress
authors: Changjun Li, Jinjun Cheng, Xiaoxu Chu, Haizhen Zhu, Bincheng Wen, Fawen Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To investigate the internal response of power devices under transient electrical stress in amplification mode, a physical simulation model of the Darlington transistor is established using semiconductor device simulation software, and the model is validated by the device datasheet. Subsequently, the response analysis under transient collector voltage stress is explored via simulation for power devices operating in amplification mode. The results demonstrate that the current gain of the device increases and then gradually stabilizes under collector voltage stress. The findings of this paper can provide a theoretical basis for the safety protection of Darlington power devices employed in power electronic systems.

title: Adaptive Channel-Shuffled Deep Learning for Hormone-Conditioned Ovarian Ultrasound Analysis
authors: S. Mohamed Rafiq, A. Indira, A. Nasreen Banu, L. Ibby Sharona, G. A. Mejella Jasmine
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is a common endocrine disorder in women of reproductive age, which is usually difficult to diagnose because it is characterized by subtle morphological changes in the ovaries and diverse clinical manifestations. Timely intervention depends on the accurate early detection, which is challenging in a standard clinical and point-of-care setting. This paper suggests a deep learning-based adaptive and lightweight system to detect PCOS on ovarian ultrasound images, supported by patient hormone profiles. The algorithm adds to the better ShuffleNet design so that the architecture can be computationally efficient and rich in features. An innovative hormone-conditioned channel attention system incorporates the most important biochemical indicators luteinizing hormone, follicle-stimulating hormone, and androgen levels to the process of feature learning, and prioritize dynamically hormonally sensitive regions of the ovary. Also, both local follicular and global ovarian variations that are present in adaptive grouped convolutions and multiscale feature encoding are also captured. The combination of imaging features and clinical data is done by multimodal embedding to provide full risk assessment. Experimental findings show that it has high diagnostic performance and low model complexity and can thus be used in real-time and resource-constrained clinical settings. The framework provides efficient and scalable support that is interpretable to aid in screening and clinical decision-making of PCOS in early stages.

title: Global vs. Regional: A Comparative Measurement of Token Efficiency in State-of-the-Art Large Language Models
authors: Wenguo Zhang, Qiang Du, Zhangwei Feng, Zening Fan, Feng Tian, Yingbo Zhou, Yuanye Tao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Tokenizer efficiency is a critical determinant of inference cost and latency in Large Language Models (LLMs). This paper presents a comparative measurement of efficiency across seven languages using four state-of-the-art models. Empirical results reveal a significant 'Global vs. Regional' divergence. While Western models dominate in Indo-European languages, they remain inefficient for Chinese, with a Relative Token Ratio (RTR) reaching 1.20. In contrast, regionally optimized models like DeepSeek-V3.2 achieve a breakthrough, with an RTR of 0.96 and a Token Compression Ratio (TCR) of 1.45 characters per token for Chinese. This surpasses the English baseline and slashes operational costs by up to 83x compared to global baselines.

title: Watermarking Deep Neural Networks with Invisible Trigger Pattern and Low-weight Masking Strategy
authors: Guangyong Gao, Kun Li, Yi Shen, Zhangjie Fu, Zhihua Xia, Yun-Qing Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: The training of deep learning models necessitates the collaboration of large datasets, high-performance computing resources, and specialized expertise to be successfully accomplished, which raises a pressing demand for safeguarding these inventions from theft and redistribution. Due to the specificity of the DNN structure, the copyright owner of the model can embed watermarks in the model using backdoor or other methods. However, most existing backdoor watermarking methods have room for improvement in defending against removal attacks, and their trigger patterns are visible, making them easily detectable. To overcome the above limitations, a backdoor watermarking method with invisibility and robustness is proposed for image classification models in the multimedia domain. Specifically, an image structure is filled with colored edge information to generate trigger pattern. Subsequently, the trigger pattern is embedded into the cover image with a deep injection network to ensure invisibility. For enhancing the coupling between watermarking and model functionality, a portion of low-weight neurons are selectively masked in the training phase. In this way, our model watermark scheme is robust to fine-tuning, pruning and overwriting. Experimental results using VGG19, ResNet18, WRN28_10, and ResNet50 on MNIST, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and FOOD-101 classification tasks, respectively, illustrate that the success rate of watermark authentication is almost 100% under aggressive watermark removal attacks, which is significantly better than state-of-the-art methods.

title: CAFF-Net: A Cross-Attention Feature Fusion Network for Robust Multi-Type Brain Tumor Classification
authors: J.Steephan Amalraj, R. Jeevitha, Sakthivel. M, K. Saravanan, Raghavendran. N, Satheesh N. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Brain tumor classification is a significant aspect that requires attention in order to detect and treat such diseases in the early stages. However, most models such as RFENet have limitations such as high computational cost, less interaction between feature branches and high dependence on data augmentation techniques. In this context, this work proposes a novel and efficient model called Cross-Attention Feature Fusion Network (CAFF-Net) that seeks to overcome these challenges and provide better efficiency and classification results. The model uses a shared shallow encoder and three sub-branches that specialize in edge features, fine features and semantic features, respectively. A Dynamic Cross-Attention Fusion (DCAF) module allows for more interaction between branches and Dual Attention Pyramid (DAP) module is used to refine and obtain more accurate representations that are tumor-related. In order to overcome class imbalance in brain tumor images, a novel module Focal Balanced Data Blending (FBDB) is proposed and implemented in this study. In addition, Soft Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) is proposed and implemented in order to obtain more accurate results with less computational cost. The proposed model achieves state-of-the-art results with 99.12% accuracy, 98.40% precision, 98.55% recall, 99.93% F1-score, and 99.80% AUC values.

title: Channels with Input-Correlated Synchronization Errors
authors: Roni Con, João Ribeiro
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: “Independent and identically distributed” errors do not accurately capture the noisy behavior of real-world data storage and information transmission technologies. Motivated by this, we study channels with input-correlated synchronization errors, meaning that the distribution of synchronization errors (such as deletions and insertions) applied to the i-th input xi may depend on the whole input string x. We begin by identifying conditions on the input-correlated synchronization channel under which the channel’s information capacity is achieved by a stationary ergodic input source and is equal to its coding capacity. These conditions capture a wide class of channels, including channels with correlated errors observed in DNA-based data storage systems and their multi-trace versions, and generalize prior work. To showcase the usefulness of the general capacity theorem above, we combine it with techniques of Pernice-Li-Wootters (ISIT 2022) and Brakensiek-Li-Spang (FOCS 2020) to obtain explicit capacity-achieving codes for multi-trace channels with runlength-dependent deletions, motivated by error patterns observed in DNA-based data storage systems.

title: An Adaptive Hybrid Framework for Complex Indoor Scene Analysis and Object Recognition
authors: Praveen Kulkarni, Naina Kalyanshetti, A Sachin, Aryan Penukonda, M Naveen, Pramod Gurunath Chitrapur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Indoor scene understanding is a crucial task in robotics, smart spaces, AR/VR, and assistive systems; but clutter, occlusions, non-uniform illumination, and noise pose severe challenges to the performance of models in real-world scenarios. The researchers present an Adaptive Hybrid Framework which connects traditional image processing methods with deep learning through a system that uses 81 different pipeline combinations. The scoring system uses Laplacian variance and gradient magnitude and normalized PSNR and noise estimation to select the best preprocessing pipeline for each image. The self-collected Indian indoor dataset tests showed a major enhancement in image quality because all test images achieved higher normalized PSNR values and edge sharpness improved through various lighting conditions. The processed images showed a 50-100% improvement in detected objects which ranged from 1 to 3 objects in the test scenarios and the average detection confidence improved from 0.62-0.64 to 0.85-0.88. The results show that adaptive preprocessing enhances structural clarity and noise removal which leads to better detection accuracy. The combined pipeline enables organizations to perform segmentation and object detection and scene classification in real-world environments that require complex indoor operations. The study uses hybrid preprocessing together with adaptive systems to detect objects and analyze scenes in indoor spaces.

title: IoT Enabled Solar Powered Smart Watercraft for Automated River Waste Collection and Sustainable Transportation
authors: K Valarmathi, S Mahalakshmi, P A Mathina, P Angel, R Kartheeswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The degree of river contamination, as well as the absence of any prospects of developing sustainable methods of water transportation put into perspective the necessity of the comprehensive environmentally friendly system. In this paper IoT enabled Eco Power Boat is introduced that would be used to transport passengers and to clean the Automatic River. The offered system involves using the renewable sources of energy such as solar panels and battery storage to provide the sustainability of the system in terms of environmental safety. A conveyor belt system installed to the front of the boat automatically gathers floating trash including plastics and organic waste and them into an on board storage bin. The system is powered by ESP32 microcontroller which controls the propulsion, collection of waste and real time communication with Blink IoT platform. The major parameters under constant surveillance like battery level, motor status and bin fill level are sampled with the help of sensors, one of which is an ultrasonic sensor that detects the waste. The effectiveness of waste collection and dependability of real time monitoring with less manual intervention is proven through experimental testing. The suggested system to clean the river and provide the eco-friendly transportation.

title: Performance Evaluation of High-Voltage Pulsed Power Modulator for Variable Pressure and Gap of DBD Plasma Load
authors: Brijendra Kumar Verma, Subhash Kumar Ram, Ram Prakash Lamba, Dheerendra Singh, Udit Narayan Pal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: High-voltage pulsed power modulators (HV-PPMs) play a key role in driving dielectric barrier discharge (DBD)-based excimer sources, where pulse rise time, voltage amplitude, and waveform shape directly influence discharge behavior and optical efficiency. In this work, an HV-PPM capable of delivering up to 8.0 kV is characterized using four DBD excimer tubes arranged into two operating configurations: one with fixed gas pressure and variable discharge gap, and another with fixed discharge gap and variable pressure. For both configurations, electrical-to-optical efficiency is measured as a function of applied voltage, enabling a comparative analysis of the efficiency–voltage characteristics. Rise-time variations with DBD voltage are also evaluated for all the tubes. At 8.0-kV applied voltage, the 222-nm emission intensity and the corresponding average electrical power to the DBD tubes are quantified to assess peak operating performance. The results show clear differences in efficiency trends between the two configurations, reflecting the influence of gap and pressure conditions on the generation of excimer radiation.

title: CFD-Based Analysis of Submarine Drag with Vertical Foreplane Placement Variation
authors: Sutiyo, Ardi Nugroho Yulianto, I Ketut Aria Pria Utama, Martin Renilson
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: This study investigates the effects of vertical foreplane placement on the drag characteristics of a submarine using Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD). The analysis was conducted on the Joubert BB2 model with a length of 3.826 m, using the ANSYS CFX package and adhering to the International Towing Tank Conference (ITTC) procedures. Three variations of foreplane placement were developed at 20% of the length from the bow and positioned vertically in the upper, middle, and lower sections of the hull. The model was subjected to a simulation involving complete submersion in water at an inlet velocity of $\mathbf{1 0}$ knots. Grid convergence independence and uncertainty analyses were performed to ensure the reliability of the results. The simulation was validated based on previous research, achieving a remarkable accuracy of 2.86%. The results demonstrate that all three alternative models exhibited a reduction in drag compared to the original model, with the lower section placement (Model 3) achieving the most significant decrease (7.36%). The reduction in resistance is attributed to the smoother interaction between the fluid and foreplane in the optimized placement of the foreplane. The fluid flow was symmetrical in the original and middle-section models, whereas the upper and lower section models displayed asymmetrical flow patterns. These findings highlight the importance of precise foreplane placement in optimizing submarine design to enhance hydrodynamic efficiency and reduce drag, ultimately contributing to improved operational capabilities in various underwater environments.

title: Welcome Remark by Chair of OESTEMS Joint Chapter
