publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)

title: TensExaMig: A Topology- and Interaction-Aware Framework for Scalable Process Migration in Distributed Exascale Computing Systems
authors: Fatemeh Hamdollahzadeh, Ehsan Mousavi Khaneghah, Mario Francesco Pavone, Abdel Rahman Alzoubaidi, Weam Farhan Oliwi, Ha Huy Cuong Nguyen, Mehdi Hajirajabi, Reza Safari, Zahra Fathali, Abolghasem Khamseh, Fateme Zahmatkesh
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: This paper introduces TensExaMig, an adaptive framework for improving process migration in large-scale distributed systems. The approach combines tensor-based state representation, interaction-aware communication modeling, and symbolic planning using Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to guide migration decisions. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are used to capture topological features and workload behaviors. TensExaMig has been tested in simulation environments based on PMamut and Cactus, which simulate dynamic execution in distributed systems. Experiments with two scientific applications, MM5 and Nike, assessed the framework's effectiveness under different system loads and communication intensities. The results show notable improvements in key metrics such as process downtime, communication overhead, and load balancing, compared to traditional strategies and learning-based baselines that ignore interaction or topology. These findings suggest that combining symbolic reasoning with structured system models can lead to more efficient and stable process migration in distributed environments. Future research will aim to expand the framework for deployment on physical testbeds and to incorporate additional system-monitoring components.

title: Autonomous Weed Detection and Removal System using CNN and Python-Controlled Embedded Platform
authors: V Mangaiyarkarasi, A Julie, K Rajasree, S Malarkodi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Meeting the rising demand for sustainable agriculture and lowering work in the fields, intelligent external plant control systems are becoming increasingly necessary. This document describes autonomous external plant recognition and elimination systems using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) combined with a Python-driven embedded platform. The system being proposed makes use of a camera attached to a moving robot to take live pictures of the field. The images are then analyzed by a CNN model to distinguish between crops and weeds with accuracy. After identification, an embedded controller sends a signal to the mechanical actuator for weed removal. The model is qualified for running on the ESP32 platform which is a low-computation complexity platform and capable of real-time performance with an inference time of less than a second per image. Testing shows great classification accuracy, with even better precision and recall when field conditions vary. The system drastically cuts down on chemical herbicide use and manual labor, which is in line with farming practices that are environmentally friendly. The technique presented offers a cheap, extendable, and effective tool for precision farming. It can be used by small and medium-sized farms.

title: DSLA: An Energy-Efficient Dual-Sparsity LLM Accelerator With HiMix-BFP
authors: Zikang Zhou, Siyao Dai, Yaqi Chen, Kaiqi Chen, Jun Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved great success in areas such as language understanding and text generation. However, their massive parameters incur high computational, storage, and energy costs, making deployment on resource- and power-constrained edge devices particularly challenging. Block Floating Point (BFP) reduces storage and computational overhead by grouping data into blocks and aligning them to the maximum exponent within each block, converting them into low-bit fixed-point numbers. Bidirectional Block Floating Point (BBFP) extends BFP by aligning data within a block to two different exponents, reducing quantization error for small values. However, at ultra-low bit widths, both methods suffer from severe accuracy degradation due to their sensitivity to outliers, which limits their ability to achieve aggressive energy savings. In addition, the exponent alignment procedure in these schemes inherently introduces bit-sliced sparsity, an opportunity that remains largely unexplored for further improving energy efficiency on edge accelerators. To address these challenges, we propose an energy-efficient dual-sparsity LLM accelerator (DSLA) that supports the HiMix-BFP data format. HiMix-BFP improves low-bit accuracy by preserving extra mantissa bits for the maximum value and adaptively selecting BFP or BBFP per block. The DSLA architecture efficiently exploits both value and bit-sliced sparsity across different bit widths to further enhance energy efficiency by employing low-bit computational units, load-balancing mechanisms, and a hierarchical bit-accumulation array. Experimental results demonstrate that HiMix-BFP reduces perplexity by up to 76%, while DSLA achieves up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.39\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher throughput and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.59\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> greater energy efficiency compared to SOTA accelerators.

title: Reconstruction Codes for Deletions and Insertions: Connection, Distinction, and Construction
authors: Yubo Sun, Gennian Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Let B(·) be an error ball function. A set of q-ary sequences of length n is referred to as an (n, q, N; B)-reconstruction code if each sequence x within this set can be uniquely reconstructed from any N distinct elements within its error ball B(x). The main objective in this area is to determine or establish bounds for the minimum redundancy of (n, q, N; B)-reconstruction codes, denoted by ρ(n, q, N; B). In this paper, we investigate reconstruction codes where the error ball corresponds to either the t-deletion ball Dt(·) or the t-insertion ball It(·). Our primary technical contributions include: • Establishing a fundamental connection between reconstruction codes for deletions and insertions. Specifically, for any positive integers n, t, q, N, any (n, q, N; It)-reconstruction code is also an (n, q, N; Dt)-reconstruction code. This leads to the inequality ρ(n, q, N; Dt) ≤ ρ(n, q, N; It). • Identifying a significant distinction between reconstruction codes for deletions and insertions when N = O(nt−1) and t ≥ 2. For deletions, we prove that there exists some constant K such that ρ(n, q, 2(q−1)t−1/qt−1(t−1)!nt−1 + Knt−2;Dt) = O(1), which disproves a conjecture posed in [1]. In contrast, for insertions, we show that there exists some constant K′ such that ρ(n, q, (q−1)t−1 (t−1)! nt−1 + K′nt−2; It) = log log n + O(1), which extends a key result from [2]. • Constructing (n, q, N; B)-reconstruction codes, where B ∈ {D2, I2}, for N ∈ {2, 3, 4, 5} and establishing respective upper bounds of 3 log n + O(log log n), 3 log n + O(1), 2 log n + O(log log n) and log n + O(log log n) on the minimum redundancy ρ(n, q, N; B). This generalizes results previously established in [3].

title: General method for the ultralow-loss and compact adiabatic mode evolutions on photonic chips
authors: Yi Hu, Guojiang Yang, Laiwen Yu, Yingying Peng, Zhihao Wang, Jingshu Guo, Daoxin Dai
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Achieving low-loss and compact optical guiding between arbitrary waveguide ports is crucial for high-performance and highly integrated large-scale photonic integrated circuits. However, existing research on waveguide jumpers has focused on reducing bending radii and has struggled to provide compact, low-loss solutions for various waveguide jumpers with narrow port spacing. In this work, we propose a general method for ultralow-loss and compact adiabatic mode evolutions based on a semi-inverse design strategy and Bezier-shaped waveguide structures. The search-space dimension is incrementally increased through the elevation of Bezier curve orders in multiple optimization rounds, thereby achieving efficient optimization convergence and high design flexibility. As a result, the obtained 180° waveguide jumpers with 1 μm and 2 μm port spacings demonstrate compact footprints and low excess losses below 0.029 dB/180°, which cannot be simultaneously achieved through cascading the existing waveguide jumpers. Additionally, the 270° and micro-ring transition region waveguide jumpers confirm the versatility of our method. This general design framework enables adiabatic mode evolution between arbitrary ports and can be extended to other integrated photonic platforms, such as silicon nitride and thin-film lithium niobate.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Fraud Detection Using Hybrid Transformer with Gated Token Mixing and Conformal Risk Control
authors: Srikumar Nayak, A. R. Bushara
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Financial fraud detection remains challenging because transaction data are highly imbalanced, fraud patterns evolve rapidly, and reliable decision-making is required in high-stakes financial environments. This paper proposes ARGUS, an uncertainty-aware fraud-detection framework that integrates a hybrid Transformer-based model with gated token mixing with Bayesian Monte Carlo dropout and conformal risk control. The proposed architecture combines tokenised tabular embedding, multi-head self-attention, dilated convolution, and gated depthwise-convolutional tokenmixing to capture both global and local feature dependencies. Predictive uncertainty is estimated using Monte Carlo dropout, whereas the conformal-style selective prediction allows the model to drop out on transactions with low confidence and subsequently minimize the risk of decision-making. The results of the experiments conducted on the Credit Card Fraud Detection dataset indicate that ARGUS has an area under the precision-recall curve of 0.880, area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.975, F 1 -score of 0.860, a precision of 0.910 and a recall of 0.820. Besides this, the proposed framework has a coverage of 0.88 and produces selective risk of 0.015, which is much better than both the classical machine learning and recent deep learning baselines. The analysis of ablation also demonstrates that the combination of Bayesian uncertainty and conformal risk control leads to the decrease of the risk to 0.181, 0.082, and 0.015. These findings indicate thatARGUS not only offers highly accurate fraud-detection capabilities but also efficient, understandable and risk-sensitive decision support to support viable financial usages.

title: Real-Time Multimodal AI-Based Human Detection and Localization for Maritime Search and Rescue Using LiDAR and Thermal Imaging
authors: Hung Nguyen, Hai Duong Nguyen Duc, Dang Bui Hai, Ninh Pham Quang, Kien Do Trung
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Maritime search and rescue operations at sea face many difficulties, especially in low-visibility conditions and complex marine environments. The traditional methods, such as RGB cameras, radar, and visual inspection, are often ineffective in detecting people in distress. This paper proposes an Artificial Intelligence (AI) based human detection and positioning system that integrates Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) sensors and thermal cameras to serve maritime rescue work and minimize missed detections. The research method uses the YOLOv8 deep learning model to detect objects and classify input data in real time. An intelligent search algorithm, combined with data from LiDAR and thermal cameras, accurately estimates target coordinates and distances. In addition, the system applies the Denoising Convolutional Neural Network (DnCNN) to reduce interference with the marine environment and help track targets with higher performance. Research results show that the system operates with a high accuracy of about 90% in challenging ocean environments. Multi-sensor data helps improve accuracy and object detection performance compared to traditional single methods.

title: Deep Semantic Segmentation with Combined Focal-Dice Loss for Multi-Class Land Cover Classification: A Comprehensive U-Net Evaluation
authors: N. Chandramouli, Balajee Maram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper provides a thorough assessment of U-Net-based deep learning models for multi-class land cover image segmentation from satellite images in the DeepGlobe dataset. We developed the basic U-Net model with a new hybrid focal-Dice loss function for handling class imbalance. The six-class land cover dataset (urban land, agriculture land, rangeland, forest land, water, and barren land) was handled with extensive data augmentation techniques like random flipping, rotation, and brightness change. The basic U-Net model had a Mean IoU value of 0.57 on the validation set, with the best performance on agriculture land (IoU = 0.818) and the worst on barren land (IoU = 0.478). Ablation experiments proved that data augmentation contributed substantially to improving the generalization capability, preventing overfitting with a relative improvement of 25% in IoU on the validation set. The hybrid focal-Dice loss function was very effective in dealing with the class imbalance problem while preserving spatial consistency. The experiments clearly show that U-Net models with proper design and loss functions can be very effective for pixel-wise land cover classification of satellite images.

title: How2AIHeap: Construct An Agent to Analyze Heap Exploit and Recognize Exploit Pattern
authors: Shaoqin Chen, Jian Wang, Kaijie Huang, Honglue Hu, Kun Huang, Yuxuan Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The rapid progress of large language models (LLMs) has made it possible for security analysts to analyze uncommented exploit scripts. However, without dynamic debugging information from the victim software, LLM-based analyses are often unsatisfactory. To address this issue, we take CTF-Pwn challenges as our research subject, model exploitation patterns from the how2heap repository, and develop an agent system that steps an exp and analyzes the program’s runtime memory state. The system ultimately produces a write-up for the uncommented exp and recognizes the corresponding exploitation pattern. Experiments show that, under reasonable computational overhead, our system attains a deep understanding of uncommented exps, generates write-ups comparable to human-authored documents, and achieves high accuracy in exploitation-pattern recognition.

title: Perfect Privacy for Discriminator-Based Byzantine-Resilient Federated Learning
authors: Yue Xia, Christoph Hofmeister, Maximilian Egger, Rawad Bitar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Federated learning (FL) shows great promise in large-scale machine learning but introduces new privacy and security challenges. We propose ByITFL and LoByITFL, two novel FL schemes that enhance resilience against Byzantine users while preventing eavesdroppers from learning users’ private data. To ensure privacy and Byzantine resilience, our schemes are built on having a small representative dataset available to the federator and crafting a discriminator function allowing mitigating corrupt users’ contributions. ByITFL employs Lagrange coded computing and re-randomization, making it the first Byzantine-resilient FL scheme with perfect Information-Theoretic (IT) privacy, though at the cost of a significant communication overhead. LoByITFL, on the other hand, achieves Byzantine resilience and IT privacy at a significantly reduced communication cost, but requires a Trusted Third Party (TTP), used only before training in a one-time initialization phase. We provide theoretical guarantees of privacy and Byzantine resilience, along with convergence guarantees and experimental results validating our findings.

title: SPA: Stable and Precise Alignment for Efficient Cross-Domain Palmprint Recognition
authors: Song Ruan, Yantao Li, Huafeng Qin, Naeha Sharif, Farid Boussaid, Mohammed Bennamoun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Palmprint recognition has been extensively studied as an effective biometric technique for personal identification. With the rapid development of deep neural networks (DNNs), palmprint recognition methods have achieved remarkable progress. However, their performance often deteriorates significantly under domain shifts. Moreover, existing unsupervised domain adaptation approaches for palmprint recognition typically suffer from unstable training and imprecise feature alignment, thereby limiting their effectiveness. To address these challenges, we propose SPA, a Stable and Precise Alignment framework for cross-domain palmprint recognition. Specifically, we design a lightweight yet robust Style Transformation Module (STM) to mitigate variations in style, color, and illumination. With the aid of STM, we further align joint feature distributions across all high-level layers, achieving more accurate feature alignment and enhancing recognition robustness. We conduct extensive experiments on two public multi-domain palmprint databases encompassing 42 cross-domain scenarios. The results demonstrate that SPA consistently delivers superior performance across both databases, achieving higher recognition accuracy with lower computational overhead compared to existing methods. In particular, SPA improves the average identification accuracies to 94.21% and 81.93%, while reducing the average equal error rates (EER) to 1.36% and 3.62% on the two databases, respectively.

title: DGC-TAMNet: A Dynamic Graph-Convolutional Transformer Framework for Imbalanced Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
authors: Biyyapu Vishnu Priya, Kadiyam Rahul Prasad, M. Senthil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Due to the increasing volume of traffic in the networking environment, the continuous evolution of attack patterns has become a major challenge for contemporary intrusion detection systems. Although deep-learning-based approaches have shown substantial improvement in detecting anomalies in network traffic, many existing methods still suffer from high complexity, limited capability to capture relationships among different traffic flows, and reduced efficiency when handling imbalanced datasets. To address these limitations, this study proposes a Deep Generalized Collaborating TAM Network (DGC-TAMNet) that integrates a graph-convolutional transformer-based neural architecture with an adaptive minority-aware learning strategy for effective anomaly classification in internet traffic networks. In the proposed approach, related traffic flows are modeled as a graph structure where a graph convolutional neural network extracts dependency information among flows. In addition, a lightweight temporal transformer encoder is employed to capture temporal characteristics from traffic flow signals. The collaborative integration of these components improves the ability of the system to identify anomalies in complex and imbalanced network traffic scenarios. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves a classification accuracy of 96.8%, while also improving precision and recall, thereby providing an efficient and reliable solution for anomaly detection in modern internet traffic environments.

title: Multilayered Magnetic Recording Media by Flip-Disk Bonding
authors: Brandon L. Reese, Julia Chang, David E. Laughlin, Jian-Gang Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Wafer bonding is a common technique used within the semiconductor community. We provide a first demonstration of this technique as a method to potentially realize multilayered media for multi-level magnetic recording. The magnetic and structural properties of dual-layer L10-FePt / h-BN magnetic recording media were characterized using three different bonding conditions. It is shown that L10-FePt magnetic recording media can be joined using an intermediate bond layer with thickness of roughly 5 nm while retaining excellent magnetic and structural properties. Notably, no step-like feature is observed for magnetically hard dual-layer structures, the magnetic hysteresis of combined dual-layer recording media exhibited coercivities of 28.6 and 3.6 kOe along the out-of-plane and in-plane directions respectively.

title: An Intelligent Time-Series Forecasting Approach for Power Consumption using ML and DL Techniques
authors: Harish S S, Jothi Krishna G, Mohamed Riyaz S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Predictable electricity demand is very important in the current energy management, stability in smart grid, and sustainable electricity distribution. A single-model machine learning and traditional statistical forecasting approaches do not generally perform well in nonlinear relationships and intricate time dependencies in large-scale energy data sets. A smart hybrid forecasting system with the help of both Machine Learning and Deep Learning methods is thus presented. The proposed system is a combination of Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) models with innovative time-series feature engineering such as attributes of time, lag features, and rolling statistical values. The Python programming platform is used to implement model development and experimentation against tensorflow, Keras as well as Scikit-learn libraries. It is shown through the performance assessment of Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and Mean Squared Error (MSE) that the predictive powers are enhanced by about 15-20 % of the previous machine learning models. The analysis of feature importance also provides the temperature and the temporal attribute as the most influential factors in energy demand. The model offers a sound information-based estimate of smart energy systems that makes predictions of smart power consumption.

title: Intelligent IoT-Enabled Battery Management System for Electric Vehicles
authors: T. Rampradesh, R. Chandravignesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: With the increasing prevalence of Electric Vehicles (EVs), it has become essential to ensure the secure, efficient and intelligent utilization of batteries. This initiative seeks to create and deploy an affordable, intelligent Battery Management System (BMS) using Arduino technology and the Internet of Things (IoT) for smart electric vehicles. The proposed system continuously tracks crucial battery metrics, such as voltage, current, temperature and charge/discharge cycles, through sensors linked to an Arduino microcontroller. Utilizing real-time data, the system evaluates the State of Charge (SoC) and State of Health (SoH) of the battery, both critical for assessing battery performance and ensuring safety. A key feature of the system is the incorporation of IoT capabilities with modules like ESP8266 or NodeMCU, which facilitate wireless transmission of data to a cloud server or mobile application. This functionality enables remote monitoring, live updates and immediate alerts regarding the battery's status and health. Users have the ability to access this information from anywhere with an internet connection, making the system particularly advantageous for fleet management and smart electric vehicle applications. Additionally, the BMS is designed with basic cell balancing logic to guarantee even charging across all battery cells, thereby extending battery life. Testing was conducted on a EV prototype battery pack and the findings indicated effective monitoring, dependable IoT connectivity and precise fault detection. The experimental configuration showcases a cost-effective, scalable, and intelligent BMS solution that not only safeguards and optimizes battery use but also includes smart functionalities such as remote tracking and predictive maintenance.

title: A High-Linearity Shunt-Based In-Line Current Sensor With Self-Heating Compensation and 14.4 V 2 MHz PWM Rejection
authors: Heng Ma, Huajun Zhang, Yuyan Liu, Marco Berkhout, Qinwen Fan
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a cost-effective, fully integrated shunt-resistor-based in-line current sensor that delivers high linearity and strong PWM rejection, enabling precise current measurement and control in dynamic driving systems like robotics, imaging, and audio. To mitigate the self-heating of the on-chip shunt resistor, which degrades linearity, a location-based thermal compensation technique is proposed. This technique reduces self-heating-induced distortion, improving THD+N by up to 23.6 dB over the full operating range without calibration. In addition, a floating transconductance (FG<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {m}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) stage is introduced to reject the high dv/dt, high-voltage PWM components at the switching node of the power stage, enabling robust current sensing with this PWM common mode voltage. The prototype is implemented in a 180 nm BCD process and demonstrates a peak THD+N of–87.2 dB and a dynamic range (DR) of 89.1 dB. It achieves up to 2 MHz, 14.4 V PWM rejection with 2 V/ns <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$dv\mathrm {/}dt$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and supports a ±6 A bidirectional current range.

title: AI-Enabled Vocabulary Learning with Adaptive Scheduling and Chatbot Practice: toward Lexical Network Aware Modeling
authors: Jiaze Dai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Vocabulary mastery underpins reading comprehension, listening ability and overall second language proficiency, yet many learners still rely on one size fits all drills that ignore both individual forgetting curves and the lexical interdependencies that shape real word networks. Recent work on spaced repetition and knowledge tracing offers a means to adapt review schedules based on learner responses, but most implementations treat each word as an isolated skill. This paper presents an AI enabled vocabulary learning system that couples knowledge tracing with a lexical network aware item representation, a utility based scheduler with explicit parameter settings, and a conversational practice module enhanced with gamified micro challenges. To improve transparency and move toward more realistic usage assumptions, we introduce a Monte Carlo evaluation that simulates 60 intermediate learners over a 14 day intervention across 10 000 runs. Unlike prior synthetic benchmarks, our simulation incorporates fatigue, off task behaviour and random guessing to approximate noisier real world usage. Results from the Monte Carlo evaluation indicate that, under the stated simulation assumptions, the integrated adaptive condition yields higher expected vocabulary gains and engagement efficiency than the fixed interval baseline. The simulations also suggest that explicitly modelling semantic and morphological relationships can reduce interference and support associative reinforcement. The protocol is specified in sufficient detail to support methodological scrutiny and to inform future human subject studies.

title: Towards Trustworthy Digital Twins: Verifiable Simulation via Recursive Zero-Knowledge Proofs
authors: Mohammadhossein Homaei, Fatemeh Moradi, Mehran Tarif, Ruben Molano, Pablo García Rodríguez, Mar Avila
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Industrial Digital Twins (DTs) require cryptographic verification for external audits without exposing proprietary models—a challenge for continuous-time simulations with thousands of ODE integration steps. Existing methods fail: monolithic SNARKs exceed memory limits beyond 3000 steps, while naive recursive approaches impose > 200× overhead. We present ZKP-DS, combining sensitivity-driven precision allocation (35% circuit reduction), hierarchical proof batching (26-39× speedup via parallelization), and probabilistic epoch sampling (78% verification reduction with > 99.99% fraud detection). Fixed-step Runge-Kutta integration in adaptive fixed-point arithmetic ensures both cryptographic soundness and IEEE-compliant numerical accuracy. Experiments on full-scale power systems (2000 states, 10,000 steps) achieve 5.6−8.5× computational overhead with sub-60ms constant-time verification on university GPU clusters. Results demonstrate that zero-knowledge proofs can provide mathematical integrity guarantees for industrial cyber-physical systems at practical costs, enabling trustworthy multi-stakeholder monitoring and regulatory compliance.

title: Carelink-A: A Transformer-based Intelligent Assistive Communication and Safety Framework for Differently Abled Individuals
authors: M.S. Thanabal, K. Sundara Pandi, Ma. Shunmuga Subramanian, S. Vasantha Kumar, S. Udhayaprakash
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Differently-abled individuals encounter persistent challenges in everyday communication, personal safety, and routine task management. Communication barriers arise due to speech and hearing impairments, limiting effective interaction in social, medical, and emergency contexts. Many existing assistive applications provide either speech conversion or reminder services independently, resulting in fragmented usage. Switching between multiple applications increases cognitive burden and reduces accessibility efficiency. Inaccurate speech recognition in noisy environments further restricts dependable communication. Limited naturalness in generated speech can also affect clarity and user confidence. Routine management remains another significant challenge. Individuals requiring structured support for medication, appointments, and daily activities often rely on basic reminder systems that lack personalization and voice-enabled interaction. Absence of integrated communication support restricts seamless scheduling through natural interaction. Emergency response mechanisms in conventional applications often require manual navigation through several screens, leading to potential delays during critical situations. Furthermore, many applications lack accessibility-centered interface design, resulting in small fonts, complex layouts, and limited offline usability. The proposed CARELINK methodology addresses these limitations through an integrated mobile framework that combines Communication Aid, Routine Reminders, and Emergency SOS within a single accessible platform. Real-time speech-to-text and text-to-speech conversion enhances communication clarity for individuals with speech or hearing impairments. Structured reminder scheduling with customizable notifications improves daily task organization and medication adherence. Rapid emergency alert activation ensures immediate assistance during critical conditions. Accessibility-focused interface elements such as adjustable fonts, voice feedback, and offline capability enhance usability across diverse impairment categories. Unified system architecture reduces complexity, improves reliability, and strengthens independence and safety.

title: Power Quality Assessment of Microgrid using Shunt Active Power Filter
authors: Sejal Puri, Namra Joshi, Sejal Patil, Hemangi Thakare, Anamika Patil, Purva Sonawane
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The increment in integration of renewable energy sources & nonlinear loads in micro grids give rise to power quality issues like harmonic distortion, reactive power imbalance & poor power factor. These problems have a significant impact on reliability & performance of power system. In micro grids, nonlinear loads produce large-scale current harmonics, voltage distortion, and also have poor power factor, which in turn affects system reliability and performance. To put forth solutions to these issues, a MATLAB/Simulink model that included a three-phase AC source, a nonlinear load, a PV-supported DC link, and a SAPF, which was controlled via PI and hysteresis current strategies. This paper explains how SAPF works. The SAPF cancels out the unwanted currents at the Point of Common Coupling and enhances the waveform quality by injecting compensating currents. In the simulations, state-of-the-art performance was demonstrated through the considerably lower THD, along with improvements in the power factor and in the sinusoidal shape of the source currents. As a result, the source currents were in compliance with the IEEE-519 standard of performance. Therefore, concludes that the SAPF shapes power quality and solidifies the micro grid’s stability with hybrid renewable energies. In addition, power factor improves and reduces harmonic as well.

title: AI-Enabled Skin Lesion Recognition for Multi-Insect Bite Diagnosis with Integrated Severity Analysis and First-Aid Guidance
authors: Dinesh S, Dhanusya S, Rohini R, Gowriha B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: It is difficult to differentiate between skin conditions caused by insect bites and allergic reactions simply based on the visual characteristics of the affected skin. As a result, this will require an accurate medical diagnosis by a qualified physician with expertise in diagnosing skin conditions. A project is working on creating a computer-based, automated skin lesion recognition system that will be able to use Artificial Intelligence to identify insect bites and skin allergies based on their severity level, and to provide first-aid information in real time. The method will utilise deep learning and computer vision technologies. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) will be used to analyse photographs taken of the affected area(s) of skin. Pretrained, state-of-the-art CNN architectures such as Efficient Net and Res Net will be used to achieve a high level of accuracy in detecting different types of insect bites (mosquito, tick, and spider, etc.) and allergic reactions (skin rash, redness, and/or irritation, etc.). The automated system will also classify skin lesions according to their severity level: mild, moderate, and/or severe, using either multi-class classification methods or regression methods.

title: Circularly Polarized Wearable IIoT Antenna for ISM Band
authors: Ramalakshmi Gali, VS Muralidhar Palanki
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The quick growth of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) applications, particularly in factory automation, has increased the demand for reliable wearable communication devices operating in Industrial, Scientific, and Medical (ISM) bands. However, maintaining stable wireless links is challenging due to non-line-of-sight (NLOS) conditions, human body movements, and multipath interference. To address these issues, this work proposes a compact wearable antenna design employing circular polarization (CP) to minimize polarization mismatch losses and enhance signal robustness. Initially, a slotted antenna operating at 4.5 GHz was developed with satisfactory impedance characteristics. Further design modifications, including slot dimension optimization and partial ground plane reduction, were introduced to improve bandwidth and performance. The final design incorporates truncated rectangular slots, resulting in dual-band operation at 2.45 GHz and 4.53 GHz, covering key ISM bands relevant to IIoT standards such as Wireless HART and ISA100.11a. The antenna achieves an enhanced bandwidth of 100 MHz with acceptable return loss and VSWR characteristics. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed antenna offers improved reliability, bandwidth, and polarization efficiency, making it a suitable candidate for wearable IIoT applications in dynamic industrial environments.

title: Autonomous Pipeline Inspection Using Lightweight Vision-Based UAV Tracking_supp1-3693978.mp4
authors: Praful Hambarde
publish: N/A
abstract: Long-range pipelines are indispensable infrastructures requiring continuous and rigorous inspections for structural health monitoring. Multi-copter (UAV) inspections face critical challenges including low-altitude flight constraints, limited computational resources for integrated visual perception and navigation control for the purpose of continuous inspection in real-time. The present work proposes an autonomous vision-based UAV low altitude inspection framework for industrial pipeline inspection. The proposed framework seamlessly integrates three innovative components: a lightweight deep learning architecture (MLA-Net) for robust pipeline segmentation, a vision-based control strategy for smooth tracking, and a sliding mode controller for real-time navigation. MLA-Net combines Cascaded Feature Extractor (CFE) blocks for hierarchical feature learning with Multi-Scale Receptive (MSR) blocks for multi-scale environmental adaption, achieving efficient pipe detection under varying conditions. The vision-based control strategy processes MLA-Net outputs through perspective projection and oriented rectangle extraction to coordinate yaw-lateral control, while a non-linear forward velocity function prevents overshooting during pipeline turns. The sliding mode controller fuses visual features with altitude data for autonomous navigation across complex pipeline networks. Extensive validation through simulation and real-time outdoor experiments demonstrates superior performance across diverse pipeline configurations including intersections and multipipe scenarios. The proposed MLA-Net achieves 0.8166 IoU while operating at ≈27 FPS with only 0.202 GFLOPs and 0.178M, significantly outperforming existing methods in computational efficiency. The integrated framework enables stable autonomous tracking across various pipeline geometries, providing a practical solution with enhanced accuracy and computational efficiency for resource-constrained UAV applications. Source code available at Autonomous vision-based pipline tracking by UAV

title: Axial-Relation Guided Fusion State Space Model for Optical-Elevation Sensing Image Segmentation
authors: Feng Gao, Zhilin Jin, Yanhai Gan, Junyu Dong, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Semantic segmentation of multi-source remote sensing images is a fundamental task for Earth observation applications. Existing methods often struggle with insufficient multi-scale context modeling and suboptimal cross-modal feature fusion, limiting their performance in complex high-resolution scenes. To this end, we propose Axial-Relation Guided Fusion Mamba (ARG-Mamba), a state space model–based framework for opticalelevation remote sensing image segmentation. Specifically, we introduce a Multi-Scale State Space Module to capture both fine-grained local details and global contextual dependencies with linear computational complexity. Moreover, an Axial-Relation Guided Fusion Module is designed to explicitly model global cross-modal correlations along horizontal and vertical axes, enabling efficient feature fusion between optical and elevation modalities. Extensive experiments conducted on the ISPRS Vaihingen and Potsdam datasets demonstrate that our ARG-Mamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods while maintaining favorable computational efficiency. The code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/oucailab/ARG-Mamba.

title: Vision based Road Damage and Speed Breaker Detection for Municipal Complaint System
authors: Ashok Dasari, Omkar Reddy Chenchireddy, Ramya Rayarapu, Rushikesh Bodigundla, Rahul Reddy Devulapalle
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Especially for fast-growing regions, the top alternative provides a serious issue of public road safety and transportation durability. Typically, the traditional methods of road inspection are performed at regular intervals and rely on manual observation, rendering them ineffective in identifying hazards in real time. Therefore, this paper a proposes an AI-based system based on YOLOv11 deep learning model to detect road damage and speed breakers. The YOLOv11-Nano architecture, trained on a curated dataset of images with varying road conditions such as potholes, surface damage and both labeled and unlabeled, unmarked speed breakers. Image preprocessing techniques are applied to enhance detection performance and improve model accuracy. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves a precision of 0.82 and a recall of 0.78 while performing detection within an average processing time of 2–4 seconds on a CPU-based environment. The developed system provides an efficient and automated solution for monitoring road hazards, which can assist municipal authorities in improving road maintenance and enhancing overall transportation safety.

title: Text Mining and Machine Learning for Workplace Accident Severity Classification in Industry 4.0
authors: Shyji Ealias, Anitha Rani K S, Addanki Mounika, Ranjeeth Kumar M, Win Mathew John, S. Rukmani Devi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Sensitive classification in accident reports work is essential for enhancing industrial safety, facilitating prompt risk assessment, and promoting data-supported preventive measure. However, the manual handling is time consuming, it sometimes turns out inconsistent and subjective. This paper investigates the potential of transformer-based models to automatically predict injury severity based on free-text accident descriptions. A total of 400 accident scenarios were gathered from multiple industries such as extraction, manufacturing, maintenance and production. Contrary to classic machine learning techniques which involve a lot of text processing and feature engineering, this method leverages BERT and DistilBERT models utilizing subword tokenization and contextual embeddings. Both models were finetuned with supervised training and compared by accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix and precision-recall curve interpretation. The results indicate that BERT achieved an accuracy of 95.83%, however, DistilBERT outperformed the BERT model marginally with an increased computational efficiency. These results suggest that transformer-based models can be a robust and viable solution for injury severity classification in the OHS domain. The research advances safety analytics within Industry 4.0 environment by showing how contextual language models can facilitate speed and consistency in assessments of workplace accidents.

title: A Lightweight Face Detection Framework Based on ShuffleNet and Coordinate Attention
authors: Jiayu Zhao, Chengming Zhou, Haoyi Xie, Changlin Li, Tongtong Wan, Lifu Cao, Yifan Jiang, Chaomeng Hao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Face detection serves as a fundamental prerequisite for diverse computer vision applications, including security surveillance, biometric recognition, and intelligent human-computer interaction. However, achieving an optimal trade-off between superior detection accuracy and real-time inference speed remains a persistent challenge, particularly for deployment on resource-constrained embedded systems. In this paper, we propose a highly efficient, attention-improved lightweight RetinaFace architecture. Specifically, we integrate ShuffleNet as the backbone network to drastically reduce computational overhead and incorporate a Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism to formulate a direction-aware feature enhancement module. This module leverages spatial coordinate encoding to explicitly guide attention weight distribution, effectively compensating for the representation limitations typical of lightweight backbones. Evaluated on the formidable WIDER FACE benchmark, the proposed method achieves competitive Average Precision (AP) scores of 92.70%, 90.42%, and 76.02% across the Easy, Medium, and Hard subsets, respectively. Empirical results demonstrate that our approach significantly enhances localization robustness for small-scale and severely occluded faces while maintaining an ultra-lightweight profile of merely 1.73M parameters and 5.94 GFLOPs, thereby exhibiting exceptional deployment adaptability for real-time edge applications.

title: A multi-agent collaboration framework for scene understanding and work order generation in smart farms
authors: Zhipeng Song, Qi Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Manual inspection is insufficient for large-scale smart farming, which requires near-real-time monitoring and closed-loop task management. This study proposes a multi-agent framework for scene understanding and automatic work order generation in cattle farms. To handle complex environments, large illumination changes, and low-frequency abnormal events, we design a state-flow-driven architecture with five agents: visual perception, semantic analysis, rule evaluation, temporal confirmation, and work order generation. The framework integrates multimodal large language models, using a fine-tuned InternVL3.5-8B model for visual perception and scene understanding and DeepSeek-V3.2 for semantic reasoning, rule-based evaluation, and task generation. A structured state object enables stepwise decision-making across layers. We further introduce a consecutive-frame confirmation mechanism to impose temporal consistency and reduce false triggers caused by single-frame errors. A structured work order model maps detected events to executable tasks, forming a closed loop from perception to action. We evaluate the method on a real farm dataset covering indoor and outdoor scenes, multiple time periods, camera views, and task categories, with frame-level manual annotations. Results show that the proposed framework is stable and robust in complex farm conditions and can reliably detect abnormal events and generate work orders automatically.

title: Hierarchical Deformation Planning and Neural Tracking for DLOs in Constrained Environments_supp1-3688805.mp4
authors: Yunxi Tang
publish: N/A
abstract: Deformable linear objects (DLOs) manipulation presents significant challenges due to DLOs’ inherent high-dimensional state space and complex deformation dynamics. The densely distributed obstacles in realistic workspaces further complicate DLO manipulation, necessitating efficient deformation planning and robust deformation tracking. In this work, we propose a novel framework for DLO manipulation in constrained environments. This framework combines hierarchical deformation planning with neural tracking, ensuring reliable performance in both global deformation synthesis and local deformation tracking. Specifically, the deformation planner begins by generating a spatial path set that inherently satisfies the homotopy constraints associated with DLO keypoint paths. Next, a path-set-guided optimization method is applied to synthesize an optimal temporal deformation sequence for the DLO. In manipulation execution, a neural model predictive control approach, leveraging a data-driven deformation model, is designed to accurately track the planned DLO deformation sequence. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through extensive constrained DLO manipulation tasks.

title: SF-QC: Explore the Selection Bias of Large Language Models in Zero-Shot Out-of-Distribution Intent Detection
authors: Heng-yang Lu, Xin-yi Liu, Jia-ming Zhang, Chenyou Fan, Wei Fang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Out-of-distribution (OOD) intent detection aims to identify user queries that exceed predefined intent categories and is a crucial technology for ensuring interaction reliability and user experience in practical applications such as dialogue systems and intelligent customer service. Although large language models (LLMs) perform excellently in natural language processing tasks, they encounter difficulties with zero-shot OOD intent detection. This article reveals the dual biases of LLMs in zero-shot OOD intent detection: 1) LLMs tend to prefer the intents that are presented earlier in the intent list; and 2) LLMs frequently misclassify unknown intent as in-distribution (ID) intent. These biases severely limit the deployment of LLMs in intent-recognition systems that require high reliability. To address this problem, we propose a novel semantics-based sorting-filtering and querying-confirming (SF-QC) method: the semantic sorting-filtering (SF) module dynamically adjusts the intent list to mitigate position preference, and then the querying-confirming (QC) module deeply validates the initial ID response to reduce OOD misclassification, starting from the root cause of biases to guide the LLM to make unbiased judgments. Experiments on two mainstream intent datasets show that our proposed SF-QC approach has up to 7.82% (Macro-F1) and 9.76% (ACC) improvement in overall performance, and up to 14.9% (Macro-F1) and 20.66% (ACC) improvement in OOD performance over the chain-of-thought (CoT) method. The excellent detection performance of SF-QC provides key technical support for the robust deployment of LLMs in practical applications such as dialogue systems, helping to reduce system risks and enhance user experience.

title: Unified Semantic Alignment and Cross-Graph Fusion Model for Multimodal Named Entity Recognition in Social Media
authors: Weinan Niu, Qingni Qin, Haoyi Huang, Wenan Tan, Jun Huang, Lida Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multimodal named entity recognition (MNER) aims to identify predefined entity types from text by incorporating auxiliary information such as images. Compared with traditional named entity recognition methods, MNER introduces visual context to recognize entities in ambiguous text. To more effectively utilize the visual modality for text understanding, most current approaches rely on static cross-modal feature alignment and fusion. However, when text-image correlations are weak, such strategies fail to align the semantic information between modalities. In addition, existing methods tend to model local and global semantic representations independently, lacking a unified framework that can jointly capture both local and global semantic information. To address these issues, we propose a unified semantic alignment and cross-graph fusion model framework (SAGF) for MNER. First, we introduce a multimodal adaptive semantic alignment mechanism that uses a trainable bilinear similarity function to adaptively align cross-modal semantic information under weak text-image correlation. Second, we propose a cross-modal graph fusion strategy. It combines local semantic representations based on attention mechanisms, with global structure features encoded in a unified graph structure. This joint local with global modeling enables the framework to capture semantic relations and contextual structures among entities. Experiments on two well-known social media datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the SAGF model, achieving strong performance in MNER.

title: LSTM-based Event-Driven Cyber Threat Detection Networks Deep Learning with CICIDS 2018
authors: Vudumula Lakshmi Priya, Jasthi Praveen Kumar, Moka Pannaga, Somanchi Sriteja, Bala Gangadhara Gutam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The evolving nature and magnitude of modern cyberattacks has emphasized the severe need of intelligent, scalable, automation and cyber-threat identification. The traditional security features cannot keep pace with attack vectors evolve rapidly resulting in lagging in reaction and increased vulnerability. To address these limitations, this paper presents a state-of-the-art intrusion detection framework with deep learning framework grounded on the utilization of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). neural networks. The characteristic of LSTM to acquire temporal relatedness allows it to be very useful in the detection of patterns aberrations to chain network traffic. The suggested approach first subjected the raw security logs and network traffic are preprocessed and outliers are minimized. key characteristics are mined to form lessons of event profiles. These profiles are a summary of vital time and action profiles. oral properties of network events, which is a potent input. sequences to the LSTM model. The model then acquires the lessons of the serial patterns to become aware of the identified and the previous clandestine invasions are very accurate. Experimental the validation is performed with respect to the CICIDS 2018 dataset, which is a popular one agreed standard that reflects a wide variety of modern cyberattacks. The results show that the LSTM-based model generates significant accuracy gains of detection, reduces false and improves the correctness of true positive alerts. The LSTM model is even superior in terms of performance of time pattern recognition and situational perception of network flows, Keywords- Cyber-threat detection, LSTM, network intrusion event profiling detection system (NIDS).

title: Megahertz Soft-Switching Wireless Power Transfer for Capsule Robots Under Dynamic Position and Orientation Variations_supp1-3693630.avi
authors: Chi Kwan Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Capsule robots for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics have gained increasing attention in recent years owing to their miniature form factor and non-invasive, painless operation. However, their limited size restricts the integration of large batteries, which poses a major challenge when advanced functions such as high-frame-rate, high-resolution video recording are required. Wireless power transfer (WPT) offers a promising solution to this limitation. In this work, we propose an MHz-range WPT scheme that leverages the high-Q characteristics of coils at MHz frequencies, enabling a significant reduction in both transmitter and receiver coil sizes, which is essential for miniaturized capsule robots. Furthermore, a three-dimensional receiver coil structure is analyzed and compared, identifying configurations with improved efficiency. A key challenge in capsule WPT is that capsule motion through the digestive tract alters the reflected impedance seen by the Class-E driver, thereby disrupting its zero-voltage switching (ZVS) operation. To address this issue, two control methods are introduced to ensure consistent soft-switching under varying capsule orientations. In addition, a power regulation algorithm is developed to stabilize the output voltage and its performance is experimentally validated. Finally, the average received power along the entire GI trajectory is evaluated under a constant transmitter current. These results establish a foundation for the development of next-generation wirelessly powered capsule robots.

title: Propelling DNA-Based Archival Storage with an Algorithmic Mindset
authors: Yaoyu Yang, Wei Wei, Minghao Mou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Molecular, Biological, and Multi-Scale Communications
abstract: The era of big data is creating an overwhelming demand for long-term data storage solutions. Among different storage media, DNA stands out for its compactness, stability, and natural compatibility. These advantages have spurred researchers’ interest from both experimental and theoretical perspectives. In this paper, we survey recent advances across the full pipeline of DNA-based storage, with an emphasis on the bottlenecks in data writing and reconstruction. Moreover, we summarize coding-theoretic problems in response to different experimental setups.We then zoom in on DNA-based image storage, which incorporates compression and post-processing algorithms into the workflow. We also note that artificial intelligence (AI) has played an increasingly important role in the DNA-based storage architecture. As careful algorithmic considerations are integrated with biochemical innovations, we envision that archival storage on DNA will become a reality in the near future.

title: Micromagnetic Simulation Method Incorporating Scaled Magnetostatic Structures: A Case Study on Demagnetization Behavior in Halbach Arrays
authors: Xinyu Zheng, Liqun Wang, Zhengping Sun, Lei Li, Guolai Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Halbach permanent magnet arrays offer enhanced magnetic field efficiency but are prone to self-demagnetization due to their complex internal fields. Traditional methods, such as residual flux density and nonlinear magnetization models, fail to accurately capture demagnetization under such conditions. This study proposes a micromagnetic simulation method incorporating scaled magnetostatic structures, enabling micromagnetism–magnetostatics coupled simulations. The model is applied to analyze self-demagnetization in Linear Halbach arrays used in permanent magnet eddy current dampers (PMECDs) and Ring Halbach arrays in permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs). Results show that in Linear Halbach arrays, localized magnetization reversal on axially magnetized magnet surfaces creates intermediate magnetic poles, leading to notable field attenuation. In Ring Halbach arrays, asymmetric demagnetization results in multi-domain configurations and severe field collapse over certain phase angles. The proposed model provides a novel tool for demagnetization analysis,and can offer practical guidance for the optimization of Halbach-based magnetic devices.

title: Stateless packet ordering function with end-to-end delay guarantee and jitter control for deterministic networking
authors: Juhyeok Kwon, Pusik Park, Jinoo Joung
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Packet replication and elimination mechanisms have been proposed to overcome packet loss caused by link failures or transient errors in deterministic networks. However, the process of ordering these replicated packets can distort the flow's burst characteristics, rendering the guarantee of end-to-end (E2E) delay bounds unfeasible. Furthermore, these altered characteristics can adversely affect other flows, potentially leading to the divergence of E2E delays as such effects propagate through the network. In this study, we propose a stateless packet ordering function (SOF) which adds the ordering function to the elimination node, allowing a single node to perform both elimination and ordering, while preserving the flow’s initial burst characteristics. SOF limits the jitter bound of the flow without flow state by utilizing a buffer and packet metadata. Since the approach is stateless, it is easy to implement in core nodes. By applying the buffered network concept to the ordering process, SOF restricts the size of the burst generated after ordering, thereby guaranteeing the delay bound and limiting the flow's jitter bound. We mathematically prove the theoretical E2E delay and jitter bounds of the flow with SOF, and analyze its performance in terms of E2E delay and jitter through simulation. Simulation results demonstrate that SOF satisfies the theoretical upper bounds for both E2E delay and jitter, while achieving a minimal level of jitter. Furthermore, the results show that a trade-off between jitter and average delay is achievable by adjusting the level of the jitter control function within SOF.

title: Welcome Speech by Conference Chair
authors: N/A

title: AdaS-Net: Adaptive Structure-aware Network for Camouflaged Object Detection
authors: Hongchen Wang, Shuzhen Xu, Cuicui Lv, Qiang Zheng, Kaile Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Camouflaged Object Detection (COD) aims to identify and segment objects that are visually blended into their surroundings, where object and background share highly similar color, texture, and structure. Such intrinsic resemblance suppresses discriminative cues and leads to weak contrast, blurred boundaries, and inconsistent scales, posing persistent challenges for conventional detection or segmentation pipelines. To address these issues, this paper presents an Adaptive Structure-aware Network (AdaS-Net) that unifies global semantic reasoning and local structural perception within a “see–enhance–reconstruct” paradigm. A Swin Transformer backbone first establishes hierarchical long-range representations to capture subtle semantic differences under high background similarity. On top of this representation, a Feature Enhancement Module (FEM) magnifies weak structural cues through orientation-aware depthwise convolutions and a dynamic kernel weighting mechanism, selectively emphasizing discriminative object regions while suppressing background interference. A Structure-Aware Pyramid Decoder (SAPD) then performs deformable multi-scale fusion and progressive top–down refinement, aligning high-level semantics with low-level boundary details for coherent object reconstruction. Extensive experiments on public COD benchmarks, including CAMO, COD10K, NC4K, and CHAMELEON, demonstrate that AdaS-Net consistently surpasses state-of-the-art convolutional and Transformer-based approaches in both detection accuracy and structural fidelity. By integrating adaptive structural enhancement with hierarchical semantic reconstruction, AdaS-Net establishes a new structure-aware paradigm for COD and offers a general perspective for structure-sensitive segmentation in complex visual environments. Codes and results are released on https://github.com/tian-xiu/AdaS-Net.

title: Accurate Impairment Modeling and PCS-Based Cross-Layer Optimization for Throughput Enhancement in C+L-Band Optical Networks
authors: Yuxin Xu, Hongyu Zhu, Weigang Hou, Xiaoxue Gong, Maite Brandt-Pearce
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: To enhance the throughput of state-of-the-art C+L-band optical networks, three significant factors are considered: spectral efficiency (SE) enhancement, margin reduction, and resource provisioning techniques that coordinate the benefits of these two factors. For margin reduction, an accurate physical-layer impairments (PLIs) model, namely the C+L-band enhanced component-wise Gaussian noise (C+L-ECWGN) model, is proposed to account for the modulation correction terms on PLIs and the impact of spectral shaping in C+L-band transmission, thereby providing a solid foundation for the application of probabilistic constellation shaping (PCS) in cross-layer network provisioning. The proposed model maintains a similar computational complexity to the traditional enhanced Gaussian noise (EGN) model, which neglects the actual spectral shape of demands while considering them as rectangular. The proposed model can reduce overestimation of modulation correction terms by up to 39.8% under typical roll-off conditions, with a maximum reduction in nonlinear interference (NLI) estimation error of 26.2% across various modulation formats compared with state-of-the-art models. Finally, by integrating PCS technology and the benefit of an accurate PLI estimate, the PLI-aware PCS cross-layer optimization (PAPCO) algorithm is developed to achieve continuous and adaptive modulation-to-SE mapping. Simulation results indicate that, compared with a benchmark scheme based on conventional EGN modeling with rectangular spectra and uniform QAM modulation, the proposed PAPCO-ECWGN algorithm reduces the normalized signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) margin to near zero, thus improving network throughput by 41.3% and average SE by 35.2 %.

title: Quantum Clock Synchronization Networks: A Survey
authors: Uman Khalid, Muhammad Shohibul Ulum, Mujirin, Giuseppe Thadeu Freitas De Abreu, Emil Björnson, Hyundong Shin
publish: IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials
abstract: Quantum clock synchronization (QCS) aims to establish a shared temporal reference between distant nodes by exploiting uniquely quantum phenomena such as entanglement, single-photon interference, and quantum correlations. In contrast to classical synchronization and time-transfer techniques, which are limited by signal propagation delays, atmospheric disturbances, and oscillator drift, QCS protocols offer the potential to surpass classical precision bounds and enhance resilience against adversarial manipulations. As precise and secure time synchronization underpins distributed quantum networks, navigation systems, and emerging quantum Internet infrastructures, understanding QCS principles, capabilities, and implementation challenges has become increasingly important. This survey provides a unified and critical overview of the rapidly growing QCS research landscape, highlighting fundamentals, protocol types, enabling resources, performance constraints, security considerations, and practical implementations of QCS. We first introduce the theoretical underpinnings of QCS, including entanglement-assisted time transfer, Hong-Ou-Mandel interference-based synchronization, and quantum slow-clock transport. We then categorize the main QCS protocols, ranging from ticking-qubit and entanglement-based schemes to time-of-arrival correlation methods, conveyor-belt synchronization, and quantum-enhanced two-way time transfer. This organization clarifies the relationships between protocol families and their achievable precision advantages over classical methods. Key quantum resources such as spontaneous parametric down-conversion-based entangled photon pairs, Greenberger–Horne–Zeilinger and W multipartite states, squeezed and frequency-entangled light, quantum frequency combs, and quantum memories are reviewed in the context of scalability and robustness. Practical limitations arising from decoherence, channel loss, dispersion, detector timing jitter, and relativistic effects are examined alongside emerging noise mitigation strategies. Security aspects—including eavesdropping on time correlations, intercept-resend attacks, and adversarial delay manipulation—are evaluated with respect to trusted and untrusted network architectures. Recent proof-of-concept demonstrations in fiber networks, free-space optical links, chip-integrated photonic systems, and satellite-ground platforms are summarized, followed by discussions of open challenges toward precise and secure global quantum-synchronized networks.
