title: Evaluating the Impact of Active Wire Fence Placement on Remote Power Side-Channel Attacks in Multi-Tenant FPGAs
authors: Sina Ojaghi, Ali Ghorbani Bargani, Sana Babayan Vanestan, Mona Hashemi, Siamak Mohammadi, Ahmad Khonsari
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: The increasing adoption of field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) within cloud infrastructures has concurrently intensified concerns over security within spatially shared, multitenant environments. Although logical isolation prevents direct interference among tenants, shared physical resources, particularly the on-chip power delivery network (PDN), enable remote power side-channel attacks that threaten cryptographic confidentiality. This work investigates the effectiveness of active wire fences as a lightweight, fabric-native countermeasure to mitigate such attacks. The primary goal of this study is to examine how the distance and configuration of the active wire fence affect the sensor response and the overall characteristics of side-channel attacks. We consider an adversarial tenant performing correlation power analysis (CPA) on a co-located AES-128 core using a routing-delay sensor (RDS) under realistic spatial isolation constraints. Through systematic experiments on an Artix-7 FPGA, we evaluate multiple fence placements between the victim and the attacker to identify configurations that maximize resistance without impairing functionality. Our results show that the fence’s spatial placement critically determines its defensive efficacy: positioning the fence nearest to the attacker’s sensor increases the necessary number of traces to achieve a stable key retrieval from 13k to over 105k and prevents full key extraction within 500k traces. These findings confirm that proximity-aware deployment of active wire fences significantly suppresses exploitable PDN leakage, providing a practical pathway toward secure and efficient multi-tenant FPGA virtualization in datacenter and cloud settings.

title: Voice-Controlled Robotic Arm for a Physically Challenged Person
authors: A.Vijayasri, S. Banupriya, D.Gayathri, V. Hemamalini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research work proposes a voice-controlled robotic arm for physically challenged people. The proposed system is a combination of speech recognition algorithms and a microcontroller, controlled actuation to make the robotic arm capable of voice command response. The algorithm applies speech signal processing operations such as Mel, Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCC) for speech recognition together with the implementation of machine learning classifiers to ensure high recognizing accuracies. The performance test has resulted in an average accuracy of 92, 95% in carrying out the commands, thus the system can be trusted. The robotic arm is planned to be constructed by the use of lightweight materials and operated by a servo motor for the convenience and lower cost of the device. The next phase of the project will focus on the application of natural language processing to handle complex command structures, IoT, based remote monitoring, and adaptive learning models for custom control of users. This technology will be a game-changer for the assistive technology sector as it will provide a cost-effective and user-friendly solution for physically disabled people to enhance their autonomy and quality of life. The system architecture has been designed with a focus on modularity, which makes it easier to upgrade and customize as per the requirements of the users. The accuracy of the algorithm has also been enhanced by the use of noise reduction techniques. The comparison of the proposed system with the existing assistive technology devices shows an improvement in the responsiveness and user satisfaction. The future scope of the project includes the application of natural language processing for complex commands, IoT-based remote monitoring, and adaptive learning models for personalizing control for individual users. This innovation will help in assistive technology by providing a cost-effective and user-friendly solution for improving the autonomy and quality of life of physically disabled individuals.

title: Battery Energy Storage Based Wide Area Damping Controller to Mitigate Small Signal Oscillations
authors: Lakshmi Nair, Vignesh V, Anurag Srivastava
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Small-signal oscillations pose a persistent challenge to power system stability, particularly under increasing penetration of converter-interfaced resources and wide-area control. Battery energy storage systems (BESS) offer fast and flexible damping capability, but their effectiveness is highly sensitive to communication delays, packet loss, and timing errors in wide-area measurement and control systems. This paper presents a communication-aware wide-area damping controller (WADC) for BESS based on robust <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> control with adaptive mode switching. The controller is designed using a two-step optimization framework: offline LMI-based <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> synthesis followed by anomaly-aware, mode-dependent adaptation. Formal Lyapunov-Krasovskii analysis provides explicit stability and performance guarantees under time-varying communication uncertainties. Validation on the IEEE 39-bus system and the Southern Regional Power Grid (SRPG), including real-time hardware-in-the-loop testing with commercial PMUs and PDCs, demonstrates sustained damping performance with delays of up to 800 ms, timing errors of 100 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>s, and packet loss up to 15%.

title: Interference Management in ISAC: A Synergistic TSTAR-RIS and RSMA Co-Design
authors: Anal Paul, Keshav Singh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is increasingly relevant to next-generation consumer electronics, where immersive, connected, and context-aware devices must support reliable data transmission, fine-grained sensing, and low-latency environmental awareness within compact hardware platforms. However, ISAC performance is fundamentally limited by coupled interference among communication streams, sensing probes, and users. Although rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) and simultaneously transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (STAR-RIS) are promising enablers, existing RSMA-STAR-RIS ISAC designs still rely on static electronically controlled surfaces, leaving most interference management to the signal layer. To overcome this limitation, we propose a telescopic STAR-RIS (TSTAR-RIS) aided RSMA-ISAC framework, where mechanically tunable element heights provide an additional physical degree of freedom for electrical-path-length tuning and channel preconditioning. We formulate a joint ISAC utility-maximization problem that couples RSMA common/ private precoders, sensing beamforming, quantized STAR coefficients, and telescopic heights under practical hardware and quality-of-service constraints. To solve the resulting non-convex problem, we develop a three-block alternating-optimization algorithm that combines weighted mean squared error minimization (WMMSE) updates for the RSMA block with projected-gradient updates for the sensing beamformer and TSTAR-RIS variables within a primal-dual framework. Simulation results show that the proposed TSTAR-RSMA design consistently enlarges the communication-sensing tradeoff region relative to static STAR-RSMA and TSTAR-NOMA baselines. Across the considered power range, it achieves up to 38.24% communication gain over TSTAR-NOMA and up to 27.27% over STAR-RSMA, while maintaining sensing performance clearly above TSTAR-NOMA.

title: SFID: An Azimuthal Structure-Guided Robust and Adaptive Interference Detector for SAR Imagery
authors: Lunhao Duan, Xingyu Lu, Jianchao Yang, Huizhang Yang, Hong Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) is highly susceptible to Radio Frequency Interference (RFI). A novel RFI detector (SFID) based on left singular vector characteristics is proposed for Single-Look Complex (SLC) SAR images. The core principle is to exploit the azimuthal structure difference between focused targets and interference: the target response manifests as a sinc function, while the interference response exhibits a Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) form. Specifically, the method segments images into patches, performs Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) on each patch, applies Fractional Fourier Transform (FRFT) at the optimal angle to the left singular vector matrix, and realizes Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) detection by comparing the squared global FRFT peak p1 with an adaptive threshold. Theoretical derivation shows that p1 obeys the Gumbel distribution in clean SAR images, providing a basis for adaptive threshold calculation. For TOPSAR mode, the linear variation of the focused target Doppler bandwidth centroid degrades detection performance; thus, an interference model for TOPSAR mode is established and detection preprocessing tailored to this mode is introduced to enhance detection robustness. Experiments on measured data from Gaofen-3 and Sentinel-1 demonstrate that SFID can effectively detect low-Interference-to-Signal Ratio (ISR) and dense interference, outperforming traditional detection methods.

title: Subarray-Based Victim-Centric Per-Row Counting for Mitigating RowHammer
authors: Hwayoung Jeong, Juyong Lee, Hayoung Lee, Sungho Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: RowHammer remains a critical security and reliability challenge in DRAM, despite numerous mitigation techniques. Existing solutions face limitations depending on their implementation: memory controller (MC) based approaches lack visibility into internal DRAM mappings, hindering precise victim row identification, while DRAM-based methods are limited by interface constraints that prevent timely mitigation. Recently, per-row activation counting (PRAC) and ALERT-back-off (ABO) protocol have been introduced as a JEDEC standard, enabling MC-DRAM interaction to track and mitigate vulnerable rows. However, most existing tracking-based solutions including PRAC enforce aggressor-centric counting, leading to degraded mitigation efficiency. In this paper, SubArray-based Victim-cEntric per-Row counting (SAVER) is proposed as an advanced RowHammer mitigation technique. In SAVER, a counter subarray is implemented in each bank using low-cost DRAM cells, allowing vulnerable rows to be deterministically tracked. By leveraging subarray parallelism, count updates are performed concurrently with row activation, ensuring that DRAM timing constraints are preserved. Additionally, victim-centric counting is enabled by this structure, improving mitigation efficiency. To further address the interface-related limitations, the ABO protocol is utilized in SAVER to mitigate the identified vulnerable rows. Experimental results demonstrate that SAVER deterministically mitigates RowHammer while reducing performance overhead compared to existing state-of-the-art solutions.

title: Real-Time Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Urban Air Quality using Multi-Source Environmental Sensor Data
authors: Devi T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Reliable real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) classification systems are desperately needed because of the sharp rise in air pollution levels caused by rapid industrialization and urbanization. Risks to data privacy, significant transmission overhead, and unequal data distribution among geographically dispersed monitoring stations are some of the issues that traditional centralized prediction models must deal with. This work suggests FHRM, a Federated Hybrid Classification model for AQI prediction using the Air Quality Dataset in order to get over these restrictions. The proposed FHRM framework operates on decentralized environmental sensor networks, where model training is performed locally to preserve data confidentiality and reduce communication costs. Federated learning is employed to aggregate local model knowledge into a global representation capable of handling dynamic pollution patterns. The effectiveness of the proposed model is evaluated by comparing it with the TFT (Temporal Fusion Transformer) approach. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FHRM achieves an accuracy of 94.68%, significantly outperforming the TFT-2024 model, which achieves 84.23%. The results confirm that FHRM provides improved accuracy, robustness, and suitability for real-time AQI classification in large-scale urban environments.

title: Research on Automatic Generation of Ophthalmic Medical Records Based on Large Language Models
authors: Ying Li, Qi Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Doctor–patient dialogue in ophthalmology outpatient settings is highly colloquial and informationally fragmented, making automatic extraction of key clinical information for structured medical record generation an urgent challenge in medical informatics. This paper proposes a two-stage "continual pre-training + domain-specific supervised fine-tuning" method using four large language models including Qwen3-8B. Domain knowledge is first injected using ophthalmic textbooks, expert consensus documents, and disease classification standards, followed by supervised fine-tuning on real dialogue–record paired data to enable structured record generation from unstructured dialogues. Experiments on 823 real outpatient records from Baotou Chaoju Eye Hospital show that Qwen3-8B achieved optimal performance after two-stage training, with BLEU-4 of 69.65%, ROUGE-1 of 74.15%, ROUGE-2 of 65.54%, and ROUGE-L of 77.26%, substantially improving over pre-fine-tuning baselines. Comparisons with Qwen2-7B-Instruct, Llama3-8B-Chinese-Chat, and Qwen3-4B-Thinking further validated the method's effectiveness. Public benchmark evaluations confirm no significant degradation on general understanding tasks, demonstrating that the training strategy effectively mitigates catastrophic forgetting while enhancing domain-specific capabilities.

title: Physics-guided Latent Diffusion Model for Intensity- and Extremity-aware VIL Nowcasting
authors: Chulin Gao, Yichuan Luo, Liang Leng, Jie Liu, Tong Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Nowcasting heavy precipitation events poses significant challenges for both operational forecasting and scientific research due to their small spatial scale, rapid evolution, and high variability. Existing models often struggle to represent the spatial location and intensity of heavy precipitation, leading to a rapid loss of predictive skill as precipitation intensity increases and with longer forecast lead times, partly because they lack targeted model designs or dedicated mechanisms for representing heavy-precipitation structures. To address these challenges, we develop a physics-guided latent diffusion model informed by physical priors to better represent the spatiotemporal evolution and intensity of heavy precipitation. The model integrates optical flow methods and atmospheric motion equations to learn motion patterns across different precipitation intensities, with particular strength in representing heavy precipitation regions. A spatiotemporal autoencoder reduces computational cost and preserves temporal consistency by encoding background and motion-related features into three latent representations. Two additional objectives—an extreme-event-aware loss and a physical consistency loss—are introduced to enhance the model's ability to reproduce extreme precipitation and maintain physical coherence. Experiments on benchmark datasets show that our model outperforms the best baseline by 14%, 18%, and 19% in CSI for intense, very heavy, and extreme precipitation, respectively, while also demonstrating more stable performance over longer forecast horizons through the frame-by-frame CSI curves. These results highlight the model's potential for improving heavy-precipitation nowcasting and early warning.

title: Deep Learning–based Intelligent Traffic and Road Safety Monitoring System
authors: K. Pavanikrishna, Vardhineedi Nithin Sai Narayana, Vedireswarapu Pavan Kumar, Thota Pavan Krishna Rama Mahesh, Rayapureddy Phaneendhra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Traditional systems of traffic observation make use of manual inspection and rule-based systems. Which are not scalable, flexible and detect incidences in time in case of dynamic traffic. Even though the newest intelligent transportation systems include camera-based surveillance, most of the current solutions do not offer a proper automated safety analysis and real-time decision aid. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper suggests an intelligent traffic and road safety monitoring system based on a deep learning that can be used to detect vehicles in real-time, estimate the traffic density, identify accidents, and monitor the road condition using live video streams. The suggested model uses a convolutional neural network to extract the features and classify them automatically and combine them with real-time video processing and real-time alert generation algorithms. Experimental testing performed on real-world traffic video datasets shows that the proposed system has a total detection accuracy of 95.1, precisions of 94.3 and recall of 95.8 and F1-score of 95.0 in detecting safety-critical traffic events. The system also has a means of average processing latency of 72 ms per frame, which allows it to be operated in near real time as per the application of continuous traffic surveillance. Also, the model demonstrates strong behavior in different traffic density, lighting and urban road conditions and the false alarm rate is low, 3.1. The findings affirm that the combination of deep learning-based video analytics with real-time processing leads to a notable increase in the accuracy of traffic monitoring, the time of incidents response, as well as the overall safety of the roads. The suggested system offers a next-generation intelligent transportation system and scalable solution to smart cities.

title: Machine Learning Enabled Movement Pattern Recognition and Monitoring Using a Pomegranate Membrane Based Sensor
authors: Harwinder Singh, Harminder Singh, Ravinder Singh Sawhney, Kuldeep Singh, Khushmeet Singh
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Wearable Intelligent activity recognition has emerged as a rapidly growing research area with significant relevance to ubiquitous computing and intelligent human–computer interaction, enabling applications in healthcare, fitness assessment, gaming, tactical military systems, and indoor navigation. Wearable sensing technologies, particularly triboelectric nanogenerator (TENG)–based sensors, have gained considerable attention due to their self-powered operation, mechanical flexibility, stability, and cost-effectiveness. In this work, a biodegradable and sustainable triboelectric nanogenerator sensor is designed and developed to promote environmentally friendly electronic systems. The proposed device utilizes a pomegranate membrane as the primary triboelectric material, resulting in a biodegradable pomegranate membrane–based TENG (PM-TENG). The PM-TENG demonstrates a maximum output voltage of 48.3 V and is capable of charging multiple commercial capacitors. Furthermore, the sensor effectively captures, monitors, and distinguishes human activities, including walking, running, and jumping. To enhance activity recognition performance, a machine learning–based framework is introduced for efficient signal processing and accurate classification of human activities. The integration of machine learning models with the PM-TENG sensor shows superior performance, with the histogram gradient boosting (HGB) model achieving the highest classification accuracy of 97.78%. These results highlight the potential of the proposed PM-TENG sensor as a sustainable, self-powered, and intelligent wearable platform for high-precision human activity monitoring.

title: Learning One-Step Inverse for Performance Improvement of Nonlinear Control Systems: Application to Quadrotor Control
authors: Hamin Chang, Jonathan Lane, Nak-seung Patrick Hyun
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a learning-based control framework to enlarge the region of attraction of controllers for nonlinear systems subject to model uncertainties. When a nominal controller designed for an assumed nominal model is applied to a physical system, discrepancies between the model and the actual dynamics act as perturbations and degrade stability and performance. To mitigate this issue, we construct a controller that learns a one-step inverse of the dynamics via kernel methods. Unlike conventional methods that augment the nominal controller, the proposed approach does not rely on specific structural assumptions on the system dynamics and uncertainties. We provide a theoretical analysis guaranteeing that, under sufficient data-density conditions, the proposed controller yields smaller perturbations than those of the nominal controller in the closed-loop system. As a proof of concept, the effectiveness of the proposed method is experimentally validated on a micro-quadrotor. The results demonstrate an enlarged region of attraction compared to the nominal controller, even when using limited data (500 training input-output pairs) from a single episode.

title: Lateral and Longitudinal Position Estimation and Control for Dynamic WPT Using Receiver Current and Vehicle Dynamics Information
authors: Ryota Tauchi, Yusaku Takagi, Yutaka Shikauchi, Aoi Oyane, Daisuke Gunji, Osamu Shimizu, Hiroshi Fujimoto
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) promises to extend the driving range of electric vehicles. However, efficiency is severely reduced by coil misalignment. Especially when decelerating and stopping in urban driving, both longitudinal and lateral positions become critical. Achieving precise alignment without costly dedicated WPT sensors remains challenging because roadembedded coils are invisible. To address this, this article proposes an integrated position estimation and control framework with a simple current sensor and other vehicle motion sensors. An Unscented Kalman Filter simultaneously estimates both lateral and longitudinal positions by fusing receiver side DC current and vehicle dynamics with a pre-measured mutual inductance map, eliminating the need for dedicated WPT sensors. To ensure accurate alignment, a feedback controller is developed utilizing the estimated states. Experiments using a full-scale electric vehicle, which achieves a maximum received electric power of approximately 1.5 kW, demonstrate that the proposed method achieved a stopping accuracy of 31.4mm and 13.9mm in Root Mean Square Error in the longitudinal and lateral directions, respectively. This precise alignment improves energy efficiency by approximately 6.7% compared to a lateral misalignment of 60mm. Furthermore, through benchmarking with standard Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) functions, specifically Lane Keeping Assist and Adaptive Cruise Control, the results verify that the proposed framework possesses the potential to effectively augment the ADAS system to achieve the precise alignment essential for efficient DWPT.

title: Event-Driven Restoration of Interconnected Distribution Systems with R-SOPs Adapting to Continuous Disasters and Uncertainties
authors: Zihao Li, Xiaodong Yang, Jing Ma, Da Xu, Yue Yang, Qiuwei Wu, Lijian Ding, Jinyu Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: Extreme disasters lead to continuous and large scale faults in distribution systems (DSs), posing challenges in developing practical restoration strategies. This paper proposes a pre-disaster prevention and post-disaster restoration framework for R-SOP-enabled interconnected distribution systems (IDSs), which accounts for the persistence of fault evolution and the uncertainties of renewable energy and load. Unlike most existing studies that focus on individual DSs and fixed fault scenarios, this paper employs a reconfigurable soft open point (R-SOP) with asymmetric converter design. Through its feeder selection and converter reconfiguration mechanisms, the R-SOP enables efficient capacity utilization and dynamic interconnection between multiple DSs, and additionally addresses, in a unified manner, the dual uncertainty of stochastic fluctuations and sudden faults under continuous disasters. In the pre-disaster phase, electric buses (EBs) are deployed based on restoration simulations to reduce potential load shedding and shorten travel distances post disaster. In the post-disaster phase, an event-driven restoration is developed, where predefined fault events and error events dynamically update restoration strategies to adapt to continuous faults and uncertainties. By combining R-SOP-assisted recon figuration with coordinated scheduling of flexible resources, the proposed method overcomes the limitations of individual-network restoration, enhancing system resilience. Simulation results based on a modified 102-node IDS show that the proposed method not only accelerates service restoration under continuous extreme events but also ensures voltage security.

title: Characterization of Sub-Wavelength Spherical Particles by Electromagnetic Scattering
authors: Nikhil Kumar, Utpal Dey
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This work explores the electromagnetic scattering from sub-wavelength-sized non-resonating spherical particles for characterization in V-band from 50GHz to 67 GHz. A scaled experimental test setup is engineered, which excites a quasi-planar wave incidence onto the test particle, and subsequently, forward scattered fields are captured using dielectric probes of small numerical apertures. The modeling of the measurement setup in frequency domain follows a robust wave propagation model with and without the test-particle and it is complemented by time domain analysis. It is followed by system calibration which is formulated from the Mie scattering model to post-process the measured raw scattering data for spherical particles of both conducting and non-conducting material. The extracted diameters of dielectric and metallic particles agree well with their physically measured values. The extracted complex permittivity agree well with corresponding values reported in the literature. As demonstrated in this work, the measurement of electromagnetic scattering signatures from non-resonant particles of radii going down to one-tenth of the operating wavelength opens a low-cost, low-power, fast, robust, and non-destructive solution for particle sensing and characterization.

title: Multi-label classification of medical images based on multi-scale feature fusion
authors: Shuye Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Due to the characteristics of medical images such as high image complexity and disease correlation, traditional deep learning may not be suitable for multi-label classification of medical images. To address these issues, this paper proposes the DFEFNet prediction model, which extracts features through a local branch based on convolutional neural networks (CNN) and a global branch based on window self-attention, aiming to capture the local details and global semantics of the image. The features are further fused through a feature fusion module to enhance the model’s expressive ability. A combined loss function is used to solve the problems of data imbalance and label co-occurrence. This method achieved an ROC curve area of 82.14% and an accuracy of 86.13% on the ChestX-ray14 dataset. Compared with traditional methods in multi-label medical image classification, it exhibits superior performance and robustness advantages.

title: Blockchain-driven Freshness-keeping Efforts in Community Group-buying Platforms
authors: Jianghua Wu, Yuqian Wang, Junzhu Yao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: The loss of freshness-keeping efforts in fresh product supply chains not only diminishes consumers' purchasing experiences but also leads to significant economic losses. This challenge is particularly pronounced in community group-buying (CGB) supply chains. This study investigates how blockchain adoption can influence CGB supply chains, which involve a platform that sources products from the supplier and delegates order fulfillment to the group leader. By leveraging blockchain-enabled real-time traceability, a platform can precisely attribute freshness-keeping efforts to the respective parties, thereby facilitating an incentive mechanism tailored to the effort level. The analysis yields three key findings. First, counterintuitively, blockchain adoption under platform-determined pricing induces a free-riding effect: freshness-keeping efforts are intensified by the group leader and reduced by the supplier. However, the supplier's efforts rebound when pricing power is granted with a higher incentive coefficient. The acquisition of pricing power facilitates blockchain adoption in enhancing supplier's freshness-keeping efforts, yet proves more challenging in improving group leader's freshness-keeping efforts. Second, the platform adopts blockchain only when the traceability cost is sufficiently low and the incentive coefficient remains moderate. Third, lower traceability costs result in a win–win–win (platform-supplier-group leader) outcome, expanding with consumer sensitivity to freshness-keeping efforts. The robustness of these findings is verified through extensive numerical simulations and sensitivity analyses. Furthermore, the optimal incentive coefficient decreases monotonically with the commission rate and exhibits an inverted U-shaped relationship with consumer freshness-keeping effort sensitivity. Our study contributes to existing theoretical frameworks regarding the value of blockchain in fresh product supply chains and provides novel managerial insights for CGB platforms.

title: Insider Log Poisoning Attack on Enterprise SIEM Pipeline via Compromised Aggregation Database
authors: Preetish Madhu, Prabhakar Krishnan, Hari N. N., Sethu Subramanian N, Kurunandan Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Modern IoT deployments rely on logging pipelines for attack detection, with aggregation layers implicitly trusted by SIEM systems like the ELK stack. This work identifies a critical vulnerability in aggregation databases that has received limited attention in prior research. The research project uses a dual store MinIO system including a Raw WORM and Parsed JSON bucket together with the CICIoT2023 and CICIoTDIAD2024 datasets to create a simulation. A privileged insider performs two attack methods: appending semantically corrupted logs and modifying the parsing scripts. These attacks (timestamp shifting and label flipping) reduce malicious event visibility by ∼77.8%, significantly increase false negatives, and render SIEM alerts and dashboards largely ineffective. Our solution requires the implementation of cryptographic log signing together with hash chain verification and dual-path parsing methods.

title: Personalized Sleep Pattern AI Coach: IoT Enabled
authors: Padmaja Kadiri, Palagati Anusha, Velkuru Rishipriya, Idamakanti Lasya Reddy, Tadimarri Vishnu Vardhan, T S Bharath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Sleep is a crucial constituent of cognitive functioning, metabolic equilibrium and the whole physiological health yet the traditional methods of monitoring like a polysomnography (PSG) is invasive and does not have suitability to continue use at home. To overcome this shortcoming, this paper suggests an IoT-enabled Personalized Sleep Pattern AI Coach that combines EEG and EMG wearable sensors with a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM-Attention deep learning model in a real-time sleep stage classification. The CNN derives spatial information out of physiological records, whereas the BiLSTM cuts across the sleep interim with a view to recognize temporal associations, and the attention system maximizes the recognition of significant patterns of transition. To cope with the issue of imbalance in the number of classes, Focal Loss is utilized in cases of sleep data. The model has been tested with subject-wise 5-fold cross-validation performance on Sleep-EDF dataset, with the final result of the model being an overall accuracy of 96.7% and the average F1-score of 0.96, and the low-latency aspect of inference performances is suitable when run on edges. The system also facilitates intelligent integration of the home so that it becomes adaptive towards optimizing the environment. The strategy offers a scalable, effective and customized solution of continuous sleep tracking and real-time improvement of sleep quality in the home settings.

title: Static Label Graph Augmented Multi-modal Multi-label Tabular Classification Method
authors: Tianxi Chen, Bo Liu, Yunfei Zhao, Yuanyuan Cai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Multi-label learning is of great practical importance in healthcare and insurance applications. However, existing tabular classification methods are mainly designed for unimodal inputs or single-label outputs, making them unsuitable for real-world tasks where structured fields and OCR-derived text fields coexist and each sample may correspond to multiple labels. To address this issue, this paper constructs a multimodal multi-label tabular classification task based on real medical expense list data, and further designs four subtasks with different label granularities, namely L2, L3, L4, and L5, under a unified sample set and feature space. For this task, a static label-graph enhanced mixture-of-experts method is proposed. Specifically, structured fields and OCR text fields are first unified into a shared representation; then, a gated mixture-of-experts encoder is employed for adaptive sample representation learning; finally, a static label graph is built according to label co-occurrence in the training set, and a graph attention network is used for label representation learning and multi-label prediction. Experimental results show that the proposed method consistently outperforms multiple baselines on all four subtasks, and its advantage becomes more evident as the label space grows, demonstrating its effectiveness in multimodal feature modeling and label correlation learning.

title: A CNN-based Path Loss Prediction Scheme Assisted by Satellite Imagery
authors: Kunlei Du, Hengzhi Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In path loss prediction for suburban scenarios, satellite aerial imagery, as a cost-effective source of geographic data, has been demonstrated to enable the extraction of environmental information related to radio wave propagation through neural networks, thereby enhancing prediction accuracy. However, such images contain both geographical features strongly correlated with channel attenuation and background noise with weak correlation. These redundant features not only increase the computational burden but also interfere with the model's effective learning, emerging as a bottleneck limiting performance improvement. To address this, this paper focuses on the computational cost issue in the channel prediction process and proposes a satellite image feature optimization scheme tailored for suburban environments, aiming to achieve efficient and accurate path loss prediction by filtering out critical information.

title: Complex Task Planning for Large Language Models via Text-Code Collaborative Reasoning
authors: Chanjuan Tang, Zefei Long, Xinqi Wang, Zhengtao Wang, Guanying Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often face challenges such as insufficient planning capabilities and numerical hallucinations in complex tasks requiring real-time interaction and precise reasoning. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Text-Code Collaborative Reasoning framework. This framework establishes an iterative loop "Thought-Code-Result", utilizing a code interpreter as an external cognitive extension interface to deeply integrate natural language planning with external tool invocation. Furthermore, to address the scarcity of high-quality instruction data, a reverse instruction distillation method based on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is proposed to automate the construction of long-sequence task planning data. The method is validated in the high-dynamic, high-precision scenario of online financial analysis using a constructed benchmark of 15,000 trajectories. Experimental results indicate that the method achieved a key code element recall of 0.89, significantly improving tool invocation accuracy and logical completeness. These findings demonstrate the value and robustness of this collaborative reasoning paradigm in solving complex domain-specific tasks.

title: AQUO Feature-Driven Machine Learning Prediction for Far-Field Ship Radiated Noise
authors: Wenlong Yan, Yao Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a neural network methodology to predict far-field URN based on near-field acoustic measurements and ship speed. By fitting both near-field and far-field acoustic data to the AQUO underwater noise prediction model, core coefficients reflecting machinery and propeller mechanisms are extracted for effective dimensionality reduction. The extracted near-field AQUO fitting features, combined with real-time ship speed parameters, serve as joint inputs for a Back Propagation (BP) neural network to predict the corresponding far-field AQUO coefficients. Comparative evaluations demonstrate that traditional spectrum data direct-driven models are highly prone to overfitting background noise during cross-speed extrapolation. Conversely, this dimensionality-reduced prediction model, which fuses physical features and speed conditions alongside the Bayesian Regularization (BR) algorithm, achieves optimal comprehensive accuracy and successfully enables the precise extrapolation of radiated noise characteristics at lower speeds. This study provides a reliable engineering approach for deducing far-field radiated noise using near-field signal self-perception.

title: Implement Digital Longitudinal Monitoring for Intelligent Optical Networks
authors: Xian Zhou, Runzhe Fan, Rundong Xie, Zhudong Shi, Guanlin Liu, Xulong Yan, Yuyang Gao, Fei Liu, Fan Zhang, Keping Long
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Optical fiber link monitoring has become a cornerstone for achieving high reliability and autonomous operation in next-generation intelligent optical networks. With the continuous growth of network capacity, node density, and transmission data rates, physical-layer impairments require real-time, in-service, and distributed monitoring capabilities that surpass the limitations of traditional reactive maintenance strategies. This paper provides a comprehensive and systematic overview of digital longitudinal monitoring techniques derived from the nonlinear Schrödinger equation (NLSE), including digital backpropagation, correlation-method, and mean-squared error based approaches. We discuss their mathematical foundations and clarify how they can monitor the profiles of physical parameters using only the received signals. Application cases in core, metro, access, and submarine networks are analyzed, demonstrating their feasibility and potential in power profile estimation (PPE), transient event capture, loss localization, amplifier gain analysis, fiber-type identification, and multi-parameter monitoring such as polarization dependent loss and differential group delay. Beyond profiling current achievements, we analyze practical challenges in real network deployment, including model-accuracy mismatches, computational complexity, sensitivity to parameter drift, and so on. Finally, we outline future research directions toward complexity reduction, parameter self-adaptation, enhanced robustness, and seamless integration with software-defined and data-driven control planes. These perspectives are expected to support the evolution of NLSE-based link monitoring from laboratory demonstrations to scalable deployment in large-scale operational optical systems.

title: Research on a Lightweight Key Management Mechanism for ADS B Systems
authors: Enming Liang, Hui Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The Automatic Dependent Surveillance–Broadcast system relies on plaintext broadcasting and is therefore vulnerable to security threats such as identity spoofing and data tampering. To address the high complexity and limited real-time performance of existing key management schemes in highly dynamic aviation environments, we proposes a lightweight certificateless key management mechanism for ADS-B systems. The proposed mechanism uses the unique identifier of an aircraft as the seed for public key generation and achieves mutual authentication and session key agreement based on elliptic curve cryptography, thereby eliminating the overhead associated with certificate management. Security analysis indicates that the proposed mechanism can effectively resist common attacks, including man-in-the-middle and replay attacks.

title: UG-HDR: Uncertainty-Guided HDR Imaging under Large Motion and Extreme Exposure
authors: Jialiang Huang, Lijia Gao, Rong Xiao, Yan Wang, Lituan Wang, Chenwei Tang, Jiancheng Lv
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multi-exposure High Dynamic Range (HDR) imaging aims to reconstruct high-quality HDR images by fusing multiple Low Dynamic Range (LDR) images captured under varying exposures. Existing methods suffer from severe ghosting artifacts and heavy computational loads under large motion and extreme exposure, which restricts their practical application. To overcome these limitations, we propose UG-HDR, an uncertainty-guided framework with the “Align-Fuse-Refine” paradigm to eliminate ghosting artifacts under critical conditions, as well as to achieve a balance between high-quality HDR imaging and efficient performance. Concretely, we identify the lack of differential treatment for large motions and extreme exposures in existing methods, and innovatively introduce a Mixture-of-Laplace (MoL) distribution to estimate pixel-wise uncertainty maps in the “Align-Fusion” stage. These maps provide quantitative guidance for the Refine stage by locating motion-blurred and extreme exposure areas. Then, based on the MoL distribution, we also design an Align-MoL loss based on the luminance mask that enforces geometric consistency in key regions. Finally, an attention-guided multi-scale large convolution kernel module is proposed to extract features from the uncertainty map, and the ghost area is optimized through the spatial-channel attention. Extensive experiments prove that our method achieves an excellent balance between imaging quality and computational efficiency.

title: Gridless DoA Estimation in Semi-Passive IRS-Assisted Sensing via Atomic Norm Minimization and an Accelerated Proximal Gradient Method
authors: Yuan Wang, Xianpeng Wang, Yuehao Guo, Mingcheng Fu, Linqiang Wen, Han Wang, Guan Gui
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) enable radar sensing in blocked environments by reconfiguring propagation and creating virtual apertures, which is crucial for non-line-of-sight (NLoS) localization. This work addresses high-accuracy direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation in semi-passive IRS-assisted sensing. We introduce a virtual-domain lifting that vectorizes the received echoes and induces a structured atomic set, leading to an atomic norm minimization (ANM) formulation. The ANM estimator is formulated as a semidefinite program (SDP) via convex relaxation, and we develop an accelerated proximal gradient (APG) solver that leverages the problem structure and avoids interior-point steps, resulting in substantial computational savings. Compared with spatial-domain and grid-based approaches, the transformed-domain estimator delivers an optimal accuracy-complexity tradeoff. It achieves gridless (ANM-level) high resolution while reducing runtime. Extensive simulations across array sizes, transmit power, and IRS configurations confirm accuracy, robustness to off-grid mismatch, and scalability, demonstrating the practicality of the proposed method for IRS-enabled NLoS sensing in complex environments.

title: An Electromagnetic Coupling Model of Faraday Shields in High-Frequency Transformers
authors: Zhan Shen, Kaiyuan Liu, Xiangbo Wang, Md Ahnaf Tahmid Anon, Wu Chen, Fujin Deng, Jian Ai, Jianxi Lan, Frede Blaabjerg, Huai Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In high-frequency transformers, Faraday shields divert the coupling of the common-mode (CM) noise to the ground and contribute to losses in both magnetic fields and electric fields. However, the electrical model is not found in existing research, which brings challenges to the shield design. To address it, an analytical model is proposed to calculate the electrical voltage distribution, CM current, and its generated loss in the Faraday shield. With the method to couple the electric and magnetic fields, the shield is modeled as a resistance-inductance-capacitance-voltage source based circuit. The voltage distribution along the shield is derived to analyze the Electromagnetic Interference (EMI). Moreover, the criteria to determine the electrical losses and to keep the shielding function are given in the possible scenarios of transformer design we foresee, where a quantitative impedance ratio and the corresponding current-shunting ratio are introduced for the shielding-function assessment. The model is applicable to shields with single and multiple turns and layers, arbitrary winding-voltage distributions, and electrical shields of magnetics. With a practical shielding criterion of $\kappa = 10$, the proposed method provides a guideline for shield-thickness design under low-loss insulation conditions.

title: A Novel Dual-Module Iterative Residual Decision Detection for MIMO-OTFS Systems
authors: Meng Tang, Lei Wang, Hao Wang, Kequan Duan, Peng Liu, Chuixiang Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Multiple-input multiple-output orthogonal time frequency space (MIMO-OTFS) has been proven to enhance the reliability of symbol transmission in high-mobility communications. However, the presence of inter-Doppler interference in fractional Doppler channels and the large dimensionality of the channel matrix pose challenges to signal detection. To address this issue, we propose an iterative residual decision detection (IRDD) algorithm. Specifically, signal residuals are employed to transform the system model into a sparse representation. Based on the modified least squares minimal residual detection model, we develop a fast Bayesian compressed-sensing search algorithm for efficient decision feedback, which constitutes a dual-module iterative signal detection framework. Simulation results show that the proposed IRDD achieves faster convergence speed and superior bit-error-rate performance.

title: IDGM-DD: Interactive Decision-Making Model for Autonomous Vehicles Integrating Dynamic Game Theory and Driving Tendency
authors: Lan Zhao, Yuanyuan Ren, Naiwei Zou, Lin Li, Zhipeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: To resolve decision-making conflicts between autonomous and human-driven vehicles in mixed traffic scenarios, this paper proposes an Interactive Decision-making Game Model with Dynamic Deduction (IDGM-DD). This method innovatively integrates short-term driving tendency prediction of other vehicles with incomplete-information dynamic game theory. Via the Weight Dynamic Inference (WDI) and Strategy Dynamic Adjustment (SDA) modules, it realizes real-time inference of interactive vehicles’ decision preferences and intelligent optimization of ego-vehicle strategies. Specifically, the WDI module uses Bayesian Inference to dynamically adjust payoff function weights based on real-time perception of the opponent’s driving tendency, while the SDA module establishes a response mechanism between ego-vehicle strategy execution intensity and the opponent’s tendency. Experiments on a multi-vehicle collaborative simulation platform show that the method outperforms static models significantly in efficiency (32% shorter decision-making time), payoff (45% improvement), and human-like driving similarity (86.03%). Further tests on the NGSIM dataset confirm a human-like similarity exceeding 93%. It effectively enhances the adaptability and safety of autonomous decision-making in complex scenarios, offering a novel theoretical framework and technical pathway for optimizing intelligent transportation systems.

title: Study on Fiberglass Boat Strength and Durability under Different Types of Damage
authors: Triwilaswandio Wuruk Pribadi, Rizky Chandra Ariesta
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Small boats in Indonesia still largely use fiber materials due to considerations of ease of repair and speed of building process with fairly good durability. Environmental issues related to fiber certainly require attention, so the use of fiberglass ship structures requires monitoring and knowledge about their effective lifespan. The lack of studies assessing the effective lifespan of fiber poses a problem in determining how long fiber structures can endure. For this reason, an investigation was conducted on boat structures that have been in use for over 20 years to determine their effective lifespan by conducting destructive testing on tensile strength and bending strength for analysis of these vessels.

title: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems

title: Enhanced Partitioned RIS-Assisted SSK System Under Nakagami-$m$ Fading Channels and Hardware Impairments
authors: Tushar Shivam Pathak, Anshul Jaiswal
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: The partitioned RIS-assisted SSK (P-RIS-SSK) system reduces hardware complexity by updating RIS phases once per coherence time but suffers from weakly distinct channel signatures (CSs), limiting performance. Its existing error analysis, mainly for Rayleigh fading, is also not comprehensive. This work proposes an enhanced partitioned RIS-SSK (EP-RIS-SSK) system that improves CS distinctness via signal processing–based phase design at the RIS, without hardware changes, thereby boosting performance. Closed-form expressions for error performance and average mutual information are derived under generalized Nakagami-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$m$</tex-math></inline-formula> fading with path loss and hardware impairments. Performance metrics for P-RIS-SSK are obtained as a special case and validated via Monte Carlo simulations. A time-cost analysis under practical RIS switching constraints further shows the practical advantage and feasibility of EP-RIS-SSK over existing RIS-SSK schemes. Finally, EP-RIS-SSK is compared with P-RIS-SSK under various system parameters to highlight its superior performance.

title: Inferring Causality Between Entities and Events from CTI Reports
authors: Chang-Hong Jiang, Ren-Hung Hwang, Ying-Dar Lin, Po-Ching Lin, Yuan-Cheng Lai, Eric Hsiao-Kuang Wu
publish: ACM Transactions on Internet Technology
abstract: None

title: LICO: An SIMD-Aware High-Performance Learned Inverted Index Compression Framework
authors: Xianyu Zhu, Qiyu Liu, Guangyi Zhang, Zhibing Sha, Jianwei Liao, Sha Hu, Lei Chen
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data

publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
