title: Toward Privacy Preservation in Federated Learning: A Framework Integrating Client-Side Shuffling and Model Compression
authors: Jinguo Li, Ruyang Xiao, Le Yu, Kai Zhang, Jianting Ning
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables multiple clients to train models on local data and collaboratively optimize a global model without sharing raw data. However, client heterogeneity, such as differences in data distributions and system capabilities, poses challenges like reduced training efficiency and slower convergence. Furthermore, there is a risk of inference attacks during the transmission of model parameters. To address these issues, we propose the client-side shuffling and compressed-model FL (CSCP-Fed) framework, which is efficient and privacy-preserving, based on client-side shuffling and compressed-model techniques. The framework combines differential privacy (DP) with client-side shuffling to ensure data anonymity and enable secure weighted aggregation of model parameters. It also designs an asymmetric-encryption-based secure communication protocol to safeguard data transmission. In addition, it introduces a hybrid-weighted attention aggregation algorithm and a compressed-model-driven client selection strategy to mitigate the impact of heterogeneity, accelerate convergence, and maintain model generalization ability. Rigorous security analysis and experiments show that CSCP-Fed can effectively protect privacy without relying on any centralized entity. It reduces the expected global loss per round by 15%–38%, cuts communication overhead by 19.3%–44.3%, speeds up convergence by 10%–41%, and improves learning accuracy by 4%–13% compared to traditional methods.

title: Energy-Efficient Reversible 8-Bit Polar Encoder and Decoder on Artix-7 FPGA for Low-Power and Quantum Applications
authors: M. Mahesh Kumar, M. V. Subramanyam, M. Vinay, G.Mukesh, Kolla Pavan Kalyan, K. Maruthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Using Peres reversible gates, this study provides an energy-efficient reversible 8-bit Polar encoder and decoder that is built on an Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA. To ensure full logical reversibility throughout all Polar transform phases, the proposed design replaces the irreversible logic gates used in the current architecture with Peres reversible gate architectures. Utilizing the Xilinx Vivado simulation and synthesis tool, the design and functional verification are completed. Results from FPGA implementation show notable gains over the traditional method. Approximately 12.8% less static power, 22.9% less dynamic power, 22.7% less on-chip power, 42.8% less LUT usage, and 44.4% less flip-flops are used in the encoder. The decoder also sees a reduction in static power of 13.1%, dynamic power of 14.7%, on-chip power of 14.7%, LUT usage of 33.3%, and flip-flops of 37.5%. These outcomes demonstrate that the proposed Peres gate-based design offers a compact, low-power, and FPGA-efficient solution suitable for applications involving reversible computation and wireless communication.

title: A Knowledge-Driven Networking Framework for Detecting LoFT Attacks Using LSTM-Based Time Series Analysis
authors: Kourosh Daniel Seifi, Parvaneh Asghari, Hamid Haj Seyyed Javadi, MohammadHadi Alaeiyan
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Network programmability and control have been enhanced by the growing use of Software-Defined Networking (SDN), but it has also created new weaknesses for covert Low-Rate Flow Table (LoFT) attacks, which progressively deplete flow table resources while eluding conventional threshold-based detectors. In order to facilitate adaptive, time-aware, and semantically informed LoFT attack detection, this paper suggests a knowledge-driven detection framework that combines an LSTM-based time series analysis with a Knowledge-Driven Networking (KDN) plane. The system uses LSTM and complementary forecasting algorithms to model typical traffic behavior, gathers flow statistics from SDN switches, and uses semantic reasoning in the knowledge plane to improve detection and initiate mitigation. Experiments show that the suggested framework performs noticeably better than current baselines, surpassing ARIMA (85.4%), Transformer (92.8%), and GRU (91.2%) with a detection accuracy of 94.7%. Additionally, it lowers detection latency to 74 ms as opposed to 120 ms (ARIMA), 88 ms (Transformer), and 95 ms (GRU). All metrics show a similar 2–12% improvement in precision, recall, and F1-score. These findings demonstrate that temporal deep learning and knowledge-driven reasoning work together to provide a strong and flexible defense against LoFT attacks, allowing for high-fidelity and real-time detection in contemporary SDN environments.

title: Prediction-Driven and Evolutionary Optimization for Proactive Content Caching in Edge Computing Networks
authors: Jinchao Wu, Hongjie Guo, Jianxu Shen, Tianchou Yang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: To support the rapidly increasing demand for low-latency content delivery from massive end-users, edge computing networks have emerged as a pivotal architecture by deploying computing and storage resources in close proximity to users. However, devising an effective caching strategy in such dynamic environments remains a critical challenge, primarily due to the fluctuating nature of content popularity and the constrained storage capacity of edge servers. In this paper, we propose PEPO, a Prediction-Enhanced Proactive Optimization framework for edge caching. PEPO models the content prediction problem as a time-series forecasting task and employs a BiLSTM-based algorithm to accurately predict future content popularity by learning from historical request patterns. For the caching strategy, PEPO formulates it as a multi-objective optimization problem, aiming to minimize a comprehensive system cost including data retrieval latency and quality of service penalty. Then, we design an evolutionary optimization mechanism that integrates a dynamic weighting scheme based on indicator-based evolutionary algorithm with the whale optimization algorithm to efficiently navigate the solution space and derive near-optimal caching decisions. Finally, comprehensive simulations under diverse system conditions demonstrate that PEPO consistently achieves the lowest system cost, showing particular strength in resource-constrained and high-load scenarios.

title: Feedforward Neural Networks Model for SPAD Parameter Analysis with Experimental Validation
authors: Haoxiang Li, Zhixiang Cao, Zixuan Wang, Wei Liu, Yuxuan Yang, Xiangliang Jin
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: The performance of single photon avalanche diodes (SPADs) is highly dependent on their structure and dimensions. However, performance predicting through technology computer aided design (TCAD) simulation is often computationally expensive and time-consuming. This paper proposes a SPAD performance prediction method that integrates feedforward neural network (FFNN) with TCAD simulation. This method utilizes TCAD simulation results as the dataset for the FFNN model, enabling accurate prediction of three critical performance parameters: breakdown voltage (BV), dark count rate (DCR), and photon detection probability (PDP). To ensure model reliability, the TCAD parameters were first calibrated against experimental data obtained from SPAD fabricated in a standard 0.18 μm CMOS process. Results show excellent agreement between the FFNN model predictions and the TCAD simulations, with coefficients of determination (R²) of 0.996, 0.994, and 0.999. Furthermore, compared to traditional simulation methods, the FFNN model achieves simulation speed improvements of approximately three orders of magnitude. These results demonstrate the FFNN model's advantages of rapid and high precision performance in SPAD design, providing a powerful tool for achieving fast iterative design of high-performance SPAD.

title: A Hybrid Evaluation Architecture for Electronic Components Selection Integrating Large Language Models and Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Jiaqi Zhang, Xu Wang, Hong Cheng, Zhibo Yang, Huaxing Gao, Chao Zhou, Jinying Huang, Shengge Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Electronic components serve as the physical foundation of various devices and systems, and their reliability directly determines the stability of the entire system. Given the significant data island effect, lack of data governance, and absence of intelligent recommendation methods in the traditional component selection process, this paper proposes an evaluation framework for component selection that integrates large language models (LLMs) and retrieval-enhanced generation (RAG) technology. This framework integrates three core modules: knowledge extraction, intelligent verification, and selection recommendation. Through RAG technology, it effectively suppresses the "hallucination" phenomenon of LLMs, providing a reliable technical path for intelligent component selection. The system developed based on this evaluation framework has been verified through actual cases in the aerospace field. The accuracy rate of key indicator identification is as high as 95%, the efficiency of intelligent verification data processing has increased by 80%, and it successfully covers the selection requirements of more than 10 typical semiconductor components. The results show that this framework significantly improves the intelligence and accuracy of component selection, providing strong technical support for the reliability guarantee of electronic systems.

title: Enhancing Resilience of Power Distribution Systems Under Cyber-Physical Hybrid Attacks: A Coordinated Localization and Restoration Strategy with Feeder Automation
authors: Rong Zeng, Xin Zhang, Rui Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: The widespread deployment of distributed feeder automation (DFA) has increased the vulnerability of power distribution systems to cyber-physical hybrid attacks. To enhance the resilience of power distribution systems under hybrid attacks, this paper proposes a two-stage integrated cyber-physical system (TICPS) recovery strategy. This strategy integrates hybrid attack localization and isolation, and cyber-physical collaborative restoration, while effectively coordinating multiple resources. In the first stage, a hybrid attack localization module is developed, incorporating an attack descriptive model and a network equivalent transformation method to accurately locate cyber and physical attacks. The localization results are then used to guide DFA to isolate attacked agents and line faults resulting from the attacks. In the second stage, a grid reconfiguration and island formation module is first proposed to achieve efficient load restoration while considering the operational constraints of DFA under a compromised cyber layer. Subsequently, a repair crew dispatch module is designed to optimize crew routing based on value functions of cyber-physical faulty components, thereby facilitating complete cyber-physical system recovery. To leverage sequentially restored and controllable resources during the repair process, a repair event-triggered mechanism is introduced to coordinate grid reconfiguration and crew dispatch. The effectiveness and scalability of the proposed strategy are validated through simulations on the IEEE 33-bus and IEEE 123-bus systems, which demonstrate promising results.

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT-Enabled Real-Time Borewell Safety and Surveillance System with Integrated Sensor Fusion, Geolocation Tracking, and Multi-Platform Alert Mechanisms for Rural Infrastructure Protection
authors: H Vinothkumar, A Dhivya, K Varsha, M Sivagurunathan, A K Prasanna, T Thishva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The incidents relating to borewells, especially in rural India, are very dangerous to the safety of children, animals, and the local population due to open or unattended wells. The traditional approaches of manual checking are ineffective and not repetitive and are not able to give real-time feedback. The present project presents an IoT-based monitoring system (smart borewell) developed on the idea of the identification of open borewells and suspicious activity on the location with the help of infrared or ultrasonic sensors. The data is processed by a microcontroller and alarmed through GSM, Wi-Fi or LoRa. There is an inbuilt GPS that gives out accurate notification in relation to location that will be relayed to the closest local authorities or guardians, who will then act promptly. On top of this, the aforementioned system has a dashboard/ mobile interface in which a number of sites could be monitored simultaneously. Safety is given the highest importance in this case, and there will be the avoidance of all accidents and demonstration of how IoT can practically damage the rural infrastructures. The new system is not the last one as it has real-time continuous monitoring, automatic alarm system, and extendable usage which can be easily used in the rural location requiring minimum manpower and it has saved a lot of life.

title: Deep Learning-Enabled Medical Image Interpretation for Early and Explainable Disease Identification
authors: Abdul Aziz
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rapid growth of medical imaging data and shortage of expert clinicians have created a critical need for automated, accurate, and interpretable diagnostic systems. This study proposes a unified deep learning framework for early disease identification from multi-modal medical images, including X-rays, MRI, CT scans, and histopathological slides. The approach integrates a hybrid CNN–Transformer architecture to effectively capture both local spatial features and global contextual dependencies, enhancing diagnostic performance. To address the "black-box" limitation of deep learning models, explainable artificial intelligence techniques such as Grad-CAM and SHAP are incorporated to provide visual and interpretable insights into model predictions. Transfer learning and data augmentation strategies are employed to overcome limited annotated medical datasets and improve generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves high accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity across multiple disease detection tasks, outperforming existing baseline models. Furthermore, the generated explainability maps enhance clinical trust by highlighting diagnostically relevant regions. The findings emphasize the potential of explainable deep learning systems as scalable, reliable, and ethically aligned solutions for next-generation computer-aided diagnosis.

title: Lifelong Learner: Discovering Versatile Neural Solvers for Vehicle Routing Problems
authors: Shaodi Feng, Zhuoyi Lin, Jianan Zhou, Cong Zhang, Jingwen Li, Kuan-Wen Chen, Senthilnath Jayavelu, Yew-Soon Ong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Deep learning has been increasingly adopted in intelligent transportation systems (ITS) to address vehicle routing problems (VRPs) that arise in real-world operations such as fleet management, urban logistics, and dynamic dispatching. However, most neural solvers are trained to tackle VRP instances in a relatively monotonous context, e.g., simplifying VRPs by using Euclidean distance between nodes and adhering to a single problem size, which harms their off-the-shelf application in different scenarios. To enhance their versatility, this paper presents a novel lifelong learning framework that incrementally trains a neural solver to manage VRPs in distinct contexts. Specifically, we propose a lifelong learner (LL), exploiting a Transformer network as the backbone, to solve a series of VRPs. The inter-context self-attention mechanism is proposed within LL to transfer the knowledge obtained from solving preceding VRPs into the succeeding ones. On top of that, we develop a dynamic context scheduler (DCS), employing the cross-context experience replay to further facilitate LL looking back on the attained policies of solving preceding VRPs. Extensive results on synthetic and benchmark instances (problem sizes up to 18k) show that our LL is capable of discovering effective policies for tackling generic VRPs in varying contexts. The source code is available: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/fsdfsd123/Lifelong-Learner-Discovering-More-Versatile-Neural-Solvers-for-Routing-Problems</uri>.

title: TinyArtifactNet: A Resource-Efficient Deep Learning Model for On-Device Motion Artifact Detection in PPG
authors: Meftahul Jannat Nuha, Abid Hasan, Sumaiya Kabir, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals are widely used in wearable devices for continuous monitoring of cardiovascular vital signs. However, PPG signals are highly susceptible to motion artifacts, which severely degrade signal quality and lead to inaccurate physiological measurements. Consequently, any vital signs derived from contaminated PPG lead to wrong clinical or health-related decisions. To address this challenge, this study proposes TinyArtifactNet, a lightweight hybrid multi-scale temporal deep learning model for efficient and accurate motion artifact detection in PPG signals. The proposed architecture integrates ghost modules, multi-scale dilated convolutions, and squeeze-and-excitation mechanisms to achieve a favorable trade-off between performance and computational efficiency. The effectiveness of TinyArtifactNet was evaluated using three publicly available datasets and one self-curated private dataset. This work presents three key contributions: the design of a lightweight architecture, its deployment on an embedded platform, and real-time validation, all combining within a unified framework. On the PPG-DaLiA dataset, the proposed model achieved state-of-the-art performance among embedded-deployable lightweight models with an accuracy of 88.17% and a Dice score of 88.77%, while requiring only 21.3K parameters and 0.16 MB of memory. Furthermore, cross-dataset evaluation on two completely unseen datasets, TROIKA and WESAD, yielded accuracies of 81.84% and 90.34%, respectively, and Dice scores of 82.93% and 90.05%, respectively, demonstrating strong generalization. In addition, real-time evaluation using raw PPG signals acquired from a low-cost MAX30102 sensor and deployed on an STM32 microcontroller achieved an accuracy of 87.2%. These results confirm the suitability of TinyArtifactNet for real-time motion artifact detection on low-cost, resource-constrained platforms, making it a promising solution for wearable healthcare systems.

title: A Sliding Mode Control For a Three-Phase SAPF Using A Non-Linear Transformation
authors: Jose Chico, Sergi Zapater, Ramon Guzman, Luis Garcia de Vicuña, Ángel Borrell, Miguel Castilla
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: This paper introduces a novel switching strategy for a three-phase two-level shunt active power filter (SAPF) based on sliding mode control (SMC). Unlike traditional SMC implemented in the natural reference frame, which suffers from cross-coupling among phase-leg controllers due to the neutral point voltage and results in sliding losses, the proposed method achieves full axis-decoupling. To overcome this limitation, a nonlinear transformation maps the voltages and currents into a new two-dimensional reference frame. Within this transformed frame, a nonlinear model is developed, and two sliding-mode surfaces are implemented using hysteresis band comparators. The resulting switching strategy removes sliding losses, allows third-harmonic injection in the control signals, and extends the effective control range by approximately 15% without saturating the controller. Experimental results validate the theoretical framework and show that the proposed method reduces the grid-current THD from 2.839% with conventional SMC to 1.138%. The method also preserves stable DC-link regulation and sinusoidal grid currents under load-step, voltage-sag, and distorted-grid-voltage conditions.

title: A Plug-and-Play Robust $H_{\infty }$ Voltage Tracking Control Strategy for Islanded AC Microgrids Based on Structured Matrix Decomposition
authors: Bolin Xu, Chen Peng, Wenbo Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article proposes a novel Plug-and-Play robust <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> control strategy for islanded AC microgrids composed of multiple interconnected distributed generation units (DGUs). The proposed control strategy aims to achieve both real-time voltage regulation at the point of common coupling to track given reference values and seamless Plug-and-Play operation. The Plug-and-Play capability ensures that controller gain updates are avoided when DGUs are added or removed from the system. First, different from existing methods that rely on prefilters or disturbance compensators for disturbance rejection, this article ingeniously employs parameter transformation to facilitate the application of the Elimination Lemma, thereby simplifying the controller synthesis process and ensuring the solvability of robust <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math></inline-formula> controllers. Second, the interconnection coupling term is well approximated via structured matrix decomposition, the global stability of the studied system can be guaranteed based on local stability conditions. This effectively circumvents the restrictive matrix-structure assumptions required by some existing methods. Finally, two examples are presented to verify that the proposed method can effectively achieve both real-time voltage regulation and Plug-and-Play operation with satisfactory performance, validating its feasibility and superiority.

title: Identification with Orthogonal Basis Functions: Convergence Speed, Asymptotic Bias, and Rate-Optimal Pole Selection
authors: Jiayun Li, Yiwen Lu, Yilin Mo, Jie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper is concerned with performance analysis and pole selection problem in identifying linear time-invariant (LTI) systems using orthogonal basis functions (OBFs), a system identification approach that consists of solving least-squares problems and selecting poles within the OBFs. Specifically, we analyze the convergence properties and asymptotic bias of the OBF algorithm, and propose a pole selection algorithm that robustly minimizes the worst-case identification bias, with the bias measured under the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {H}_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> error criterion. Our results include an analytical expression for the convergence rate and an explicit bound on the asymptotic identification bias, which depends on both the true system poles and the preselected model poles. Furthermore, we demonstrate that the pole selection algorithm is asymptotically optimal, achieving the fundamental lower bound on the identification bias. The algorithm explicitly determines the model poles as the so-called Tsuji points, and the asymptotic identification bias decreases exponentially with the number of basis functions, with the rate of decrease governed by the hyperbolic Chebyshev constant. Numerical experiments validate the derived bounds and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed pole selection algorithm.

title: Active Semi-supervised Evidential Clustering with Uncertainty-guided Query Strategy
authors: Zhuobin Hao, Fengqing Tang, Yanshuang Feng, Lipeng Pan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Semi-supervised evidential clustering, as a soft clustering approach with label constraints, improves clustering performance while reducing reliance on labeled data. However, labeled samples are often costly to obtain, making it essential to identify informative constraints. To address this issue, this paper proposes an Active Semi-supervised Evidential C-Means (ASECM) method under the Dempster–Shafer framework. The method first employs ECM to obtain basic probability assignments (BPAs) and constructs pairwise constraints based on the Jousselme distance. Quantile-based thresholds are used to distinguish neighboring and distant sample pairs, and potential constraints in the gray-band region are validated and selectively refined through an active query mechanism. An uncertainty-driven query strategy based on plausibility entropy is further introduced to select informative samples for annotation. Experimental results on multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior clustering performance while requiring fewer labeled samples.

title: A Feature-Enhanced Machine Learning Approach for Reliable Stroke Risk Prediction using CLOA-XGB
authors: V. Usha, Vyshnav R, Devdath Suresh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Stroke has been a leading cause of mortality and disability in the entire world, which has the importance of precise early risk prediction to assist in early intervention. The common traditional assessment measures which are demographic, lifestyle and clinical are ineffective in recognizing nonlinear relations between the predictors. To address this weakness, this paper suggests a machine learning-based stroke risk prediction CLOA-XGB model, which incorporates feature selection through the Camellama Optimization Algorithm with the XGBoost classifier. The CLOA method is suitable in eliminating redundant features and increasing the identification of clinically relevant risk factors, which leads to stronger and more interpretable models. A comparative analysis of the proposed approach with other supervised learning models shows that it has a high performance where the accuracy is 99.66 and F1-score is 0.997. These findings show that CLOA-XGB model can be an effective decision-support tool that can be used to predict early stroke in a clinical environment.

title: DiffuCL: Diffusion-based Reconstruction with Contrastive Learning for Incomplete Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Mingyang Ye
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: While Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) has advanced significantly, handling missing modalities remains a critical challenge. Existing reconstruction-based methods often rely on simple mappings that directly transform features from available modalities to the missing ones, which fail to bridge the modality gap, leading to low-quality feature recovery. To overcome this, we introduce DiffuCL, a novel architecture that integrates diffusion-based reconstruction with contrastive learning. Unlike traditional mapping methods, our approach leverages modality-specific diffusion models to recover missing modalities through forward diffusion and reverse denoising. Furthermore, we incorporate a cross-modal contrastive learning mechanism to enforce inter-modal consistency and facilitate robust representation learning. Extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that DiffuCL achieves state-of-the-art performance, with significant improvements under missing modality conditions.

title: A Coplanar Waveguide Approach Using Substrate-Integrated Bridges for Impedance Control, Circuit Miniaturization, Ground Balancing, and Crossover Applications
authors: A. Cherif, M. Himdi, X. Castel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a novel coplanar waveguide (CPW) approach using substrate-integrated bridges for impedance control, circuit miniaturization, ground balancing, and crossover applications. An improved analytical model is introduced, including new expressions for the effective width, blended slot participation, and bridge-induced capacitance. Family-dependent calibration parameters (for 70 Ω and 50 Ω baselines) are also proposed, enabling a much closer agreement between analytical predictions and full-wave simulations. The model achieves an overall RMS error below 5 Ω, validating its accuracy and practical relevance. By systematically adjusting bridge parameters such as spacing (d) and width (Wp), we achieve significant impedance reductions: from 70 Ω to 50 Ω and 35 Ω, and from 50 Ω down to 25 Ω and 16 Ω. Simulation and measurement validate these effects. For example, reaching 35 Ω on a quartz substrate (500 μm thick) would require impractically wide conductors (>1300 μm), highlighting the advantage of this technique for compact designs. We apply this concept to the design of two compact 3 dB/90° couplers: one using stepped-impedance CPWs, and another employing uniform 70 Ω lines with impedance tuning performed exclusively by bridges. Experimental results show strong agreement with simulations, confirming the relevance of this approach for advanced RF and microwave applications.

title: Learning Artistic Image Aesthetics from Multi-level Text Prompts Generation
authors: Hancheng Zhu, Xinya Xu, Rui Yao, Yixuan Li, Kunyang Sun, Leida Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Artistic Image Aesthetics Assessment (AIAA) aims to emulate human artistic perception to evaluate the aesthetics of artistic images. Due to the highly specialized nature of human artistic perception, obtaining large-scale aesthetic annotations for model analysis presents significant challenges. Furthermore, the subjectivity of artistic aesthetics makes it difficult for existing AIAA methods to quantify aesthetic scores solely based on visual features. To address the two challenges, we introduce an AIAA model based on multi-level text prompts generation. Firstly, we leverage a text prompt-based self-supervised learning approach to augment artistic image data and adopt a multi-task learning paradigm to pre-train our multi-modal AIAA model. To further capture the abstract aesthetic characteristics of artistic images, we then adopt a domain-specific multi-modal large language model (MLLM) to simulate human artistic perception and generate aesthetic textual descriptions for images, and employ a multi-modal fusion module to integrate image features with the text features of artistic aesthetics for better feature representation. Finally, the proposed multi-modal AIAA model is trained by text-prompt learning based on the aesthetic quality levels of artistic images. By generating multi-level text prompts, our method can introduce human-perceived artistic aesthetic knowledge and obtain a more effective AIAA model. Experimental results on several AIAA datasets demonstrate that our method is superior to the state-of-the-art AIAA methods. The code of our model is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/cicixuxy/MTPG</uri>.

title: Curation from Thousands: Composing the Best-Aligned Diffusion Models with Improved Model Retrieval and Fusion
authors: Pu Cao, Rupeng Yan, Xuekun Zhao, Feng Zhou, Wenyi Zhao, Qing Song, Lu Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: In recent years, the community has witnessed a surge of open-sourced diffusion models tailored for various specialized tasks. As the number of available models continues to grow rapidly, selecting and merging relevant models to meet user needs has become a critical challenge in real-world applications. Although prior studies have explored this task, their model retrieval and fusion strategies typically rely solely on user-provided textual prompts and off-the-shelf large language models, leading to suboptimal performance. In this work, we extend the user-provided information by incorporating a reference image and propose a multimodal model curation framework, ChamGen, allowing model curation to better align with user intent. Then, we conduct an in-depth investigation into model retrieval and propose a lightweight modality fusion method based on contrastive learning, which encodes both textual and visual information into a shared embedding space, significantly boosting retrieval performance. Furthermore, benefiting from the introduction of the reference image, we estimate the optimal combination weights by minimizing the denoising loss of the fused model with respect to the reference image. To avoid the overhead of iterative optimization, we reformulate the parameter weight learning problem as a guidance weight assignment task, enabling us to estimate fusion weights in a single forward pass. Experiments show that our retrieval framework achieves 76.72% Recall@1 and 95.43% Recall@100 on a 10,000-scale diffusion model dataset. And the generation quality is significantly improved when combined with our fusion strategy.

title: Active DC-link Split-Battery Quasi-Single-Stage Inverter: Architecture, Modulation, and Flexible Management of Battery Modules
authors: Reyhaneh Eskandari, Alan J. Watson, Prasanth Venugopal, Patrick Wheeler, Thiago Batista Soeiro
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper provides a systematic investigation of active DC-link split-battery inverter (ADC-SBI), a three-phase quasi-single-stage topology that synthesizes a multilevel, pulsating DC-link by integrating multiple split-battery modules into a cascaded half-bridge string connected to the three DC terminals of a three-phase neutral-point clamped (NPC) inverter working as a voltage selector stage. Herein, a synergetic modulation scheme is used to coordinate the battery converter strings and voltage selector so that the high-voltage-rated switches operate only at low frequency with zero voltage switching transitions, while the low voltage-rated devices commutate at high frequency. Owing to its multilevel voltage-source behavior, it improves output power quality and EMI, easing filter requirements. In addition, this study provides modulation strategies for the cascaded strings that enable flexible battery module management, including symmetric and asymmetric module voltages operation, an analytical semiconductor current-stress model under level-shifted pulse-width modulation (LSPWM), and a comparative assessment against related split-battery architectures. The proposed architecture and modulation approaches are validated in simulation and experiments.

title: SVD-Based Image Compression: A Comprehensive Survey of Methods, Metrics, Optimization Strategies, and Emerging Trends
authors: Dhanush Naidu, Phanindra Varma, Keerthan Reddy, Reena Panwar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid growth of digital images in communication, storage, and edge-device applications has sustained interest in compression methods that are both interpretable and quality-aware. Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) is a mathematically grounded approach to lossy image compression because it exploits the low-rank structure of image matrices and provides the optimal rank-k approximation in the Frobenius-norm sense. Although SVD is widely used in image-processing research, the compression literature is scattered across pure rank truncation, hybrid transform methods, optimization-guided rank selection, and learning-assisted variants. This survey reviews more than 50 SVD-centered works from 2007 to 2024. Additional contextual references published after 2024 were added during revision but were not counted in the primary 2007–2024 corpus. The retained works are organized into a revised five-category taxonomy: (C1) pure SVD rank-k approximation, (C2) hybrid SVD-DCT methods, (C3) hybrid SVD-DWT methods, (C4) optimization-assisted SVD, and (C5) learning-assisted SVD. Application-area papers outside the core compression mechanism are treated only as related evaluation contexts unless SVD is the central compression mechanism. The paper expands the compression pipeline from input image to stored singular triplets and reconstructed output, adds visual rank-k reconstruction examples, and compares compression ratio, PSNR, SSIM, MSE, and computational complexity across method families. The analysis also positions SVD relative to modern codecs such as JPEG XL, HEVC, AV1/AVIF, and neural compression. The synthesis shows that hybrid SVD methods typically improve PSNR over pure SVD at comparable compression ratios, while optimization-guided and patch-adaptive approaches provide the strongest quality-control mechanisms. Finally, the survey identifies open challenges in real-time high-resolution processing, adaptive color-channel rank allocation, perceptual metrics beyond PSNR, standardized benchmarking, and practical integration of SVD modules with modern compression standards.

title: The Effect of Gate Scaling on Drain Current in Ultra-Scaled Nanosheet and Nanowire FETs: A 3D Monte Carlo Simulation Study
authors: Murad G. K. Alabdullah, N. Seoane, A. J. Garcia-Loureiro, K. Kalna
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: The gate scaling to an ultimate length of 8 nm in Si nanosheet (NS) and nanowire (NW) field-effect transistors (FETs) results in a notable reduction in the drain drive current (IDD), despite conventional scaling theory predicting an increase. Using advanced 3D finite element ensemble Monte Carlo (MC) simulations with Schrödinger equation quantum corrections, we investigate the impact of gate scaling from 22 nm to 8 nm in gate-all-around (GAA) NS and NW FETs. Our results indicate that while IDD initially increases for a gate length scaled from 22 nm to 16 nm as expected, further scaling to sub-16 nm lengths leads to a decline of up to 18% in NS FETs and 20% in NW FETs at 8 nm. This IDD reduction is mainly due to enhanced long-range Coulomb interactions between the source and the drain, inducing fringing electric fields at the source gate-edge in addition to already present fringing electric fields at the drain gate-edge, leading to increased channel back-scattering.

title: Optimization-Based Trajectory Planning With Behavior Cells for Autonomous Driving
authors: Shihao Li, Wenshuo Wang, Zhide Zhang, Xin Xia, Boyang Wang, Chao Lu, Haiou Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Safe and executable trajectory planning in urban environments requires jointly considering traffic-related elements, ego behavior, and vehicle kinematics, posing challenges to the real-time performance and convergence of optimization-based methods. This paper proposes a behavior-guided optimization framework that structures the solution space at the spatiotemporal drivable domain level to facilitate fast and stable convergence. The planning space is partitioned into modular spatiotemporal domains, termed Behavior Cells (BCs), which encode ego motion feasibility and traffic-induced decisions. Feasible high-level behaviors are systematically enumerated through structured BC combinations and evaluated via a finite-horizon Markov decision process. The selected BC combination defines a continuous, behavior-consistent solution space, within which a dynamic two-stage optimization progressively restores the full planning formulation, enabling efficient and robust trajectory generation. Extensive simulations across diverse traffic scenarios demonstrate consistent reliability and real-time performance under varying traffic densities. On-road experiments further validate effectiveness in real-world urban environments. More detailed results are available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://lshasd123.github.io/Behavior-Cells/</uri>

title: Stochastic Twin Regression Machine for k–class Classification
authors: Tara Mohammadi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: This paper is an improved version of the Twin–KSVC model. In the Twin–KSVC model, it is based on certain inputs, while in the real world, we deal with uncertain data due to the inherent error. In this paper, the slack variables are considered random by inducing a noise caused by the data error. Also, in this case, the constraints are probabilistic. Hence, using the distribution function definition, we remove the constraints from the probabilistic state and transform the objective function into a problem without the probabilistic constraints. Finally, the results and experiments among some benchmark data sets demonstrated how the model with uncertain inputs performs better than the one with deterministic inputs.

title: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM based Real-Time Sign Language Recognition using Segmentation and Feature Extraction
authors: Akalya K, Dr.Gopikha.S, Keerthika R G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This research work proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM-based real-time sign language recognition system, which aims to bridge the communication gaps between hearing-impaired people and non-signers. This study focuses on addressing various issues, such as background variation, modeling of dynamic gestures, and segmentation accuracy. In this paper, convex hull-based geometric feature extraction, CNN-based spatial feature extraction, and LSTM-based temporal modeling have been used. Data is collected under controlled indoor environments, which is essential to ensure the reliability of the model. From the experiment, it is found that the proposed system achieves high accuracy, i.e., 92.4%, compared to other traditional methods, such as KNN and CNN. The experiment shows that the system is stable even while using different classes of gestures and signers. This paper is helpful to develop real-time sign language recognition systems, which is efficient and scalable.

title: Beauty-Naturalness Dissociation in AI-Edited Faces: An Eye-Tracking Study of Visual-Cognitive Mechanisms
authors: Yi Cao, Yuting Shen, Jialin Zhou, Ying Li, Yiru Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: With the growing use of AI-based facial editing and synthesis, understanding how people perceive the naturalness and realism of edited faces has become increasingly important. This study investigates that process in controlled AI-edited faces from a cognitive perspective. Using MediaPipe Face Mesh, we constructed a standardized facial stimulus pipeline and systematically manipulated four facial factors—eye size, mouth size, skin whitening, and face slimming—under a 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 within-subject design. Eye tracking and behavioral ratings were jointly collected to examine the dissociation between Beauty and Naturalness. The results revealed a clear separation between the two judgment dimensions: Beauty was more strongly associated with attention to the eyes and skin, whereas Naturalness was more strongly associated with attention to the facial contour, mouth, and nose. Based on these findings, we propose a dual-mechanism cognitive framework in which Beauty is linked to an attraction-oriented process and Naturalness is linked to a structure-oriented authenticity-checking process. These findings provide a visual-cognitive account of authenticity-related judgments in AI-edited faces.

title: An adaptive differential evolution algorithm with a multi-probability selection mechanism guiding individual selection
authors: Xiaoyu Song, Zhiyang Yuan, Ming Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address issues such as low search efficiency in traditional Differential Evolution (DE) algorithms when solving high-dimensional complex optimization problems, and inspired by the fundamental framework of recent advanced adaptive DE variants, this paper proposes an adaptive Differential Evolution algorithm with multiple probability selection mechanisms guiding individual selection, named RPSDE. Firstly, a novel DE/rand-to-pbest/p strategy is introduced, and the value ranges of its parameters are improved. Secondly, distinct probability-guided individual selection mechanisms are adopted for the pbest individuals and the differential individuals within the strategy, respectively. Finally, effective mechanisms from DE algorithms, including the parameter adaptation mechanism, population size reduction mechanism, and external archive mechanism, are retained. To validate the algorithm's performance, it is compared with seven state-of-the-art DE variants. Experimental results on the 30 benchmark functions from the CEC2017 test suite demonstrate that the proposed algorithm performs equally well or outperforms the seven advanced algorithms in terms of solution accuracy, stability, and convergence speed.

title: Security Issues and Evaluation of Large-Scale Inference Models: An In-Depth Analysis Based on Long Chain-of-Thought
authors: Enze Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The integration of large language models into critical applications has introduced significant security vulnerabilities, particularly prompt-based attacks that exploit the coupling of user inputs with system instructions. This paper systematically evaluates prompt attack methodologies and safety risks in large reasoning models (LRMs) with long chain-of-thought (CoT) capabilities. Using the SafeChain framework, we design three reasoning control strategies—ZeroThink, LessThink, and MoreThink—to investigate the relationship between reasoning depth and model safety on the StrongReject-Small dataset across multiple models. Our results demonstrate that extended reasoning does not inherently threaten or enhance safety; rather, its impact is determined by underlying alignment strength. Weakly aligned models exhibit amplified jailbreak success rates under deeper reasoning, while strongly aligned models maintain stable or improved safety through self-scrutiny. Lightweight models face systemic risks including contextual drift. We propose a defense framework incorporating real-time thought monitoring and process-supervised alignment to guide the development of robust safety mechanisms for next-generation reasoning systems.

title: Real-Time 3D Printing Defect Detection and Temperature Optimization using Digital Image Processing
authors: Garre Sai Pranay Satvik, Ansh Hosabettu, Soumyapriyo Kundu, Rajeswari D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Additive production, also called 3-D printing, makes it viable to create complex designs with less material waste but errors at some stage in printing, such as extruder mistakes and layer shifts, remain as a challenge for 3-D printing generation. Monitoring print jobs is labor intensive for lengthy print instances, making it an increasing need to address the 3-D printing errors. This research work proposes a digital image processing and deep learning-based framework for defect detection all through fused deposition 3-D printing modeling. In this paper, a convolutional neural network-primarily based method, ResNet50, is proposed for defect detection all through 3-D printing. To manage domain shifts due to distinct filament shades and lighting fixtures situations, all input images are transformed to grayscale and processed. A comparative evaluation of the proposed approach with YOLOv8 is carried out to evaluate the change-off between accuracy and complexity in defect detection. Experimental effects display that the proposed approach primarily based on ResNet50 provides promising accuracy with strong convergence. The findings highlight the effectiveness of transfer getting to know, preprocessing and statistics augmentation to reliably discover defects in additive production, at the same time as revealing possibilities for future improvements along with area localization and distribution.

title: A Predictive Dashboard for Ocean Plastic Accumulation using Remote Sensing and Marine Meteorology
authors: Kalingarani G, Savitha S K S, Sri Karthika Ganapathi.R
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Marine ecosystems, biodiversity and coastal economies are all impacted by plastic pollution, which has grown to be a serious environmental problem. It is still challenging to monitor and forecast floating marine plastic on a large scale using conventional manual surveys and rule-based image analysis techniques. In order to identify and predict ocean plastic buildup, this research suggests an integrated intelligent monitoring framework that incorporates deep learning methods, marine meteorological parameters, and multispectral satellite imagery. To separate floating plastic debris from ocean features like waves, foam, and marine vegetation, a convolutional neural network based on U-Net performs pixel-level segmentation. A predictive drift modelling module uses wave characteristics, wind speed, and ocean current to estimate short-term plastic movement in order to facilitate proactive environmental monitoring. The real-time detection maps, hotspot visualisations, structural grid analysis, and temporal trend analytics are all provided by the system's interactive Streamlit-based dashboard. In comparison to traditional threshold-based segmentation and classical CNN approaches, experimental results show strong segmentation performance with high accuracy, precision, recall, IoU and dice scores. By facilitating automated monitoring, predictive risk assessment, and effective planning of plastic cleanup and environmental management strategies, the suggested framework offers marine conservation authorities a scalable decision-support system.

title: Adaptive Machine Learning Framework for Real-Time Medication Adherence Prediction in Chronic Disease Management
authors: K.Nagaraju, K Srinivasulu Achari, Potturu Manisha, Kuppa Rupa Sri, Natuva Uttejetha, D K Shareef
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Medication adherence is a critical factor in effective chronic disease management, yet a significant proportion of patients fail to follow prescribed treatment regimens, leading to poor health outcomes and increased healthcare costs. Existing adherence monitoring systems are largely based on static models and retrospective analysis, limiting their ability to provide timely and personalized interventions. To address these challenges, this study proposes an adaptive machine learning framework for real-time medication adherence prediction. The approach integrates temporal feature modeling, a sliding window mechanism, and an adaptive learning strategy to continuously update model parameters based on evolving patient behavior. A proactive risk prediction module is incorporated to estimate the likelihood of future non-adherence events, enabling early intervention. The framework is evaluated using a publicly available dataset transformed into time-series format to simulate real-world conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms baseline methods, achieving an accuracy of 0.91, F1-score of 0.88, and ROC-AUC of 0.93, along with improved early prediction capability. These findings highlight the effectiveness of adaptive and real-time learning approaches in enhancing personalized healthcare systems and improving medication adherence outcomes.

title: Fast and Robust Sparse Bayesian Self-Calibration for Decoupling Gain-Phase and Mutual Coupling in Super-Resolution DOA Estimation
authors: Zhexian Liu, Shuai Shao, Hongwei Liu, Tao Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In modern compact linear arrays, strong electromagnetic mutual coupling driven by space-wave interactions and surface-wave leakage, along with inherent radio-frequency gain-phase mismatches, severely distorts active element patterns and degrades super-resolution direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation. Traditional hardware-centric decoupling techniques inevitably increase design complexity, introduce insertion losses, and are inapplicable to already-deployed systems. To overcome these barriers, this paper proposes a hardware-free decoupling approach: Knowledge-Aided Sparse Bayesian Multi-Error Calibration (KASB-MEC). By computationally compensating for electromagnetic distortions at the digital baseband, KASB-MEC effectively neutralizes adjacent scattering and channel inconsistencies, which is physically equivalent to terminating elements with virtual impedances. To resolve the phase-DOA ambiguity and the highly non-convex optimization landscape, the framework introduces a robust soft-constraint mechanism using von Mises and Rician priors. This strategy structurally decouples spatial angles from array errors, completely bypassing fragile hard-constraints. Furthermore, an adaptive dictionary learning strategy breaks the computational bottleneck of high-dimensional Bayesian inference. Validated by extensive numerical simulations and practical anechoic chamber measurements, KASB-MEC achieves fast, accurate, and robust multi-error calibration, offering a plug-and-play, software-defined calibration upgrade for real-time super-resolution sensing arrays.

title: Intelligent Penetration Testing through Integrated Knowledge Graph and Historical Decision Enhancement
authors: Qianyu Li, Li Wen, Anupam Chattopadhyay, Cheng Tu, Miao Hu, Fan Shi, Min Zhang, Ruipeng Wang, Zulie Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Penetration Testing (PT), a key network security assessment technique that simulates real cyber attacks to identify vulnerabilities, is traditionally manual and expert-dependent, leading to low efficiency and high costs. Automating and intelligentizing PT has thus become a critical research focus, yet current technologies face two core challenges: lack of standardized, reusable simulated network scenarios (hindering unified experiments and result comparison) and intelligent models' failure to integrate historical decision experience or utilize attack chain temporal correlations (restricting adaptability). To address these, this study proposes an intelligent PT method integrating knowledge graph-driven automated scenario construction and historical decision enhancement. Two innovations are introduced: a network knowledge graph-based mechanism to generate standardized, real-characteristic testing environments; and a historical decision enhancement scheme with a collaborative state temporal processing and action filtering architecture. Experimental results show the method reduces average iterations by 69%, eliminates redundant executions, and enhances decision rationality, offering a new path for automated PT advancement.

title: FPSVS:Disentangled Prompt-based Style Control for Singing Voice Synthesis with Reference Timbre via Flow Matching
authors: Sijia Li, Wenhuan Lu, Shiguang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Prompt-based singing voice synthesis (SVS) has become a research hotspot in multimedia research, enabling users to generate diverse singing styles with textual prompts and musical scores. Previous methods, exemplified by PromptSinger, utilize text and averaged melodies to control singer identities, which can synthesize vocals such as soprano or bass. However, this existing acoustic token-based SVS approach suffers from the constraints of audio codecs, leading to an upper bound on synthesis quality. Meanwhile, the timbre for generated audio is uncontrollable, failing to replicate specific speaker characteristics for personalized synthesis. Moreover, the sufficient analysis and fusion of input text prompts also need to be considered. To address these challenges, we present FPSVS (Flow Matching Prompt-based Singing Voice Synthesis), a novel generative framework that synthesizes high-fidelity mel-spectrograms from textual prompts and averaged melodies. FPSVS features a disentangled prompt style analyzer module that decomposes text prompts into independent audio attributes like pitch and volume, allowing for subsequent fine-grained control over singing characteristics. Subsequently, a series of variation adapters leverage these disentangled style representations as inputs to generate diverse acoustic variations, thereby enhancing vocal expressiveness. Furthermore, we also propose a prompt fusion acoustic decoder that deeply integrates stylistic information throughout the synthesis process, ensuring that the generated audio reflects the intended style of the prompt. Additionally, by incorporating a reference speaker timbre embedding mechanism, FPSVS enables replication of specific speaker timbre. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that FPSVS can synthesize natural singing voices that align with the provided prompts and outperforms various methods.

title: An Improved Spatial Gated Attention-aided Efficient ResNext for Multiple Disease Detection using Medical Image in IoMT Environment
authors: Karunakar Chappidi, L.N.B. Srinivas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Motivation: Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) transforms the healthcare systems with digital technologies. Therefore, various types of IoMT-based existing disease detection approaches were designed. Yet, it fails for handling high-dimensional data, and global feature representations in the training process and also, it is complex for managing the limited availability of large-scale and well-annotated datasets persist within a limited duration. Approach: In this research work, an enhanced multiple disease detection approach is designed. Here, the relevant medical images are collected from online databases using IoMT devices and it is fed to Spatial Gated Attention-based Efficient ResNext (SGA-EResxt) for classifying the multiple diseases. The features from different spatial resolutions and attention maps are fused to highlight the features that are more predictive for each disease. Finally, the proposed SGA-EResxt model classifies the presence of several diseases from the input image, which leads to a more accurate and reliable classification outcome. Key outcomes: In the validation process, the designed approach has attained 97.36% accuracy at 75<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> hidden neuron to prove its superior performance. Relevance of the study: Early detection of multiple disease helps to plan proper treatments with minimal symptoms, improving patients’ healthcare and minimizing the mortality rates.

title: SFGANet: Smoke and Flame Global Aggregation Network
authors: Yuhao Wang, Bingbing Qiao, Guojun Xie
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Detecting smoke and flame targets in complex scenes is challenging because of scale variation, irregular boundaries, background interference, and weak early-stage visual cues. To address these issues, this paper proposes SFGANet, an improved detector based on YOLOv11n. The proposed model enhances the backbone, neck, regression loss, and detection head through SCS-RepGELAN, DGBFPN, AGS-IoU, and ASFFHead, respectively. Experiments on a self-built dataset of 6,312 images show that SFGANet achieves 81.2% mAP@0.5 and 60.0% mAP@0.5:0.95, outperforming the baseline YOLOv11n by 3.4 and 5.7 percentage points, respectively. Compared with several mainstream YOLO-based detectors, the proposed method delivers better overall detection performance in complex smoke-andflame scenes.
