title: An AIoT-Enabled Fault-Tolerant Crop Recommendation System for Precision Agriculture
authors: Chidananda T G, Nirmala M B, Swathi Paapanna Gari, Chandan B Ram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Modern farming and agricultural practices have evolved towards using sophisticated decision support systems for effective management of the farmlands. This research presents an Artificial Intelligence of Things (AIoT) based real-time crop recommendation system using an ESP32 microcontroller with temperature, moisture and pH sensor along with an Edge computing and Cloud based with a Web interface for smart decision making. The environmental parameters are monitored using hardware module and transmitted to the ThingSpeak cloud platform via Wi-Fi. It is then retrieved using a Flask server in the Cloud backend and the Real time crop recommendations are provided by applying a Random Forest classifier algorithm trained on curated agricultural datasets obtained from Kaggle which yielded 97.5% accurate crop recommendations. The proposed system uses buffering and retransmission techniques to tackle packet loss, thus enabling the smooth working of the proposed system even in resource constrained areas such as farms in rural regions. The proposed system is shown to be highly efficient and modular and can be effectively used in small and medium scale farming practices. Also, it is found to give highly accurate crop recommendations in an instant which proves that it can be effectively used as a practical tool for precision farming in developing countries.

title: ConsisHOI: Layout-Controlled Consistent Human-Object Interaction Image Generation via Frequency-Aware Guidance
authors: Caixia Yan, Yinghao Wang, Jiahao Wang, Qika Lin, Zhihui Li, Yugui Xie, Weizhan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Text-to-image diffusion models have significantly boosted visual-content creation efficiency with high-fidelity image generation. However, in scenarios involving human-object interaction (HOI) generation, they still struggle to maintain a character's facial identity across images while plausibly placing them in layout-constrained interactions. For this issue, we pioneer a novel L2C-HOI task that aims to produce layout-controlled consistent HOI images. To address this challenging task, we propose ConsisHOI, a frequency-aware generation framework underpinned by the diffusion model's inherent frequency bias property. Specifically, we design a dual-stream frequency encoding architecture to construct dual identity-interaction embeddings that capture both high-frequency facial details and low-frequency interaction contexts. To provide precise frequency-aware guidance throughout the denoising process, we develop a stage-wise fusion strategy that dynamically filters and blends the extracted dual-stream features across U-Net's sampling stages from the frequency perspective. For model optimization, we design a frequency-guided joint training paradigm to optimize the balance between identity preservation and interaction realism. To support model training, we construct the first dedicated dataset for the challenging L2C-HOI task, HOI-ID, integrating both real and synthetic images with an efficient annotation pipeline. Extensive experiments demonstrate ConsisHOI achieves superior performance in both identity consistency and interaction plausibility, establishing a new benchmark for layout-controlled consistent HOI image synthesis.

title: Mitigation of UE Antenna Calibration Errors via Differential STBC in Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Marx M. M. Freitas, Stefano Buzzi
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates the use of differential space–time block coding (DSTBC) to address antenna array calibration impairments at multi-antenna user equipment (UE) in the downlink (DL) of cell-free massive MIMO (CF-mMIMO) systems. We show that, by exploiting DSTBC, reliable DL communication can be achieved without explicit UE-side calibration or channel phase knowledge. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DSTBC-based transmission effectively mitigates the impact of antenna-dependent phase offsets, restoring near-coherent performance in CF-mMIMO networks.

title: Yazh Smart Glasses: A Multimodal AI-Enabled Wearable Integrating Facial Recognition, Context-Aware Cognitive Assistance, and IoT-Driven Remote Care for Enhancing Elderly Autonomy
authors: S Sakthi Vinayagam, V Ashok Kumar, R Avinash, G Lakshmipathi, P S Malan Amirtha Yogan, P Mahendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The elderly is a challenge with memory lapses, inability to recognize faces, forgetfulness of the everyday routine, and this factor often results in the reliance of the elderly on the caregiver and absent of a social life. There are traditional memory aids, including alarms and pillboxes, which are not effective in dealing with these problems. Yazh Smart Glasses: Yazh Smart Glasses are an assistive device (non-invasive, wearable) that currently operated with AI and was created to help elderly users to maintain their memory and gain independence in their lives. It can identify familiar faces and one can control his voice by using the voice recognition feature and it includes sensors embedded into it, a mini camera allowing the identification of the familiar face and the immediate voice response and assist in planning daily activities such as taking medicine, attending a doctor, and special events. IoT and web dashboard integration give the options of remote scheduling and information update to the caregiver. Simultaneously, its lightweight, simple and economical design gives it a large accessibility. Yazh is new in including the facial recognition feature, context-aware voice assistant, and real-time activity scheduling in a wearable with which the elderly would be able to remain in touch and be independent without losing their dignity.

title: An Optimized Crop Recommendation System using Soil pH, Rainfall, and Temperature
authors: Sridhar D, K Vinod Kumar Reddy, Boge Sumanth Kumar Reddy, Thoshitha M, Sindhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This work describes a crop recommendation system that can be used to support agricultural decision-making in agriculture under varying environmental conditions. The traditional approach to crop selection is based on static knowledge of soil conditions and experience gained in the past. This approach may not be adequate to ensure optimal crop productivity when there are variations in climatic conditions. Therefore, this study proposes a system that incorporates Internet of Things (IoT) for soil sensing and real-time weather data collection using machine learning algorithms. The proposed system is based on various agronomic factors such as soil pH, nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), potassium (K), temperature, humidity, and rainfall. The system retrieves real-time environmental data using an external weather API and soil data using embedded sensors in the system. The proposed system uses an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) algorithm for predicting crop suitability based on these factors. The experimental outcomes show that the proposed system has high predictive accuracy of about 98.6%, and the results are consistent when cross-validation is applied. The proposed system is implemented as a web-based system that supports multiple languages for wider acceptance by farmers in various communities.

title: Toward Intelligent PID Control: A Simplified MDOF Intelligent PID Framework for Robust MIMO Systems
authors: Mohamed I. Abu El-Sebah, Feaka M.H. Khater, Essam Hendawi, Mohamed Fawzy El-Khatib
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Designing high-performance controllers for multi-input multi-output (MIMO) systems remains a challenging problem due to nonlinear dynamics, parameter uncertainties, and strong coupling effects. Although proportional–integral–derivative (PID) controllers are widely adopted for their simplicity and robustness, their performance is often limited in complex systems. This paper proposes a novel multi-degree-of-freedom (MDOF) intelligent PID control framework that combines the simplicity of classical PID with the adaptability of intelligent control strategies. Two simplified intelligent controllers are introduced: the Simplified Optimum Intelligent PID (SOI-PID) and the Simplified Universal Intelligent PID (SUI-PID), along with an enhanced MDOF-based fuzzy logic controller (MDOF-FLC). The proposed framework integrates multiple control actions using adaptive weighting based on the tracking error, enabling simultaneous improvement of transient and steady-state performance. The effectiveness of the proposed controllers is validated on two benchmark systems: a coupled MIMO dynamic system and a nonlinear four-degree-of-freedom (4-DOF) robotic manipulator. Comprehensive simulations, robustness analysis under severe parameter variations, and hardware-in-the-loop experimental validation are conducted. The results demonstrate that the SUI-PID controller achieves superior performance in terms of rise time, overshoot reduction, tracking accuracy, and disturbance rejection compared with conventional fuzzy logic and two-degree-of-freedom PID controllers. Furthermore, the proposed approach maintains low computational complexity and does not require an accurate system model, making it suitable for real-time applications.

title: TBR-Mamba: Hierarchical Transfer Learning and Boundary-Aware Spatiotemporal Interaction Network for Forest Change Detection
authors: Jingxiang Yuan, Xiaobo Tan, Gufeng Gong, Guoxiong Zhou, Ming’e Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-resolution forest change detection (FCD) is essential for carbon assessment and sustainable forest management, yet reliable detection in complex multi-temporal scenes remains difficult because phenology-induced pseudo-changes, irregular and blurred boundaries, and unstable model adaptation under limited supervision jointly degrade performance. In this work, we propose Temporal and Boundary Refined Mamba (TBR-Mamba), a Siamese state-space framework tailored for high-resolution forest change detection. To support this task, we further construct HNF-CD, a public, large-scale, high-resolution benchmark for complex multi-temporal forest scenes, featuring expert-guided annotation of semantically verified cross-category transitions while retaining phenology-related and other non-semantic hard negatives. To handle temporal discrepancies, the Phenology-Resistant Temporal Contrast (PRTC) module suppresses phenology-induced pseudo-changes through temporal contrast-guided attention and asymmetric refinement in a dual-stream interaction scheme. To resolve spatial ambiguity, the Edge-Prior Guided Decoupling (EPGD) module sharpens blurred boundaries by decoupling boundary cues from region semantics under boundary-aware joint optimization. Finally, to stabilize convergence under limited annotations, the Hierarchical Transfer Fine-Tuning Encoder (HTFT-E) adapts the Swin-UMamba backbone through stage-wise transfer control, promoting effective transfer of generic visual priors to forestry-specific semantics. Experiments on HNF-CD, Forest-CD, and Zhejiang-CD show that TBR-Mamba achieves the best overall performance among the compared methods, reaching F1 / cIoU scores of 89.81% / 81.50%, 95.14% / 90.74%, and 92.60% / 86.23%, respectively. Cross-dataset transfer experiments further suggest that HNF-CD serves as a useful forestry-specific intermediate source, complementing generic ImageNet pre-training. Overall, these results indicate that TBR-Mamba is effective for fine-grained forest change detection in heterogeneous forest environments.

title: Intelligent Video Surveillance System with Violence Detection
authors: Nikhil Korada, Kuruba Vijayenda Varma, Adabala Nagesh Satya Vinay, D Rajeswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The growing application of smart video surveillance systems requires the creation of viable and live violence detection systems. In this paper, a multimodal-based violence recognition system is proposed, which combines visual and audio evidence to increase the robustness and accuracy of the systems. A human pose estimation model based on the YOLO is used to extract visual features by capturing skeletal key points of human figures to examine their dynamic movement. Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) are used to derive audio features that capture the acoustic and emotional features of the environment. These features are time stamped and combined at feature level to form a single sequential representation of the scene. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network is used to catch the temporal dependence and classify the activities as violent or non-violent. The experimental findings of various datasets prove that the multimodal approach is superior to unimodal methods, revealing better resistance to detecting violence. The corresponding model had shown precision of 94%, recall of 92% and f1-score of 93% which helps in detecting the violence in low light and various environments.

title: Hybrid Supervised Physics-Informed Neural Network for Robust Power System State Estimation
authors: Shiwei Xu, Dongjian Huang, Qingsong Fu, Wenxu Yan, Wenyuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Power System State Estimation (PSSE) is fundamental for the secure operation of power grids, relying on measurements to estimate real-time conditions. However, conventional Weighted Least Squares (WLS) is vulnerable to non-Gaussian noise and bad data. Meanwhile, existing data-driven and Physics-Guided Deep Learning (PGDL) methods often suffer from poor interpretability, reliance on ”soft” constraints, and systematic biases from simplified models. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a Hybrid Supervised Physics-Informed Neural Network (HSPINN). The architecture integrates a differentiable power flow model as a ”hard” constraint within the decoder to ensure physical consistency. A hybrid supervision mechanism, combining physics-consistency and true-state supervision losses, mitigates model-induced biases, while establishing a theoretical analysis of the framework’s structural robustness against stealthy False Data Injection Attacks (FDIAs), which is further hardened via adversarial training. Furthermore, an uncertainty-based adaptive weighting scheme dynamically balances these competing losses. To evaluate the performance of the proposed framework, a comparison is made between the traditional Mean Squared Error (MSE) loss and robust alternatives, including the Huber and Generalized Correntropy Loss (GCL) functions. Simulation results on the IEEE 14-bus and 118-bus systems, tested under various scenarios with Gaussian and non-Gaussian noise, severe bad data, and stealthy FDIA, show that the proposed HSPINN framework significantly outperforms the traditional WLS method in both accuracy and robustness.

title: Multi - Purpose Autonomous Boat for Sea Monitoring with Machine Learning
authors: Kami Reddy Jaya Prakash, Pothula Rajesh Yadav, P.Kavipriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rise in demand for real-time ocean data on environmental monitoring, maritime safety, or resource tracking involves increasing interest in developing autonomous systems with minimum human input to carry out complex tasks. This paper describes the design and deployment of a multipurpose autonomous boat equipped with advanced sensors and machine- learning tools built for performing sea-monitoring tasks. Combining GPS-guided navigation with the ability for obstacle detection and environmental sensing, all powered by a lightweight onboard computer capable of decision-making in real time, the vessel can identify marine objects, detect water quality anomalies, and plot efficient routes adapting to changing sea conditions through machine learning. Built with modularity and growth in perspective, the platform will keep a wide range of applications open, from pollution tracing and fisheries oversight to disaster responses and surveillance. Simulation scenarios and tests conducted in real life demonstrated the robustness of autonomous steering of the craft, its resistance under dynamic conditions, and precision in all data-driven monitoring tasks. Taken together, these findings emphasize how promising this method will be for combining autonomous surface vessels with ML to ensure cost-effectiveness, sustainability, and smart solutions for ocean observation and natural resource management.

title: Joint Relay Location and Power Allocation Optimization for Full-Duplex Relay Systems in Industrial URLLC Networks
authors: Nann Win Moe Thet, Kenichi Takizawa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a joint relay location and power allocation optimization scheme for relay-assisted ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) systems. The framework employs an amplify-and-forward full-duplex (AF-FD) relay with residual self-interference (RSI) in cluttered indoor industrial environments. An achievable data rate maximization problem is formulated under total power and location constraints. The problem is non-convex due to the nonlinear dependence of the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) on both relay location and power allocation variables, as well as the logarithmic finite-blocklength (FBL) rate expression. To address this challenge, fractional programming techniques based on the Lagrangian Dual Transform (LDT) and Quadratic Transform (QT) are employed to obtain tractable surrogate formulations. A joint iterative optimization scheme is then developed, where relay placement and transmit power allocation are alternately updated using a gradient-based approach with Armijo backtracking to ensure monotonic convergence. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms benchmark approaches with fixed relay placement and/or power allocation under various RSI levels, highlighting its effectiveness for industrial URLLC applications.

title: Agentic AI-Driven Machine Learning Framework for Proactive Cyber-Attack Detection and Early Threat Warning Systems
authors: Ankita Sharma, Meenal Sharma, Bebesh Tripathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The history of the development of cyber threats has exposed the shortcomings of the traditional reactive cybersecurity that is based on the detection of cyber threats only after the desecration of computer systems. In order to succeed in defeating this challenge, this research study is a proactive cybersecurity model founded on Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning that is capable of identifying early danger signs of cyber attacks and issuing predictive advice on possible cyber threats taking place before large-scale cyber attacks. The suggested system deploys autonomous Agentic AI agents and supervised and unsupervised machine learning models to learn and understand trafficking, anomaly behaviors, and systemlevel events continuously on network traffic. The machine based system has intelligent agents, dynamic decision-making, adaptive learning and collaborative threat analysis which help in detecting malicious activities at an early stage in the system. Feature extraction and anomaly detection techniques in the system are used to detect covert attack patterns, including Distributed Denialof-Service (DDoS) attacks, malware injection attacks, phishing attacks, and insider threats. The experimental results obtained using standard cybersecurity datasets show improved earlystage threat prediction accuracy and reduced response latency times compared to conventional intrusion detection systems. The proposed framework provides accuracy of 97.8%, precision of 96.9%, recall of 97.2%, F1-score of 97.0%, detection rate of 98.1%, false positive rate of 2.1%, and threat prediction latency of less than 120 ms, thus supporting real-time autonomous warning generation.

title: A Precision Noise Modeling and Cost-Performance Optimization Framework for Discrete Biopotential Sensor Interfaces
authors: Yuwen Liu, Zheng Qian, Lu Wei, Jingyue Wang, Chengyao Shi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Addressing the critical lack of systematic noise quantification for complete signal chains in existing literature, this paper presents a comprehensive noise modeling framework and a multi-objective optimization methodology for a typical EEG acquisition topology incorporating a DC-Servo Loop. The paper rigorously analyzes a cascaded architecture comprising a non-inverting amplifier, an instrumentation amplifier, a DC-Servo Loop, and a second-order low-pass filter. By establishing a precise mathematical model, this paper systematically elucidates the complex interplay between resistor values, the gain distribution across amplification stages, and the input voltage noise density of amplifiers, along with their cumulative impact on total output noise. The analysis suggests that the total output noise is predominantly dominated by the first-stage preamplifier, while the contribution from the subsequent instrumentation amplifier is relatively marginal. Leveraging this insight, a design optimization strategy is proposed to significantly reduce the Bill of Materials cost while stringent noise performance requirements. Cross-validation through theoretical calculation, SPICE simulation, and hardware measurement demonstrates an input-referred noise of 0.49 μV root-mean-square, with a prediction error of less than 2%. Furthermore, comparative analysis quantifies the sensitivity of noise to gain allocation across different amplifier combinations, establishing the optimal trade-off between resistance values and gain ratios. This paper provides theoretical foundations and practical guidelines for the cost-effective design of high-performance EEG circuits.

title: Physics-Aware Multi-Modal Sensing: Mitigating IMU Drift in Single-Anchor AUV Tracking
authors: Seunghyun Kim, Junghyun Kim, Yeongho Park, Yujae Song
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: This letter presents a physics-aware sensor fusion framework to mitigate the rapid stochastic drift of low-cost micro electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) inertial measurement units in autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs). To enable precise tracking without expensive Doppler velocity logs, we propose a hybrid sensing model that integrates raw inertial signals with single-anchor acoustic range constraints. The core innovation lies in a physics-based residual learning architecture: a kinematic dead reckoning baseline captures the AUV's motion dynamics, while a deep neural network explicitly learns and corrects the sensor's intrinsic bias and geometric ambiguities. High-fidelity simulations based on rigorous MEMS noise models validate that the proposed method effectively compensates for hardware imperfections, achieving 73.8% RMSE reduction over conventional DR systems.

title: Violent Video Detection Based on Multimodal Video-Text Fusion
authors: Zhenjiang Li, Kai Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a violent video detection method based on video-text multimodal fusion, which makes up for the semantic and perceptual limitations of single visual features in complex scenes. The method extracts keyframes via a frame difference-based sampling strategy, captures video spatiotemporal features with unfrozen EfficientNet and temporal attention (TA) module, generates video captions through a captioning model, and deeply fuses visual and text features by the Video Text Fusion (VTF) module. Fused features are input into a classifier for binary prediction. Experiments on three public datasets verify that the method significantly improves the detection accuracy and robustness of the model.

title: Dual-Domain Diffusion Networks for Ultra-Low Light Image Reconstruction in Real-TIme Autonomous Systems
authors: R. Prabu, John Thangavel P, S. Sivakumar, P. Dhinakar, Rajat Kumar Dwibedi, Maruthupandi V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The challenge of ultralow light image reconstruction is a serious issue to the autonomous systems in real time because of the serious noise corruptions, low count of photons and the speed at which visual perception is required. The article presents a new and integrated Dual-Domain Diffusion Network (D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>DN) that is created to reconstruct quality of images through combined model of spatial and frequency activities in a coupled diffusion process. As opposed to conventional denoising/enhancing pipelines, which operate on domains separately, D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>DN uses both pixel and frequency space diffusion, allowing to correct features complementary to one another with a lower compute cost. A cross-domain consistency model is used to guarantee structural integrity, and a simple time-sensitive block is used to guarantee constant reconstruction in case of changing the scenes. The architecture proposed has been shown to have real-time performance and much-enhanced brightness, detail retention and color accuracy in harsh low-illumination environments. Extensive experimental results have proven that D<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>DN is superior in PSNR, SSIM as well as inference speed compared to existing state-of-the-art approaches and so it can be adopted in robotics, autonomous navigation and embedded vision systems operating in night or low-visibility conditions.

title: DSTFEformer: Dual-Scale Temporal-Frequency Ensemble Transformer for Highway Traffic Volume Prediction
authors: Jiazhan Hu, Tao Feng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Highway Traffic Volume prediction is an essential component of intelligent transportation systems and mobility service operations. It aims to predict the temporal evolution of Highway Traffic Volume in a given region over a future period, thereby supporting decision-making. At present, although deep learning models have made certain progress, they often fail to adequately model the global and local information in Highway Traffic Volume time series, which limits prediction accuracy. To address this issue, this paper proposes the Dual-Scale Temporal-Frequency Ensemble Transformer, whose core components are the Dual-Scale Sampling Module and the Temporal-Frequency Attention module. The former transforms raw data into two types of tensor representations that emphasize global and local information, while the latter captures temporal dependencies from both global and local perspectives. Finally, based on a generative decoder, the model accurately predicts future Highway Traffic Volume values. Experimental results on a real-world dataset recorded by the Japanese Electronic Toll Collection System show that the proposed model can obtain satisfactory results and outperform eight state-of-the-art baseline models, providing strong support for intelligent transportation systems and mobility service operations.

title: Degradation-Resistant Infrared-Visible Image Fusion With Auto-Generated Textual Objectives and Embedded Contrastive Learning
authors: Yuhao Wang, Lingjuan Miao, Zhiqiang Zhou, Yajun Qiao, Yixuan Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Infrared-visible image fusion aims to combine multi-modal image information to generate informative and robust scene representations, thereby enhancing perception capabilities and reliability in intelligent transportation systems. However, captured images often suffer from complex degradation issues, leading to low-quality source data. Existing methods are deficient in adapting to multiple degradation conditions, which limits their fusion performance. In this paper, we aim to develop a degradation-resistant image fusion method that automatically adapts to various degradations. For this purpose, we first construct an auto-generation prompt pipeline based on cascaded multi-modal and language models. It utilizes the vision-language understanding capabilities of large models to comprehensively detect degradation, then produces degradation prompts and corresponding text-based fusion objectives for each image. To resist degradations and produce the fusion results as described by fusion objectives, we next propose an embedded contrastive learning method within CLIP space to supervise the model training. This method ensures that the image fusion process is free from degradation and better aligned with the fusion objectives, which enhances the fusion model’s anti-degradation capability. Extensive experiments on public datasets validate the superiority and generalization ability of our method, and its robust degradation-adaptive capability makes it particularly suitable for complex scenes. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/wyhlaowang/AutoFuse</uri>.

title: Parallel Fusion of Swin Transformer and SKNet for Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xiaochun Sun, Hua Ding, Ning Li, Guoshu Pu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To address the issue of insufficient feature extraction from nonlinear and non-stationary vibration signals of rolling bearings under strong noise environments, which limits the diagnostic accuracy of existing fault diagnosis models, this paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis method based on the parallel fusion of Swin Transformer (SwinT) and selective kernel network (SKNet). The proposed method enables cooperative modeling of global and local features. It consists of a simplified SwinT branch and a lightweight SKNet branch. The SwinT branch incorporates an improved Bottleneck Attention Module (IBAM) to enhance the model's capacity for capturing global features. The lightweight SKNet branch utilizes blueprint separable convolution (BSConv) to replace traditional convolution operations and adopts a 1D convolutional structure instead of the original fully connected layers in SKNet, thereby reducing the number of parameters and model complexity, which may facilitate deployment in resource-constrained environments. Experimental verification was carried out based on the data collected from the public bearing dataset of the University of Paderborn in Germany and the rolling bearing fault simulation platform built in the laboratory. The results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several comparative models in terms of accuracy, precision, and recall. It maintains a recognition accuracy close to 90% even at a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of -8 dB, indicating its strong noise immunity and robustness.

title: DataStates-LLM : Scalable Checkpointing for Transformer Models Using Composable State Providers
authors: Avinash Maurya, M. Mustafa Rafique, Franck Cappello, Bogdan Nicolae
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The rapid growth of Large Transformer-based models, specifically Large Language Models (LLMs), now scaling to trillions of parameters, has necessitated training across thousands of GPUs using complex hybrid parallelism strategies (e.g., data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism). Checkpointing this massive, distributed state is critical for a wide range of use cases, such as resilience, suspend-resume, investigating undesirable training trajectories, and explaining model evolution. However, existing checkpointing solutions typically treat model state as opaque binary blobs, ignoring the “3D heterogeneity” of the underlying data structures–varying by memory location (GPU vs. Host), number of “logical” objects sharded and split across multiple files, data types (tensors vs. Python objects), and their serialization requirements. This results in significant runtime overheads due to blocking device-to-host transfers, data-oblivious serialization, and storage I/O contention. In this paper, we introduce DataStates-LLM, a novel check pointing architecture that leverages State Providers to decouple state abstraction from data movement. DataStates-LLM exploits the immutability of model parameters during the forward and backward passes to perform “lazy”, non-blocking asynchronous snapshots. By introducing State Providers, we efficiently coalesce fragmented, heterogeneous shards and overlap the serialization of metadata with bulk tensor I/O. We evaluate DataStates-LLM on models up to 70B parameters on 256 A100-40GB GPUs. Our results demonstrate that DataStates-LLM achieves up to 4× higher checkpointing throughput and reduces end-to-end training time by up to 2.2× compared to state-of-the-art solutions, effectively mitigating the serialization and heterogeneity bottlenecks in extreme-scale LLM training.

title: Generative Motion In-betweening by Diffusion over Continuous Implicit Representations_supp1-3693336.mp4
authors: Edmond S. L. Ho
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent advances in generative models have yielded impressive progress on motion in-betweening, allowing for more complex, varied, and realistic motion transitions. However, recent methods still exhibit noticeable limitations in preserving keyframe information and ensuring motion continuity. In this paper, we propose a novel pipeline and sampling optimization strategy for latent diffusion models (LDM) based on motion implicit neural representations (INR). By establishing a mapping between INR and sparse spatial or temporal information within latent diffusion, our model can sample the INR parameters from extremely sparse and ambiguous keyframe data and reconstruct plausible and smooth motions from the manifold. Our experiments demonstrate the superior performance of our model, which significantly improves motion generation quality in scenarios with few keyframes while ensuring both keyframe accuracy and diversity of in-between motions.

title: Design and Analysis of Circularly Polarized Microstrip Patch Antenna for IRNSS S-Band Applications
authors: Rajesh S R, Murthi Mahadeva Naik G, Sushma N, Deepika K C, Santhosh Kumar K B, Usha Desai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Reliable satellite-based navigation systems Accurate position information and timing data are provided by high gain, stable radiation characteristics, and polarization robustness antennae For the Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS) satellite, which works in the S-band frequency region, circularly polarized antennae are used because they are very effective against the demands of polarization mismatch losses and multipath interference for mobile and fixed receiver systems With this work, the circularly polarized microstrip patch antenna (CP-MPA) is designed and analyzed based on HFSS simulation tools for IRNSS-S band applications. The introduced antenna is well designed to work in the 2.5 GHz corresponding frequency range and exhibits good impedance match with the minimum reflection coefficient S11 of about -14.6 dB at 2.55 GHz. The simulated, with a central frequency of 2.44 GHz, −10 dB impedance bandwidth is approximately from 2.36 GHz to 2.66 GHz, which completely covers the IRNSS S-band. The peak realized gain of the antenna is nearly 8.6 dBi and the circularly polarized gain is about 8.56 dBic at the center frequency. Smith Chart analysis showing stable impedance behavior throughout the operating band. The compact structure, sufficient bandwidth and excellent circular polarization performance of the proposed antenna make it suitable for the IRNSS navigation and receiver applications.

title: Predictive Multi-Domain Dynamic Body-Bias Architecture for Energy-Adaptive System-on-Chip Platforms
authors: Harini P, Ajitha M, Martin Leo Manickam J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Considering the increased complexity in System-on-Chip (SoC) platforms, managing power dissipation, leakage current, and thermal stability is becoming a significant challenge in low-power Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) design. The body-bias control methods used in traditional low-power VLSI design are generally reactive in nature and are unable to respond efficiently to workload changes in heterogeneous processing domains. To address this problem, this research proposes a predictive multi-domain dynamic body-bias control architecture for energy-adaptive SoC platforms. The proposed architecture includes Gradient Aware Body Bias Modulation, Thermal-Elastic Bias Balancing, Predictive Leakage Shaping Filter, and Multi-Domain Bias Coordination Protocol for efficient body-bias control. Experimental results for low, medium, high, and peak workload conditions show that the proposed architecture achieves high predictive accuracy of 99.3%, leakage reduction ranging from 31% to 44%, and energy efficiency improvement ranging from 18% to 29%, while demonstrating performance scalability and thermal stability. The proposed architecture is thus seen to be an efficient solution for next-generation low-power SoC platforms..

title: Smart Agriculture Utilizing Attention-Guided Granger Causality Graph Neural Network for Enhanced Crop Monitoring and Management in IoT Enabled Environment
authors: L. Febin Rani, Devi.T, N. Deepa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) technology used more and more in smart agriculture to gather massive amounts of real-time data for farm management and crop monitoring. However, intricate spatiotemporal and causal interactions among soil, environmental and agricultural variables were frequently missed by classic models. In this manuscript, Smart Agriculture Utilizing Attention-Guided Granger Causality Graph Neural Network for Enhanced Crop Monitoring and Management in IoT Enabled Environment (AGCGNN-CMM-IoT) is proposed. The input data are collected from Smart Agriculture and Plant Health Monitoring using IoT. The input data are fed into Residual-Compensated Adaptive Kalman Filter (RCAKF) to data cleaning and normalization. The preprocessed data are given into feature extraction is performed using the Learnable Wavelet Packet Transform (LWPT) to extract the key feature like Soil moisture content, Soil temperature, Soil pH value, Environmental Temperature. Then the features are fed into the Attention-Guided Granger Causality Graph Neural Network (AGCGNN) for crop monitoring and predict agricultural field conditions. The proposed AGCGNN-CMM-IoT method is implemented and the metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, ROC, F1-score, and computational time are evaluated. The AGCGNN-CMM-IoT approach attains 6.41%, 4.29% and 9.35% better accuracy and 5.31%, 6.25% and 7.15% better precision compared with existing methods IAI-IoT-PA, IoT-ML-MSA and ESA-IoT-LD respectively.

                    A Holistic Multi-Tier KV Cache Management System for Long-Context LLM Inference
authors: Jian Lin, Jiazhi Mi, Zicong Hong, Haodong Wang, Qianli Liu, Haoyue Zhang, Peng Li, Song Guo

abstract: None

title: MoDora: Tree-Based Semi-Structured Document Analysis System
authors: Bangrui Xu, Qihang Yao, Zirui Tang, Xuanhe Zhou, Yeye He, Shihan Yu, Qianqian Xu, Bin Wang, Guoliang Li, Conghui He, Fan Wu
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Optimized Pricing and Stochastic Modeling for EV Charging Stations using a MDP
authors: Prabin Devkota, Pratyush Adhikari, Pratyush Shrestha, T. V. Smitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: This paper presents a dynamic pricing model, which is optimal in the case of the electric vehicle (EV) charging station with the Markov Decision Process (MDP) with a Lyapunov Drift-Plus-Penalty (DPP) controller, which will be solved using the Line Search method. The suggested solution allows adjusting the prices in real time and remaining profitable, stable on the grid, and satisfied with the customers, which contributes to Sustainable Development Goals 7, 9, and 11. The DPP-MDP framework, in contrast to conventional reinforcement learning or heuristic-based algorithms, is model-free and online; that is, the algorithm only takes into account the observed system states like demand, queue length, and grid load without an explicit transition model. Virtual queues provide grids and Quality of Service limits, which can guarantee operational stability in the long term. The model is capable of handling the stochastic arrival of vehicles and variable energy usage, which is a scalable and theoretically based solution to EV charging management at smart grids.

title: Cloud based File Storage with Encryption, Upload, Store and Manage Files with Strong AES- GCM Encryption, Uses AWS S3 for Storage and DynamoDB for Metadata Tracking
authors: Suryadevara Ramnikhil, Trivedh Karanam, Prabu S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Cloud computing has significantly transformed the way data is stored and accessed, but it also raises critical concerns related to data security, privacy, and integrity. When sensitive information is stored on third-party cloud infrastructures, the risk of unauthorized access and data leakage increases. To address these challenges, this paper presents a secure cloud-based file storage system that integrates client-side encryption with scalable cloud infrastructure. The proposed system employs Advanced Encryption Standard in Galois/Counter Mode (AES-GCM) to ensure both confidentiality and integrity of files before they are transmitted to the cloud. Encrypted files are stored using a cloud object storage service, while metadata and file management information are maintained in a structured database to support efficient retrieval operations. The system also incorporates behavioral monitoring to identify unusual user access patterns and improve overall security. Experimental observations demonstrate that the proposed framework maintains secure data storage while introducing only minimal performance overhead. The results indicate that the system provides a practical and scalable solution for protecting sensitive data in modern cloud storage environments.

title: A Hyper-Local Rainfall and Flood Alert System using Deep Learning and Geospatial Intelligence
authors: R. Prem Kumar, S. Mukilarasan, A. Rajesh, S. Yokesh, R. Jeevananth, M. Sakthivel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Due to the rapid urbanization and climate change, extreme rainfalls and flash floods have intensified and have done tremendous harm to life, infrastructure, and urban resilience. Classical flood forecasting models typically assume spatial scales and do not work well in localizing rainfall variations and small-scale flooding. The proposed paper presents an ultra-local rainfall and flood early warning system which is a combination of deep learning and geospatial intelligence to have accurate and location-specific notifications. The model takes into account high-resolution rainfall data, satellite data, topography and land-use data and past floods to represent sophisticated patterns of rainfall-runoff. It has been experimentally demonstrated that prediction accuracy and warning time are better, and this facilitates effective disaster management and community-level preparedness to floods.

title: Joint Energy and Reliability Optimization in Hybrid Optical Acoustic Underwater IoT Networks
authors: Reshma P Vengaloor, S. Jagir Hussain, Reshmi Krishna Prasad, Neethu Susan Alex, Rahul R, T.Jaya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The integration of high-speed short-range optical communication with long-range acoustic communication has become a viable option to solve the underwater Internet of Things (IoT) communication network. Nevertheless, in dynamic underwater conditions, there is a great challenge in ensuring energy efficiency and reliable communication at the same time. The paper introduces a hybrid optical underwater IoT network based on deep learning and reinforcement learning that optimizes the energy consumption and transmission reliability jointly in hybrid networks to achieve the goals of deep learning and reinforcement learning. An adaptive control strategy is built on learning to dynamically negotiate transmission power and frequency as a result of real-time channel state feedback, whereas a reinforcement learning agent chooses appropriate modulation and error-control strategies in order to reduce transmission errors in changing signal-to-noise. The suggested structure allows coordination in decision-making among layers of communication, which leads to a consistent performance in the underwater space with variable conditions. Widespread simulation experiments prove that the suggested strategy yields substantial energy usage savings and still ensures low bit error rates, better signal quality, quicker adaptation, and enhanced throughput than the traditional underwater routing and energy management methods. The findings support the usefulness of having learning-based joint optimization to enable reliable, energy-efficient communication over the underwater IoT.

title: Intelligent Multi-Layer Penetration Testing System using Hybrid AI Models
authors: K. Dinesh, Prakash Royal B, Manikandan R, R. Sai Kavya Sri, C. Sai Keerthana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Due to today’s adversarial computing landscape, continuous and scalable security assessment is needed. However, penetration testing still suffers from primarily a highly manual effort emerged from exist complimentary yet disjointed tools. The capabilities of penetration testers are outstripped by the threat landscape and full testing is lagging on completion. To overcome these limitations, this paper presents an Intelligent Multi-Layer Penetration Testing System that integrates different artificial intelligence procedures within a single architecture to automate and speed up the end-to-end penetration testing process. In particular, the framework uses a supervised machine learning classifier obviously, which is Random Forest. In addition to that, it uses a deep learning sequence prediction model, which is Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory. Furthermore, it uses a deep reinforcement learning agent which is Deep Q-Network. Most importantly, it helps to model hypotheses, map attack steps, plan and learn inside a constrained network of pen-testing tools. An assessment conducted on a validated virtualisation-enabled testbed of a large enterprise network demonstrates that the system achieves a classification accuracy of 97.5% and an F1-score of 97.4%. Moreover, the system substantially outperforms Q-learning as well as deep reinforcement learning baselines. End-to-end estimation requires 20 hours of time as compared to 160 hours of time needed for manual testing. Many strict controls on access and data validation ensure non-destructive verification and suitability to an authorized security assessment and academic research environments.

title: Probabilistic Reinforcement-Optimized Hierarchical Temporal ECG Intelligence Framework with Immersive Augmented Reality Cardiac Electrophysiological Visualization for Translational Clinical Decision Support
authors: Sherwin Anand, R.R.Sanjay Chelliah, J.Thresa Jeniffer
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The problem with cardiac arrhythmia diagnosis with the help of electrocardiogram (ECG) signals is seen in variability of signals, noise, complex temporal relationships that can potentially lower the diagnostic accuracy. The proposed study is based on the idea of creating an intelligent clinical decision-support system that is based on deep learning classification with Augmented Reality (AR) visualizing 3D heart in 3D visualization. Variational Inference Techniques make it possible to extract features probabilistically, and Deep Belief Networks are learned to learn hierarchical representations of ECG. Long Short-term memory networks remember the temporal dependency, and Deep Q-network is optimized to make the adaptive classification decisions. Parametrically ECG signals are preprocessed and segmented and converted into discriminative features which can be most accurately used to identify arrhythmia. Abnormal results are dynamically overlaid to an interactive heart model in 3D AR and the dinosaur effect is utilized to visualize an abnormal pathway of abnormal electrical conduction in real time. The classification accuracy, strength, and latency are high and experimental validation shows high validity. The offered system has greater interpretability, training in clinical settings, and patient interaction as it offers a new generation of AI-guided cardiology systems in smart healthcare settings.

title: From Silent Automation to Conversational Intelligence: Empowering Smart Homes with LLM-Based Agents
authors: Dhyego Tavares, Erlon Almeida, Enio Santana, Frederico Durao, Gustavo Bittencourt, Maycon Peixoto, Cassio Prazeres, Ivan Machado, Eduardo Santana de Almeida
publish: IEEE Software
abstract: Smart home systems have evolved from simple automation to AI-driven ecosystems. Nevertheless, user interaction often remains limited to rigid interfaces. This paper presents ECLIPS (Environmental Command Language Intelligent Processing System), an extension of a previously proposed Smart Home Orchestration Framework for a more intelligent and responsive environment. ECLIPS introduces a Two-Layer Modular Hierarchical Multi-Agent Architecture that integrates Large Language Model (LLM) agents as a semantic interpretation layer, while preserving established predictive models based on LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) and MLP (Multi-Layer Perceptron) networks. The proposed approach enables natural language interaction, contextual information retrieval, and transparent explanation of system behavior without altering the underlying automation logic. Evaluated through a design science–oriented case study within an industrial smart home project, results demonstrate improved user understanding, flexibility, and control. Combining predictive machine learning with agentic LLM architectures enhances usability, transparency, and adaptability in smart home ecosystems.

title: Exploration of Customer Behaviour in Real-Time E-Commerce & Bi-Directional Forecasting using Adaptive Multi-Domain Attention Networks
authors: Lakshmana Rao Kawluri, S.Suresh Kumar, Tulasi Raju Nethala, Yugandhar Bokka
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Knowledge of the real-time customer behaviour is essential to the enhancement of user experience, conversion rate, and business decision-making in the contemporary e-commerce setting, but the conventional models such as ARIMA, SARIMA and exponential smoothing are based on the unchanging historical data and do not capture the swiftly changing behavioural patterns and cross-domain interactions. To address these shortcomings, this paper proposes the Adaptive Multi-Domain Attention Network (AMDA-Net) that combines various types of data, including demographics, browsing history, purchasing behaviour, search intent, and sentiment and does the bi-directional prediction between customers and products. AMDA-Net based on a hybrid architecture, i.e., bi-LSTM with multi-head self-attention and cross-domain feature alignment, is used to identify the most significant behavioural signals and learns to respond to changes in real-time, e.g. promotions or customer intentions. It was experimentally found that AMDA-Net achieves greater forecasting precision, superior customer classification, more valuable recommendations, and enhanced behavioural understanding, compared to conventional statistical and machine learning baselines. In general, the framework provides a networkable and smart real time e-commerce analytics platform to help enhance marketing, demand forecasting, inventory planning, as well as customer retention.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Maintenance System using Multisensor Fusion
authors: S. Suriyamoorthy, B.M. Arul Victor Raj, D. Ragavan, A. Gopikrishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Pumping systems are very important in industrial wastewater treatment and production sites, as their sudden breakdowns result in significant production losses and costs. This paper proposes an edge-based intelligent predictive maintenance system involving multi-sensing, anomaly identification, temporal forecasting, and explainable diagnosis through cheap hardware components. A Raspberry Pi 5 is used to extract real-time vibration, temperature, and sound data from industrial machines in a wastewater treatment plant. Relevant statistical features are extracted and adaptively normalized based on varying conditions of the machine in operation. An Isolation Forest technique is used for identifying the current anomalies in an unsupervised manner, and a Gated Recurrent Unit network examines temporal patterns to forecast future risk levels of a machine. The results of both models are combined using a decision logic approach for generating machine health status and a continuous health index. Further, a rule-based explanation module is used to provide interpretable explanations of the faults discovered by the sensor behavior. The proposed architecture functions completely on the edge, hence no longer dependent on the cloud for its functioning. The results show that the reliable trend-based indication of faults as well as real-time functioning, providing a scalable, cost-effective, and explainable solution for the upcoming predictive maintenance tasks in the industrial sector.

title: Pixel Watch: Robust Heart Rate Sensing from Multipath PPG and On-Device Deep Learning Trained on 10,000 hours of Free-Living and Fitness Data
authors: Daniel Roggen, Megan Walker, Yojan Patel, Shyam Tailor, Dimitris Spathis, Matt Wimmer, Brennan Garrett, Dan Howe, Abhinuv Pitale, Hamed Vavadi, Tien Le, Steve Diamond, Oleksiy Vyalov, Vik Sharma, Pete Richards, Tracy Giest, Erika Siegel, Tuan Phan, Sam Mravca, Derrick Vickers, Benjamin Stone, Katarina Vukosavljević, Justin Phillips, YongSuk Cho, Stefanie Hollidge, Antony Siahaan, Soren Brage, Shwetak Patel, Robert Harle
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: The Pixel Watch 2 (PW2) is the first Google smartwatch to combine multipath photoplethysmography (PPG) with deep learning-based heart rate inference, designed to significantly improve sensing accuracy during motion-heavy activities. The device processes 10 optical channels using an on-device, 15-layer temporally dilated convolutional neural network (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>300K parameters) to yield a 1 Hz heart rate output. Crucial to this model's performance was its training on a massive dataset comprising 10,000 hours of data from 962 participants, curated from a broader corpus of controlled and free-living activities. We evaluated the PW2's sensing performance across two independent validation sets: an in-house fitness dataset (229 participants, 250 hours) and an external free-living dataset (27 participants, 1000+ hours). The system achieved 95% Limits of Agreement of -10.34 to 8.66 BPM during exercise and -6.57 to 7.48 BPM during free-living activities, demonstrating substantially tighter error margins than previous Google devices. Finally, we discuss key design lessons, emphasizing that large-scale deep learning was instrumental in fully leveraging multipath PPG hardware over traditional signal processing approaches.

title: SADP: Spatially-Aware DCT Priors for Low-Light Image Enhancement_supp1-3693598.pdf
authors: Xiaoqi Chen
publish: N/A
abstract: Low-light image enhancement (LLIE) remains challenging because insufficient illumination is often accompanied by color distortion, detail loss, and noise amplification. These coupled degradations further vary across spatial regions, making a uniform enhancement strategy prone to suboptimal restoration. To address this issue, we propose SADP, a spatially-aware LLIE framework that explicitly converts local frequency statistics from overlapping block discrete cosine transform (DCT) analysis into spatial priors for degradation-aware enhancement. Specifically, SADP first uses a luminance prior to construct a stable luminance base in the YCbCr space while preserving the original chrominance. It then exploits complementary color, detail, and noise priors in the RGB space to jointly guide degradation-aware RGB restoration. Experimental results on mainstream LLIE datasets demonstrate that SADP achieves high-quality enhancement with competitive efficiency.

title: TrustVTON: Trustworthy AI-Enabled Video Virtual Try-On with Differential Privacy for Consumer Mobile Applications
authors: Qiuyuan Gao, Haoquan Fan, Chenyuan Liu, Xiangdong Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Virtual try-on technology (VTON) has been widely adopted across mobile e-commerce platforms. However, the technology has introduced new ways for cybercriminals to exploit consumers. Criminals can infer biometric parameters, create digital representations of consumers without their consent, and make model inversion attacks. Current VTONs and Private VTONs have three privacy-related limitations. First, they cannot adequately handle the changing nature of attackers. Second, there is a significant reduction in the privacy vs. utility trade-off due to excessive noise added during processing. Third, current VTON solutions will not work on mobile devices. TrustVTON addresses all three issues and provides the first differential privacy plus proactive protection framework for VTON applications. The contributions of TrustVTON are (1) a privacy budget allocation method that uses only 35.7% more noise and still meets an ϵ = 3.2 differential privacy guarantee, (2) a lightweight architecture for detecting anomalies with 94.3% accuracy, and (3) a hierarchical trust management framework that enables users to verify the integrity of their data. TrustVTON performs better than existing VTON frameworks based on results from the VITON-HD, VVT, and ViViD benchmarks, providing an increase of 5.8% (0.897 vs. 0.848) in Structural Similarity Index(SSIM), a decrease of 5.56% (0.068 vs. 0.072) in Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity(LPIPS), an increase of 4.7% in resilience to black box attacks, and 3.2× faster inference times (FID = 10.84, comparable to baselines) on mobile devices (14.6 FPS). TrustVTON is the first step towards building trustworthy VTON systems with strong privacy protections and practical mobile implementation.

title: First-Order Impedance Model and Impedance-Frequency Analysis of Co-Phase Traction Power System Considering Control Coordinates and Integrated Compensation
authors: Fan Zhong, Shaofeng Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: The co-phase traction power system (CTPS) has attracted widespread attention due to its advantages in solving power quality problems and eliminating the neutral section. However, the diversity of converter control strategies such as dq decoupling control (DQDC) and quasi-proportional resonant control (QPRC) involves different control coordinates, and the multi-dimensional compensation requirements including negative sequence, reactive power and harmonic compensation together pose challenges to the accurate description of the system's impedance characteristics. In view of this background, this paper focuses on the integrated compensation model and impedance-frequency characteristic analysis of CTPS. Firstly, a first-order impedance model is proposed for CTPS under DQDC and QPRC. Subsequently, the impedance dynamic response of the CTPS under operating conditions including traction energy fluctuation, reactive power compensation, full and selective compensation of voltage and current harmonics are investigated. The impedance maximum peaks and admittance maximum peaks involving phase characteristics are defined, and the low-frequency oscillation nature induced by these peaks is clarified. Finally, verification is conducted through control hardware-in-the-loop experiments, and the effects of the co-phase compensator on impedance and admittance modifications as well as oscillation suppression are investigated.

title: Research on Deep Reinforcement Learning for Natural Language Generation and Semantic Understanding
authors: Xiangming Duan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Natural language generation (NLG) and natural language understanding (NLU) remain two foundational tasks in modern natural language processing. Although large pre-trained models have significantly improved task performance, standard supervised fine-tuning still suffers from exposure bias, weak cross-task semantic consistency, and high dependence on densely annotated data. This paper proposes a joint deep reinforcement learning framework that couples hierarchical text generation with adaptive semantic understanding. The generation branch uses reward shaping with semantic-similarity and task-level feedback to stabilize long-horizon optimization, while the understanding branch adopts PPO-based policy refinement and active sample selection to improve label efficiency and out-of-domain robustness. A bidirectional coupling mechanism is further introduced so that understanding scores guide generation and generated rationales provide auxiliary semantic evidence for understanding. Experiments on GLUE, CLUE, CNN/Daily Mail, and open-domain dialogue tasks indicate consistent improvements over strong RoBERTa-Large and BART-Large baselines. The proposed method improves average understanding accuracy by 3.2 percentage points and raises the core ROUGE score by 1.9 points. Under a 10% annotation budget, it still preserves an 8.6-point advantage, suggesting good applicability for question answering, machine translation, and low-resource intelligent interaction systems.

title: TCG-CTG: A Thinking-based Constraint Guidance Dataset for Controllable Text Generation
authors: YuQiao He, Yan Zhuang, Yuan Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Controllable Text Generation (CTG) aims to produce content that meets users’ requirements based on their input, serving as a key technology for enhancing system reliability and user experience. This paper conceptualizes CTG as a three-step process consisting of type prediction, constraint construction, and guided generation, and proposes a framework based on thinking-based constraint guidance. In the proposed framework, user requests are first analyzed through a structured reasoning process that decomposes the request into intermediate reasoning steps before constructing generation constraints. For different topic types, we design differentiated reasoning processes: a lightweight two-step reasoning process for daily topics, a four-step reasoning process for professional and sensitive topics, and a guiding three-step process for violation topics. We build a dataset of 82,204 constraint–text pairs that includes structured reasoning annotations. Using this dataset, we train constraint generation models based on LLaMA3-8B and GPT-2.Experimental results show that the proposed framework more accurately detects and avoids toxic content in toxicity control tasks. At the same time, it enhances generation quality across various scenarios, while preserving controllability, relevance, and practicality of model output.

title: An AHP-Based Decision Model for Applying the Principle of Proportionality to Cyber Operations
authors: Deshuo Xiong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: The principle of proportionality remains applicable to cyber operations under international humanitarian law, but its practical implementation is complicated by dual-use infrastructures, uncertain cyber effects, and limited effective human control. These features make purely qualitative balancing insufficient for ex ante legal review. To address this problem, this paper proposes an Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP)-based decision model for cyber proportionality assessment. The model structures review around five criteria: anticipated military advantage, target distinction reliability, effect foreseeability, human controllability, and incidental civilian harm risk. A publicly documented incident is used for preliminary validation, suggesting that the framework can support transparent and explainable evaluation of planned cyber operations. The proposed model does not replace legal judgment, but provides a structured decision-support tool for improving the consistency of cyber proportionality assessment.

title: Analysis of Impact Load Effect on The Ship Superstructure Sandwich Panel Using Finite Element Method
authors: Alya Rahmah Satira, Rizky Chandra Ariesta, Achmad Zubaydi, Teguh Putranto, Septi Hardy Sujiatanti
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Sandwich panel structures offer high structural efficiency and are increasingly used in ship superstructures. This study investigates the impact performance of a V-Core sandwich panel with varied faceplate and core thickness. The VCore configuration shows superior crashworthiness, achieving a peak force of 242 kN and Specific Energy Absorption (SEA) of approximately $0.575 \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{kg}$. Analysis of deformation patterns and velocity graphs reveals an effective distribution of plastic deformation across the faceplates and core, contributing to significant energy dissipation. These results demonstrate that V Core successfully combines structural strength with lightweight efficiency, making it a promising design for resisting highvelocity impact loads in marine applications.

title: Design and Characterisation of Rutin–Zinc Oxide Nanoparticle-based Smart Nano-Systems for Targeted Cancer Therapy
authors: A. Surya, G. Gnanamathy, R. Jeevitha, P. Elumalai, M. Sridevi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Lung carcinoma remains the leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, highlighting the application of biocompatible and targeted therapeutics due to toxicity and limited standard treatments. This study involved the synthesised rutin-functionalised zinc oxide nanoparticles (Rutin-ZnO NPs) by an eco-friendly, green wet-chemical mediated by L. grandifolia plant extract produce a smart nanotherapeutic system for lung cancer. The structural and physicochemical characterization of hexagonal ZnO nanorods through the use of XRD, FTIR, SEM, EDX and UV-Vis spectroscopy with an average particle size of about 218 nm, elemental composition of 78.1% Zn and 21.9% O and a characteristic absorption peak at 382 nm. The flow cytometry analysis showed that Rutin-ZnO NPs (30 μM) treatment significantly changed the cell cycle distribution of A549 lung cancer cells, population rising in S-phase (11.78%) and G<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/M (5.00%) after the treatment, which shows effective cell cycle arrest. Expression of TGF-β/SMAD2 gene considerably reduced (fold change < 0.5; p < 0.05) and wound-healing assays indicated a decrease in cell migration. These findings suggest that Rutin-ZnO nano-system is anti-cancerous by regulating proliferation, inducing apoptosis, and suppressing migration. This highlights their potential as a targeted nanotherapeutic platform to manage lung cancer, requiring further mechanistic and in-vivo studies.

title: Efficient Net-Enhanced YOLOv8/YOLOv9 for Early Lung Cancer Detection in CT: Multi-Scale Feature Fusion for Precise Lesion Localization
authors: P.S. MayuraVeena, J.B. Seventline
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The early recognition of lung cancer and correct identification are paramount for the prolongation of patient lives; however, contemporary procedures of diagnosis usually have a hard time with the slight variations in lesions and the large differences between patients. In this paper it suggests a new deep-learning-based system for lung cancer detection which combines the EfficientNet backbone with the currently popular YOLOv8 and YOLOv9 frameworks. The major feature of our technique is the replacement of the conventional YOLO backbone with a light yet powerful EfficientNet that produces feature maps very similar to those of the large-scale networks, thus allowing to better represent different scales while keeping the real-time inference capability. Furthermore, the performance of the modified YOLOv8 and YOLOv9 models were evaluated on a specifically selected set of lung cancer images, where the training stability and convergence was watched through loss and metric analyses. The altered YOLOv8 and YOLOv9 architectures exhibited a steady increase in performance measured by every metric, similar to the large increases in mean Average Precision. The EfficientNet-YOLOv8 model reached around 0.82 mAP@50 and 0.47 mAP@50–95 while the EfficientNet-YOLOv9 model reached about 0.87 mAP@50 and 0.50 mAP@50–95, thus, respectively, outperforming the standard versions. These findingsdemonstrate that YOLO with EfficientNet backbone is able to distinguish very well even small pulmonary lesions that are low contrast at the same time. Thus, our proposed method is a potential avenue toward the development of computer-aided systems that are accurate, robust, and computationally efficient in the detection of lung cancer for clinical decision support.

title: Graph Neural Networks with Dynamic Temporal Encoding for Large-Scale Social Network Analytics
authors: Naitik S T, Sridhar S K, Tanvir Habib Sardar, Gousia Thahniyath, Benaka Santhosha S, Kumar Dilip
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Large-scale social network analytics requires models that can simultaneously reason over complicated irregular graph topologies while managing changing temporal dynamics, but such models have approached the structural and temporal dimension in a way that treats them as separate concerns. This paper introduces DyTEGNN, a Dynamic Temporal Encoding Graph Neural Network framework that unifies continuous-time temporal embeddings with multi-relational graph convolutions for scalable social network analysis. DyTEGNN incorporates a Hawkes-process- inspired temporal event encoder, a heterogeneous message- passing scheme with edge-type-aware attention, and a hierarchical graph pooling module supporting million-node graphs through neighbor sampling. The framework is evaluated on three benchmark social network datasets — OGB-Twitter, Reddit-Threads, and DBLP-Citation — across four canonical tasks: node classification, link prediction, community detection, and influence maximization. DyTEGNN achieves macro F1-scores of 91.3%, 89.7%, and 88.4% on node classification, outperforming seven baselines by up to 7.8%. On link prediction, it achieves AUC of 0.974 on OGB-Twitter. Ablation studies confirm the role of temporal encoding (+4.1% F1) and heterogeneous attention (+3.2% F1). DyTEGNN scales sub-linearly, handling 50-million-edge graphs with 3.7x speedup over full-batch GNNs.

title: SM3ChainGuard: Time-Anchored Hash Chain on Blockchain for Tamper-Evident Multimodal Data in Elderly Care Environments
authors: Ningyang Tang, Yuxing Duan, Qingsong Li, Hang Cui, Mengchio Lin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Multimodal temporal data integrity is crucial for trustworthy post-incident auditing in smart elderly care environments, yet existing mechanisms often fail to preserve temporal context and cross-modal consistency against sophisticated tampering. This paper presents SM3ChainGuard, a tamper-evident framework that integrates the SM3 cryptographic hash with the ChainMaker blockchain to construct a provably time-anchored hash chain. The framework employs a three-layer hierarchical hashing—unimodal, multimodal aggregation, and temporal chaining—to cryptographically bind consecutive frames and ensure cross-camera consistency, with the root hash immutably anchored on the blockchain. Extensive evaluation on the OpenRoboCare dataset under 15 distinct attack types, including structural manipulations and realistic RGB forgeries, demonstrates that SM3ChainGuard achieves 100% detection with zero false positives. Frame-level detection attains an F1 score of 0.909 and a mean localization error of only 0.13 frames, while a single modified frame triggers a cascading inconsistency affecting 99.8% of subsequent frames. The chain proof incurs merely 0.9% storage overhead. Compared to conventional per-frame hashing and simple chaining, our framework offers superior detection accuracy and robust cross-modal protection, providing a scalable, regulation-compliant solution for end-to-end data integrity in smart elderly care environments.

title: Ray Tracing Enabled Residual Map for Indoor Localization of Non-Cooperative Interference Source
authors: Yuying Bian, Xiaona Li, Hang Zhang, Dong Wei, Shixiang Zhang, Zongning Gao
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Interference source localization is crucial for reliable indoor wireless communication. However, localizing interference sources remains challenging due to limited prior information. To address this issue, we propose Localization with Ray Tracing (LocRT), which combines ray tracing generated path loss radio maps with received signal strength measurements to construct a power consistency residual map for localizing interference sources. Moreover, we introduce a regional residual decision mechanism to reduce the effect of model errors. Experiments show that, with only three receivers, LocRT reduces the average localization error by 12.1% compared with ResNet and by 18.7% compared with LocUNet.

title: Octopus: Efficient Hypergraph Pattern Mining with Practical Processing-in-Memory Architecture
