title: Finite Time Combination Synchronization of Four-dimensional System Based on DNA Strand Displacement
authors: Yanfeng Wang, Yanbin Zhu, Junwei Sun, Yingcong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on NanoBioscience
abstract: With the application of DNA strand displacement (DSD) in the synchronization of chaotic systems within a finite time, the finite time synchronization of a single drive system and a single response system has been realized by DNA. Within this article, the finite time combination synchronization of four-dimensional systems is realized by using DSD technology. First, the four-dimensional system and the combination synchronization controllers are realized by using the designed strand displacement reaction. Second, the dynamic characteristics of the designed four-dimensional system are verified by simulation. Third, by cascading the designed four-dimensional system and the combination synchronization controllers, the combination synchronization of three four-dimensional systems in finite time is realized. Numerical simulation results show that DSD can realize combination synchronization of three four-dimensional systems within a finite time. This study highlights the potential of DSD techniques for achieving complex synchronization tasks in chaotic systems. The research in this article further expands the application prospects in security communication, biological computation and other fields.

title: Replay-Enhanced Adaptive Hybrid Intrusion Detection (REA-HID) for IoT Edge Gateways
authors: Kashmeera R, Prabhakar Krishnan, Hari N. N., Sethu Subramanian N, Kurunandan Jain
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) devices via edge gateways has become commonplace and has also opened the door for all sorts of security breaches; particularly, adversaries can now execute low-and-slow, timing-aware-attacks that are mimicking normal telemetry very closely even to the point of being undetectable by static intrusion detection systems (IDS). It must be said that previous studies have shown lighter model installations on Raspberry Pi-class gateways, but temporal realism, concept drift, and reproducibility issues remain. The proposed REA–HID system (Replay-Enhanced Adaptive Hybrid Intrusion Detection), an IDS that uses a light-weight model, features both deterministic and synthetic flow generators that produce benign telemetry and parametrically defined timing aware adversarial flows. The system implements an auditable Page Hinkley drift detector which uses replay buffer gated retraining to achieve secure system adaptation while combining a supervised MLP classifier with an OR-fusion system that merges reconstruction-based anomaly signals. The float16 TFLite model achieves an F1-score of 0.9792 for hybrid detection and 0.0417 for false positives on a controlled synthetic low-and-slow benchmark which tests the model on the prescribed hardware. The NF-BoT-IoT-v2 dataset undergoes zero-shot evaluation which shows major feature-distribution divergence proving the requirement for drift-aware adaptation. The system provides all components with deterministic manifests and drift logs which enable researchers to reproduce tests.

title: Research on Roof Crack Detection under Complex Illumination and Water Accumulation Interference
authors: Ruiheng Li, Zhihao Quan, Yi Di, Binghua Shi, Xiao Yang, Wanyu Qiu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address false crack detections caused by glare and waterlogging artifacts in roof waterproofing inspection, this paper proposes a roof crack artifact-suppression method with explicit interference modeling. A glare and waterlogging artifact suppression module is introduced to estimate interference-prone regions by jointly modeling color cues and local texture complexity. The confidence of candidate boxes is then adaptively recalibrated through soft spatial gating during inference. Experimental results show that the proposed method effectively reduces false detections and improves robustness under strong reflection and wet-surface conditions without significantly increasing model complexity. The method provides reliable visual support for intelligent roof crack inspection.

title: LIMCA: LLM for Automating Analog In-Memory Computing Architecture Design Exploration
authors: Deepak Vungarala, Md Hasibul Amin, Pietro Mercati, Arnob Ghosh, Arman Roohi, David Z. Pan, Ramtin Zand, Shaahin Angizi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: In this paper, we introduce LIMCA, a novel fine-tuning-free Large Language Model (LLM)-driven framework for automating the design and evaluation of In-Memory Computing (IMC) crossbar architectures. Unlike traditional approaches, where the manual, knowledge-intensive design process and the lack of high-quality circuit netlists have significantly constrained Design Space Exploration (DSE) and optimization to behavioral system-level tools, LIMCA employs a No-Human-In-Loop (NHIL) automated pipeline to generate and validate circuit netlists for SPICE simulations, eliminating manual intervention. LIMCA systematically explores the IMC design space by leveraging a structured dataset and LLM-based performance evaluation. Our experimental results on MNIST classification demonstrate that LIMCA successfully generates crossbar designs achieving ≥96% accuracy while maintaining a power consumption ≤3W, making this the first work in LLM-assisted IMC DSE. Compared to existing frameworks, LIMCA provides an automated, scalable, and hardware-aware solution, reducing design exploration time while ensuring user-constrained performance trade-offs. Artifacts are open-sourced at https://github.com/ACADLab/LIMCA.

title: GMS-Net: Global Mamba and Multi-Scale Convolution-Based Medical Image Segmentation Network
authors: Dengdi Sun, Longlong Liu, Shiwei Zhou, Bin Luo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Medical image segmentation is essential in intelligent healthcare systems for automated diagnosis. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) excel at extracting local patterns, yet they struggle to capture long-range contextual dependencies, whereas Mamba-based models handle long-range dependencies well but lack fine-grained detail extraction. Integrating local and global information remains challenging. To address this, we propose a module-level Mamba–CNN collaborative mechanism based on the GMS block (Global Mamba and Multi-Scale Convolution). In each encoder unit, the GMS module contains a global branch with stacked VSS blocks for long-range dependency modeling, and a local branch using multi-scale depthwise separable convolutions for fine feature extraction. The outputs of the two branches are first concatenated along the channel dimension and then integrated using a 1 × 1 convolution, with residual connections introduced to enhance training stability. Experimental results show that incorporating this module into different backbones consistently improves performance on the ISIC17, ISIC18, and CXRS datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of module-level local–global modeling.

title: Multi-Scenario Stochastic Programming-Based Distributed Cooperative Optimization for DER Aggregation Framework and Use-Based SoC Decomposition and Visualization of DER Operation
authors: Masao Shinji, Yutaka Iino, Tsunayoshi Ishii, Yasuhiro Hayashi, Satoshi Yamashita, Jumpei Doi, Kentaro Sakamoto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: As the mass deployment of low-voltage distributed energy resources (DERs) progresses toward achieving carbon neutrality by 2050, concerns over insufficient grid flexibility are growing, and aggregator-based business models that pool low-voltage DERs for market participation are attracting increasing attention. However, bidding strategies that proactively account for uncertainty in individual consumer photovoltaic output and demand are yet to be established. This study proposes a market bid quantity determination method that explicitly addresses the imbalance risk arising from such uncertainty using simultaneous multi-scenario optimization based on extreme scenarios. By decomposing the battery energy storage system state-of-charge into three operational functions—consumer self-consumption, market bidding, and imbalance mitigation—battery operations can be communicated transparently to individual consumers. Compared to the conventional Monte Carlo multi-scenario method, proposed method introducing the extreme scenario approach enables DERs operation planning and market biding strategies that account for worst-case scenarios with a reduced number of cases. Furthermore, the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM)-based distributed cooperative optimization framework was introduced to reduce optimization calculation. Based on a simulation, for a representative day, the proposed method reduced imbalance costs by ~34% and improved the overall net balance by ~1.3%, relative to the conventional method. An annual evaluation revealed that 67% of the improvement attributable to dynamic confidence interval selection stems from managing exceptional high-cost days concentrated in November–February (the boreal winter). These simulation-based results demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively balances market revenue maximization and imbalanced risk management while respecting individual consumer constraints, thus establishing it as a practical framework for managing DER fleets.

title: An AI-Driven Hybrid BiLSTM-Attention and Ensemble Learning Framework for Threat Detection in Smart Grids
authors: Shaik Rajiya, Shaik Thahaseen, Pidaparthi Trinadh Reddy, Shaik Mohammed Uzair, N.V. Chinnasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: However, the increasing adoption of artificial intelligence and Large Language Model (LLM)-aided tools in smart power grid monitoring systems has also introduced new cyber-physical and semantic security threats. In response to these new challenges, this paper proposes a hybrid threat detection framework that combines Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks with a multi-head attention mechanism and ensemble learning. The proposed framework is capable of simultaneously examining electrical operational data and LLM interaction data, thus providing a comprehensive monitoring system for both physical and AI-assisted attack surfaces. A synthetic dataset was created to represent five operational scenarios: normal operation, False Data Injection (FDIA) attack, Semantic Divergence (SDA) attack, Denial-of-Service (DoS) attack, and Privacy Invasion attack, where each sample is expressed as a 20-step multivariate time series with eight features. The stacked BiLSTM-attention model is able to extract essential temporal information, and the Random Forest and Gradient Boosting classifiers improve the model's robustness. The proposed framework is shown to have 92-96% accuracy with excellent precision, recall, and ROC-AUC values in a 10,000-sample experiment, thus validating the framework's effectiveness in real-time smart grid security applications. This framework provides an efficient and scalable solution for protecting AI-enabled smart grid systems from emerging hybrid threats.

title: Deep Learning-based Multi-Channel CNN Framework for Early Detection of Thyroid Cancer from CT Images
authors: Mubeena Anjum, Mohit Arora
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Recent publications of the Global Burden of Disease project show that there has been a growing development in the incidence of thyroid cancer across the globe necessitating the proper and effective early diagnostic techniques. Deep learning methods have been demonstrated to have great potential in medical image analysis and computer-aided diagnosis. The present research suggests a multi-channel convolutional neural network (CNN) system to classify multi-class thyroid disease by using the paired computed tomography (CT) images of the right and left lobes. The given architecture brings together tomography-based characteristics, as well as multi-scale spatial representations, by merging features channel-wisely. Different combinations of convolutional kernel sizes were fairly tested to improve the performance in feature extraction and classification. It has been shown that the proposed model, with a kernel size of 1 and 8, has excellent performance in terms of accuracy (93.21 +-0.048) and recall (89.52 +- 0.046), specificity (93.65 +- 0.001), and F1-score (91.70 +- 0.058), which is superior to baseline architectures. The performance values are presented in the form of mean +- standard deviation that is determined by 10-fold stratified cross-validation. The results suggest that the presented framework offers the strong and generalized performance in the process of multi-class thyroid disease detection on the basis of bilateral CT scanning.

title: Deep Fake Face Detection using Multi-Domain Feature Fusion and Ensemble Learning
authors: Jackson J, P.Jayanthi, Rizwana Banu H, Dr.A.M.Ravishankkar, Gomathi N.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: As a result of the increasing sophistication of deep-fake generation techniques, there has been an urgent need to address the authenticity of digital content. The purpose of this research is to present a new deep fake facial-image detection model based on hybrid machine learning, using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) in combination with ensemble classifiers (random forest, extreme gradient boosting and feedforward neural networks). A combined approach of using both spatial and frequency-domain features will help identify the subtle artifacts created as a result of manipulations. In addition to the new hybrid deep fake detection system, this research also created an easy-to-use interface that allows users to view the results of near real-time detection and to access both the predicted category of deep fake and a confidence score regarding that prediction. Experimental evaluations of the hybrid model on several benchmark data sets demonstrate superior performance relative to previous-to-this research methods, with an accuracy of 96.3% and mean/median precision and recall values exceeding 95%. Together, these results demonstrate that an increase in the number of features extracted from multiple domains using an ensemble-learning approach increases the robustness of deep-fake image detection. The findings of this study provide an efficient and effective solution for detecting and countering deep-fake threats related to digital forensics, validation of social media content, and cybersecurity.

title: 310Gbps Transmission and Centimeter-Level Ranging Photonic THz ISAC System
authors: Dayu Shi, Weiqi Lu, Yuhao Fang, Puzhen Yuan, Haojie Zhu, Lei Pang, William Shieh
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: In this paper, we demonstrate a 300-GHz dual-polarization (DP) IM/DD photonic THz integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) prototype. The system reuses burst-estimated per-subcarrier SNRs for both probabilistic shaping and distance ranging. Unlike radar-style or learning-based THz ISAC schemes that require pre-training, specific sensing pilots, or extra hardware architecture, we propose a one-reference, training-free ranging method that maps the aggregated burst SNR statistic to distance via a calibrated path-loss model and lightweight Newton refinement. Experiments validate a peak data rate of 310.4 Gb/s (33 GBaud, NGMI ≈ 0.88) corresponding to 8.18-bit/s/Hz spectral efficiency, while achieving centimeter-level ranging across 0.3–1.0 m with a maximum error of 2.7 cm.

title: Autonomous Pipeline Inspection Using Lightweight Vision-Based UAV Tracking_supp2-3693978.mp4
authors: Praful Hambarde
publish: N/A
abstract: Long-range pipelines are indispensable infrastructures requiring continuous and rigorous inspections for structural health monitoring. Multi-copter (UAV) inspections face critical challenges including low-altitude flight constraints, limited computational resources for integrated visual perception and navigation control for the purpose of continuous inspection in real-time. The present work proposes an autonomous vision-based UAV low altitude inspection framework for industrial pipeline inspection. The proposed framework seamlessly integrates three innovative components: a lightweight deep learning architecture (MLA-Net) for robust pipeline segmentation, a vision-based control strategy for smooth tracking, and a sliding mode controller for real-time navigation. MLA-Net combines Cascaded Feature Extractor (CFE) blocks for hierarchical feature learning with Multi-Scale Receptive (MSR) blocks for multi-scale environmental adaption, achieving efficient pipe detection under varying conditions. The vision-based control strategy processes MLA-Net outputs through perspective projection and oriented rectangle extraction to coordinate yaw-lateral control, while a non-linear forward velocity function prevents overshooting during pipeline turns. The sliding mode controller fuses visual features with altitude data for autonomous navigation across complex pipeline networks. Extensive validation through simulation and real-time outdoor experiments demonstrates superior performance across diverse pipeline configurations including intersections and multipipe scenarios. The proposed MLA-Net achieves 0.8166 IoU while operating at ≈27 FPS with only 0.202 GFLOPs and 0.178M, significantly outperforming existing methods in computational efficiency. The integrated framework enables stable autonomous tracking across various pipeline geometries, providing a practical solution with enhanced accuracy and computational efficiency for resource-constrained UAV applications. Source code available at Autonomous vision-based pipline tracking by UAV

title: Vulnerability-Guided Node Injection Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Jining Zhang, Shuang Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have been widely adopted for graph-structured learning tasks, but their robustness to adversarial perturbations remains a major concern. Compared with graph modification attacks, node injection attacks are more practical because they introduce a small number of malicious nodes without directly altering the original graph. In this paper, we propose Vulnerability-Guided Node Injection Attack (VGNIA), a black-box and untargeted attack framework that improves attack effectiveness while preserving global structural consistency. VGNIA first estimates node vulnerability from a surrogate model by combining predictive uncertainty and prediction margin, and then selects class-diverse yet fragile target neighbors through a score-guided strategy. To generate realistic injected nodes, we constrain their features within the empirical range of the clean graph and further optimize them with selective homophily-aware regularization, which allows stronger perturbations on influential dimensions while preserving similarity on the others. Experiments on four benchmark datasets and six victim GNNs show that VGNIA consistently outperforms representative node injection baselines under the same attack budget.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning and Machine Learning Framework for Multimodal Skin Lesion Diagnosis
authors: Jayamangala Sristi, M.Poojitha, B.Snehalatha, K.Vyshnavi, K.Jayanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Classification of skin lesions has a direct impact on the prognosis of patients and is important for early detection and accurate diagnosis of skin diseases. By using complex feature representations, deep learning-based techniques have shown great potential in automated skin lesion analysis. However, diagnostic performance is hindered when using single-modality images. To improve lesion classification and detection, this work presents a unique transfer learning framework that incorporates multimodal feature extraction techniques. The classification framework demonstrated state-of-the-art performance, achieving an accuracy of 99.7% with Feature and Feature Extraction-Support Vector Machines (FE-SVM). Advanced object detection algorithms were used to detect lesions; YOLOv8 had the highest average precision (MAP). Multimodal data and transfer learning has a huge effect on the identification accuracy and classification flexibility. The framework offers an accurate and scalable dermatological diagnostic solution because it proves the usefulness of deep feature extraction and transfer learning methods in analyzing skin lesions. This technique enhances clinical decision making and skill in early detection of disease because it allows automated and real-time evaluation of skin lesions.

title: An Explainable Hybrid CNN-Transformer Meta-Ensemble for Chest X-Ray Diagnosis: Automated Clinical Narratives and Knowledge-Grounded Patient Communication
authors: K. Visala, Rakesh Bhagavan Vajrapu, Tekkali Kasi Naga Venkata Ajay Kumar, Vatsavai Rahul Satyanarayana Jagapathi Varma, Varuvuri Prasanna Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Automated detection of pulmonary abnormalities in Chest X-Rays (CXRs) is hampered by single-architecture blind spots and a growing health literacy gap between clinical reports and patient understanding. This paper presents an end-to-end Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) to resolve these bottlenecks using an explainable Hybrid Meta-Ensemble. Our method mathematically interpolates localized inductive biases of deep convolutional networks (DenseNet-121, ConvNeXt V2) with global geometric self-attention of a Vision Transformer (MaxViT) with a dynamic Logistic Regression Meta Learner. We used a large dataset of 20,805 chest X-ray images from different sources and trained an ensemble model utilizing a "Clean Cooldown" calibration approach and the ensemble has achieved state-of-the-art multi-class accuracy of 89.33 and a macro-sensitivity of 89.53. Also, the system ensures clinical transparency through Next-Gen Grad-CAM++ spatial heatmaps. More importantly, diagnostic results are initiated by an enterprise-level, Azure OpenAI-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline, which can simultaneously produce standard radiologist reports and non-technical patient stories written with jargon and on an empathetic tone. This highly deployable open sourced paradigm dramatically increases the accuracy of its diagnosis and the health literacy of its patients.

title: Identification of Inconsistent Recommendations Based on Feature Matching and Screening of Recommendation Opinions
authors: Lei Kang, Jinguang Gu, Yinghui Jin, Yongbo Wang, Feng Gao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Version, regional, and conclusion differences in clinical practice guidelines (CPGs) cause inconsistent recommendations. A structured modeling method maps recommendations to multi-field representation. Logistic regression-based weighted scoring selects candidate pairs to reduce data scale and annotation cost; structured-text semantic fusion fine-tunes LLMs for fine-grained recommendation relationship identification. Experiments show the method outperforms baselines in screening efficiency and classification performance, supporting clinical guideline knowledge integration and intelligent decision support.

title: Locatability and Locatability Robustness of Visual Variables in Single Target Localization
authors: Wei Wei, Miguel A. Nacenta, Michelle F. Miranda, Charles Perin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Finding a particular object in a display is important for viewers in many visualizations, for example, when reacting to brushing or to a highlighted object. This can be enabled by making the target object different in one of the visual variables that determine the object's appearance; for example, by changing its color or size. Certain interpretations of the visual search literature have promoted the view that using visual variables such as hue—often labeled as preattentive—would make the target object automatically “popout,” implying that an object can be located almost instantly, regardless of the number of objects in the display. In this paper we present a study that serves as a bridge between the extensive visual search literature and visualization, establishing empirical base measurements for the localization task. By testing displays with up to hundreds of objects, we are able to show that none of the common visual variables is immune to the increase in the number of objects. We also provide the first empirically informed comparisons between visual variables for this task in the context of visualization, and show how different visual variables have varying robustness with respect to two additional dimensions: the location of the target and the overall visual arrangement (layout). A free copy of this paper and all supplemental materials are available on our online repository: https://osf.io/z68ak/overview.

title: P3S-DRL: A Personalized Privacy Protection Scheme Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning and Multi-factor Optimization in Spatial Crowdsourcing
authors: Hui Wang, Zhenquan Ge, Peiqian Liu, Kun Liu
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Spatial crowdsourcing (SC) recruits online workers to perform geographically relevant tasks based on their real time locations, which exposes sensitive location information. Workers’ heterogeneous privacy needs make uniform protection ineffective and degrade service quality. To address these issues, this paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning-based personalized privacy protection scheme for spatial crowdsourcing. First, the proposed scheme constructs a personalized privacy protection model based on spatiotemporal features, which dynamically assigns personalized privacy protection intensity to different users by quantitatively analyzing the association between factors such as location, semantic sensitivity, access frequency, and access duration with individual privacy needs. Secondly, deep reinforcement learning algorithms are employed to adjust the model dynamically according to changes in the environment, thereby achieving an adaptive balance between privacy protection and task completion efficiency, and a bidirectional privacy-preserving mechanism combining Local Differential Privacy (LDP) and homomorphic encryption is designed to protect the privacy of both workers and requesters. Again, to achieve a more comprehensive task allocation strategy, this scheme proposes a multi-factor optimized task assignment mechanism that combines parameters such as worker reputation and task response latency. Finally, compared with the existing schemes, the proposed scheme can maintain a high accuracy rate for task assignment while ensuring personalized privacy protection, thereby improving the efficiency and reliability of the crowdsourcing system.

title: Predicting RANDU Using Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Sara Boancă
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: RANDU is one of the most famous Pseudorandom Number Generators (PRNGs) in history. Although statistical tests have long asserted its vulnerabilities, Machine Learning (ML) techniques are yet to achieve equally solid results for dismantling its security. Statistically, it is well-known that RANDU assumes a number of regularities. The most famous regularity lays in the distinct configuration of the RANDU 3-dimensional output lattice. This lattice forms 15 parallel planes and stays at the basis of the generator insecurity. The present paper addresses the problem of approximating RANDU using a ML approach by exploiting its lattice regularity. The 15 RANDU parallel planes are regarded as components of a non-linear separation which is learned through binary classification: points located between the planes are sampled and categorized by means of an Artificial Neural Network (ANN). The ANN-inferred separation coincides to the RANDU 3-dimensional lattice. Due to this property, the separation is later employed by an approximation algorithm which requires only 2 previous RANDU outputs to estimate the value of the following one. This RANDU prediction approach obtains a 0.0636 MAE, 0.0271 MSE and 0.1648 RMSE score. The quality of the approximations dismantles the security of RANDU from a ML numerical approximation standpoint. The present work is intended to complement the results for statistical approaches and cement RANDU insecurity from a ML perspective.

title: Numerical Study on Hydrodynamic Performance of Bio-Inspired Turbine Blade using CFD
authors: Md. Mashud Karim, Mahin Hasan Moon
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: We present time-accurate Unsteady Reynolds-Averaged Navier-Stokes (URANS) Shear Stress Transport (SST $k-\omega$) simulations to assess bio-inspired leading-edge tubercles (LET), trailing-edge serrations (TES), and a combined LET+TES modification on a NACA0018 turbine blade. The Computations used a high-resolution body-fitted mesh (production target $\approx 24.0 \times 10^{6}$ cells; comparative fine mesh $2.385 \times 10^{6}$ cells, 15 prism layers, first-cell $y^{+} \approx 1$) and conservative Richardson/GCI uncertainty estimates Grid Convergence Index $\left(\mathbf{G C I}_{C L} \approx 4.7 \%, \mathbf{G C I}_{C D} \approx 25.1 \%\right)$. Across a $0^{\circ}-30^{\circ}$ angle-ofattack sweep (time-resolved metrics: mean, RMS, and spectral diagnostics of forces and moments; $R e \approx 3.9 \times 10^{5}$), LET generates persistent crest-aligned streamwise vortices that reenergize the suction side, while TES fragments the trailing shear to accelerate wake recovery. The combined LET+TES geometry preserves attachment to higher AoA and yields the best trade-off: 30-40 % higher post-stall lift and $\approx 15-20 \%$ greater $L / D$ versus the smooth baseline. Validation at $\alpha=15^{\circ}$ captures the onset of the stall within $\approx 2^{\circ}$, and transparent data filtering and sensitivity checks are reported. These results provide a viable, validated approach to enhance turbine blade off-design robustness and motivate focused mesh refinement and rotor-scale follow-up investigations.

title: Spatial-Semantic Alignment and Change-aware Network for Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning_supp1-3690564.pdf
authors: Qi Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote sensing image change captioning aims to generate natural language descriptions of dynamic changes between bitemporal images. This task faces challenges of aligning high-level semantics with sparse change regions and modeling long-range dependencies in diverse change patterns. To alleviate these limitations, we propose the spatial-semantic alignment and change-aware network, dubbed SACNet. It introduces a semantic-spatial collaborative encoding module synergizing a CNN-based spatial encoder with a CLIP-based semantic encoder, using precise spatial cues to refine global semantic features. A change-driven adaptive feature calibration module emphasizes discriminative change signals via intertemporal feature dissimilarity modeling, suppressing background noise and highlighting key changes. In addition, a multipath state space model (SSM)-guided spatial representation module is designed to capture diverse complex spatial change topologies. Extensive experiments on the LEVIR-CC and WHU-CDC datasets demonstrate that SACNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, generating both high-precision textual descriptions and approximate change masks. Code will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/CVer-Yang/SACNet</uri>

title: YOLO-MSFF: An Improved YOLO11-Based Multispectral Feature Fusion Method for Object Detection
authors: Changxin Guo, Ting Zhao, Zhaoxia Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Multispectral object detection remains challenging due to inefficient feature fusion, limited cross-modal interaction, and performance degradation under complex illumination and adverse weather conditions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a lightweight multispectral object detection network termed YOLO-MSFF, built upon the YOLOv11 architecture. The proposed framework adopts a dual-branch RGB–IR backbone to process visible and infrared modalities separately and incorporates modality-specific feature enhancement and fusion modules. In the visible branch, a Multi-scale Feature Reconstruction (MFR) module is introduced to enhance texture and structural representation. In the infrared branch, an Euler-enhanced Global Grouped Coordinate Attention (EGGCA) module is designed to improve directional perception and global contextual modeling of thermal radiation features. Furthermore, a Multi-head Cross-Attention Fusion (MCAF) module is employed to enable effective cross-modal feature interaction, thereby reducing feature redundancy and improving semantic consistency. Experimental results demonstrate that, with only 1.75% additional parameters (2.75×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>), YOLO-MSFF consistently outperforms YOLOv11-RGBT on RGBTDronePerson, KAIST, VEDAI, and FLIR datasets, improving mAP50 by 3.68%, 1.13%, 5.88% and 2.11%, and mAP50:95 by 5.48%, 2.83%, 5.27% and 1.53%, respectively. Furthermore, YOLO-MSFF outperforms the state-of-the-art Fusion-Mamba method on the M3FD dataset, with an increase of 2.05% in mAP50 and a 10.17% improvement in mAP50:95.These results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an effective balance between detection accuracy and model complexity, showing strong potential for real-time multispectral perception applications.

title: VulD-SRCM: A Source Code Vulnerability Detection Model Based on Semantic Residual Compensation
authors: Mengjia Li, Weiyu Dong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: To address the limitations of vulnerability detection methods based on deep learning, specifically the oversmoothing of representations and the loss of fine grained semantic features that arise during multi-layer Transformer encoding, this paper proposes VulD-SRCM, a source code vulnerability detection model grounded in semantic residual compensation. The proposed model employs GraphCodeBERT as its foundational encoder, achieving multimodal code representation through the joint modeling of token sequences and data flow graphs. Furthermore, a semantic residual compensation module is introduced to mitigate the phenomenon of deep representational convergence, thereby preserving critical discriminative features. Experimental evaluations conducted on three publicly available vulnerability detection benchmarks, namely Big-Vul, Devign, and Real-Vul, demonstrate that VulD-SRCM consistently outperforms existing baseline methods across multiple evaluation metrics, including accuracy, recall, F1-score, and false positive rate, exhibiting robust generalization capability and considerable practical utility.

title: Structure-Aware Knowledge Composition: Enhancing Multi-hop Question Generation via Entity Relation Injection
authors: Yana Lv, Lang Yang, Xiuli Du
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Multi-hop question answering (QA) tasks have long been hindered by data sparsity, which renders language models prone to learning spurious correlations within the data. To mitigate this issue, existing studies primarily focus on enhancing question diversity through content planning or paraphrasing. However, these methods are often restricted to generating simple questions and struggle to effectively integrate crucial knowledge across documents. Furthermore, the reliance on implicit semantic modeling results in a lack of logical coherence in the generated knowledge compositions. In this paper, we propose Structure-Aware Knowledge Composition (SAKC) to improve both the generation quality and diversity of multi-hop questions by sampling diverse and logically coherent knowledge compositions. Specifically, SAKC formulates knowledge composition selection as a sentence-level sequence pre diction task. It incorporates triples extracted from sentences into the input representations as structured prompts, enabling the model to perceive explicit entity relations. Additionally, we design as tructure-aware contrastive loss function that imposes stronger penalties on distractor sentences that share entities but are semantic ally irrelevant, thereby effectively suppressing hallucinations. During the inference phase, the model employs a stochastic decoding strategy to strike a balance between selection accuracy and the diversity of knowledge compositions. Experimental results demonstrate that SAKC outperforms existing baselines on both the diversity metric Pairwise-BLEU and the generation quality metrics BL EU-4 and ROUGE-L.

title: YOLOv8-based Deep Learning for Ocean Plastic Detection
authors: S P Sreeja, V Asha, Meenakshi J H, Manoj G L, Neel Johnson, Arpana Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The rising numbers of plastic in the oceans and coastlines is an increasing global crisis. Plastic affects not only marine wildlife, but affects all of us. This study describes the use of a deep learning object detection system using the YOLOv8 algorithm to detect and identify the different types of plastic in the ocean based on real-time images. Traditional methods of monitoring plastic in the ocean have limited capability to provide reliable, scalable, and cost-effective means to locate and quantify plastic in the ocean. This detection system will provide these capabilities in addition to providing high accuracy. The model is trained and validated on a data set containing more than 2,000 annotated photographs of different types of marine debris. The results of this research shows that the proposed detection system performs with a high degree of accuracy in the application of large scale monitoring of marine debris by measuring precision, recall, and mAP performance in terms of detecting marine debris.

title: Robust Field-Focused Digital Twin for Real-Time Water-Quality Estimation and Alerting
authors: Hariharan R, Subash N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Sensors and communications should be model-driven in the digital twin that itself accurately represents control logic, so the treatment plant has accurate sanitation evaluations for assimilation decisions (and alarm notifications) even store-and-forward hours later. This work presents a high-fidelity and hardware-realistic digital twin framework to model probe physics, temperature-dependent conductance, reference-electrode drift, telemetry loss, fouling, biofilm-growth mechanism, and the maintenance cycles in order to quantify estimator and alarm performance under normal operational stressors. The twin closes a research gap by integrating a parametric state-space model with a hybrid assimilation toolchain: lightweight Kalman updates for near-real-time denoising, Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoothing for retrospective optimization, and robust residual screening for outlier rejection/gap filling. An alert engine uses standards-aligned thresholds, hysteresis, and time-over-threshold (TOT) rules (with continuous monitoring of residuals using Cumulative SUM [CUSUM] statistics at individual sensor nodes), as well as residual-driven CUSUM and Bayesian online change-point detection to trade off early warning versus nuisance reduction. Results for semisynthetic scenarios calibrated to reservoir, treatment plant, and wellfield conditions are presented, showing reductions of 48.6% in RMSE for pH and 42.3% for TDS; a decrease of the false alarm rate from 12.4% to 3.1%; and a mean reduction of detection delay as high as 38%. The platform consists of a reusable field-focused testbed for algorithm selection, calibration planning, operational SLO development, and operator training guidance.

title: Security Governance Methods and Quantitative Evaluation for Enterprise SMS and Verification Code Systems
authors: Guangbin Gao, Runda Gao, Chenghe Lu, Runhang Gao, Yihang Kuang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This study analyzes security governance methods for communication systems addressing security risks in enterprise SMS and verification code services, including identity spoofing, malicious requests, and resource abuse. By introducing identity verification, permission control, and routing-level policy management into the communication process, it achieves identification and isolation of abnormal communication behaviors. Under mixed traffic test conditions with abnormal requests accounting for 5%–20% of total traffic, quantitative evaluations were conducted on the system's abnormal request interception rate, false interception rate, and communication performance changes. Results demonstrate that this governance approach maintains an abnormal request interception rate above 95% while keeping the false positive rate for legitimate requests below 2%. The average system response delay increase does not exceed 5%, achieving a balanced outcome between security and communication efficiency.

title: Generative AI-Driven Adaptive Consistency Maintenance for Distributed Simulation Systems
authors: Menghan Liu, Gangyi Ding
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Conventional consistency maintenance methods in distributed simulations degrade under unstable networks due to fixed rules and limited adaptability. To address this, we propose an AI-driven, two-layer adaptive consistency framework. In Tier-1, lightweight diagnostic tools use rule-based checking for immediate anomaly detection and initiate a protective "Soft-pause." In Tier-2, an edge-deployed Large Language Model acts as a decision engine, analyzing multi-dimensional network metrics to generate adaptive repair strategies via structured JSON. Extensive experiments under dynamic network degradation demonstrate our framework achieves a 98.6% Detection Accuracy and over 93% Consistency Maintenance Rate, significantly outperforming traditional baselines in preventing system crashes despite seconds-level repair latency.

title: To Adapt or Not to Adapt, That is the Ski Question
authors: Yuvaraj Chesetti, Prashant Pandey
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: LLM-Triaged Vulnerability Analysis for Compiled DNNs
authors: Minghui Li, Yang Li, Changchun Yin, Mingwei Li, Liming Fang
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Transmission-Line Circuit Modeling and Simulation Through the Theory of Periodic Sequence (TPS)
authors: Anlan Liu, Ke Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: The increasing bandwidth (BW), dynamic behavior, and reconfigurability of emerging wireless systems, such as future 6G platforms, have led to increasingly complex periodic excitations, posing significant challenges to conventional time-domain (TD) and frequency-domain (FD) circuit simulation paradigms. In this article, a transmission-line (TL)-based analytical-numerical algorithm is proposed for circuit simulation under arbitrary time-periodic excitation. Unlike traditional TD and FD methods with inherent limitations in efficiently handling such signals, the proposed approach employs the theory of periodic sequence (TPS) to reformulate the TL telegrapher’s equations in a discrete transform domain, referred to as the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$w$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-domain. Applying the discrete Fourier transform (DFT) directly to the governing equations achieves intrinsic diagonalization, establishing an eigen-based solution space that represents the spectral structure of a periodic signal. Consequently, the transformed equations are fully decoupled and solved in parallel. Voltages and currents are solved in the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$w$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-domain and transformed back to the TD to recover periodic transient responses. Mathematical derivations, parameter analyses, and validation examples with various time-periodic waveforms are presented. Compared with conventional TD and FD simulations, the proposed method significantly reduces computational complexity without loss of accuracy, making it well suited for signal distortion analysis in high-speed radio frequency (RF) circuits and broadband transmission applications.

title:
Striations and Species in Low Pressure CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    and CH
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    Dielectric Barrier Discharge Plasmas
authors: Riley A. Yager, Andrei Stanishevsky
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: The striations in nonthermal dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) plasmas of molecular gases such as CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> remain underexplored compared to the well-documented behavior in air or noble gas DC discharges. In this study, striation formation was systematically investigated as a function of gas pressure (0.1–20 torr) and applied AC frequency (25.5–40kHz) in two different DBD reactor configurations. Optical emission spectroscopy (OES) was employed to correlate plasma species with striation characteristics. Striation size and separation were found to increase with decreasing pressure, while elevated pressures above ~20 torr suppressed striations and led to the onset of uniform glow or unstable discharges, depending on the reactor configuration and gas. CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> plasmas indicated formation of dissociation products such as CO and O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> or carbonaceous deposits, while CH<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub> plasmas predominantly generated CH and H2. Electrical characterization using Lissajous figures, voltage-current (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) waveforms, and several power calculation methods revealed that frequency-dependent changes in plasma impedance strongly affect discharge development and striation stability. These results demonstrate a direct relationship between electrical behavior, energy deposition, and the spatial structuring of the plasma.

title: An Air-Gap Field Modulation Model for Hybrid Stepper Motors Considering Magnetic Saturation
authors: Yu-Chen Kuo, Min-Fu Hsieh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Hybrid stepper motors (HSMs) are widely used in industrial automation and precision positioning due to their accurate motion control. However, their performance is strongly affected by nonlinear magnetic saturation, which is insufficiently addressed in conventional analytical models. In modern HSM designs, reduced air-gap lengths and high-energy permanent magnets significantly intensify step-tooth saturation, rendering the infinite core permeability assumption in conventional field modulation theory increasingly inadequate. This work presents an air-gap field modulation model for HSMs and develops a correction method employing a saturation-adjusted MMF modification curve to predict the air-gap flux density under tooth saturation by explicitly accounting for the magnetic circuits of both the air gap and stator/rotor teeth. Furthermore, the structural assumptions associated with the three-dimensional characteristics of HSMs are verified through finite-element analysis. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model accurately represents nonlinear magnetic saturation effects in HSMs, providing a more comprehensive analytical framework for future HSM design and applications.

title: Non-singular Fast Terminal Sliding Mode Controller Design for USV Based on the Predefined-Time Observer and Neural Network
authors: Yaodong Wang, Yibo Zhang, Gang Cao, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Wenlong Feng, Di Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a control framework integrated with a predefined-time observer and a neural network for unmanned surface vehicle (USV) trajectory-tracking. To achieve rapid estimation of the system state, a predefined-time observer (PTO) has been designed to efficiently estimate and compensate for trajectory errors. Subsequent, a neural network estimator is designed to estimate the uncertainty and disturbance set of the USV. Then, a control law is constructed using the non-singular fast terminal sliding mode control (NFTSMC) method by integrating the predefined-time observer and the neural network (NN). This approach overcomes the singularity issue and accelerates the state convergence speed of the system at different stages. Finally, the stability of the system is proved using Lyapunov stability theory, and the effectiveness of the method is verified by a simulation example. The results show that the control scheme ensures that the tracking error converges to zero in a predefined-time and to have improved tracking accuracy and control performance.

title: Uncertainty Quantification in Detection Transformers: Object-Level Calibration and Image-Level Reliability_supp1-3686715.pdf
authors: Navid Azizan
publish: N/A
abstract: DEtection TRansformer (DETR) and its variants have emerged as promising architectures for object detection, offering an end-to-end prediction pipeline. In practice, however, DETRs generate hundreds of predictions that far outnumber the actual objects present in an image. This raises a critical question: which of these predictions could be trusted? This is particularly important for safety-critical applications, such as in autonomous vehicles. Addressing this concern, we provide empirical and theoretical evidence that predictions within the same image play distinct roles, resulting in varying reliability levels. Our analysis reveals that DETRs employ an optimal “specialist strategy”: one prediction per object is trained to be well-calibrated , while the remaining predictions are trained to suppress their foreground confidence to near zero, even when maintaining accurate localization. We show that this strategy emerges as the loss-minimizing solution to the Hungarian matching algorithm, fundamentally shaping DETRs' outputs. While selecting the well-calibrated predictions is ideal, they are unidentifiable at inference time. This means that any post-processing algorithm—used to identify trustworthy predictions—poses a risk of outputting a set of predictions with mixed calibration levels. Therefore, practical deployment necessitates a joint evaluation of both the model's calibration quality and the effectiveness of the post-processing algorithm. However, we demonstrate that existing metrics like average precision and expected calibration error are inadequate for this task. To address this issue, we further introduce Object-level Calibration Error (OCE), which evaluates calibration by aggregating predictions per ground-truth object rather than per prediction. This object-centric design penalizes both retaining suppressed predictions and missed ground truth foreground objects, making OCE suitable for both evaluating models and identifying reliable prediction subsets. Finally, we present a post hoc uncertainty quantification (UQ) framework that predicts per-image model accuracy.

title: Radio-Frequency Power Amplifiers in MRI Systems: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Mert Karademir, Oguzhan Kizilbey, Baki Karaboce
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The radio-frequency power amplifier (RFPA) serves as one of the critical subsystems in Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), directly influencing the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and overall image quality. This paper provides a comprehensive review of recent advancements in RFPA technology, synthesized from a systematic analysis of 21 selected studies spanning from 1.5-T clinical systems to 11.7-T ultra-high-field (UHF) research platforms. The review covers device technologies, architectures, and control/linearization methods. The review discusses the transition from silicon LDMOS architectures to wide-bandgap devices, particularly gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC), which enable higher power density and improved efficiency. The review highlights the shift toward modular design strategies, such as 15-kW and 64-kW arrays, and the integration of sophisticated digital control mechanisms including Digital Pre-Distortion, Frequency-Offset Cartesian Feedback, and order-reduced state-space controllers for gradient subsystems. In addition, several studies investigate switch-mode power amplifiers and delta-sigma modulation as enabling technologies for portable, low-field MRI scanners. By evaluating diverse power combining topologies, ranging from coaxial Wilkinson to lumped-element Gysel designs, and robust safety interlock protocols, this paper delineates the technical trade-offs between linearity, thermal management, and physical footprint. The review concludes with a comparative summary of published RFPA designs and their reported performance.

title: Systematic Fraction Guided Learning for Neural Partial Differential Equation Solvers
authors: Mahboubeh Molavi-Arabshahi, Khalid Sumiea Munshid
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Fractional differential equations (FDEs) have gained increasing attention due to their ability to model systems with long memory and nonlocal dynamics, which pose significant challenges for conventional numerical simulation. Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising approach for solving such equations; however, standard PINN architectures often struggle to accurately represent fractal and nonlocal behaviors. This work introduces a systematically integrated fractional-guided learning framework that embeds fractal calculus directly into the architecture and loss formulation of physics-informed neural networks. The proposed method is evaluated on benchmark problems and practical applications to assess its inference capability. The numerical experiments demonstrate improved accuracy and convergence behavior relative to selected baseline methods in terms of better accuracy and converging rates when solving problems related to fractal diffusion-transport equations as against other traditional methods of physics-assisted neural networks. The proposed framework demonstrates strong generalization capability to various demonstrated to achieve accuracies exceeding 99% on selected benchmark problems mechanics of fracture and pollutant flow modeling in hydrology as observed with improved capacity in capturing nonlocal behaviour and long memory solution. Originated in the integration of an approach for the implementation of fractal principles on philosophy guided machine learning, there emerge therefore improvements with respect to formulation accuracy and also set the grounds for prospective methodology either in due course to be applied in the various fields of science and engineering in which fractal principles promise major advancement toward bridging a very considerable gap between fractal modeling and machine learning.
