title: LECY-Net: Joint Low-light Image Enhancement and Classification Y-Net
authors: Choon Chen Lim, Yuen Peng Loh, Lai-Kuan Wong, Ching-Chun Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Research on enhancing images captured in ill-light environments has become common nowadays, to improve the quality and illumination of low-light images (LLIs). Nonetheless, most of the approaches emphasize solely on LLI enhancement, ignoring the influence of the enhanced images on high-level computer vision tasks. Thereby, we tackle this issue by proposing a Y-Net architecture, namely Low-light image Enhancement and Classification Y-Net (LECY-Net), to execute the LLI enhancement and classification tasks concurrently while also serving to explore the potential correlation between these interconnected tasks. The integrated framework offers a sharable backbone that extracts the common features for the successive enhancement and classification branches, promoting feature sharing while efficiently optimizing both tasks. Meanwhile, the proposed network is further optimized by integrating a supplementary convolutional block (SCB), to suppress some inevitable image noises. Several benchmark datasets, including LOL, Microsoft COCO, and ExDark datasets, are applied to appraise the robustness of our model. The findings highlight that our model offers an effective solution for perceptual quality enhancement in comparison to other standalone LLI enhancement methods. When compared to the only joint enhancement and classification network that also generates simultaneous outputs, our strategy presents its clear advantage in both enhancement and classification fields, particularly on the COCO dataset. Additionally, ablation studies justified that the integrated network is advantageous for establishing a correlation between visual appearance enhancements and object classifications, while substantially boosting their performances.

title: Dynamic Trust-Driven Access Control Model for Cloud Security
authors: Badveli Jeevana, Konereddy Jasrini, Meram Hemanth Sai, Angalagunta Jaswanth, Cuddapah Anitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: Cloud storage platforms provide scalability and cost efficiency; however, they are vulnerable to insider threats, fake reputation building, and coordinated manipulation attacks. Moreover, traditional role-based access control mechanisms focus primarily on authentication and authorization, which are insufficient to address dynamic behavioural threats such as Sybil attacks, collusion, and identity spoofing. Thus, this paper presents a Trust-Driven Task-Role-Based Access Control (T-RBAC) framework for secure cloud data sharing that integrates behavioural trust evaluation with role-based permissions. The system works by calculating a dynamic trust score for each user based on behavior, feedback credibility, file usage patterns, anomalies. Advanced detection mechanisms identify malicious activities such as fake rating injection, multiple identity creation, and collusive behaviors. Only users who meet the predefined trust score threshold set by data owners are allowed to access the data. The system is implemented using a Spring Boot-based web architecture with modules for administrators, data owners, and users, including simulated attackers for experimental validation. Experimental results validate the system’s ability to filter malicious users, maintain trust consistency for genuine users, and increase protection against reputation-based attacks without compromising performance.

title: A Behavioral Benchmark Dataset for Evaluating Access Control Compliance in Large Language Models
authors: Yunsong Xin, Chen Yang, Zhihui Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as a unified natural-language access gateway inside organizations, yet adversarially crafted queries can induce violations of role constraints. Existing access-control benchmarks largely assume non-adversarial requests and focus on permission-reasoning correctness, leaving decision-level behavioral compliance under adversarial phrasing insufficiently measured. We present ACBench, a query-only adversarial benchmark for evaluating access-control compliance of LLMs under role-based access control (RBAC), namely, whether a model denies when it should deny and avoids unnecessary refusals on authorized requests. ACBench is built on an explicit RBAC permission matrix, instantiates representative adversarial strategies, and provides two difficulty splits (L1/L2) to capture the escalation from explicit to semantically implicit requests. We report overall accuracy (ACC), attack success rate (ASR; DENY→ALLOW), and over-refusal rate (ORR; ALLOW→DENY). Experiments on open-source instruction models and a commercial model (GPT-4.1) show non-zero privilege-escalation risk on L1 and a sharp increase in ASR on L2, with generally low ORR on benign queries though non-zero false denials can occur. These results highlight the practical risk of implicit adversarial access requests, particularly for open-source LLMs, and motivate stronger defenses for secure deployment.

title: Resilient Distributed Extended Object Tracking Under FDI Attacks
authors: Wei Xia, Kaidi Yang, Xuefei Feng, Mengqing Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: We consider tracking an extended object modeled by an ellipse with an adversarial network, each node of which may be compromised by false data injection (FDI) attacks. We develop a resilient distributed extended object tracking algorithm with enhanced tracking accuracy, leveraging the diffusion strategy. Specifically, we develop an attack detection and defense mechanism such that each node could determine whether it is compromised or not, so as to restrain the propagation of malicious data owing to FDI attacks. We further propose a computationally efficient distributed extended object tracking algorithm for secure networks as a by-product. Moreover, we analyze the computational complexity of the proposed algorithms. Illustrative simulation results validate the efficacy of the proposed algorithm for adversarial networks under FDI attacks, especially under potentially time-varying attacks.

title: Linearly and Circularly Polarized Fabry-Perot Cavity Antennas Applied for Radar Detection Systems
authors: Bing Fan, Juhua Liu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Fabry-Perot cavity antennas (FPCAs) with linear polarization and circular polarization are designed and studied in a radar detection system. The designed linearly polarized (LP) FPCA and circularly polarized (CP) FPCA exhibit wide impedance bandwidths of 15% and 21%, 3 dB gain bandwidths of 12.7% and 23.1%, and high peak gains of 17.9 dBi and 18.7 dBi, respectively. A radar detection system with the LP and CP antennas is studied in three target-detection scenarios: wall, street lamp poles, and power lines. Results show that the radar system yields a high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in all three scenarios. Moreover, in the power line scenario, the radar system with CP-FPCA exhibits higher received signal intensity (exceeding 3–10 dB) and higher SNR (by 6–16 dB) than that with LP-FPCA.

title: A Residual Attention Root-MUSIC Network-based DOA Estimator for Mixed Circular and Non-circular Signals
authors: Yanyan Guo, Liping Teng, Hua Chen, Wei Liu, Jinho Choi, Hing Cheung So
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: A joint source enumeration and direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation method for mixed circular and non-circular signals based on a residual attention root-MUSIC network (RARN) is proposed by leveraging both the conjugated covariance and unconjugated covariance matrices of mixed signals with the circularity difference. The RARN consists of two sub-networks: Source Enumeration Network (SEN) and Angle Estimation Network (AEN). The SEN adopts a dual-branch structure and attention mechanisms to extract distinct features from both types of signals, ensuring the accuracy of multi-task learning. Additionally, using residual connections increases the depth of the network and improves its generalization ability. Meanwhile, AEN includes the conjugated covariance and unconjugated covariance matrix reconstruction blocks with the prior source number estimates from SEN, and utilizes the root-MUSIC block for DOA estimation. Simulation results show that RARN outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms in estimation accuracy and generalization ability, especially in scenarios with low signal-to-noise ratio or small number of snapshots.

title: Knowledge Guided Conditional Diffusion with Dual-Contrastive Regularization for Real-world Image Dehazing
authors: Haoran Wei, Qingbo Wu, Chenhao Wu, Xiaofeng Cong, Mingzhou He, King Ngi Ngan, Hongliang Li, Fanman Meng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Existing image dehazing methods often struggle with the practical applications due to the domain gaps between the synthetic training data and real-world hazy scenes. In our previous work, a Knowledge guided Conditional Diffusion (KCDiff) model is developed to jointly learn the synthetic data based domain specific knowledge (DSK) and real-world images based general knowledge (GK), which significantly improves the generalization performance of image dehazing. However, due to the potential conflicts between these two kinds of knowledge, the naive KCDiff may produce plausible artifacts especially in coping with images with heavy haze. To address this issue, we propose a KCDiff+ model to selectively incorporate the DSK into the GK with Dual-Contrastive Regularizations, i.e., Contrastive Perception Regularization (CPR) and Contrastive Instruction Regularization (CIR). More specifically, the KCDiff+ is firstly trained with synthetic paired hazy/clear data to learn the DSK and applied to the real-world hazy images to produce the initial dehazing results as their pseudo labels. Then, we utilize the real-world unpaired hazy/clear images to train a new KCDiff+ model, which learns the GK and refines the DSK based pseudo labels simultaneously. In this process, CPR is introduced to pull the real-world dehazing images closer to the positive samples that are selected from the initial and the refined pseudo labels with higher image qualities. Meanwhile, CIR is used to pull the real-world hazy images closer to negative samples, which is generated by feeding the KCDiff+ model with the DSK based pseudo labels and hazy weather based instructions. By means of these two contrastive regularizations, we efficiently suppress the knowledge conflict and enhance the hazy/clear instruction discrimination ability of KCDiff+ when jointly learn from the synthetic and real-world data. Experiments show KCDiff+ surpasses numerous state-of-the-art dehazing models in terms of both objective and subjective performances.

title: Energy-Efficient VLSI Architecture for Edge-based CNN Inference using Approximate Computing
authors: Dhanumjaya K, Sudharsan G, Balamanikandan A, Dhanalakshmi M, Venkata Karthika V, Sudhakar Reddy N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: With the proliferation of IoT and sensor-network nodes, on-device CNN inference must operate within milliwatt-scale power and sub-5 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area envelopes. This work presents a unified 16×16-PE accelerator that integrates three orthogonal low-power techniques error-bounded approximate multipliers, dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS), and fine-grained clock/power gating under a single Power Management Unit. Post-layout simulations on LeNet (MNIST) and MobileNet (CIFAR-10) show that the combined configuration delivers up to 50 % dynamic power reduction (150 mW to 75 mW), 33 % static power savings (30 mW to 20 mW), and 24 % area shrinkage (4.2 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 3.2 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), at the cost of just 1.4 % accuracy loss (99.2 % to 97.8 %) and a moderate 1.5 ms latency increase (5 ms to 6.5 ms). Across SS/TT/FF corners and supply rails from 0.6 V to 1.2 V, the accelerator maintains timing closure at 200 MHz and achieves over 150 FPS for LeNet at 0.8 V in an FPGA prototype. These results demonstrate a Pareto-optimal energy-accuracy trade-off for edge-AI deployments.

title: Geometry-Driven Performance Optimization of Dispense-Printed Interdigitated Electrodes for Potassium Biosensing
authors: Shamim Torkian, Pietro Ibba, Giulia Elli, Ahmed Rasheed, Sahira Vasquez, Paolo Lugli, Luisa Petti
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Printed impedimetric ion-selective sensors based on interdigitated electrodes (IDEs) enable low-cost and flexible ion sensing, yet the role of electrode geometry under practical printing constraints remains poorly understood. Here, dispense-printed carbon IDEs were systematically optimized by varying finger length and inter-electrode gap to study their effect on interfacial capacitance and impedimetric response. Capacitance measurements and analytical IDE modeling reveal a trade-off between electrical performance and printability, yielding an optimized geometry with a 7 mm finger length and a 250 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m gap. Dispense-printed potassium sensors integrating a PEDOT:PSS solid-contact layer and a drop-cast ion-selective membrane (ISM) show stable, concentration-dependent responses from 0.1 to 100 mM. At 10 kHz, the normalized impedance change reaches up to 39% per decade. These results highlight geometry-aware IDE design for scalable printed impedimetric ion sensors.

title: EvoAR-SFNet: Evolutionary Autoregressive Spatial-Frequency Network for Remote Sensing Pansharpening
authors: Shaohua Duan, Chunjie Zhang, Xiaolong Zheng, Yao Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Pansharpening aims to generate high-resolution multispectral (HRMS) images by fusing high-resolution panchro matic (PAN) images with low-resolution multispectral (LRMS) inputs. However, existing methods often face difficulties in effectively using spatial and frequency information, mainly due to limited fusion strategies and the lack of progressive refinement mechanisms. To overcome these limitations, we propose EvoAR SFNet (Evolutionary Autoregressive Spatial-Frequency Network), a novel framework for remote sensing pansharpening, comprising two specialized branches: a spatial branch employing Adaptive Rectangular Convolutions (ARConv) to capture spatial details, and a frequency branch adopting Frequency-Dynamic Convolu tions (FDConv) to extract frequency-aware features. Within each branch, Evolutionary Autoregressive (EvoAR) module performs multi-stage progressive refinement using autoregressive feedback. In addition, it adaptively adjusts loss weights using evolutionary optimization. The refined features from both branches are sub sequently integrated using a Bi-Domain Fusion Block (BDFB), which enhances cross-domain feature interactions through im plicit modulation, producing high-fidelity high-resolution mul tispectral outputs. Extensive experiments on WV3, QB, and GF2 datasets demonstrate that EvoAR-SFNet achieves competitive overall performance, especially in full-resolution evaluation, where it obtains strong spatial-detail preservation and leading QNR results.

title: Joint Optimization of 3D UAV Trajectory and Task Scheduling with Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Jiahui Chai, Yun Meng, Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-assisted Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) has emerged as a promising paradigm for supporting computation-intensive applications of ground User Equipments (UEs). However, with the growing computational demands for UEs, it becomes crucial and extremely challenging to jointly optimize the Three-Dimensional (3D) UAV trajectory and task scheduling decision, given UAVs’ limited coverage and scarce resources. To address these challenges, we develop a Multi-UAV Trajectory Optimization and Task Scheduling (MUTOTS) algorithm based on Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL). The proposed MUTOTS framework jointly optimizes multi-UAV 3D trajectories and task scheduling decisions, with the objective of maximizing total system throughput under strict flight-safety constraints. To further improve training stability and convergence, MUTOTS integrates prioritized experience replay and L2 regularization technologies. Moreover, MUTOTS can adapt to different take-off locations, and effectively guide UAVs approach near-optimal deployment locations during the execution stage. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the effectiveness, convergence and stability of the MUTOTS algorithm, and it significantly improves the system throughput compared with the other existing benchmark schemes.

title: Single Stage Three Phase Module and Modulation with Current stress Minimisation for Solid State Transformer based Fast Charging Stations
authors: P V Harisyam, Surjakanta Mazumder, Kaushik Basu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Direct integration with a medium-voltage AC (MVAC) grid is crucial for DC fast charging stations. Conventional solutions rely on step-down line-frequency transformers (LFTs) followed by low-voltage AC-DC and isolated DC-DC converters. However, LFTs’ size and weight increase the system footprint, reduce power density, and necessitate outdoor medium-voltage substations. One of the promising approaches that replaces the LFT with a high-frequency transformer that employs a number of isolated single-phase LVAC to LVDC converters in an input-series output-parallel (ISOP) combination. Single-stage LVAC-DC modules are preferred as they do not require large DC capacitors to absorb second harmonic power. The combination of DC side bridges to reduce the component count in some solutions leads to restricted freedom in modulation, higher current stress, only unidirectional-power-flow capability, or hard-switched operation. In this work, a three-phase bridge leg is introduced with a delta-connected DC side winding of the high-frequency transformers for a three-phase module. A DAB-derived novel optimal triple-phase shift modulation strategy is proposed that ensures bidirectional power flow, zero voltage switching over a wide range of line cycle, high quality power factor corrected input current without low frequency harmonics, and a significant reduction in current stress compared to the existing DAB-based solution. The paper details the converter’s design for the proposed modulation strategy and provides a comparative analysis. Experimental validation using a three-phase module demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach with high efficiency (96.1%) and power quality (THD = 4.3%).

title: Experimental Validation of 5G Positioning With Inter-Cell Clock Bias Correction
authors: Penghui Xu, Mattia Brambilla, Bernardo Camajori Tedeschini, Monica Nicoli
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The advancement of fifth generation (5G) cellular networks offers significant opportunities for positioning services, yet the performance of these systems is currently hindered by the clock offset found in commercial networks among 5G base stations, here referred to as inter-cell clock bias (ICCB). These biases introduce systematic errors in time of arrival (TOA) measurements, degrading localization accuracy. This paper addresses this challenge by utilizing a stationary reference receiver to estimate and correct the ICCBs. By aligning the timing references of multiple base stations, our single-difference correction method enables accurate standalone positioning using opportunistically received synchronization signal block (SSB) data. The proposed method was validated through three independent measurement campaigns at the same rural location on broadcast signals from a commercial 5G network. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the ICCB correction, showing a 73%–84% reduction in positioning error compared to uncompensated methods. Applying a smoothing filter to the ICCB corrections further improves the mean accuracy by about 18%–19%, achieving a mean error between 12.7m and 20.6m across the three tests. While this performance is constrained by the limited bandwidth of the current commercial 5G deployment at 3.68 GHz, our findings confirm the feasibility of leveraging public 5G signals for location-based services.

title: Robust GhostNet Architecture for Network Anomaly detection
authors: Akshatha Rao K J, Hanumanthappa J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: As the internet continues to develop, the security risks facing individuals are becoming increasingly complex. Standard methods of network attack detection are often ineffective against the growing variety and difficulty of cyber threats are led to issues like poor anomaly detection accuracy and rising frequency of false alerts. After reviewing the latest progressions in network anomaly detection from both global and local perspectives, this paper proposes a novel Deep Learning (DL)-based solution: Enhanced GhostNet (En-GNet) anomaly detection strategy to tackle the accuracy limitations of existing detection systems. This strategy is performed using two stages: preprocessing and detection. During preprocessing, the input data is normalized via Min-Max normalization. The normalized data is then given to the detection stage, here the Multihead Axial Cross Attention based GhostNet (MACA-GNet) model is utilized to identify anomalies. The model applies Multihead Axial Cross Attention (MACA) and Piecewise Derivative of Sigmoid-weighted Linear Unit (PD-SiLU) to extract multiscale spatial features and enhance decision boundaries for more effective anomaly detection. The MACA-GNet method has distinguished the normal and anomalies attack accurately to meted the higher efficiency level of 97.3% in 90%.

title: The Effect of Engine and Cargo Hold Redesign on the Trim Characteristics of a Purse Seine Fishing Vessel
authors: Alif Nur Rochmad, Syafril Mayu Dinata, H Moh SaifulRahman
publish: 2025 IEEE Ocean Engineering Technology and Innovation Conference: Management and Conservation for Sustainable and Resilient Marine and Coastal Resources (OETIC)
abstract: Purse seine fishing vessels in Indonesia face high stability risks due to their operational method. Conventional layout designs often fail to accommodate these dynamic demands, there by compromising the vessel’s safety, especially when returning with a full catch. This study aims to improve the stability performance of a 30 GT purse seiner through a strategic redesign of its internal layout. Using numerical simulation methods with Maxsurf software, a stability analysis was conducted on the initial and proposed designs under two critical loading conditions: departure and arrival. The results were then evaluated against the IMO A.749(18) standard. The analysis shows that the initial design failed to meet the safety criteria in the arrival condition. The redesign intervention, which included relocating the engine room, modifying the fuel tank design, and optimizing the storage position of the fishing gear to lower the vessel’s center of gravity, proved to be highly effective. This redesign resulted in a 44.4% increase in the metacentric height (GM) in the critical condition and ensured that all GZ curve parameters met the IMO standard. This study concludes that the optimization of the internal layout is a practical and impactful engineering approach to significantly enhance the operational safety of the purse seine fleet.

title: Robust Malicious Network Traffic Detection Framework with Automated Drift Detection, Identification, and Adaptation
authors: Xueying Han, Jian Qin, Changzhi Zhao, Weike Fang, Junrong Liu, Weihang Wang, Bo Jiang, Susu Cui, Zhigang Lu, Baoxu Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The rise in network attacks has made robust malicious traffic detection crucial. However, the dynamic nature of network traffic causes concept drift, undermining the efficacy of traditional detection methods, which often rely on a static i.i.d data environment and struggle to adapt to new patterns. To overcome these limitations, we propose Argus, a novel framework for malicious traffic detection that operates in a comprehensive, automated, and adaptive manner. Argus tackles three core challenges: accurately classifying known traffic while detecting drift, automatically identifying malicious drifting traffic, and maintaining performance through continuous updates. To address these challenges, Argus integrates a contrastive learningbased module to produce compact representations of traffic and implements a fine-grained drift detection method using category-specific reconstruction loss distributions. For drifting traffic, Argus uses clustering-based automated identification to detect attacks without human intervention. Furthermore, a distance-constrained update mechanism ensures smooth model adaptation, preserving stability and accuracy. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Argus achieves superior performance, with an average F1 score exceeding 95% under various conditions and retaining robust performance even under extreme drift scenarios.

title: Drift-Aware Agentic ETL for Schema-Evolving Data Lakes
authors: Weican Wang, Ye Zhang, Zichen Pan, Ziruo Ke, Yilin Sun, Yun Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Schema evolution in data lakes frequently causes downstream ETL pipeline failures, silent data corruption, and metric drift. Existing approaches address schema matching or data cleaning in isolation but lack a closed-loop mechanism that detects structural changes, generates repair strategies, and validates corrections before deployment. We propose Drift-Aware Agentic ETL (DAETL), a framework that integrates schema drift detection with LLM-powered repair agents constrained by data contracts, historical lineage, and sample-based validation. Central to our design is a Repair Confidence Score (RCS) that combines semantic matching confidence, historical transformation consistency, sample verification pass rate, and downstream distribution divergence to decide between automatic patch acceptance and human escalation. We evaluate DAETL on a controlled schema evolution benchmark spanning relational, semi-structured JSON, and event-stream datasets with five categories of injected drift. Results show that DAETL achieves 92.4% pipeline recovery rate and 91.8% data correctness under controlled drift injections, outperforming rule-based, detection-only, and unconstrained LLM baselines. Ablation studies confirm that each constraint source contributes measurably to repair quality.

title: Wearable IoT–Driven Activity and Calorie Insights Using Gradient Boosting on Real-World Sensor Streams
authors: Rana Alotaiby, Sayyed Khawar Abbas
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: Recent advances in wearable Internet of Things (IoT) technologies have enabled continuous health monitoring by collecting multimodal physiological, behavioral, and environmental signals. However, achieving accurate, interpretable, and computationally efficient analytics for such heterogeneous data remains a key challenge. This paper presents a gradient boosting–based framework for real-time activity recognition and energy expenditure estimation using data from multi-device wearable sensors. The proposed approach leverages CatBoost to model nonlinear interactions in tabular sensor streams while natively handling categorical variables and temporal dynamics through rolling statistical features and circadian encodings. Using a dataset of 1,000 time-stamped observations from ten devices across multiple activity types and locations, the CatBoost classifier achieved approximately 94% accuracy and a high macro-F1 score for multiclass activity recognition, while the regressor attained a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.54 and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.76 for calorie estimation.

title: DCHPC 2026 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)

title: An Effective Rotor Current-Based Method for Automatic Stator Turn-to-Turn Fault Detection and Fault Severity Classification in DFIGs
authors: Abdelwahab D. Bebars, Etornam Komla Ahorsu, Mahdi Mehrtash, Hanif Livani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This paper presents a novel method for detecting stator Inter Turn Short Circuit Fault (ITSCF) in Doubly-Fed Induction Generators (DFIGs) using Rotor Current Signature Analysis (RCSA) combined with convolutional neural networks (CNNs). First, a comprehensive space-vector-based dynamic model of the DFIG was developed in Matlab/Simulink® to represent a variable-speed wind turbine system, including detailed modeling of wind speed variations, mechanical dynamics, and a four-quadrant back-to-back converter. The model was then validated offline under various wind and fault scenarios by analyzing rotor current residuals with the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT), enabling the identification of fault-induced harmonic components. Next, the system was implemented in a real-time simulation environment on the OPAL-RT platform with RT-LAB, demonstrating both real-time feasibility and computational efficiency. Finally, synthetic rotor current data comprising 898,907 labeled samples across eight wind speeds and seven fault severity levels were used to train a CNN with three convolutional blocks (64, 128, 256 filters), global average pooling, and dropout regularization. The proposed method achieved 95.71% classification accuracy, with individual class accuracies ranging from 86.37% to 99.65%, outperforming other machine learning approaches reported in the literature, confirming that rotor current residuals are effective indicators of stator faults in DFIG-based wind turbine systems.

title: Dynamic Residual Self-Attention Mechanisms for Securing IoT Wireless Sensor Systems Against Malicious Nodes
authors: Natrayan L, M.Siva Ramkumar, Kiruthika Balakrishnan, R.V.Rangarajan, Anand Rajendran, Ramya M
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: WSNs function as essential IoT components to provide purposes across healthcare systems and military operations and security applications. WSN architecture faces severe security risks because they distribute their resources while also operating with limited platform capabilities. Security protocols today experience difficulties in preserving the best possible transmission consistency levels while performing threat detection effectively. The proposed solution addresses this issue through Dynamic Residual Self-Attention Network with Snow Ablation Optimizer (DRSANet-SAO) for malicious node detection along with secure data transmission. The DRSANet model evaluates node activities through SAO that optimizes the weight parameters to obtain enhanced precision. The selection of Cluster Heads through Fennec Fox Optimization (FFO) together with its secure routing mechanism ensures better stability across the network. DRSANet-SAO reaches 99.8% accuracy combined with 99.8% sensitivity and 99.8% specificity while maintaining a 0.2% error rate in detection tests. The security infrastructure reaches 55 Mbps of transmission speed and 8.9 milliseconds of delay time ensuring fast and protected data transfer operations. The security capabilities of IoT-WSN grow through implementation of the proposed model.

title: A Semi-Supervised Entropy-Aware Machine Learning Model for DDoS Attack Identification
authors: SVSV Prasad Sanaboina, R.Meghana, Nausheen Begum, K.Siddartha Reddy, K.Shilpa, G.Swathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: DDoS attacks which distribute denial of service attacks through multiple sources to create high volumes of malicious traffic which aims to disrupt server and network operation present a major danger to current network systems. The system becomes inaccessible to authentic users while network capacity experiences substantial decline. This research aims to develop an automatic system that uses machine learning methods for DDoS detection through analysis of network traffic patterns and flow data. The research treats network traffic flows as structured data instances which allow researchers to identify various features including packet rate and flow duration and protocol usage and source–destination communication patterns. The system uses both labeled data and unlabeled traffic data to solve the limitations faced by traditional supervised systems which need complete attack data in actual environments where attack data is not available. The study utilizes semi-supervised learning methods together with statistical methods and entropy-based feature selection techniques to find network traffic patterns which can separate normal system operations from DDoS attacks. The proposed model maintains its ability to identify and categorize different attack types through its use of adaptive techniques which enable it to handle changing traffic patterns and detect attacks with low rates and new patterns that traditional security systems fail to identify. The experimental results show that the proposed method achieves better detection performance and decreases false positive rates while showing improved capacity to adjust which makes it appropriate for detecting DDoS attacks in networks that change over time.

title: RAG-Based LLM in Intellicise Networks: A Comprehensive Review and A Semantic Information Transmission Framework
authors: Jiarong Lu, Xi Li, Shanghe Shi, Heli Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Semantic information transmission assisted by large language model (LLM) is regarded as an efficient paradigm in intellicise networks. This paper provides a comprehensive review of semantic communication based on LLM with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in intellicise networks. On this basis, we propose an innovative semantic information transmission framework that incorporates RAG-based LLM to address vertical domain knowledge and resource allocation. In this framework, RAG is utilized to improve the accuracy of semantic information in vertical domains. Given the dependencies among multiple LLM agents in RAG, it is necessary to design a resource allocation scheme to balance semantic richness and latency. Therefore, regarding heterogeneous computing and transmission resources, a multi-objective problem aimed at maximizing semantic richness and minimizing latency is formulated. Then, the problem of jointly optimizing user-server matching, graphics processing unit allocation, semantic information length, and power allocation is formulated. The problem is nonlinear mixed-integer programming. Gibbs sampling and Lagrangian duality are applied to transform it. Finally, an algorithm for resource allocation is designed. Simulations demonstrate that the proposed algorithm has superiority in improving semantic richness and reducing latency.

title: AdaGNN: Adaptive-Boosting Graph Neural Networks for Node Classification
authors: Kai-Hsiang Fan, Cheng-Te Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have become the de facto standard for learning over graph-structured data, yet they often falter when labels are scarce, graphs are noisy, or structural patterns are complex. While boosting is a powerful ensemble paradigm that sequentially corrects model errors, existing adaptations to graphs either break end-to-end training by introducing external boosters or resort to explicit sample reweighting that undermines relational inductive biases. To overcome these challenges, we introduce adaptive-boosting graph neural networks (AdaGNN), a fully integrated boosting framework designed specifically for graph learning. AdaGNN trains a series of GNN learners in a unified pipeline: each learner is warm-started from the previous one and ingests the progressively refined node embeddings. After each round, correctly classified nodes are frozen, i.e., their embeddings locked and excluded from subsequent updates, so that remaining learners focus solely on residual errors. This Embedding Freeze mechanism implicitly propagates error signals through the graph topology without requiring explicit weight adjustments or multi-stage coordination. Comprehensive evaluations across varied supervision levels demonstrate that AdaGNN consistently elevates classification accuracy and robustness, delivering substantial gains under limited labels while preserving or enhancing performance when labels are plentiful. AdaGNN establishes a novel, graph-native adaptive boosting paradigm—melding sequential GNN refinement, implicit error handling, and end-to-end training—to significantly advance the accuracy and robustness of GNN applications.

title: Enhancing Cloud Network Resilience via a Robust LLM-Empowered Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework
authors: Yixiao Peng, Hao Hu, Feiyang Li, Xinye Cao, Yingchang Jiang, Jipeng Tang, Guoshun Nan, Yuling Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: While virtualization and resource pooling empower cloud networks with structural flexibility and elastic scalability, they inevitably expand the attack surface and challenge cyber resilience. Reinforcement Learning (RL)-based defense strategies have been developed to optimize resource deployment and isolation policies under adversarial conditions, aiming to enhance system resilience by maintaining and restoring network availability. However, existing approaches lack robustness as they require retraining to adapt to dynamic changes in network structure, node scale, attack strategies, and attack intensity. Furthermore, the lack of Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) support limits interpretability and flexibility. To address these limitations, we propose CyberOps-Bots, a hierarchical multi agent reinforcement learning framework empowered by Large Language Models (LLMs). Inspired by MITRE ATT&CK's “Tactics-Techniques” model, CyberOps-Bots features a two-layer architecture: (1) An upper-level LLM agent with four mod ules—ReAct planning, IPDRR-based perception, long-short term memory, and action/tool integration—performs global awareness, human intent recognition, and tactical planning; (2) Lower-level RL agents, developed via heterogeneous separated pre-training, execute atomic defense actions within localized network regions. This synergy preserves LLM adaptability and interpretability while ensuring reliable RL execution. Experiments on real cloud datasets show that, compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, CyberOps-Bots maintains network availability 68.5% higher and achieves a 34.7% jumpstart performance gain when shifting the scenarios without retraining. To our knowledge, this is the first study to establish a robust LLM-RL framework with HITL support for cloud defense.

title: Initial characteristics of positive streamer discharge under impulse voltage in high-speed airflow
authors: Qiang Wu, Yeqiang Deng, Zeliang Wu, Zhengxian Du, Yu Wang, Haotian Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The streamer discharge is the earliest discharge stage under impulse voltage and usually carried out in stationary air in the previous studies. However, the breakdown discharge characteristics of high-speed moving objects or those exposed to strong winds differ from those of traditional stationary targets, and the streamer discharge is important for subsequent discharge process. Therefore, it is significant to study the effect of air flow on the initial characteristics of streamer discharges. In this study, positive streamers under impulse voltage were investigated using a 2.5 mm diameter rod electrode in a wind tunnel, with a maximum airflow speed of 100 m/s. The tests were carried out in a darkroom, where discharge voltage and current were measured, and streamer morphology was captured. The experimental results indicate that regardless of the airflow speed, the initiation time and initiation voltage of the streamer decreases with increasing voltage rise rate. As the airflow speed increases, the initiation time of the streamer also decreases, accompanied by a reduction in dispersion. The streamer initiation voltage follows a similar trend to the initiation time, but with less consistency. From the discharge images, the streamer stem and its branches form a conical distribution, and increasing airflow causes the streamer stem to shorten and narrow. The injected charge of the streamer depends quadratically on the inception voltage; while the airflow alters the operating point along this relationship, the functional form of the dependence remains unchanged. Finally, the concept of critical volume was used to explain the experimental results.

title: An Integrated Parallel Computing and Metabolic Biomarker Framework for Enhanced Prediction of Type 2 Diabetes and Insulin Resistance
authors: Mina Gholami, Mohammad Mahdi Gorzin, Radin Ashrafi, Benyamin Safipourian, Soroush Bayat, Negar Rezaei, Amirparsa Rahmani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Distributed Computing and High Performance Computing (DCHPC)
abstract: This study presents an integrated computational framework that synergistically combines novel metabolic biomarkers with parallel computing architecture to achieve breakthrough performance in type 2 diabetes mellitus prediction. The research introduces two innovative biomarkers—the Glucose-Insulin Index and Advanced Metabolic Risk Score—specifically engineered to capture insulin resistance dynamics, while implementing a parallel computing system using joblib that reduces model training time by sixty-five percent through efficient multi-core processing. Our methodology integrates diverse datasets comprising three thousand two hundred thirty-three clinical samples from North American, European, and South Asian populations and employs rigorous preprocessing pipelines, advanced feature engineering, and comprehensive hyperparameter optimization across six machine learning algorithms. The XGBoost model demonstrated superior performance, achieving exceptional metrics including 0.9816 area under the receiver operating characteristic curve, 93.27% cross-validation accuracy, and 0.9425 F1-score on independent test data. Crucially, effect size analysis revealed the Glucose-Insulin Index as the dominant predictor with a standardized effect size of 3.67, strongly validating insulin resistance as the central pathway in diabetes pathogenesis. SHAP analysis ensured clinical interpretability, identifying skin thickness, insulin, and glucose as key predictive drivers. This research establishes a new paradigm in diabetes prediction by simultaneously addressing computational scalability, clinical interpretability, and metabolic pathway specificity. The framework's demonstrated performance superiority over existing methods—which typically achieve seventy-eight to eighty-eight percent accuracy—combined with its computational efficiency and clinical transparency, positions it as a cornerstone for next-generation precision medicine in diabetes prevention and early detection strategies across diverse populations.The framework integrates novel metabolic biomarkers with a lightweight parallel hyperparameter optimization pipeline using joblib on multi-core hardware, achieving a ~65% reduction in tuning time (speedup ≈2.88× on 12 cores) while maintaining exceptional predictive performance.

title: Construction and Application of an Agent-Driven Active Learning Ecosystem under Human-AI Collaboration
authors: Wei Nie, Zhanjiang Yuan, Jianhua Hu, Wen Ha, Xiangfei Feng, Qixiang Zhang, Renzhi Wang, Juxiu Zhu, Wenjing Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: Based on the concept of human-AI collaboration, this paper constructs and applies an agent-driven active learning ecosystem. This ecosystem integrates intelligent tools such as project-based learning, large language model questioning, and AI programming assistants. Centered on students, it leverages heuristic activities to stimulate learning motivation, promotes active thinking, and enhances critical thinking and problem-solving abilities. Specifically, the ecosystem organizes agents as pedagogical tutors, co-creative partners, metacognitive coaches, and orchestration agents; a hybrid design (LLM + lightweight rules/analytics) with teacher-in-the-loop guidance is intended to move students toward constructive and interactive learning engagement.

title: A Dynamic Security Warning Mechanism for SDN Based on Alert Information
authors: Ruixin Hu, Liguo Fang, Haitao Lv
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: In response to the current shortcomings of software-defined network (SDN) security assessment systems in dynamic threat quantification, especially the lack of real-time security-warning capability, this paper proposes a dynamic security early-warning mechanism for SDN that is based on alarm information. The mechanism first uses the XGBoost machine learning algorithm to the alarm-related attributes and determine their weights, so as to evaluate the early warning intensity of each alarm information. At the same time, the vulnerability scoring system is used to calculate value of the vulnerability, combined with the importance of the SDN device calculated by the PageRank (PR) algorithm, to deduce the threat transfer probability between the devices. On this, the potential attack path is analyzed by constructing the Bayesian attack graph, and the real-time early warning intensity of different devices is dynamically calculated by comprehensively warning intensity and threat transfer. The experimental results show that this mechanism can achieve real-time dynamic security early warning of devices on the attack path in SDN, and provide effective basis for the implementation of and timely security protection strategy.

title: Research on Brain Tumor Detection System on FA-YOLO Model
authors: Mingchang Huang, Binnan Yan, Ziwen Xiong, Yichenghan Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Generative Artificial Intelligence and Information Security (GAIIS)
abstract: This study proposes the FA-YOLO model for detecting brain tumor, introducing a new attention mechanism in the backbone network to improve detection accuracy. The system identifies brain tumor through MIT images. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model excels in accuracy and recall, and the system has broad application potential for tumor detection. This work provides significant support for brain health initiatives and offers new perspectives for the application of deep learning technologies in this field.

title: Intelligent Closed-Loop Control of a BLDC Motor using ANN-based PWM Regulation and IoT Integration
authors: Ajith B Singh, Madumitha S, Justin J, Libin Jebel I, Dhanaselvam J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Electronic Devices and Intelligent Systems (ICSEDIS)
abstract: The intelligent ANN-based PWM control strategy for the brushless DC (BLDC) motor drive is presented in this paper. The proposed system is based on the use of an artificial neural network to determine the non-linear relationship between the measured electrical parameters and the optimal PWM duty cycle for the operation of the motor. The PWM values are predicted by the ANN controller and sent to the microcontroller through NodeMCU. The microcontroller generates equivalent command signals to drive the motor by modulating the ESC. The proposed technique requires no specific analytical model of the motor like other standard model-based control techniques. Experimental results demonstrate significantly better transient response and lower current oscillations than fixed-duty PWM operation. This modular architecture enables the system for scalable motor drive applications. Furthermore, IoT communication integration via the NodeMCU platform allows for real-time monitoring and flexible system interfacing for sophisticated motor drive applications. The suggested architecture improves flexibility to changing loads by dynamically altering the PWM control signals depending on the learned ANN behavior.
